title: Forge proxy: cache parsed worker log + parse only new bytes since last seek
description:
The Mezzanine page polls `/api/forge/workers/{id}/log/parsed?tail=500` every few seconds. On long-running smiths the log file grows to 1MB+ (chatty opus-4-7 transcripts). Each poll re-reads and re-parses the *entire* file just to extract the tail, even though 99% of the bytes haven't changed since the last call.

When this collides with the smith itself burning CPU and the rest of Mezzanine's polls hitting the same Hytte process, the whole Hytte web app stalls — observed today: `/api/workhours/flex` (119-byte response) took 44s, `/api/forge/queue/all` took 21s, load avg hit 24 on a 4-core box. Once the smith finished, everything snapped back to normal.

## Fix: cache parsed entries with an offset, parse only new bytes per request

Server-side in `internal/forge/handlers.go` `WorkerParsedLogHandler`:

- Keep an in-process LRU-style cache keyed by `(workerID, resolvedLogPath)`. Each cache entry holds:
    - `mu          sync.Mutex` (serialises concurrent reads for one log)
    - `entries     []LogEntry` (the cumulative parsed entries)
    - `nextOffset  int64` (byte offset in the file where parsing should resume)
    - `modTime     time.Time` (file mtime when we last read it)
    - `size        int64` (file size when we last read it)
- On request:
    1. Stat the file. If `size < nextOffset` (log was rotated/truncated/replaced) or `modTime` jumped backwards, **invalidate** the cache for this key and parse from byte 0 again. Otherwise:
    2. Open the file, `Seek(nextOffset, io.SeekStart)`, scan only the new bytes, append the new parsed entries to the cached slice, update `nextOffset` to the new file size, refresh `modTime` and `size`.
    3. Slice the cumulative entries by the `?tail=N` query (or the full slice if `tail` is absent / >= len).
    4. Return JSON as today.
- Concurrency: hold the per-key mutex for the parse-and-update section so two concurrent requests for the same worker don't double-parse the same new bytes. Different workers proceed in parallel.
- LRU cap: keep at most ~32 entries (we have <10 active workers typically). Evict oldest by last-access timestamp. Memory: each cached entries slice is bounded by the file size; 32 × 1–2 MB = 32–64 MB worst case, fine.
- Cache lives on the `Server` (or whatever struct already owns the DB pointer). Don't introduce a package-global if it can be avoided.

## Smaller tweaks while we're here

- Drop the **default** `tail` value. Today the frontend always sends `tail=500`; if the frontend changes to omit it, return the full log. This is a frontend concern but worth documenting on the endpoint.
- Reject `tail` values above some sane cap (e.g. 5000) with 400 — defensive.

## Tests

- `internal/forge/handlers_test.go` (or wherever the existing parsed-log tests live):
    - First call parses the whole file; second call against an unchanged file does NOT re-parse byte 0 (assert via a parse-count counter or a fake reader).
    - Append bytes to the file → next call parses only the new bytes and returns the combined entries.
    - Truncate / replace the file → cache invalidates and re-parses from start.
    - Concurrent requests for the same worker → only one parse pass; result returned to both.
    - `tail` clamps as expected; `tail` above cap returns 400.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Manually: while a long-running smith is producing a >1 MB log, repeatedly call `/api/forge/workers/{id}/log/parsed?tail=500`. Subsequent calls return in <100 ms even when the log is 5+ MB.
- During the same scenario, other Hytte endpoints (e.g. `/api/workhours/flex`) stay snappy.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Non-goals

- Server-Sent Events streaming of new log lines. Recommended as a follow-up — option A from the diagnostic discussion. This bead is the smaller, lower-risk fix.
- Bounding the cumulative entries slice to a hard size. The log files themselves are bounded by Forge's worker lifecycle, so unbounded growth in-memory is bounded too. If we ever see >100 MB logs, revisit.
- Persisting the cache across Hytte restarts. Rebuilding on the first post-restart call is cheap.

## Reference

- internal/forge/handlers.go:1841 — `WorkerParsedLogHandler`, the target.
- internal/forge/handlers.go:1918 — current `ParseWorkerLog(resolvedPath)` call that reads the whole file.
- internal/forge/logparser.go:56 — `ParseWorkerLog` signature. Either extend with an `offset` arg, or add a sibling `ParseWorkerLogFrom(r io.Reader, startSeq int) ([]LogEntry, error)` that the cached path can call with a seeked-open file.
- Today's incident in `journalctl -u hytte` between 10:25–10:28 — `/api/forge/workers/.../log/parsed?tail=500` took 41s while a 1.6 MB smith log was active on Hytte-e313.

title: Optional: cancellation endpoint + changelog
description: If feasible without scope creep, add `POST /api/suggestions/run/{id}/cancel` (admin-only) in `internal/suggestions/handlers.go`. Implementation: a shared `map[int64]*atomic.Bool` (or context-cancel registry) keyed by run_id; the streaming goroutine checks the flag between pages and exits cleanly, updating `suggestion_runs` with `finished_at`. If this complicates the streaming sub-task, defer entirely — the bead explicitly says it is optional. Add a changelog fragment under `changelog.d/` (category Changed and/or Added) describing: streaming async run, 4-minute per-page cap, rotation default raised to 20. Connects to: caps off the bead's acceptance criteria.

title: Backend tests: SSE handler, concurrency, disconnect
description: Add tests in `internal/suggestions/handlers_test.go` (or equivalent). Reuse the existing `runPromptFn` seam (per the `bead.go` mock pattern referenced in the bead) to mock Claude calls. Tests: (1) SSE happy path — connect to `POST /api/suggestions/run`, drive N mocked per-page calls, parse SSE stream, assert `started`, `page_complete` ×N, `new_page_complete`, `done` events arrive in order with the correct shape; (2) Concurrency — fire two simultaneous POSTs, assert second returns 409 with `{error, run_id}`; (3) Disconnect — start request, cancel client context mid-stream, wait for goroutine, assert `suggestion_runs` row has `finished_at` set and final totals are correct, and persisted suggestions for completed pages exist. Connects to: validates the streaming handler sub-task's behaviour.

title: Frontend: streaming progress UI in Suggestions page
description: Modify `web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx`. Replace `handleRunNow`'s `fetch().then(refetch)` with a fetch+ReadableStream SSE consumer (native EventSource is GET-only). Reference `web/src/pages/Webhooks.tsx` for existing SSE pattern. State: `runProgress: {done, total, pageSlugs, log: Array<{slug, status, count?, cost?, reason?}>}`. On `started`: show header pill 'Run in progress: 0 / Y pages' with progress bar. On `page_complete`: increment counter, append log row ('✓ weather (3 ideas, $0.04)' or '✗ webhooks (timeout, 240s)'), refetch Pending tab so new suggestions appear. On `done`: clear in-progress UI, refetch all tabs + Recent runs panel. On stream error/interruption: keep UI with 'Reconnect' button; optionally poll `GET /api/suggestions/runs?limit=1` to detect completion. On 409 response: show banner 'A run is already in progress' with link to runs panel. Add i18n keys to en/nb/th `suggestions:` namespace: `run.inProgress`, `run.pageOk`, `run.pageError`, `run.alreadyRunning`, `run.reconnect`. Update `Suggestions.test.tsx`: mock SSE response sequence, assert progress pill updates and log appends; mock 409, assert banner. Connects to: consumes events from streaming handler sub-task.

title: Backend: bump PerPageTimeout, OverallRunTimeout, RotationDefaultN
description: In `internal/suggestions/generate.go`: change `PerPageTimeout` from `90 * time.Second` to `240 * time.Second` with comment explaining slow Claude calls on large pages. Bump `OverallRunTimeout` from 10 min to 30 min to accommodate 240s × 20 pages. In `internal/suggestions/rotation.go`: change `RotationDefaultN` from `10` to `20` with comment about ~35 eligible pages and tuning after observing cost. Confirm manual runs continue to bypass rotation quota (hit every eligible page) — the streaming handler should NOT apply the rotation cap for manual triggers. Connects to: streaming handler sub-task (handler uses these constants); ensures the streaming run has enough headroom to finish.

title: Backend: SSE streaming run handler with detached context
description: Modify `internal/suggestions/handlers.go` to replace synchronous `RunHandler` with an SSE streaming handler. Set `Content-Type: text/event-stream`, flush headers, and run the work loop on `runCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), OverallRunTimeout)` in a goroutine so client disconnect doesn't abort persistence. Reference `internal/forge/handlers.go` SSE pattern. Insert a `suggestion_runs` row at start (via existing `InsertSuggestionRun`) and add a new `UpdateSuggestionRun` function in `internal/suggestions/runs.go` to set `finished_at` + final totals. Emit events: `started {run_id, total_pages, page_slugs}`, `page_complete {page_slug, generated, errors, cost_usd, elapsed_ms, status, error?}` after each `runForPage` returns, `new_page_complete` after `RunNewPageSuggestion`, then `done {run_id, generated, errors, cost_usd}`. If `r.Context()` cancels, stop streaming but let the goroutine finish — add a comment. Concurrency guard: at handler entry, query `suggestion_runs` for `user_id` with `finished_at IS NULL`; if found, return 409 with `{error, run_id}`. Add migration for `CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_suggestion_runs_user_inflight ON suggestion_runs(user_id) WHERE finished_at IS NULL`. Verify `finished_at` is nullable per Hytte-eino. Connects to: timeouts/cap sub-task (uses bumped constants); frontend sub-task (consumes these events).

title: Suggestions: streaming async manual run + larger cap + longer per-page timeout
description:
The manual "Run now" button on the Suggestions page is broken in practice: with 35 eligible pages and a 90-second per-page Claude timeout, the run is too long for a synchronous HTTP request. In the first attempt, a single slow page (webhooks) was killed at 90s, then `r.Context()` got canceled — almost certainly because the browser/proxy dropped the long-pending response — and the remaining 28 pages were abandoned. 18 suggestions did persist (6 pages succeeded), so the data path is fine; only the run orchestration is wrong.

This bead replaces the synchronous endpoint with a streaming async run, bumps the per-page Claude timeout, and bumps the rotation cap.

## 1. Async streaming run

Replace the synchronous behaviour of `POST /api/suggestions/run` with a Server-Sent Events stream. Each per-page completion is emitted as an event so the UI can show progress in real time. The work itself runs on a context derived from `context.Background()`, so a client disconnect (browser tab closed, proxy timeout) never aborts the run.

### Backend behaviour

- `POST /api/suggestions/run` returns `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and stays open while the run executes.
- Before starting work, insert a `suggestion_runs` row with `started_at = now`, `finished_at = NULL`, `trigger = 'manual'`, page_slugs as today, generated/errors/cost = 0. Get the new `run_id`.
- Emit a `started` event with `{ run_id, total_pages, page_slugs }`.
- Run the loop on a context detached from the request: `runCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), OverallRunTimeout)` (and goroutine). Honor `notifCtx` for graceful shutdown.
- For each page completed: emit a `page_complete` event with `{ page_slug, generated, errors, cost_usd, elapsed_ms, status: "ok"|"error", error?: string }`. Persist suggestions immediately (they are already inserted in `runForPage` today; just emit the event after that returns).
- After per-page loop, run the new-page pass and emit `new_page_complete` with the same shape.
- At the end: update the `suggestion_runs` row with finished_at + final totals, then emit `done` with `{ run_id, generated, errors, cost_usd }` and close the stream.
- If the request's `r.Context()` is canceled mid-run, stop streaming events but **let the goroutine finish** so persistence is intact and `suggestion_runs` is updated with final totals. Add a small comment explaining this.
- Concurrency guard: at handler entry, check whether the user has an existing `suggestion_runs` row with `finished_at IS NULL`. If so, return 409 with `{ "error": "a run is already in progress", "run_id": N }`. The user can either wait or, if they suspect a stuck run, manually mark it finished — out of scope here.

### Schema tweak

`suggestion_runs.finished_at` is already nullable per Hytte-eino (the SuggestionRun struct already uses `*time.Time`). Verify and document.

Also add an in-progress index for the concurrency guard:

    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_suggestion_runs_user_inflight ON suggestion_runs(user_id) WHERE finished_at IS NULL;

(SQLite supports partial indexes; if it complicates anything, a plain `(user_id, finished_at)` index is fine.)

### Cancellation endpoint (optional but small)

If feasible without adding much code: `POST /api/suggestions/run/{id}/cancel` — admin-only — sets a flag the goroutine checks between pages so the user can stop a run in progress. If this complicates things, defer it; the rest of the bead is more important.

### Tests

- `bead.go`-style mock the bd-create pattern is the model: introduce a seam (`runPromptFn` already exists; reuse it) for tests.
- New SSE-handler test: connect, drive the per-page mocked Claude calls, assert `started`, `page_complete` (×N), `done` events arrive in order with the right shape.
- Concurrency test: two simultaneous POST requests; the second returns 409.
- Disconnect test: client cancels mid-stream; the goroutine still completes and the `suggestion_runs` row is updated.

## 2. Bump PerPageTimeout

Current 90s is too tight — webhooks blew through it. Bump `PerPageTimeout` from `90 * time.Second` to **`240 * time.Second` (4 minutes)**. Document the rationale in the constant's comment: bigger pages with truncated source files can still produce slow Claude calls; we'd rather wait than skip.

If a single timeout does not cover all callers, leave the per-page timeout at 240s and keep `OverallRunTimeout` at 30 minutes (raise it from the current 10 min to accommodate the longer per-page cap × the new larger rotation N).

## 3. Bump RotationDefaultN

Bump `RotationDefaultN` from `10` to **`20`** in `internal/suggestions/rotation.go`. With ~35 pages eligible, 20/night means each page is touched roughly every 1.75 days instead of 3.5. Comment: tune again after observing real cost from Recent runs panel.

For **manual** runs, keep the existing behaviour: NO rotation quota — manual runs hit every eligible page. With async/streaming, run length no longer matters for the UX.

## 4. Frontend: live progress UI

In `web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx`:

- Replace the current `handleRunNow` `fetch(...).then(refetch)` with an `EventSource` that connects to `POST /api/suggestions/run`. Note: native `EventSource` only does GET, so use the `fetch` + `ReadableStream` reader pattern to consume SSE from a POST. (See how the Webhooks page handles its SSE if there's an existing pattern; otherwise a minimal helper is fine.)
- While streaming: show a header pill "Run in progress: X / Y pages" with a small progress bar.
- On each `page_complete` event:
    - Refetch the Pending tab so newly-inserted suggestions appear immediately. (Lightweight re-call of `GET /api/suggestions` is OK; or, a small optimisation, fetch only the recent suggestions for the just-completed page slug.)
    - Append a short live log row underneath the header: "✓ weather (3 ideas, $0.04)" or "✗ webhooks (timeout, 240s)".
- On `done`: clear the in-progress UI, refetch all tabs, refresh the Recent runs panel.
- On error or stream interruption: leave the in-progress UI up with a "Reconnect" button. (Optional polish: poll `GET /api/suggestions/runs?limit=1` to see whether the current run finished while the stream was disconnected; if so, transition to done.)
- 409 from the POST: show a banner "A run is already in progress" with a link to the runs panel.

### i18n

Add new keys under `suggestions:` in en/nb/th:
- `run.inProgress` — "{{done}} / {{total}} pages"
- `run.pageOk` — "✓ {{slug}} ({{count}} ideas, {{cost}})"
- `run.pageError` — "✗ {{slug}} ({{reason}})"
- `run.alreadyRunning` — "A run is already in progress."
- `run.reconnect` — "Reconnect"

## 5. Frontend tests

Update `Suggestions.test.tsx`:
- Mock the SSE/streaming response with a sequence of events; assert the progress pill updates and the live log appends.
- Mock 409 response; assert the banner shows.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Hitting "Run now" with 35 eligible pages: the run progresses page-by-page in the UI; suggestions appear in Pending as each page completes; the request total time is no longer bounded by browser/proxy patience.
- Closing the browser tab mid-run: the `suggestion_runs` row still gets a `finished_at` and final totals; suggestions for completed pages are persisted.
- A second "Run now" while one is in progress returns 409 and the UI surfaces a clear banner.
- A page whose Claude call hangs for 90s now still has a chance to succeed (4-minute cap).
- Scheduled nightly runs cover up to 20 pages instead of 10; manual runs cover all eligible pages.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed and/or Added).

## Reference

- internal/suggestions/handlers.go — current synchronous RunHandler.
- internal/suggestions/generate.go — RunSuggestionsForPages and runForPage; PerPageTimeout constant.
- internal/suggestions/new_page.go — RunNewPageSuggestion; should be invoked at the end of the streaming run too.
- internal/suggestions/runs.go — InsertSuggestionRun; needs an UpdateSuggestionRun analogue for the start→finish flow.
- web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx — current handleRunNow.
- web/src/pages/Webhooks.tsx — existing SSE consumer pattern in the frontend, useful reference.
- internal/forge/handlers.go — has SSE handlers (`/api/forge/activity/stream`); useful reference for the backend SSE pattern.

title: Stride: scope field on notes to route between nightly eval and weekly plan gen
description:
Currently stride_notes are a single unconsumed pool — both the nightly evaluation (evaluate.go) and the weekly plan generation (generate.go) call ListNotes() with status='active' and consume whatever is unconsumed. This means a user has no way to direct a note at a specific consumer; whichever cron runs next eats everything. On Sunday→Monday transitions especially, the weekly plan gen at 02:00 CEST consumes notes the user intended for the nightly eval at 03:00 CEST.

Add a 'scope' column on stride_notes so notes can be routed to a specific consumer or left as 'any' (current behaviour).

Schema (follow the inline ALTER TABLE pattern in db.go createSchema, no migration files):
- ALTER TABLE stride_notes ADD COLUMN scope TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'any' (only when the column doesn't already exist; check against PRAGMA table_info)
- Allowed values: 'any', 'nightly', 'weekly' — enforce at the application layer (not a DB CHECK constraint, to stay consistent with the rest of the codebase's permissive schema)

Backend changes:
- internal/stride/stride.go ListNotes: accept an optional scope filter; when caller asks for 'nightly', match WHERE scope IN ('any','nightly'); when 'weekly', WHERE scope IN ('any','weekly'); empty/none = no scope filter (existing behaviour for UI listing)
- internal/stride/evaluate.go RunNightlyEvaluation: pass scope='nightly' when fetching notes for evaluation
- internal/stride/generate.go: pass scope='weekly' when fetching notes for plan generation
- CreateNote: accept a scope parameter (default 'any' for backward compatibility), validate against the allowed set
- New UpdateNote: allow editing scope, content, and target_date of an unconsumed note (must reject if consumed_at IS NOT NULL). Encrypt content on write.
- Mark-consumed logic stays as-is — only IDs that were actually fetched are marked, so scope filtering naturally limits what each consumer marks consumed.

API:
- POST /api/stride/notes — body extended with optional 'scope' field (defaults to 'any'); reject invalid values with 400
- PATCH /api/stride/notes/:id — body { content?, target_date?, scope? }; only owners of unconsumed notes can update; returns the updated note. 404 if not found, 409 if already consumed.
- GET /api/stride/notes — return scope on each note row

UI (web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx and any note components):
- Note creation form: add scope selector. Three options: 'Any next run' (default), 'Nightly evaluation', 'Weekly plan generation'. Wire to the new scope field.
- Note list: show scope as a small badge/pill next to each note (with translated labels)
- Note row: edit affordance for unconsumed notes, opens a modal/inline editor letting user update content, target_date, and scope. Hide for consumed notes.
- Mobile-first as always — selector should work on touch.

i18n:
- Add new keys to web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/ for the scope labels and the edit UI

Tests:
- Update existing evaluate_test.go and generate_test.go to reflect the new scope filtering — notes scoped 'nightly' must NOT be consumed by weekly, and vice versa; 'any' must still be consumed by either
- Add a test that PATCH on a consumed note returns 409
- Unit-test the scope validator
- Frontend smoke test for the new selector if a test pattern exists for note components

Backward compatibility:
- Existing notes get scope='any' via the DEFAULT — they continue to work exactly as today
- Old API clients that don't send 'scope' get 'any' applied — also unchanged behaviour

Non-goals:
- No changes to consumed_by values (still 'nightly', 'weekly', 'manual')
- No changes to the cron schedule
acceptance_criteria:
- ALTER TABLE adds the scope column idempotently (running twice does not error)
- All existing notes default to scope='any' after migration; existing behaviour preserved
- Nightly evaluation only consumes notes where scope IN ('any','nightly')
- Weekly plan generation only consumes notes where scope IN ('any','weekly')
- POST /api/stride/notes accepts optional scope and validates the value
- PATCH /api/stride/notes/:id updates scope/content/target_date for unconsumed notes; returns 409 for consumed notes
- Note creation UI shows the scope selector; default is 'any'; selection persists through to the database
- Note list UI shows the scope as a badge/pill
- Edit affordance lets the user change scope (and content/target_date) on unconsumed notes only
- Translations added to en, nb, and th
- go test ./internal/stride/... passes including new scope-filtering tests
- npm run lint and npm run build succeed

title: Achievement unlock overlay + fanfare
description: Create AchievementUnlockOverlay component (src/components/regnemester/AchievementUnlockOverlay.tsx) — a full-screen overlay with entry animation, showing the achievement icon/name/description, dismissible by tap or key. On mount: play fanfare sound via SoundEngine and fire navigator.vibrate([40,50,40,50,80]). Subscribe to the achievement-unlock event emitted by Hytte-ev5d (listen on the existing achievements store/event bus); queue overlays so multiple unlocks show sequentially. Respect prefers-reduced-motion (fade in/out instead of scale/bounce, skip haptic). Depends on sub-task 1 for sound/haptics primitives.

title: Confetti + milestone effects for Blitz and Marathon
description: Add canvas-confetti (or equivalent, keep added bundle <50KB gz) as confetti.ts wrapper exposing burst(intensity, palette) and screenShake(). Implement: Blitz streak milestones every 5 (small burst + streak-badge scale animation via CSS); Marathon timer milestones at 5:00/4:00/3:00 (subtle screen flash using the flash primitive); Marathon finish (new PB: standard; sub-5: golden palette; sub-3: rainbow burst + screenShake); Blitz finish (intensity scales with score; full-screen for #1 leaderboard — read leaderboard rank from existing finish payload). Hook into existing end-of-run and tick callbacks in Blitz/Marathon components. All effects gated by prefers-reduced-motion.

title: Per-answer feedback + speed bonus callouts wired into answer flow
description: Integrate the primitives from sub-task 1 into the answer submission path of all Regnemester modes (Blitz, Marathon, and any practice mode). On correct: call flashCorrect, haptics(10), sound 'correct'. On wrong: flashWrong, haptics(30), sound 'wrong'. Add SpeedCallout component (src/components/regnemester/SpeedCallout.tsx) that renders 'FAST!' (<2s) or 'LIGHTNING!' (<1s) floating up from the answer box using a CSS keyframe animation; measure response time from question-shown timestamp. Modify the existing answer handler(s) in the Regnemester game component(s) to invoke these hooks. No new game logic — presentational only.

title: Feedback primitives: answer flashes, haptics, sound engine, mute toggle
description: Build the foundational feedback layer used by every other sub-task. Create a SoundEngine module (src/lib/regnemester/feedback/sound.ts) that preloads short WebM/Ogg assets with mp3 fallback for Safari, honours a mute flag, and exposes play(name). Create haptics.ts wrapping navigator.vibrate with prefers-reduced-motion guard. Create flash.ts exposing flashCorrect(el) (green ~150ms) and flashWrong(el) (red ~250ms) via CSS class toggles defined in feedback.css. Add mute toggle persisted in user_preferences (extend existing preferences store) plus a global keyboard handler for 'M' during play. Expose a useFeedback() hook consumed by all Regnemester modes. Add sound assets under public/sounds/regnemester/ (correct, wrong, fanfare, milestone) keeping each <20KB. Respect prefers-reduced-motion throughout. This is the dependency for all remaining sub-tasks.

title: Regnemester: visual and audio dopamine pass
description:
Bead 7 of 7 for Regnemester — the polish pass. Depends on achievements (Hytte-ev5d). Apply visual and audio dopamine across all modes. This is the bead that makes the whole feature FEEL great, especially on mobile where a 10-year-old will play it.

Build:
- Correct answer feedback:
  - Brief green flash on the answer box (~150ms)
  - Haptic: navigator.vibrate(10) on mobile
  - Optional sound FX (short click/ding)
- Wrong answer feedback:
  - Red flash, slightly longer (~250ms)
  - navigator.vibrate(30)
  - Distinct sound (soft buzz)
- Streak milestones in Blitz (every 5): small confetti burst, streak badge animates/scales briefly
- Speed bonus callouts: 'FAST!' label flies up from answer box when response < 2s; 'LIGHTNING!' when < 1s
- Marathon timer milestones: subtle flash when passing 5:00, 4:00, 3:00 marks
- Marathon finish effects:
  - New PB: standard confetti shower
  - Sub-5: golden confetti
  - Sub-3: huge rainbow confetti + screen shake
- Blitz finish effects: confetti intensity scales to score (mild for any finish, heavy for PB, full-screen for #1 on any leaderboard)
- Achievement unlock: full-screen unlock overlay with animation, short fanfare sound, haptic pattern (vibrate([40,50,40,50,80])). Tap to dismiss.
- Mute toggle (persisted in user_preferences), plus keyboard 'M' shortcut during play
- All animations respect prefers-reduced-motion (reduce or disable when set)
- Prefer CSS animations for small effects; canvas-confetti is acceptable for bursts. Keep bundle impact reasonable (<50KB gzipped added)
- Sound files: use small (< 20KB each) WebM/Ogg, include mp3 fallback for Safari

Non-goals: no new game modes, no new data. This is purely presentational.
acceptance_criteria:
- All four feedback layers (color, haptic, sound, animation) active on both Marathon and Blitz
- Mute toggle works and persists across page reloads
- prefers-reduced-motion honored: animations reduce or disable
- Bundle size increase under 50KB gzipped (verify in build output)
- Manual QA on phone: haptics fire, no jank at 60fps, animations feel snappy not sluggish
- Achievement unlock overlay dismissible by tap/click or key

title: Regnemester: milestone achievements
description:
Bead 6 of 7 for Regnemester. Depends on heatmap (Hytte-iqvj). Achievement system with milestone definitions. Unlocks fire on session finish.

Build:
- Define achievement codes in Go (seeded in code, not a DB config table):
  - marathon_sub_10: Marathon completed in < 10:00
  - marathon_sub_7: Marathon completed in < 7:00
  - marathon_sub_5: Marathon completed in < 5:00 (the 'beat the hand-written benchmark' achievement)
  - marathon_sub_4: < 4:00
  - marathon_sub_3: < 3:00
  - marathon_perfect_100: Marathon finished with zero wrongs
  - streak_25, streak_50, streak_100: best streak in a single Blitz session reaches that threshold
  - first_blood: user's session landed them at #1 on any mode/period for the first time (i.e., they beat every other family member on that leaderboard)
- Each code has a Go struct: {code, title, description, tier, check func(session, user_stats) bool}
- Unlock logic: on POST /sessions/:id/finish, after session is committed, evaluate all applicable milestones; insert any newly-earned into math_achievements (UNIQUE(user_id, code) prevents dupes)
- API: GET /api/math/achievements — returns current user's earned list + all locked ones with progress text where possible (e.g., 'Marathon best: 5:42 — 42s to Sub-5')
- UI: /math/achievements page
  - Grouped by tier (Marathon / Blitz / Rivalry)
  - Earned shown in color with unlock date; locked shown greyed with progress
  - Toast or banner notification on fresh unlock (use whatever toast system Hytte already has; if none, a simple in-page banner — full celebration animation is in the polish bead)
  - Link from /math landing page
- Unlocked codes returned in /sessions/:id/finish response so the client can show them on the result screen

Non-goals: Table Master achievements (per-table mastery) — deferred. Celebration animation — polish bead.
acceptance_criteria:
- Achievements unlock exactly once per user per code (UNIQUE constraint enforced)
- All listed codes implemented with correct check logic
- /api/math/achievements returns earned + locked with progress for currently-locked ones where it makes sense
- Session finish response includes any newly-unlocked codes
- first_blood correctly detects 'this session took me to #1 on a leaderboard I wasn't #1 on before'
- Unit tests cover each check function

title: Regnemester: fact-mastery heatmap
description:
Bead 5 of 7 for Regnemester. Depends on leaderboards (Hytte-0p6m). Per-user 10×10 heatmap that visualizes mastery for each fact. This turns the game into practice-without-feeling-like-practice.

Build:
- /math/heatmap route
- Two tabs: Multiplication and Division (7×8 and 56÷8 track separately, both need drills)
- 10×10 grid: rows = first factor (1–10), columns = second factor (1–10)
- Cell coloring (from /api/math/stats data):
  - Gray: 0 attempts (unseen)
  - Red: ≥1 attempt but mastery_score low (accuracy < 80% over last 5 OR avg response_ms > 3000)
  - Yellow: intermediate (accuracy 80–99% over last 5 with avg 2000–3000ms)
  - Green (mastered): last 5 attempts all correct AND avg response < 2000ms
- Tap/click a cell to show a detail panel:
  - Total attempts, accuracy %, avg response time, last 5 attempts as a sparkline (✓/✗ marks)
- Extend GET /api/math/stats if needed to return per-cell mastery info directly (don't force the frontend to recompute)
- Link from /math landing page

Non-goals: Nemesis mode (which would use this data for weighted sampling) — that's a future bead outside this 7-bead chain.
acceptance_criteria:
- Heatmap renders on mobile (no horizontal scroll, cells tap-sized) and desktop
- Colors match the thresholds exactly — unit test the color classifier
- Tapping a cell reveals the detail panel with correct data
- Tabs for multiplication and division show independent states

title: Regnemester: leaderboards (per mode, all-time + weekly)
description:
Bead 4 of 7 for Regnemester. Depends on Blitz (Hytte-j3ll). Family-scoped leaderboards, per mode, two time windows.

Build:
- API: GET /api/math/leaderboard?mode=marathon|blitz&period=all|week
  - Returns array of { user_id, name, avatar_emoji, score, session_id, achieved_at } ordered by best score
  - Score sort: Marathon = ascending duration_ms; Blitz = descending score_num
  - One entry per user (their personal best in the window)
  - Scope: current user's family — parent_id + all child_ids from family_links, plus the parent themselves. Include family members with zero sessions (score shown as '—' in UI)
  - Weekly period starts Monday 00:00 UTC (ISO week)
- UI: /math/leaderboard route
  - Tabs: Marathon / Blitz
  - Subtabs: All time / This week
  - Table columns: rank, avatar, name, score (formatted as mm:ss for Marathon, plain int for Blitz), when
  - Highlight current user's row
  - Link to /math/leaderboard from /math landing page
- Post-finish screen in Marathon and Blitz: show user's current rank on both time windows and link to the leaderboard

Non-goals: achievement unlocks for leaderboard placement (that's the achievements bead).
acceptance_criteria:
- All family members (parent + children from family_links) appear on each leaderboard, even with zero sessions
- Sort order correct per mode (asc duration for Marathon, desc score for Blitz)
- Weekly window resets on Monday UTC
- Current user's row visually distinct
- Mobile layout is usable (no horizontal scroll for core columns)

title: Regnemester: Blitz mode
description:
Bead 3 of 7 for Regnemester. Depends on Marathon (Hytte-7v57). Blitz = 60-second sprint optimized for max score via speed and streak.

Build:
- /math/play/blitz sub-route
- Enable Blitz on the /math mode picker
- Rules:
  - 60s fixed timer
  - Questions drawn randomly from the full 200-fact pool, infinite supply
  - Wrong answer: 0 points, streak resets, move on
  - Correct answer: round(1 × speed_bonus × streak_multiplier)
    - speed_bonus = 1.5 if response_ms < 1000 else 1.2 if < 2000 else 1.0
    - streak_multiplier = min(3.0, 1.0 + streak/10) where streak is the count of consecutive corrects BEFORE this one
  - Final score = integer sum of all round points
- UI:
  - Same keypad/layout as Marathon (reuse component)
  - Prominent countdown timer
  - Live score and current streak visible
  - Small 'FAST!' label when last answer was under 1s (purely textual here — confetti is in the polish bead)
- On timer expiry: submit session with score, show result screen (score, best streak, correct count, wrong count, PB status)

Non-goals: polish effects, leaderboard integration (leaderboard is next bead).
acceptance_criteria:
- Can complete a 60s Blitz on mobile and desktop
- Scoring matches spec exactly — unit test the scorer with known streak + response-time inputs
- Session + all attempts persisted correctly (meta includes best_streak and final score)
- Result screen shows final score, best streak, correct, wrong, PB
- Timer is accurate within 100ms and stops the game immediately at 0

title: Regnemester: Marathon mode
description:
Bead 2 of 7 for Regnemester family math game. Depends on the engine from Hytte-rh4h. Marathon is the flagship mode: all 200 facts once, pure time competition. Inspired by the user's kid solving the whole 10×10 by hand in ~5 minutes — this is the digital equivalent he'll try to beat.

Build:
- /math route: Regnemester landing page with mode picker (only Marathon clickable for now, Blitz etc greyed out as 'coming soon')
- /math/play/marathon sub-route: the play surface
- Rules:
  - 200 questions (100 mult + 100 div from the engine), randomly ordered
  - Wrong answer: COUNTED against you, you move on — no retry
  - Score = duration in ms; ties broken by wrong count (fewer wrongs wins)
- UI (mobile-first, but desktop must also work well):
  - Large problem display (e.g., '7 × 8 = ?' or '56 ÷ 8 = ?')
  - Big on-screen number keypad (0–9, enter, backspace) — tap-optimized, no hunt-and-peck
  - Desktop also supports number keys + Enter + Backspace
  - Progress indicator (e.g., '42 / 200')
  - Live elapsed timer
- On finish: POST /sessions/:id/finish, show result screen with time, wrong count, and 'New PB!' if applicable (compare against user's prior best Marathon)

Non-goals: no leaderboard UI (next bead), no confetti/sounds/haptics (polish bead later).
acceptance_criteria:
- Can play full Marathon on both mobile portrait and desktop
- All 200 attempts recorded in math_attempts, session in math_sessions
- Result screen shows total time, wrong count, and PB status
- Desktop keyboard: number keys submit digits, Enter confirms, Backspace erases
- Mobile: keypad responds to taps with no delay; screen doesn't zoom on input focus

title: Regnemester: schema and core game engine
description:
Foundation for Regnemester, a family multiplication (1–10) and division math game. This is bead 1 of 7 chained beads that build the full feature. This bead delivers the data model and core engine only — no UI.

Build:
- SQLite migrations (follow existing Hytte migration pattern) for:
  - math_sessions(id, user_id, mode, started_at, ended_at, score_num, duration_ms, total_correct, total_wrong, meta TEXT)
  - math_attempts(id, session_id, user_id, fact_a, fact_b, op TEXT CHECK(op IN ('*','/')), expected_answer, user_answer, is_correct, response_ms, created_at) — indexed on (user_id, fact_a, fact_b, op) for mastery aggregation
  - math_achievements(id, user_id, code, unlocked_at, session_id, meta TEXT) with UNIQUE(user_id, code)
- Go package internal/math/ with:
  - Question generator producing the 100 mult facts (a×b for a,b in 1..10) and 100 div facts (c÷b=a where a×b=c, both divisor variants)
  - Session lifecycle: Start, RecordAttempt, Finish (computes totals)
  - Per-fact mastery aggregator: given user_id, returns map keyed by (a,b,op) with {count, correct_count, avg_ms, last5 correctness bits}
- HTTP handlers under /api/math/ (authenticated):
  - POST /sessions — body {mode}, returns {session_id, first_question}
  - POST /sessions/:id/attempts — body {a, b, op, user_answer, response_ms}, returns {is_correct, expected_answer, next_question|null}
  - POST /sessions/:id/finish — returns {summary}
  - GET /stats — returns current user's per-fact mastery for both ops
- Register routes in the API router
- Unit tests for generator (verify all 200 facts enumerated), validator, and session lifecycle

Non-goals: no UI, no achievements logic, no leaderboards — those are later beads.
acceptance_criteria:
- Migrations apply cleanly to ~/Hytte/hytte.db without breaking existing tables
- go test ./internal/math/... passes
- Authenticated user can start a session, record an attempt, and finish via the API (verify endpoints return valid JSON)
- GET /api/math/stats returns per-fact data shaped so a future heatmap can consume it directly
- No UI routes added in this bead

title: Deferred credit-card transaction invisible in target month
description: When a credit-card transaction is deferred to the next month on the budget credit-cards page, the deferred transaction is correctly reflected in the next month's monthly total, but the transaction row itself is not visible in the next month's transaction list. In the previous (source) month, the transaction appears greyed out as expected.
acceptance_criteria:
- Deferred transaction appears as a visible row in the transaction list of the target month (the month it was deferred to)
- Monthly total for target month continues to include the deferred amount (current correct behavior preserved)
- Source month continues to show the transaction greyed out (current correct behavior preserved)
- Works across month boundaries and year boundaries

title: Stride
description:
I can already backdate notes for the coach, but I need to able to manually rerun the coaches note on a previous workout in this weeks plan. A small rerun icon on every day in this weeks plan, it should use the note if there is an open one, and mark it as used.

I need this if the coach misinterpet the workout. 

Source: https://github.com/Robin831/Hytte/issues/645

title: Backend: handler, route wiring, and handler tests
description:
Add `RecordCompletionHandler` in `internal/allowance/handlers.go` (parent-gated via `requireParent`):

- Parse JSON body: `child_ids []int64`, `date string` (optional, default today), `notes string` (optional), `status string` (optional, default 'approved', accepts 'approved'|'pending').
- Validate: chore exists and belongs to parent (`GetChoreByID(db, choreID, parentID)`), chore is active, all child_ids are linked to parent via family_links (deduplicate first), if chore has assigned child_id then all request child_ids must match.
- Dispatch: if `completion_mode='team'` call `RecordTeamCompletionByParent`, else loop over child_ids calling `RecordSoloCompletionByParent` per child — on `ErrCompletionExists` add to `skipped` list instead of failing.
- Return 201 with `{completions: [...], skipped: [...]}` JSON.

Wire route in `internal/api/router.go`: `r.Post("/allowance/chores/{id}/record", allowance.RecordCompletionHandler(db))`

Add handler tests in `internal/allowance/handlers_test.go`: TestRecordCompletionHandler_ChildNotInFamily, _SoloWithSkippedDuplicates, _TeamAutoDispatches, _NotParent (403), _ChoreOwnedByDifferentParent (404).

Depends on the storage functions from sub-task 1.

title: Frontend: record-completion modal, i18n, and changelog
description:
In `web/src/pages/AllowancePage.tsx`, add a 'Record as done' button on each chore row in the Chores tab (Option A from the bead). Button opens a modal with:
- Multi-select for children (from parent's family_links; pre-filtered to assigned child if chore has one)
- Date picker (default today)
- Optional notes textarea
- Approved/Pending toggle (default approved)
- Client-side validation: for team chores, block submit if selected children < minTeamSize, show hint
- Submit calls `POST /api/allowance/chores/{choreId}/record` with the JSON body
- On success: toast 'Recorded for {names}', close modal, refresh earnings/pending views
- On skipped entries in response: warning toast per skipped child

Add i18n keys in `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/allowance.json` under the `record.*` and `errors.teamTooSmall` namespace as specified in the bead.

Add changelog fragment `changelog.d/Hytte-6gil.md` with Added category.

Depends on the backend endpoint from sub-tasks 1 and 2 being available (can be developed in parallel with mock, but integration requires backend).

title: Backend: storage functions for parent-recorded completions
description:
Add two new functions to `internal/allowance/storage.go`:

1. `RecordSoloCompletionByParent(db, choreID, childID, parentID int64, date, notes, status string) (*Completion, error)` — Inserts one row into `allowance_completions` with the given status. When status='approved', sets `approved_by=parentID` and `approved_at=now`. Notes go through `encryption.EncryptField`. Returns `ErrCompletionExists` on UNIQUE violation (chore+child+date).

2. `RecordTeamCompletionByParent(db, choreID, parentID int64, childIDs []int64, date, notes, status string) (*Completion, error)` — In a single transaction: inserts one `allowance_completions` row (child_id = first in list), then inserts all childIDs into `allowance_team_completions`. Rejects if an active team session already exists for chore+date. Enforces min team size check.

Add corresponding tests in `internal/allowance/storage_test.go`: TestRecordSoloCompletionByParent_Approved, _Pending, _DuplicateReturnsExists, TestRecordTeamCompletionByParent_Success, _BelowMinTeamSize, _ExistingActiveSessionConflicts.

This sub-task has no dependencies. The handler sub-task depends on these functions.

title: Chores: let admin/parent record a completion on behalf of one or more kids
description:
## Problem

Today only a child can register a chore completion. The sole creation path is `POST /api/allowance/my/complete/{choreId}` (`internal/allowance/handlers.go:857-974`), which is gated by `requireChild` and hardcodes `childID = user.ID` when calling `CreateCompletion`. If a kid doesn't have screen time on their phone — blocked, out of allotment, device seized — they physically can't log the chore they actually did, and the parent has no way to fill it in retroactively.

`ApproveCompletionHandler` only updates an **existing** pending row; it cannot create one. `StartTeamCompletion` has the same child-only limitation.

## Fix

Add one parent-gated endpoint that records a completion (or several, for multi-kid cases) on behalf of the listed children. The parent is asserting the chore was actually done — completions default to **approved** (not pending) since there's nothing left to verify.

### New endpoint

**`POST /api/allowance/chores/{choreId}/record`** — parent-gated via `requireParent`.

Request body:

```json
{
  "child_ids": [2, 3],
  "date": "2026-04-15",   // optional, defaults to today in YYYY-MM-DD
  "notes": "Did it together after school",  // optional
  "status": "approved"    // optional, defaults to "approved". Accepts "approved" | "pending".
}
```

Response: 201 with the created completion(s):

```json
{
  "completions": [
    { "id": 42, "chore_id": 7, "child_id": 2, "date": "2026-04-15", "status": "approved", "approved_by": 1, "approved_at": "...", ... },
    { "id": 43, "chore_id": 7, "child_id": 3, "date": "2026-04-15", "status": "approved", "approved_by": 1, "approved_at": "...", ... }
  ]
}
```

### Behavioral rules

1. **Chore validation** (mirroring the existing CompleteChore path):
   - Chore must exist and belong to the authenticated parent (`GetChoreByID(db, choreID, parentID)`).
   - Chore must be active.
   - If the chore has an assigned `child_id` (solo-assigned), all `child_ids` in the request must match — reject otherwise with a clear error.

2. **Family link validation**: every ID in `child_ids` must be linked to the authenticated parent via `family_links`. Reject any with "child X is not linked to your account". Deduplicate `child_ids` before validation (so `[2, 2]` doesn't create two rows for the same kid).

3. **Solo chore mode** (`completion_mode = 'solo'`, the default):
   - Create one row in `allowance_completions` per child.
   - If any child already has a completion for this chore+date (UNIQUE violation), skip that child and report it in the response as `skipped` rather than failing the whole request. (Rationale: if one kid logged it already, the parent attribution shouldn't fail the other kid's row.)

4. **Team chore mode** (`completion_mode = 'team'`):
   - Require `len(child_ids) >= chore.MinTeamSize`. Reject with "team size too small, minimum is N" if violated.
   - Create **one** `allowance_completions` row (with `child_id` = first ID in the list, matching `StartTeamCompletion`'s pattern) and insert **all** listed children into `allowance_team_completions`. Wrap both writes in a transaction.
   - Reject if any active team session already exists for this chore+date (same uniqueness rule as `StartTeamCompletion`).

5. **Status handling**:
   - `status="approved"` (default): insert with `status='approved'`, `approved_by=parentID`, `approved_at=now`. The admin has verified it; skip the approval queue entirely.
   - `status="pending"`: insert with `status='pending'`, `approved_by=NULL`, `approved_at=NULL`. Useful if the parent wants to apply a quality bonus later via the existing quality-bonus flow.

6. **Notes encryption**: notes go through `encryption.EncryptField` on write, same as the existing `CreateCompletion`.

### Store changes

Either add a new function or extend existing ones. Recommended: new dedicated functions so the existing child-auth paths stay untouched.

**`internal/allowance/storage.go`**:

```go
// RecordSoloCompletionByParent creates a pre-approved (or pending) completion
// on behalf of a child. Returns ErrCompletionExists if a completion already
// exists for this chore+child+date. Parent ownership of the chore must be
// verified by the caller.
func RecordSoloCompletionByParent(
    db *sql.DB,
    choreID, childID, parentID int64,
    date, notes, status string,
) (*Completion, error)

// RecordTeamCompletionByParent creates a team completion row plus team entries
// for all listed children, in a single transaction. Enforces the existing
// uniqueness rule: at most one active team session per (chore, date).
func RecordTeamCompletionByParent(
    db *sql.DB,
    choreID, parentID int64,
    childIDs []int64,
    date, notes, status string,
) (*Completion, error)
```

Both helpers set `approved_by`/`approved_at` when `status=='approved'`.

### Handler

**`internal/allowance/handlers.go`**: new `RecordCompletionHandler` (parent-gated) that parses the body, validates chore+family links, dispatches to the solo or team store helper based on `chore.CompletionMode`, and returns the created completion(s).

### Route wiring

`internal/api/router.go`, in the allowance feature group:

```go
r.Post("/allowance/chores/{id}/record", allowance.RecordCompletionHandler(db))
```

Note the path is `/chores/{id}/record` — **not** `/my/complete/{id}` — because this is a parent action, not a child action. Keeps the auth model in the URL surface legible.

## Frontend

Add a "Record as done" control on the parent's `AllowancePage.tsx`. Two placement options:

### Option A (preferred): inline button on each chore row in the Chores tab

For every chore the parent owns, show a "Record completion" button that opens a small modal:
- Multi-select for children (populated from the parent's family_links; pre-filter to the chore's assigned child if any).
- Date picker (default today).
- Optional notes field.
- "Mark as approved" / "Mark as pending" toggle (default approved).
- Submit button.

The UI should handle both solo and team chores with the same form — the backend picks the right path based on `completion_mode`. Show a hint under the children selector for team chores: "Team chore — needs at least N children".

### Option B: a dedicated "Record for kids" tab

Simpler layout but extra navigation step. Defer if Option A is enough.

### After submit

- On success, show a toast "Recorded for {children}" and close the modal.
- If the response contains `skipped` entries (solo chore where one kid already had a completion), show a warning: "{Kid} already had a completion for this date — skipped".
- Refresh the parent's earnings/pending views so the new completion appears immediately in the relevant tabs.

## i18n

New keys under `allowance` namespace in all three locales (en, nb, th):

- `record.title`: "Record completion"
- `record.button`: "Record as done"
- `record.children`: "Who did this?"
- `record.date`: "Date"
- `record.notes`: "Notes (optional)"
- `record.statusApproved`: "Approved"
- `record.statusPending`: "Pending approval"
- `record.submit`: "Record"
- `record.teamMinHint`: "Team chore — at least {{count}} children required"
- `record.success`: "Recorded for {{names}}"
- `record.skipped`: "{{name}} already had a completion for this date — skipped"
- `errors.teamTooSmall`: "Need at least {{count}} children for a team chore"

## Tests

### Backend (`internal/allowance/storage_test.go` and `handlers_test.go`)

- `TestRecordSoloCompletionByParent_Approved`: creates row with `status='approved'`, `approved_by` and `approved_at` set.
- `TestRecordSoloCompletionByParent_Pending`: creates row with `status='pending'`, `approved_by NULL`.
- `TestRecordSoloCompletionByParent_DuplicateReturnsExists`: same chore+child+date twice returns `ErrCompletionExists`.
- `TestRecordTeamCompletionByParent_Success`: creates one completion + N team entries in one tx.
- `TestRecordTeamCompletionByParent_BelowMinTeamSize`: error when `len(childIDs) < chore.MinTeamSize`.
- `TestRecordTeamCompletionByParent_ExistingActiveSessionConflicts`: same uniqueness rule as `StartTeamCompletion`.
- Handler-level:
  - `TestRecordCompletionHandler_ChildNotInFamily`: rejects with clear error when child_id isn't linked.
  - `TestRecordCompletionHandler_SoloWithSkippedDuplicates`: two kids, one already has a row → 201 with one created + one skipped.
  - `TestRecordCompletionHandler_TeamAutoDispatches`: chore is team mode, all kids end up in team_completions.
  - `TestRecordCompletionHandler_NotParent`: 403 when caller is a child.
  - `TestRecordCompletionHandler_ChoreOwnedByDifferentParent`: 404.

### Frontend

- Modal opens, loads children from family_links.
- Submit with single child → one POST → completion count updates.
- Submit with multiple children on a solo chore → server returns two completions, both appear in earnings.
- Submit on a team chore with fewer than min team size → client-side validation blocks submit.
- Skip response surfaces as warning toast without breaking the UI.

## Edge cases

- **Chore assigned to one kid, parent lists a different kid**: reject with "chore is assigned to {assignedKid}, cannot record for {otherKid}". Mirrors the existing 403 at `handlers.go:887`.
- **Date in the future**: allow it — parents sometimes log today's chore this morning knowing it'll be done, or the kid did it early. No technical restriction, but frontend defaults to today.
- **Parent is also the chore owner's approver**: already true in the current model. `approved_by = parentID` is fine.
- **Photo attachment**: the child's CompleteChore path supports a photo upload (`multipart/form-data`). For the parent-attributed version, skip photo support in the initial cut — the parent is vouching for the completion without evidence. If needed later, the endpoint can accept multipart with the same shape as the child handler.
- **Extras (non-chore bonuses)**: this bead is scoped to `allowance_chores`. If parents also want to attribute extras on behalf of kids, that's a separate follow-up — the extras flow (`MyExtrasHandler`, `CompleteExtraHandler`) has similar child-only gating.

## Files touched

- `internal/allowance/storage.go` — `RecordSoloCompletionByParent`, `RecordTeamCompletionByParent`
- `internal/allowance/storage_test.go` — new tests
- `internal/allowance/handlers.go` — `RecordCompletionHandler`
- `internal/allowance/handlers_test.go` — new tests
- `internal/api/router.go` — new route
- `web/src/pages/AllowancePage.tsx` — new modal + button on each chore
- `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/allowance.json` — new i18n keys
- `changelog.d/Hytte-<id>.md` — Added category fragment

## Acceptance criteria

- A parent can call `POST /api/allowance/chores/{id}/record` with `{child_ids: [2]}` and receive a 201 with an approved completion for that child.
- Same call with `{child_ids: [2, 3]}` on a solo chore produces two completions.
- Same call on a team chore produces one completion + two team entries.
- Specifying `status: "pending"` puts the row in the approval queue instead of auto-approving.
- The parent's AllowancePage has a "Record completion" control that opens the modal and successfully submits.
- A non-parent user calling the endpoint gets 403.
- Completions created this way appear in the parent's earnings/pending views and in the child's allowance dashboard exactly like child-created completions.

title: Stride Chat: frontend chat UI with live plan updates
description: ## Summary Fourth bead in the Stride chat chain. Adds the chat interface to the Stride page with message history, SSE streaming, and real-time plan card updates when Claude modifies the plan. ## UI placement An expandable chat drawer at the bottom of the Stride page, within the plan section. Collapsed by default (just a button/bar). When expanded, it takes up the lower portion of the viewport with the plan day cards still visible above — so the user can see the plan change in real time as Claude responds. **Why not a separate page**: the whole point is seeing the plan update live. If chat is on a different page from the day cards, the user has to navigate back to see what changed. **Why a drawer not inline**: the plan section is already dense with day cards. A full inline chat would push everything else way down. A bottom drawer keeps the plan visible and the chat accessible. ## Components ### `StrideChatDrawer` — new component at `web/src/components/stride/StrideChatDrawer.tsx` Props: - `planId: number` — current plan ID - `onPlanUpdated: (plan: DayPlan[]) => void` — callback when Claude modifies the plan, so the parent re-renders day cards State: - `messages: StrideChatMessage[]` — conversation history - `input: string` — current input text - `sending: boolean` — true while waiting for Claude - `expanded: boolean` — drawer open/closed - `streamingText: string` — accumulating delta chunks during streaming Interface: ```ts interface StrideChatMessage { id: number plan_id: number role: 'user' | 'assistant' content: string plan_modified: boolean created_at: string } ``` ### Behavior **On mount (or planId change):** - Fetch `GET /api/stride/plans/{planId}/chat` → populate `messages`. - Scroll to bottom. **On send:** 1. Optimistically add the user message to the list. 2. `POST /api/stride/plans/{planId}/chat` with `{content}`. 3. The response is an SSE stream. Process events: - `user_message` → replace the optimistic message with the server-persisted one (gets the real ID + timestamp). - `delta` → append to `streamingText`, render as a typing assistant message at the bottom. - `plan_updated` → call `onPlanUpdated(data.plan)` so the parent updates the day cards. Show a subtle inline badge on the message: "Plan updated". - `done` → replace the streaming message with the final assistant message. Scroll to bottom. - `error` → show error inline below the input. Don't lose the user's message. - `retry` → show a brief "Session expired, retrying..." status. **Message rendering:** - User messages: right-aligned, blue background (same as homework chat pattern). - Assistant messages: left-aligned, gray background. Markdown rendering for the text content (the coach's responses are conversational and may include bullet points, bold, etc.). - Messages with `plan_modified: true` get a small badge/icon indicating the plan was changed. - Streaming message: shows with a pulsing cursor at the end while deltas arrive. **Plan modification indication:** - When `plan_updated` SSE event fires, briefly flash/highlight the day cards that changed (compare old plan dates+sessions with new plan). Or simpler: just flash the entire plan section with a subtle border pulse. Don't over-engineer the diff — a simple "something changed" indicator is enough. ### Drawer UX - **Collapsed state**: a horizontal bar at the bottom of the plan section with a chat icon and text like "Chat with your coach". Tap/click to expand. - **Expanded state**: message list (scrollable, max-height ~50vh on desktop, ~60vh on mobile) + input bar pinned at the bottom. A collapse button at the top-right. - **Mobile (375px)**: full-width, input bar respects iOS safe areas. Message bubbles stack vertically with no horizontal padding waste. - **Keyboard**: Enter sends, Shift+Enter for newline. Send button also visible for mobile users. ## StridePage integration In `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx`, inside the plan section (after the day cards ``): ```tsx {currentPlan && ( { setCurrentPlan(prev => prev ? { ...prev, plan: newPlan } : prev) }} /> )} ``` The `onPlanUpdated` callback updates `currentPlan.plan` which causes `sortedPlanDays` to recompute and the day cards to re-render with the new plan. ## i18n New keys in `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/stride.json` under a `chat` namespace: ```json "chat": { "title": "Chat with your coach", "placeholder": "Ask about your plan, request changes...", "send": "Send", "sending": "Thinking...", "planUpdated": "Plan updated", "sessionRetry": "Session expired, retrying...", "error": "Failed to send message", "empty": "No messages yet. Ask your coach anything about this week's plan." } ``` All three languages (en, nb, th). ## Tests `web/src/components/stride/StrideChatDrawer.test.tsx`: - Renders collapsed by default. - Expands on click. - Loads and displays message history. - Sends a message and shows it optimistically. - Handles SSE delta events and shows streaming text. - Handles plan_updated event and calls onPlanUpdated. - Shows error on failed send. Use the existing test patterns from homework chat tests for SSE mocking. ## Files touched - `web/src/components/stride/StrideChatDrawer.tsx` — new component - `web/src/components/stride/StrideChatDrawer.test.tsx` — new tests - `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx` — integrate drawer - `web/public/locales/en/stride.json` — chat keys - `web/public/locales/nb/stride.json` — chat keys - `web/public/locales/th/stride.json` — chat keys - `changelog.d/Hytte-.md` — Added category fragment ## Acceptance criteria - The chat drawer appears on the Stride page when a current plan exists. - Sending a message streams the response via SSE with visible token-by-token rendering. - When Claude modifies the plan, the day cards above update immediately without a page reload. - Messages with plan modifications show a "Plan updated" badge. - The chat works at 375px mobile width. - Message history persists across page reloads (fetched from the API on mount).

title: Stride Chat: handlers with streaming, plan modification, and route wiring
description:
## Summary

Third bead in the Stride chat chain. Implements the HTTP handlers: send message (with SSE streaming and plan modification detection), list messages, and route registration. This is the core backend logic that ties schema (bead 1) and prompt (bead 2) together.

## Handlers

All in `internal/stride/chat_handlers.go` (new file).

### `StrideChatListHandler` — GET /api/stride/plans/{planId}/chat

Returns all messages for the given plan's chat conversation. Plan must exist and belong to the authenticated user.

Response: `{"messages": [...], "plan_id": 123}`

### `StrideChatSendHandler` — POST /api/stride/plans/{planId}/chat

Accepts JSON body `{"content": "Move Thursday's tempo to Friday"}`.

Flow:
1. Validate plan exists and belongs to user.
2. Store the user message (encrypted).
3. Load context: current plan, evaluations for this plan, user training profile, races, ACR, active notes.
4. Build system prompt via `BuildChatSystemPrompt` (from bead 2).
5. Load Claude config from user preferences (`training.LoadClaudeConfig`).
6. Get or create session ID from `stride_plans.chat_session_id`.
7. Stream Claude's response via SSE (same `streamClaude` pattern as homework chat — `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, with the homework bug fix already in place).
8. After stream completes, scan the full response for a fenced JSON plan block.
9. If plan JSON found:
   a. Parse and validate via `parsePlanResponse` (existing function in generate.go).
   b. Verify it has exactly 7 days matching the plan's week_start to week_end.
   c. Update `stride_plans.plan_json` with the new plan.
   d. Set `plan_modified=1` on the assistant message.
   e. Send an SSE event `plan_updated` with the new plan JSON so the frontend can re-render day cards without a full page reload.
10. Store the assistant message (encrypted).
11. Save the session ID for future resumption.
12. Send the `done` SSE event with the full assistant message + plan_modified flag.

### SSE event stream

Same shape as homework chat:
- `event: user_message` — the saved user message (sent immediately after step 2)
- `event: delta` — streaming text chunks from Claude
- `event: plan_updated` — sent after a successful plan modification with `{"plan": [...]}`
- `event: done` — the final assistant message + metadata
- `event: error` — on failure
- `event: retry` — on session expiry, before fresh retry

### Plan modification parsing

New helper `extractPlanJSON(response string) (string, bool)`:
- Scans for a fenced code block: `\`\`\`json\n[...]\n\`\`\``
- Returns the raw JSON string and whether one was found.
- Does NOT parse — that's `parsePlanResponse`'s job. This just extracts the block.
- If multiple fenced blocks exist, use the last one (Claude sometimes shows a "before" and "after").

New helper `validatePlanUpdate(planJSON string, weekStart, weekEnd string) ([]DayPlan, error)`:
- Parses via `parsePlanResponse`.
- Verifies all 7 dates are present and within the week range.
- Verifies no duplicate dates.
- Returns the validated `[]DayPlan` or an error.

### Session resumption with retry

Same pattern as homework (`handlers.go:534-540`): if the first call with `--resume <sessionId>` fails, log a warning, send an SSE `retry` event, and retry without the session ID. This handles session expiry gracefully.

## Route registration

In `internal/api/router.go`, inside the stride feature group:

```go
r.Get("/stride/plans/{planId}/chat", stride.StrideChatListHandler(db))
r.Post("/stride/plans/{planId}/chat", stride.StrideChatSendHandler(db))
```

Nested under the existing `/stride/plans/{id}` pattern so the plan ID is in the URL and auth is already handled by the enclosing group.

## Error handling

- Plan not found → 404
- Plan belongs to different user → 404 (don't leak existence)
- Claude not enabled → 400 with clear message
- Claude CLI exits non-zero → SSE error event (include stderr in the log per the homework fix)
- Plan modification parse failure → log warning, store the message without plan_modified, do NOT send plan_updated event. The user still sees Claude's text response; they just need to ask Claude to fix the JSON. This is a soft failure, not a hard error.
- Plan modification validation failure (wrong dates, schema mismatch) → same soft failure. Log the invalid JSON for debugging.

## Tests

`internal/stride/chat_handlers_test.go`:

- `TestExtractPlanJSON_Found` — response with a fenced JSON block, verify extraction.
- `TestExtractPlanJSON_NotFound` — response without JSON block returns false.
- `TestExtractPlanJSON_MultipleFencedBlocks` — uses the last one.
- `TestExtractPlanJSON_UnfencedJSON` — does NOT extract bare JSON (must be fenced).
- `TestValidatePlanUpdate_ValidSevenDays` — correct plan passes.
- `TestValidatePlanUpdate_WrongDateRange` — dates outside week_start/week_end rejected.
- `TestValidatePlanUpdate_DuplicateDates` — rejected.
- `TestValidatePlanUpdate_NotSevenDays` — rejected.
- Handler integration tests using the `execCommand` override pattern from homework (stub Claude CLI, verify SSE events, verify plan update persisted).

## Files touched

- `internal/stride/chat_handlers.go` — new file
- `internal/stride/chat_handlers_test.go` — new file
- `internal/api/router.go` — add two routes
- `changelog.d/Hytte-<id>.md` — Added category fragment

## Acceptance criteria

- `POST /api/stride/plans/{planId}/chat` streams an SSE response with delta events.
- A response containing a valid fenced plan JSON block updates `stride_plans.plan_json` and emits a `plan_updated` SSE event.
- A response without plan JSON stores the message normally without modification.
- Invalid plan JSON in the response is logged but does not error the request — the text response is still saved and shown.
- Session resumption works (second message in same plan week uses `--resume`).
- Session expiry is handled gracefully with a retry.

title: Stride Chat: system prompt builder with plan and training context
description:
## Summary

Second bead in the Stride chat chain. Builds the system prompt that gives Claude the coaching context it needs to have an informed conversation and make plan modifications.

## Context

The Stride chat lets the user talk to their AI coach in real time. For the conversation to be useful, Claude needs the same context the weekly plan generator has — but trimmed for conversation rather than generation. This bead builds the system prompt assembly function; the handler (next bead) calls it when starting or resuming a chat session.

## System prompt structure

New function in `internal/stride/chat_prompt.go`:

```go
func BuildChatSystemPrompt(
    profile training.UserTrainingProfile,
    plan Plan,
    evaluations []EvaluationRecord,
    races []Race,
    acr *float64, acute, chronic float64,
    notes []Note,
) string
```

The prompt is structured as:

### 1. Role and capabilities

```
You are an expert running coach using the Marius Bakken threshold-dominant model.
You are chatting with your athlete about their current training week.

You can:
- Answer questions about the plan, training load, pacing, and recovery
- Modify the weekly plan when asked (move workouts, swap sessions, adjust paces, add rest days)
- Give injury/fatigue advice grounded in the athlete's actual data

When modifying the plan, output the FULL updated 7-day plan as a fenced JSON block:
\`\`\`json
[{"date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "rest_day": false, "session": {...}}, ...]
\`\`\`
The JSON must follow the exact DayPlan schema. Include ALL 7 days, not just the changed ones.
Only output plan JSON when you are actually making a change — not when just discussing.
```

### 2. Current plan

The full `plan_json` for the current week, formatted readably so Claude can reference specific days by name/date.

### 3. Training profile

From `training.UserTrainingProfile`: zones, paces, max HR, resting HR, weekly volume targets, block/phase info. Same data that feeds plan generation, but presented as a compact reference block rather than full instructions.

### 4. This week's evaluations

All evaluations for the current plan, formatted as:
```
## Completed Sessions This Week
- Monday Apr 14: easy run — compliant. HR avg 132, within Zone 1 cap.
- Tuesday Apr 15: tempo — partial. Pace 4:45 vs target 4:40, HR drifted 7%.
```

This lets Claude reference what's already happened when suggesting modifications for the rest of the week.

### 5. Race calendar

Upcoming races with dates, distances, priorities, and target times. So Claude won't suggest dropping a key workout 2 weeks before an A-race.

### 6. Training load context

ACR (acute:chronic ratio), current acute and chronic loads. Lets Claude make load-aware decisions ("your ACR is 1.3, I'd back off intensity").

### 7. Active notes

Any unconsumed notes the user has written. These provide additional context the user has explicitly flagged ("knee feels tight", "busy week at work").

## What's NOT included

- The full Marius Bakken instruction set (900+ words). The plan is already built on those principles — Claude doesn't need them to have a conversation about adjusting Thursday's session.
- The complete plan generation output format specification. Only the DayPlan JSON schema is included (for plan modifications).
- Historical weekly summaries. The conversation is about this week; historical context is in the profile and ACR.

## DayPlan schema reference

Include a compact schema reference in the prompt so Claude knows the exact JSON shape to produce when modifying the plan. Extract from the existing `parsePlanResponse` validation or from the `DayPlan` / `SessionDetail` type definitions in `generate.go`.

## Tests

`internal/stride/chat_prompt_test.go`:

- `TestBuildChatSystemPrompt_ContainsCurrentPlan` — verify the plan JSON appears in the output.
- `TestBuildChatSystemPrompt_ContainsProfile` — verify zone/pace data appears.
- `TestBuildChatSystemPrompt_ContainsEvaluations` — verify completed session summaries.
- `TestBuildChatSystemPrompt_ContainsRaces` — verify upcoming races appear.
- `TestBuildChatSystemPrompt_ContainsModificationInstructions` — verify the fenced JSON instruction block is present.
- `TestBuildChatSystemPrompt_OmitsMariusBakkenFullInstructions` — verify the full generation instructions are NOT in the output (grep for a distinctive phrase from `mariusBakkenInstructions`).

## Files touched

- `internal/stride/chat_prompt.go` — new file
- `internal/stride/chat_prompt_test.go` — new file
- `changelog.d/Hytte-<id>.md` — Added category fragment

## Acceptance criteria

- `BuildChatSystemPrompt` produces a prompt that includes the current plan, profile, evaluations, races, and plan-modification instructions.
- The prompt does NOT include the full Marius Bakken generation instructions.
- Tests verify each section's presence independently.

title: Stride Chat: schema, store helpers, and session management
description:
## Summary

First bead in a chain to add a real-time coaching chat to Stride. This bead covers the data layer: schema, store helpers, and session management plumbing. No handlers, no frontend.

## Context

Stride is getting a conversational chat where the user can talk directly to their AI running coach. The chat is scoped per-plan-week: each `stride_plans` row gets its own conversation. The coach can read the current plan, recent evaluations, training profile, and race calendar, and can modify the plan in response to user requests ("move Thursday's tempo to Friday"). Plan modifications are handled in a later bead — this one just sets up the persistence layer.

## Schema

### New table: `stride_chat_messages`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stride_chat_messages (
    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    plan_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES stride_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    role          TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',   -- 'user' | 'assistant'
    content       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',   -- encrypted at rest
    plan_modified INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, -- 1 if this message triggered a plan update
    created_at    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
);
CREATE INDEX idx_stride_chat_messages_plan ON stride_chat_messages(plan_id);
```

Content is encrypted via `encryption.EncryptField` on write, `DecryptField` on read — same pattern as homework messages.

`plan_modified` is a flag set by the handler (in a later bead) when the assistant response contains a valid plan JSON block that was successfully persisted. It's stored here so the frontend can show a visual indicator ("plan updated") next to messages that changed the plan.

### New column on `stride_plans`: `chat_session_id`

```sql
ALTER TABLE stride_plans ADD COLUMN chat_session_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '';
```

Stores the Claude CLI session ID for `--resume` continuity within a plan week's conversation. Same pattern as `homework_conversations.session_id` and `chat_conversations.session_id`.

## Store helpers

All in `internal/stride/chat_store.go` (new file):

### Types

```go
type ChatMessage struct {
    ID           int64  `json:"id"`
    PlanID       int64  `json:"plan_id"`
    UserID       int64  `json:"user_id"`
    Role         string `json:"role"`
    Content      string `json:"content"`
    PlanModified bool   `json:"plan_modified"`
    CreatedAt    string `json:"created_at"`
}
```

### Functions

- `ListChatMessages(db, planID, userID) ([]ChatMessage, error)` — returns messages for a plan, ordered by created_at ASC. Decrypts content. Scoped to userID for safety.
- `AddChatMessage(db, msg ChatMessage) (ChatMessage, error)` — inserts a message with encrypted content, returns the inserted row with ID and created_at populated. Validates: role must be "user" or "assistant", content must be non-empty, plan must exist and belong to the user.
- `GetChatSessionID(db, planID, userID) (string, error)` — returns the chat_session_id for a plan. Returns empty string if not set.
- `UpdateChatSessionID(db, planID, userID, sessionID) error` — updates the chat_session_id on the plan row. Scoped to userID.
- `MarkMessagePlanModified(db, messageID, userID) error` — sets plan_modified=1 on a message. Called by the handler after a successful plan update.

### Tests

`internal/stride/chat_store_test.go` — using the existing `setupTestDB` pattern from `stride_test.go`:

- `TestListChatMessages_Empty` — no messages returns empty slice (not nil).
- `TestAddChatMessage_RoundTrip` — insert + list, verify content is decrypted, timestamps populated.
- `TestAddChatMessage_InvalidRole` — role "system" rejected.
- `TestAddChatMessage_EmptyContent` — rejected.
- `TestAddChatMessage_WrongUser` — plan belongs to user 1, message from user 2 rejected.
- `TestChatSessionID_RoundTrip` — update + get, verify value.
- `TestChatSessionID_DefaultEmpty` — new plan has empty session ID.
- `TestMarkMessagePlanModified` — flag flips from 0 to 1.

## Schema migration

Add to `internal/db/db.go:createSchema()`:
- `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS stride_chat_messages` with index.
- `ALTER TABLE stride_plans ADD COLUMN chat_session_id` with the standard existence check pattern.

## Files touched

- `internal/db/db.go` — schema migration
- `internal/stride/chat_store.go` — new file, types + store helpers
- `internal/stride/chat_store_test.go` — new file, tests
- `changelog.d/Hytte-<id>.md` — Added category fragment

## Acceptance criteria

- `sqlite3 hytte.db '.schema stride_chat_messages'` shows the table after deploy.
- `sqlite3 hytte.db 'SELECT chat_session_id FROM stride_plans LIMIT 1'` returns empty string (column exists).
- All store tests pass.
- Content round-trips through encryption (insert plaintext → DB has `enc:...` → list returns plaintext).

Pass them into the `EvaluateWorkout` prompt builder so Claude can consider sickness, fatigue, skipped workouts, etc. After a successful Claude call, call `MarkNotesConsumed` with `consumed_by='nightly'`. Per the edge-case requirement, only consume notes when they were actually sent to Claude (i.e., skip marking if no workouts exist for that user that night).
