title: Stride: notes should be consumed once and moved to history
description:
Currently Stride notes are re-read on every weekly plan generation (top-20 most recent, no consumed flag) and are not consulted by the nightly evaluation at all. This means a note I wrote yesterday will feed into Claude again next Sunday, and the Sunday after that, until 20 newer notes bump it off the list — and any context I add during the day never reaches the nightly workout evaluator.

## Desired behavior

I want a one-shot note model: write a note, it gets consumed by exactly one Stride job (either the nightly evaluation or the weekly plan generation), then it's marked as read and moves to a history list. It is never fed to Claude a second time.

I will decide which job reads a note by when I write it — notes written during the day get picked up by that night's evaluation; notes written before Sunday 18:00 get picked up by the weekly generator. No auto-routing needed from the backend side.

## Scope

### Backend (internal/stride)

- **Schema**: add a `consumed_at` column (nullable TIMESTAMP) and a `consumed_by` column (nullable TEXT: 'nightly' | 'weekly') to `stride_notes`. Use `ALTER TABLE` with existence check per CLAUDE.md conventions. Backfill NULL on existing rows.
- **Nightly evaluation** (`evaluate.go:RunNightlyEvaluation` / `evaluateUserWorkouts`): fetch unconsumed notes for the user and pass them into the `EvaluateWorkout` prompt builder so the coach can factor them into the workout assessment (sickness, fatigue, why a workout was skipped, etc.). After a successful Claude call, mark those notes as consumed with `consumed_by='nightly'`. Notes should be marked consumed exactly once per nightly run, even if multiple workouts exist for that user that night.
- **Weekly plan generation** (`generate.go:listAllNotes`): replace the `LIMIT 20` query with `WHERE user_id = ? AND consumed_at IS NULL` (no limit, or a generous safety limit). After a successful plan insert, mark all fetched notes as consumed with `consumed_by='weekly'`. Do this in the same DB transaction as the plan upsert so a failed plan insert doesn't silently eat the notes.
- **Edge case**: if a nightly run finds notes but has no workouts to evaluate, should notes still be consumed? My preference: **no** — only consume when the notes were actually sent to Claude, otherwise the note sits and gets picked up by the next job that runs (nightly or weekly, whichever comes first). Worth confirming in the implementation PR.
- **Handlers**: `ListNotesHandler` should accept an optional `?status=active|consumed|all` filter (default `active` for backwards-compat with the current UI). `DeleteNoteHandler` keeps working on both active and consumed notes.
- **Tests**: update `generate_test.go` and `evaluate_test.go` for the new consume-and-mark flow. Add tests covering: (a) note consumed by nightly is not re-used by the weekly, (b) note consumed by weekly is not re-used next Sunday, (c) failed Claude call does NOT mark notes as consumed, (d) unconsumed notes survive across runs.

### Frontend (web/src)

- **StridePage** (and/or the note composer component under `web/src/components/stride/`): split the notes list into two sections — 'Active' (unconsumed) and 'History' (consumed, grouped by consumed_by + consumed_at). History items should show a small badge indicating which job read them ('nightly' / 'weekly') and when.
- The existing 'add note' UX stays the same — no routing picker, I know when to write them.
- i18n: add new keys to `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/stride.json` for the History section header, the active/consumed labels, and the 'consumed by nightly/weekly on DATE' line. All three languages per CLAUDE.md.
- Mobile: History list must work at 375px width.

## Non-goals

- No automatic routing of notes to nightly vs weekly based on content/keywords. I'll time my writes.
- No editing of consumed notes. They become read-only once consumed.
- No re-consuming a note (no 'mark as unread' action). Delete + rewrite if needed.

## Files likely to touch

- `internal/db/db.go` — schema ALTER
- `internal/stride/stride.go` — Note struct, ListNotes, CreateNote, new MarkNotesConsumed helper
- `internal/stride/generate.go` — listAllNotes, post-insert consume
- `internal/stride/evaluate.go` — wire notes into evaluation prompt, post-eval consume
- `internal/stride/handlers.go` — status filter on ListNotesHandler
- `internal/stride/*_test.go` — new coverage
- `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx` + `web/src/components/stride/*` — History section
- `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/stride.json` — new i18n keys
- `changelog.d/Hytte-<id>.md` — fragment

title: HandleSendMessage with multipart upload, subject detection, and streaming Claude response
description: Add HandleSendMessage(w, r) to internal/homework/handlers.go. This is the most complex handler: parse multipart form data for text + optional image, save uploaded image to a temp directory, call DetectSubject on the text (from the prompt-generation sub-task), call BuildSystemPrompt with the current profile/help-level/subject, then invoke the Claude CLI reusing the Chat/CLI session infrastructure from Hytte-6707. Stream the Claude response back to the client (SSE or chunked transfer). Depends on sub-task 1 above for the file scaffold and common helpers, and on the prompt-generation dependency for DetectSubject and BuildSystemPrompt.
notes:
## Warden rejection feedback (must address on retry)

The warden flagged real issues, NOT cosmetic ones. Address these BEFORE the next iteration:

### 1. CRITICAL: Image is uploaded but never sent to Claude
imagePath is saved to a temp file and stored on the message, but streamClaude() is called with text only — the Claude CLI is never told about the image. The whole point of the upload is to send the homework photo to Claude for analysis.

Fix: Pass imagePath to streamClaude. The Claude CLI accepts images via the @path syntax in the prompt. Build the prompt as 'message text\n@/tmp/path/to/image.jpg' so Claude reads the image alongside the text.

### 2. Image temp file is never cleaned up
os.CreateTemp creates the file, only defer tmpFile.Close() runs. The image file leaks to disk forever. The path is stored on the message but cleanup is never scheduled.

Fix: Either:
- Move uploaded images to a permanent storage location (web/uploads/homework/{conv_id}/) so they can be displayed in the conversation history
- Or: defer os.Remove(imagePath) at the end of the handler, AFTER Claude has finished reading it

The first option is better — kids should see the image they sent in the conversation history.

### 3. seeker.Seek error ignored
After MIME detection from the first 512 bytes, seeker.Seek(0, io.SeekStart) — error not checked. If seek fails, the io.Copy starts at offset 512 and truncates the image.

Fix: Check the seek error and return 500 if it fails.

### 4. Silent SQL error on subject update
The subject UPDATE swallows errors with _, _ = db.Exec(...). Should at least log the error.

### 5. Verify cmd.Wait() handling
On the early-return paths (stdout pipe error, cmd.Start error), cmd.Wait() is not called. Probably OK since the process never started, but verify no zombie processes can result.

title: Prompt engine and subject detection
description:
Create internal/homework/prompts.go with two functions:

- `DetectSubject(messageText string) string` — keyword-based classifier that scans message text for subject indicators (math terms, writing/essay terms, reading/literature terms) and returns a subject string like "math", "writing", "reading", or "general".

- `BuildSystemPrompt(profile HomeworkProfile, helpLevel HelpLevel, detectedSubject string) string` — assembles the full system prompt by composing: (1) tutor framing preamble, (2) age-appropriate language calibration based on profile age/grade, (3) subject-specific pedagogical rules (math: enforce step-by-step reasoning; writing: never write essays, redirect to brainstorming; reading: generate discussion questions), (4) anti-cheating guardrails, (5) help-level enforcement (e.g. level 1 = hints only, level 2 = guided walkthrough, level 3 = explain solution). This is a pure-logic module with no HTTP or DB dependencies. Unit-testable in isolation. Sibling sub-tasks depend on this for prompt generation but not vice versa.

Selected help level is sent with each message. Streams AI responses.

title: Homework Helper: AI tutor for kids with photo input and graduated help levels
description:
Build a homework helper for the kids — like the Chat page but pedagogically focused. AI tutor that explains rather than just solves, with per-kid context and graduated help levels.

## Per-kid profile

Each kid (existing is_child users) has a homework profile with:
- Age and grade level
- Current subjects/curriculum context
- Preferred response language (Norwegian, English, or Thai — kids switch based on subject)
- Optional: school name, current topics they're learning

Stored in a new kids_homework_profiles table or as user_preferences. The profile feeds into the system prompt so responses are calibrated to the right level (a 7-year-old learning to read needs very different help than a 13-year-old doing algebra).

## Graduated help levels

Kid clicks one of four buttons to choose how much help they want for each question:

1. **Hint** — A nudge in the right direction, no solution
2. **Explain the concept** — Teach what's needed without solving the specific problem
3. **Walk me through it** — Step-by-step with the answer at each step
4. **Show the answer** — Full solution with explanation (last resort)

The system prompt enforces the chosen level. The kid picks from buttons in the chat UI before sending each message.

## Photo input

The kid can take a photo of their homework (math problem, text passage, worksheet). Use HTML input with capture='environment' to open the phone camera directly. Save the image to a temp location, pass the file path to the Claude CLI which supports images on the Max plan.

## Chat infrastructure

Reuse the existing Chat infrastructure (Hytte-6707 added CLI session resumption for proper multi-turn). Each homework conversation uses a Claude CLI session for context across messages.

## System prompt (the pedagogy)

The prompt should:
- Frame Claude as a tutor, not an answer machine
- Adapt to the kid's age/grade/language from the profile
- Detect the subject (math, language, science, reading, writing) and adjust approach:
  - Math: step-by-step with checks, never just an answer at the requested level
  - Language: explain words, grammar, examples — but kid writes their own answer
  - Science: concepts and examples, not just facts
  - Reading: discussion questions, not summaries
  - Writing: brainstorming and outlining, NEVER write essays for the kid
- Refuse to write essays or do creative work — redirect to brainstorming
- Use age-appropriate language and examples
- Encourage the kid when they show understanding

## Subject auto-detection

Detect the subject from the message text or photo content. Adjust the prompt template based on detected subject.

## Parent review (history log)

Per-kid conversation history is visible to the parent (admin user). Track which help level was used per question. Optional: alert if a kid repeatedly hits 'show answer' without trying intermediate levels.

## Anti-cheating

- Never write entire essays
- Detect 'just give me the answer' phrasing and route to graduated help
- Subject-aware: writing assignments get redirected to brainstorming/outlining
- The tutor framing in the system prompt is the primary defense

## Pages

- /homework — main page, kid sees their conversations and starts new ones
- /homework/:id — individual conversation with chat + camera + help level buttons
- Settings: kid profile editor (age, grade, language, subjects)
- Parent admin: review kid conversations from a dashboard

## Files
- internal/homework/ — new package: handlers, store, models, prompts
- internal/db/db.go — kids_homework_profiles, homework_conversations, homework_messages tables
- internal/api/router.go — routes (gated by feature flag, accessible to is_child users)
- web/src/pages/HomeworkPage.tsx — list of conversations
- web/src/pages/HomeworkChat.tsx — chat interface with camera + help levels
- web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx — link for kids
- New feature flag: 'homework' to enable per-user

title: Stride: nightly evaluation should target yesterday + use notes for that date
description:
Two related issues with how Stride's nightly evaluation interprets timing and user notes:

## Issue 1: Wrong target date

The nightly evaluation runs at 03:00 and evaluates 'today' (e.g. April 10 at 03:00 = the new day). But at 03:00 nothing has happened yet — the user can't have done a workout for that day. The job should evaluate **yesterday**, which is the day that just ended.

Current behavior: marks today as 'missed' before the day has even started.
Correct behavior: evaluate yesterday's compliance.

## Issue 2: User notes ignored by evaluation

The evaluation logic in evaluate.go uses template strings ('Rest day taken as planned', 'Planned X was not completed') and doesn't read stride_notes at all. Notes only feed into the next plan generation prompt.

When a user writes 'I don't have time for a workout today, I'll do it tomorrow instead', Stride should pick up that note for the relevant date and use Claude to generate a contextual evaluation instead of the dumb template.

## Fix

1. Change the nightly target date in RunNightlyEvaluation: use yesterday (today - 1 day) as the evaluation target.

2. Add target_date to stride_notes (default: created_at date so existing behavior works).

3. When evaluating a date with no workout, fetch notes targeting that date. If any exist, call Claude with the notes + planned session to get a contextual evaluation. Fall back to the template when no notes.

4. Optionally: add a date picker on the notes input so users can write notes about specific days.

## Files
- internal/stride/evaluate.go — RunNightlyEvaluation, evaluateUserDay
- internal/stride/handlers.go — note CRUD with target_date
- internal/db/db.go — add target_date column to stride_notes
- web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx — notes form

title: Mezzanine: Needs Attention missing actions (approve-as-is, force smith, rerun warden)
description:
The Needs Attention panel in the Mezzanine only has Retry and Dismiss buttons. It's missing the actions available in Hearth:

- **Approve as-is** — skip warden review and create PR with current changes
- **Force Smith** — restart the smith for another attempt  
- **Rerun Warden** — rerun the warden review without restarting smith

These are critical for handling warden rejections like 'smith made no changes in response to warden feedback' where the code is actually fine and just needs manual approval.

## Implementation

The IPC commands already exist in Forge:
- approve_as_is
- force_smith  
- warden_rerun

The Hytte API endpoints may already exist too — check internal/forge/handlers.go for these actions. The Mezzanine Needs Attention panel (web/src/components/mezzanine/NeedsAttentionPanel.tsx or similar) just needs the buttons added alongside Retry and Dismiss.

Each action should have a confirmation dialog (same pattern as Retry/Dismiss).

## Files
- web/src/components/mezzanine/NeedsAttentionPanel.tsx or NeedsAttentionItem.tsx — add action buttons
- internal/forge/handlers.go — verify IPC endpoints exist for approve_as_is, force_smith, warden_rerun

title: Grocery list: shared list with voice input and Thai→Norwegian translation
description:
A shared grocery list between family members with voice input support. Key feature: wife can speak items in Thai, and they appear on the list in Norwegian for the person doing the shopping.

## Core features

### Shared list
- Multiple family members see the same list (shared per household, not per user)
- Real-time updates (or poll-based refresh)
- Check off items as you shop (strike-through, move to bottom)
- Clear completed items

### Voice input with translation
- Microphone button on the grocery list page
- Uses the Web Speech API (SpeechRecognition) for speech-to-text — works in Chrome/Safari, no backend needed for the transcription
- After transcription, translate the text to the target language using Claude:
  - Detect source language automatically
  - Translate to Norwegian (configurable target language)
  - Claude also normalizes grocery items (e.g. 'I need two kilos of rice and some fish sauce' → 'Ris 2kg' + 'Fiskesaus')
- Show both original and translated text on the item

### Manual input
- Standard text input for typing items directly
- Support both Norwegian and Thai input (no translation needed if already in target language)

## Data model

CREATE TABLE grocery_items (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    household_id INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- shared across family
    content TEXT NOT NULL,           -- encrypted, display text (translated)
    original_text TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',  -- encrypted, original voice input
    source_language TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    checked INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    added_by INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
);

## Translation approach
Use Claude CLI (already configured) with a simple prompt:
'Translate these grocery items to Norwegian. Input language: auto-detect. Return one item per line, normalized for a shopping list (quantities, standard names). Input: {transcribed text}'

This keeps it on the Max plan (no API key needed) and reuses the existing Claude CLI integration.

## Pages
- /grocery — the shared grocery list page
- Accessible from sidebar for all household members
- Mobile-first design (primary use case: phone in the store)

## Files
- internal/grocery/ — new package: handlers, store, models
- internal/db/db.go — grocery_items table
- internal/api/router.go — routes
- web/src/pages/GroceryPage.tsx — list UI with voice input

title: Race results: link workouts to Stride races, auto-fill results, race-specific analysis
description:
Race workouts and the Stride race calendar are completely disconnected. When a race workout is uploaded, nobody knows it was a race from the calendar. The analysis doesn't know the target time, priority, or what training block preceded it. Stride can't learn what worked.

## Three connected improvements

### 1. Auto-detect and link race workouts to calendar events

When a workout is uploaded and classified as 'race' (ai:type:race), or when any workout's date and approximate distance matches a stride_races entry:
- Auto-link the workout to the race (add race_id column to workouts table, or a linking table)
- Auto-populate stride_races.result_time from the workout's duration
- Show the link on both the workout detail page and the Stride race calendar

Match criteria: same date (±1 day) AND distance within 20% of the race distance. Confirm with user if ambiguous.

### 2. Race-specific AI analysis

When the insights prompt detects a race workout (linked or ai:type:race), enrich the prompt with:
- The race calendar entry (name, target time, priority, notes)
- Whether target was hit (actual vs target time)
- Split analysis: pacing strategy (positive/negative/even splits from lap data)
- Comparison to race predictions (from Riegel formula) — did they over/underperform?
- Training block context from Stride: what phase were they in, what was the weekly volume leading up, was there a taper?
- Previous races at similar distances for progression tracking

The prompt should ask Claude to provide:
- Race execution analysis (pacing, HR management, when they faded or surged)
- Goal assessment (target hit? by how much? realistic target?)
- Training effectiveness (what in the training block contributed to this result)
- Recommendations for next race at this distance

### 3. Feed race results into Stride plan generation

Add race results to the Stride plan generation prompt so the coach can:
- See actual race performances vs predictions
- Adjust training targets based on proven race fitness
- Use race results to calibrate threshold pace (a race result is the best fitness test)
- Plan appropriate recovery blocks after races
- Evaluate if the training approach is working toward the A-goal

Add to buildGeneratePrompt in internal/stride/generate.go: a 'Race History' section showing past race results with dates, distances, times, and paces.

## Files
- internal/db/db.go — add race_id to workouts or linking table
- internal/training/insights.go — race-specific prompt enrichment
- internal/training/prompt_context.go — BuildRaceContext function
- internal/stride/generate.go — add race history to plan prompt
- internal/stride/handlers.go — auto-link on workout upload
- web/src/pages/TrainingDetail.tsx — show race link, target vs actual
- web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx — show result on race calendar entries

title: Webhooks: format Radarr and Sonarr notifications
description:
Radarr and Sonarr send webhook payloads with eventType (camelCase) and instanceName fields. The current FormatWebhookNotification in internal/webhooks/format.go doesn't recognize them — they fall through to the generic fallback which just shows 'Webhook received: POST /hooks/... — N bytes' since Radarr/Sonarr use 'eventType' not 'event'/'action'/'type'.

## Radarr event types and suggested notifications

| eventType | Title | Body |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Test | Radarr: Test | Test notification from {instanceName} |
| Grab | Radarr: Grabbed | {movie.title} ({movie.year}) — {release.quality} from {release.indexer} |
| Download | Radarr: Downloaded | {movie.title} ({movie.year}) — {release.quality} |
| Rename | Radarr: Renamed | {movie.title} ({movie.year}) |
| MovieAdded | Radarr: Movie Added | {movie.title} ({movie.year}) |
| MovieDelete | Radarr: Movie Deleted | {movie.title} ({movie.year}) |
| MovieFileDelete | Radarr: File Deleted | {movie.title} ({movie.year}) |
| Health | Radarr: Health Issue | {message} |
| HealthRestored | Radarr: Health Restored | {message} |
| ApplicationUpdate | Radarr: Updated | Updated to {version} |
| ManualInteractionRequired | Radarr: Manual Action Needed | {movie.title} — manual interaction required |

## Sonarr event types and suggested notifications

| eventType | Title | Body |
|-----------|-------|------|
| Test | Sonarr: Test | Test notification from {instanceName} |
| Grab | Sonarr: Grabbed | {series.title} S{episode.seasonNumber}E{episode.episodeNumber} — {release.quality} |
| Download | Sonarr: Downloaded | {series.title} S{episode.seasonNumber}E{episode.episodeNumber} '{episode.title}' |
| Rename | Sonarr: Renamed | {series.title} |
| SeriesAdd | Sonarr: Series Added | {series.title} ({series.year}) |
| SeriesDelete | Sonarr: Series Deleted | {series.title} |
| EpisodeFileDelete | Sonarr: File Deleted | {series.title} S{episode.seasonNumber}E{episode.episodeNumber} |
| Health | Sonarr: Health Issue | {message} |
| HealthRestored | Sonarr: Health Restored | {message} |
| ApplicationUpdate | Sonarr: Updated | Updated to {version} |
| ManualInteractionRequired | Sonarr: Manual Action Needed | {series.title} — manual interaction required |

## Detection

Radarr/Sonarr payloads are identified by the presence of both 'eventType' and 'instanceName' fields. The instanceName is either 'Radarr' or 'Sonarr'.

## Implementation

Add a formatArrNotification function in internal/webhooks/format.go, called from FormatWebhookNotification when eventType + instanceName are present. Parse the nested movie/series/episodes/release objects to build readable notification text.

## Payload structure

Radarr: { eventType, instanceName, movie: {title, year, tmdbId}, remoteMovie, release: {quality, indexer, releaseTitle, size}, downloadClient, ... }
Sonarr: { eventType, instanceName, series: {title, year, tvdbId}, episodes: [{seasonNumber, episodeNumber, title}], release: {quality, ...}, ... }

## Files
- internal/webhooks/format.go — add formatArrNotification, detect by eventType+instanceName
- internal/webhooks/format_test.go — test cases for each event type

title: PR actions: ext- prefixed forge PRs should use GitHub API, not IPC
description:
PRs in the forge state DB with ext- bead IDs (created by warden batch updates, not the normal pipeline) route through the forge IPC handlers (approve_as_is, fix comments, etc.) which don't work because these beads don't exist in the Forge daemon's pipeline.

The Approve button sends an IPC approve_as_is command that the daemon ignores. Fix Comments would similarly fail.

## Fix

For PRs with ext- prefixed bead_id, the actions should use the external PR endpoints instead:
- Approve: POST /api/forge/ext-prs/approve (runs gh pr review --approve)
- Fix Comments: should run a review fix smith or gh-based comment resolution
- Merge: POST /api/forge/ext-prs/merge (or use gh pr merge)
- Bellows: assign bellows (already works via DB flag update)

Options:
1. Frontend detects ext- bead_id and calls the external endpoints
2. Backend handlers detect ext- bead_id and internally route to the GitHub-based implementation
3. Unify the handlers to always check: if bellows_managed=false and bead starts with ext-, use GitHub CLI

Option 2 is cleanest — the frontend doesn't need to know the difference.

## Files
- internal/forge/handlers.go — ApprovePRHandler, fix to detect ext- PRs and call gh pr review --approve
- web/src/components/mezzanine/PRModal.tsx — or keep frontend unchanged if backend handles routing

title: Mezzanine: PR management modal (open PRs, bellows, CI fix, merge)
description:

title: Forge dashboard: stuck PRs (exhausted CI/review fixes) don't show in Needs Attention
description:
PRs with exhausted CI fix or review fix attempts show in Hearth's Needs Attention but not in the Hytte dashboard. Hearth reads live daemon state via IPC which includes stuck PRs. The dashboard reads from the retries table only (StatusHandler in internal/forge/handlers.go line 255), but exhausted PR fix attempts are tracked on the prs table (ci_fix_count, review_fix_count) not in retries.

## Fix

In StatusHandler (internal/forge/handlers.go), after loading retries, also query PRs where ci_fix_count >= max or review_fix_count >= max (read limits from the forge config, or hardcode the defaults 5/5). Convert those into stuck items and append to the Stuck list.

Alternatively query prs where status='open' and check:
- ci_fix_count >= 5 AND ci_passing = 0 (CI fix exhausted)
- review_fix_count >= 5 AND has_unresolved_threads = 1 (review fix exhausted)

These should appear in the Needs Attention panel alongside retries.

## Files
- internal/forge/handlers.go — StatusHandler, line ~255
- internal/forge/db.go — may need a new query like StuckPRs()

title: Work hours: make punch-in start time editable
description:
The punch-in button records the current time as start_time but makes it uneditable. Users who forget to punch in on time have no way to correct it — they have to wait until end of day and create a manual session with both times.

## Fix

Make the punch-in start time editable. After punching in, the start time field should be an editable input (pre-filled with the punch-in time) that the user can adjust. When they punch out, the end time is recorded normally.

This covers two scenarios:
1. User punches in on time — start time is correct, no edit needed
2. User punches in late — edits start time to when they actually arrived

## Implementation

### Backend — internal/workhours/handlers.go

The open session (work_open_sessions table) stores the punch-in time. Add or update an endpoint to allow editing the start_time of an open session:
- PUT /api/workhours/punch/edit — accepts {start_time: 'HH:MM'}, updates the open session's start_time
- Or modify the existing punch-in endpoint to accept an optional start_time override

### Frontend — web/src/pages/WorkHoursPage.tsx

When punched in, show the start time as an editable time input field instead of static text. On change, call the edit endpoint to update the open session's start_time.

## Files
- internal/workhours/handlers.go — punch handlers, open session CRUD
- internal/workhours/storage.go — open session storage (work_open_sessions table)
- web/src/pages/WorkHoursPage.tsx — punch-in UI, time display

title: Add stride_custom_prompt setting to UI and backend allowlist
description:

**Files to modify:**
- `web/src/pages/Settings.tsx` — Add a `<textarea>` field in the Training section for `stride_custom_prompt`. Label it clearly as persistent coaching instructions (e.g., "Custom Prompt — persistent instructions appended to all Stride plan generations"). Wire it to the existing preferences save mechanism.
- `internal/auth/settings_handlers.go` — Add `"stride_custom_prompt"` to the allowed preferences list/map so the PUT/PATCH endpoint accepts and persists it. Check existing allowed preferences pattern and follow the same convention.

**Approach:** This is a straightforward settings field addition. The textarea should support multi-line input. Include a helper text distinguishing it from per-plan Coach Notes.

title: Allow generating plan for current week
description:
Modify the plan generation flow to support a `?week=current` query parameter.

**Files to modify:**
- `internal/stride/generate.go` — Refactor `upcomingWeek()` or add a `currentWeek()` function that returns the most recent Monday (go back to Monday of the current week). Update `GeneratePlan()` to accept a week mode parameter and use the appropriate date calculation.
- `internal/stride/handlers.go` — In `GeneratePlanHandler`, parse the optional `week` query parameter from the request (`r.URL.Query().Get("week")`). Pass `"current"` or `"next"` to `GeneratePlan()`. Default to `"next"` if not specified.
- `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx` — Update the Generate Plan button to pass `?week=current` if no plan exists yet, and `?week=next` otherwise. This requires checking whether any plan already exists before making the generate API call.

**Approach:** The current week Monday calculation is: subtract `(weekday - Monday) mod 7` days from today. If today is Monday, return today. The frontend logic checks existing plans list — if empty, default to current; otherwise default to next.

**Connection to sibling:** Independent of the custom prompt sub-task. No shared code changes.

title: Stride: allow generating plan for current week + add custom prompt setting
description:
Two issues with the initial Stride experience:

## 1. Cannot generate plan for current week

upcomingWeek() in internal/stride/generate.go always targets the next Monday. If today is Tuesday, it generates for next week (6 days away). On first use, users want a plan for THIS week.

Fix: Accept an optional week query parameter on POST /api/stride/plans/generate (e.g. ?week=current). When current is specified (or when no plan exists yet), calculate the Monday of the current week instead of the next Monday. The frontend Generate Plan button should default to current week if no plan exists, next week otherwise.

Current week calculation: go back to the most recent Monday.

## 2. No custom prompt setting in UI

The plan design includes stride_custom_prompt as a user preference appended to all Stride prompts (persistent coaching instructions like I prefer trail running or left knee injury avoid downhill). But there is no UI field for it in Settings.

Add a text area in the Training section of Settings for stride_custom_prompt. Also add it to the allowed preferences list in internal/auth/settings_handlers.go if not already there.

This is different from Coach Notes (per-plan feedback) — the custom prompt is persistent across all plan generations.

## Files
- internal/stride/generate.go — upcomingWeek() and GeneratePlan()
- internal/stride/handlers.go — GeneratePlanHandler (accept ?week param)
- web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx — Generate button logic
- web/src/pages/Settings.tsx — add stride_custom_prompt field
- internal/auth/settings_handlers.go — allow stride_custom_prompt preference

title: Chat page: second message always fails — Claude CLI timeout and prompt format
description:
The Chat page works for the first message but the second always returns 'Claude failed to respond'. The log shows 'signal: killed' — the Claude CLI process is being killed by a timeout.

## Root Cause

Two issues:

### 1. 60-second timeout too short for multi-turn conversations

internal/training/claude.go:71 hardcodes a 60-second timeout for RunPrompt. The chat handler sets 120s (handlers.go:231) but the inner 60s in runPromptCLI wins. On the second message, the full conversation history is included in the prompt, making it larger and slower to process. Claude can't respond in 60s and gets killed.

Fix: Either increase the timeout in runPromptCLI, or accept the context from the caller without overriding it with a shorter timeout. The chat handler already sets an appropriate timeout.

### 2. Prompt format for multi-turn conversations

internal/chat/handlers.go:325 buildPrompt() formats history as:
  Human: first message
  Assistant: first response  
  Human: second message

This is sent as a single string via claude -p (pipe mode). Claude CLI treats the entire thing as one user message, not a multi-turn conversation. This works but is inefficient and may confuse Claude.

Consider using the Claude CLI conversation flag or the API directly for proper multi-turn support. But the timeout is the immediate blocker — fixing that alone should resolve the 'failed to respond' error.

## Files
- internal/training/claude.go:71 — hardcoded 60s timeout (the killer)
- internal/chat/handlers.go:231 — 120s timeout (overridden by the inner 60s)  
- internal/chat/handlers.go:325 — buildPrompt() conversation format

title: Fix dashboard action handlers to use Forge IPC socket
description: Modify internal/forge/handlers.go — specifically RetryBeadHandler, DismissBeadHandler, ApproveBeadHandler, and ForceSmithHandler. Instead of writing directly to state.db, each handler should open a Unix domain socket connection to ~/.forge/forge.sock and send the appropriate IPC command, then read the response. Study internal/ipc/ in the Forge source to learn the command/response wire format (likely JSON or a simple framing protocol). Implement a small helper (e.g. sendIPCCommand(socketPath, cmd) error) and replace the direct DB mutations in each handler with calls to this helper. The portal approach is most robust: wrap the dropdown menu JSX in createPortal, position it absolutely using a ref-measured bounding rect of the trigger button. No backend changes required; this sub-task is fully self-contained.

title: Forge dashboard: Needs Attention dropdown clipped and actions don't work
description:
Two issues with the Needs Attention panel on the Forge dashboard page:

## 1. Dropdown menu clipped

The action dropdown (approve/force smith/dismiss/kill) is cut off — only the first 2 options are visible. The parent panel has overflow-hidden (NeedsAttentionCard.tsx line 170) which clips the absolutely-positioned dropdown.

Fix: either remove overflow-hidden from the panel, change the dropdown to open upward when near the bottom, or use a portal to render the dropdown outside the overflow container.

File: web/src/components/NeedsAttentionCard.tsx line 170 and line 250.

## 2. Actions don't take effect

Clicking retry/approve/dismiss/force-smith from the dashboard shows a success toast but nothing actually happens. The same actions work immediately from hearth (Forge TUI).

Root cause: The dashboard handlers (internal/forge/handlers.go) likely modify the Forge state DB directly (state.db), but the Forge daemon doesn't pick up those changes because it manages state in-memory. Hearth works because it uses IPC (forge.sock) to send commands to the running daemon.

Fix: The Hytte handlers should send IPC commands to the Forge daemon via the Unix socket (~/.forge/forge.sock) instead of modifying the DB directly. Check how hearth sends these commands (internal/ipc in the Forge source) and replicate the protocol.

Relevant files:
- web/src/components/NeedsAttentionCard.tsx — frontend dropdown and action handlers
- internal/forge/handlers.go — RetryBeadHandler, DismissBeadHandler, ApproveBeadHandler, ForceSmithHandler
- internal/api/router.go lines 201-204 — route registration
- Forge IPC protocol: ~/source/Forge/internal/ipc/ — command/response format

Add a weekly calendar view (Monday–Sunday cards) rendering each day from the current plan's plan_json: card shows session type and key metrics, expandable to show warmup/main_set/cooldown/strides/target_hr_cap/description. Show completion status by matching uploaded workouts to planned sessions by date. Add a coach notes section and a 'Generate Plan' button that POSTs to /api/stride/plans/generate and refreshes. Show an empty state when no plan exists. Layout must be mobile-friendly (stacked cards, tap to expand). Depends on Phase 2b API endpoints being available; can be built against a mock/stub response first.

title: Stride Phase 3: Nightly evaluation — workout matching, daily commentary
description:
Implement the nightly evaluation system: match uploaded workouts to the plan, evaluate each workout against the planned session, and display evaluations in the UI.

## Reference Plan
See .forge/plans/hytte-stride-ai-trainer.md for full design.

## Depends on: Hytte-0kgc (Phase 2 — plan generation)

## Workout-to-Plan Matching — internal/stride/match.go

Match uploaded workouts to planned sessions:
1. Exact date match: workout date = planned session date
2. Fuzzy match: ±1 day and similar type (e.g., easy run on Wed instead of Tue)
3. Unmatched: bonus workout, Stride still evaluates it

## Nightly Evaluation — internal/stride/evaluate.go

Daily at 03:00 Europe/Oslo:
1. Find all workouts from the past day that don't have a stride_evaluation
2. For each, find the matching planned session
3. Build evaluation prompt:
   - Planned session details (type, distance, pace targets, HR caps)
   - Actual workout data (distance, duration, pace, HR, laps, samples summary)
   - Weekly context (sessions completed so far, what's remaining)
   - Athlete profile (zones, threshold)
4. Call Claude
5. Parse into evaluation JSON (compliance, notes, flags, adjustments)
6. Store in stride_evaluations

## Evaluation JSON Schema

See plan doc. Key fields: planned_type, actual_type, compliance (completed/partial/skipped/substituted),
notes (2-3 sentences), flags (injury_risk, overtraining, significant_deviation), adjustments (optional
suggestion to modify remaining week).

## Push Notifications

If evaluation contains critical flags: send push notification.
Normal evaluations: visible in UI only, no push.

## API Endpoints

- GET /api/stride/evaluations?plan_id=X — evaluations for a plan
- POST /api/stride/evaluate — manual trigger for today's workouts

## Frontend

Update /training/stride page:
- Each day in the plan shows evaluation below the session if available
- Green checkmark for completed, yellow for partial, red for skipped
- Evaluation text displayed inline
- Flags shown as warning badges
- Also show evaluation in workout detail page (TrainingDetail.tsx) if linked

Update workout detail page:
- If workout has a stride_evaluation, show "Stride says:" section with the evaluation

title: Stride Phase 2: Weekly plan generation — prompt, scheduler, UI
description:
Implement the weekly plan generation system: build the prompt from all available data, schedule Sunday 18:00 generation, manual trigger, and plan display UI.

## Reference Plan
See .forge/plans/hytte-stride-ai-trainer.md for full design.

## Depends on: Hytte-mzyo (Phase 1 — tables, races, notes)

## Plan Generation Logic — internal/stride/generate.go (new package)

Build the plan generation prompt from:
1. User profile (BuildUserProfileBlock from training/prompt_context.go)
2. Primary goal race + race calendar from stride_races
3. Current training status (ACR, training load, VO2max trend)
4. Last 8 weeks of weekly summaries (from training WeeklySummaries)
5. Previous week's plan + completion (match workouts to planned sessions by date)
6. stride_notes from current week
7. stride_custom_prompt from user preferences
8. Available training days + weekly distance cap from preferences

Call Claude using the user's configured model (claude_model preference, default opus).
Parse response JSON into plan_json. Store in stride_plans with encrypted prompt/response.

## Training Philosophy in Prompt

The prompt MUST include the Marius Bakken model instructions (from the plan doc):
- Threshold-dominant for 3-5 session/week runners
- Threshold = BELOW lactate threshold, never on or above
- One hard session per 1-2 weeks only (30/15s, hill intervals, etc.)
- Every session needs exact pace ranges, HR caps, interval structure
- Adjust pacing for current fatigue (ACR)

## Plan JSON Schema

See plan doc for full schema. Key: each day has either a session with warmup/main_set/cooldown/strides/target_hr_cap/description or rest_day=true.

## Scheduling — internal/daemon or cmd/server/main.go

Add a Sunday 18:00 Europe/Oslo scheduler (same pattern as weekly allowance payouts):
- For each user with stride_enabled=true
- Generate next week's plan (Monday to Sunday)
- Store in stride_plans
- Send push notification: "Stride has your plan for next week"

## Manual Trigger

POST /api/stride/plans/generate — generates plan for next week on demand.
Useful for first-time setup and re-generation after adjustments.

## API Endpoints

- GET /api/stride/plans — list plans (paginated, newest first)
- GET /api/stride/plans/current — current week's plan
- GET /api/stride/plans/{id} — plan detail
- POST /api/stride/plans/generate — manual trigger

## Frontend

Update /training/stride page:
- Weekly calendar view showing each day's session
- Expand a day to see full session detail (warmup, intervals, cooldown, HR targets)
- Completion status: match uploaded workouts to planned sessions by date
- Coach notes section
- "Generate Plan" button (manual trigger)
- Empty state when no plan exists yet

The plan display should be mobile-friendly — card per day, expandable.

title: Stride Phase 1: Foundation — DB tables, race calendar CRUD, notes CRUD
description: Create the foundation for the Stride AI running coach: database tables, race calendar management, and notes system.
notes:
## Reference Plan
See .forge/plans/hytte-stride-ai-trainer.md for full design.

## Database Tables

Create 4 new tables in internal/db/db.go:
- stride_races: race calendar with A/B/C priority (name, date, distance_m, target_time, priority, notes, result_time)
- stride_plans: weekly training plans (week_start, week_end, phase, plan_json, prompt, response, model)
- stride_notes: user notes that feed into plan generation (plan_id, content)
- stride_evaluations: nightly workout-vs-plan comparisons (plan_id, workout_id, eval_json)

All text fields containing user data must be encrypted using encryption.EncryptField/DecryptField.

## API Endpoints

Race calendar:
- GET /api/stride/races — list all races for user, ordered by date
- POST /api/stride/races — create race (name, date, distance_m, target_time, priority, notes)
- PUT /api/stride/races/{id} — update race
- DELETE /api/stride/races/{id} — delete race

Notes:
- GET /api/stride/notes?plan_id=X — list notes for a plan (or all if no plan_id)
- POST /api/stride/notes — create note (plan_id optional, content required)
- DELETE /api/stride/notes/{id} — delete note

Feature-gate all endpoints behind a "stride" feature flag or reuse the existing training feature.

## Frontend

New page: /training/stride (or sub-section of existing training page)
- Race calendar list: upcoming races with priority badges (A=primary goal, B=important, C=tune-up)
- Add/edit/delete race form
- Notes list with text input to add new notes
- Empty state for plans (Phase 2) and evaluations (Phase 3)

## Settings

Add new user preferences:
- stride_enabled (boolean)
- stride_custom_prompt (string, free text appended to all Stride prompts)
- stride_weekly_distance_cap (int, max weekly km)
- stride_available_days (JSON array of day names)
- stride_long_run_day (string, default "Sunday")

title: Work hours: UI to mark sessions as internal time (interntid)
description: The work_sessions table already has an is_internal column and the salary calculator reads it, but the work hours page UI has no way to toggle a session as internal. Add a toggle/checkbox on each session row so users can mark internal meetings.
notes:
## What exists

- `work_sessions.is_internal` column (INTEGER, 0/1) — already in schema
- `WorkSession.IsInternal` in Go model — already populated
- `getHoursWorkedBoth()` in salary/store.go — already splits internal vs billable hours
- Salary calculator uses internal hours × internal_hourly_rate for commission revenue

## What's missing

The work hours page (`web/src/pages/WorkHoursPage.tsx`) needs:
1. A toggle/checkbox on each session to mark it as internal
2. The session create/edit API must accept and persist `is_internal`
3. Visual indicator (e.g. different color or icon) for internal sessions
4. Internal hours shown separately in the daily summary

Check existing session CRUD in `internal/workhours/handlers.go` to see if
`is_internal` is already accepted on create/update — it may just need the
frontend toggle.

The salary page already picks up internal hours automatically once they're
marked in the work hours page.
close_reason: The UI toggle for marking sessions as internal time was already implemented in commit b2ff8f3 as part of the Hytte-0rlp bead. The WorkHoursPage.tsx has the Building2 toggle button on each session row, a checkbox in the add session form, visual purple styling for internal sessions, and all three translations (en/nb/th).

title: Salary: vacation and sick day commission tier adjustment
description: Commission tier boundaries should scale proportionally based on absence. When you take vacation or sick days, the tier thresholds are reduced so you're not penalized for working fewer days. This matches the employer's payroll calculation.
notes:
## How it works — from employer's Kontroll spreadsheet

The commission has two calculations:
1. **Full month without absence** — standard tiers (0-60k, 60-80k, 80-100k, 100k+)
2. **Adjusted for vacation/sick** — tiers scaled by attendance percentage

### Attendance percentage
`attendance_pct = (working_days - vacation_days - sick_days) / working_days`

### Adjusted tier boundaries
Each tier boundary is multiplied by `attendance_pct`:
- 0-60k becomes 0 to (60k × pct)
- 60k-80k becomes (60k × pct) to (80k × pct)
- etc.

### Examples from spreadsheet

**January**: 19 working days, 4 vacation → 15/19 = 78.95%
- Adjusted tiers: 0-47,368 / 47,368-63,157 / 63,157-78,947 / 78,947+
- Full commission would be 54,625 kr, adjusted is 43,125 kr

**February**: 21 working days, 1 vacation, 1 sick → 19/21 = 90.48%  
- Adjusted tiers: 0-54,285 / 54,285-72,380 / 72,380-90,476 / 90,476+

**April**: 22 working days, 0 vacation/sick → 100%
- Tiers unchanged, full commission = adjusted commission

### Sick day commission
Additionally, there's a "Gj. snitt provisjon v sykdom/permisjon" (average commission for sick/leave).
This appears to add back average daily commission for each sick day — so you still earn some commission
on sick days but at an averaged rate rather than the marginal rate.

Formula appears to be: `sick_day_commission = (adjusted_commission / attended_days) * sick_days`

## Implementation — internal/salary/calculator.go

Currently `EstimateMonth` and `CalculateCommission` don't account for absence.

Change `CalculateCommission` to accept an `attendancePct` parameter:
- Scale each tier's floor/ceiling by `attendancePct`
- Calculate commission on scaled tiers
- Add back sick day average commission

The vacation_days and sick_days inputs come from:
- Work hours page (future: daily absence tracking)
- Manual month override (Hytte-wpk0)
- salary_records overrides

## Reference validation data

| Month | Vacation | Sick | Pct    | Full commission | Adjusted | Sick addon |
|-------|----------|------|--------|----------------|----------|------------|
| Jan   | 4        | 0    | 78.95% | 54,625         | 43,125   | 0          |
| Feb   | 1        | 1    | 90.48% | 64,375         | 58,244   | 2,659      |
| Mar   | 0        | 2    | 90.00% | 62,950         | 53,550   | 5,318      |
| Apr   | 0        | 0    | 100%   | 69,250         | 69,250   | 0          |

comments.author: Robin Smith
comments.text:
IMPORTANT: Commission formula correction based on Kontroll spreadsheet analysis.

The commission is NOT calculated on actual billable revenue directly. It uses adjusted tiers.

## Correct formula

1. Tier boundaries scaled by attendance: tier × (working_days - absence) / working_days
2. actual_revenue = (actual_billable_hours × hourly_rate) + (internal_hours × internal_rate)
3. commission = apply actual_revenue against ADJUSTED tiers
4. sick_addon = separate calculation for sick days
5. gross = base_salary + commission + sick_addon

## Important: payslip month offset

The payslip paid in March covers FEBRUARY hours. So Feb hours → March payslip.
Commission on the payslip is calculated from the previous month's work.

## Verified with March payslip (Feb hours)

- Feb: 21 working days, 1 vacation, 1 sick → 19/21 = 90.48%
- Adjusted tiers: 0-54,286 / 54,286-72,381 / 72,381-90,476 / 90,476+
- Feb actual revenue: 149.5h × 1300 + 0h internal = 194,350
- Commission on adjusted tiers: ~62,794
- Sick addon: 2,659
- Gross: 23,000 + 62,794 + 2,659 = ~88,453 (spreadsheet says 88,453.40)
- March payslip says 93,419.12 (diff ~4,966 from unknown components)

Also: Spreadsheet uses 20 working days for March but calendar has 22 — need to verify.

title: Salary: manual month overrides for past months (hours, vacation, sick, actual)
description: For past months, allow direct input of key salary fields on the salary page instead of requiring daily work hour entries. Fields: total billable hours, vacation days, sick days, internal hours (interntid), actual gross and actual net from payslip.
notes:
## Context

Currently the salary page gets hours from the work_hours table (daily entries). For past months
this is impractical — the user would need to add entries for every single day. Instead, allow
manual overrides directly on the salary page for past months.

## Fields needed per month

- **Billable hours** (currently computed from work_hours — allow override)
- **Vacation days taken** (integer)
- **Sick days** (integer)  
- **Internal hours (interntid)** (hours spent in internal meetings, separate rate)
- **Actual gross** (from payslip, "Brutto på lønnslipp")
- **Actual net** (from payslip, "Utbetalt")

## Storage

Use the existing `salary_records` table or extend it. Currently has: month, gross, tax, net,
status, confirmed_at, budget_transaction_id. May need additional columns:
- `override_hours` (nullable — if set, overrides work_hours lookup)
- `vacation_days` (integer)
- `sick_days` (integer)
- `internal_hours` (real)
- `actual_gross` (real, from payslip)
- `actual_net` (real, from payslip)

## UI

On the salary month view, for past months (or any month), show editable fields below the
hero card. When override_hours is set, use it instead of querying work_hours. The hero card
should reflect the overridden values.

## API

Extend `PUT /api/salary/records/{month}` to accept the new fields, or add a dedicated
endpoint for month overrides.

title: Salary estimator: month navigation in hero card view
description: The salary estimator 'This Month' tab is locked to the current month. Add prev/next month navigation so users can browse past and future months in the detailed hero card view. The backend endpoint already exists (GET /api/salary/estimate/month?month=YYYY-MM) but the frontend never calls it.
notes:
## What exists

- Backend: `EstimateMonthHandler` at `GET /api/salary/estimate/month?month=YYYY-MM` — ready to use
- Frontend: `SalaryPage.tsx` has a `selectedYear` state with prev/next for the Year View tab, but no equivalent `selectedMonth` for the Month View tab
- The hero card always calls `GET /api/salary/estimate/current` which returns only the current month

## Implementation

1. Add `selectedMonth` state (default: current month YYYY-MM)
2. Add prev/next chevron buttons to the hero card header (same pattern as year selector)
3. When `selectedMonth` !== current month, call `/api/salary/estimate/month?month=YYYY-MM` instead of `/estimate/current`
4. Allow navigating forward up to 12 months ahead (for projections) and backward indefinitely
5. Show month name + year in the header (e.g. "Mars 2026")

Follow the existing year navigation pattern in the same file for UI consistency.

title: Credit card: defer transactions to next month for statement alignment
description: Allow marking individual credit card transactions as 'deferred to next month'. These are settled transactions whose date falls in the current month but won't appear on the statement until next month (late-settled at month boundary). The sync and closing balance calculations should exclude deferred transactions from the current month and include them in the next.
notes:
## Context

DNB credit card statements have a cutoff that doesn't align perfectly with calendar month boundaries. Transactions settled on the last day of the month (e.g. March 31) sometimes don't appear on the March statement — they show up on April's instead. This causes the computed closing balance to be off by the sum of those late transactions.

Example: March has two transactions on March 31 (839 kr + 1,378 kr = 2,217 kr) that aren't on the March statement. The computed closing is ~11,029 but the actual statement says 9,056.

## Design

### Database

Add a boolean column to `credit_card_transactions`:
```sql
ALTER TABLE credit_card_transactions ADD COLUMN deferred INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
```

### Backend — sync.go and history.go

When computing the settled net for a month, exclude `deferred = 1` transactions:
```sql
AND (deferred = 0 OR transaksjonsdato < periodStart)
```

For the NEXT month, include deferred transactions from the previous month:
```sql
-- Add to the next month's query:
OR (deferred = 1 AND transaksjonsdato >= prevMonthStart AND transaksjonsdato < periodStart)
```

### API

Add `PUT /api/credit-card/transactions/{id}/defer` — toggles the deferred flag.

### Frontend

Add a small button/toggle on each transaction row (maybe a calendar-forward icon) to mark it as deferred. Show deferred transactions with a visual indicator (dimmed or with a "→ Next month" badge).

### Affected queries

- `internal/creditcard/sync.go` — SyncCreditCardExpense
- `internal/creditcard/history.go` — MonthlyHistoryHandler  
- `internal/budget/handlers.go` — CreditCardSummaryHandler (Used amount)
- `internal/creditcard/transactions.go` — TransactionsListHandler (show deferred status)

title: Credit card running balance — opening balance anchor for accurate variable bill sync
description: The variable bill sync needs to reflect the actual statement closing balance (what the user owes next month), not just monthly expenses. Currently it sums expenses-only which doesn't account for carried-over balances. Need an opening balance anchor so closing = opening + expenses - innbetalinger.
notes:
## Context

Credit card statements have: Inngående saldo (opening) + transactions = Utgående saldo (closing).
The closing balance is what you pay next month. We can't compute this from transactions alone
because we need a known starting point.

The user has data from Jan 2026 onward. Their Jan 1 2026 opening balance is 24,474.97 kr.
However, transaction dates don't perfectly align with statement cutoff dates (late-settled
transactions on month boundaries), so per-month opening balances may need manual correction.

## Design

### New table: `credit_card_balances`

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS credit_card_balances (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    credit_card_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    month TEXT NOT NULL,           -- YYYY-MM
    opening_balance REAL NOT NULL, -- Inngående saldo from statement
    UNIQUE(user_id, credit_card_id, month)
);
```

Only needs one row as the anchor — the earliest month. Later months compute automatically:
closing(month) = opening(month) + sum of month's settled transactions (expenses negative, innbetalinger positive, so: opening - sum(-belop))

But allow overriding any month's opening balance for date-boundary corrections.

### Variable bill sync — `internal/creditcard/sync.go`

Change `SyncCreditCardExpense` to compute closing balance:
1. Find the nearest opening balance at or before the billing period
2. Sum all settled transactions from that anchor through end of billing period
3. Closing = anchor opening + cumulative net
4. Store closing balance as the variable bill entry (next month)

### Credit card summary — `internal/budget/handlers.go`

Used amount = closing balance for the selected month (same calculation).

### Frontend — credit card page

Add an editable "Opening balance" field at the top of the transactions view.
Shows computed closing balance below it. User can set/override the opening
balance for any month from their statement's "Inngående saldo".

### API endpoints

- `GET /api/credit-card/balance?credit_card_id=X&month=YYYY-MM` — returns opening balance (stored or computed) and closing balance
- `PUT /api/credit-card/balance` — set/update opening balance for a month

### Known data

- Jan 2026 opening: 24,474.97 kr (from Dec 2025 utgående saldo)
- Statement date boundaries don't align perfectly with transaction dates
- Late-settled transactions (e.g. March 31 transactions not on March statement) cause ~2,217 kr discrepancy

### Translation keys needed

`creditCards.openingBalance` / `Inngående saldo` / ...
`creditCards.closingBalance` / `Utgående saldo` / ...

comments.author: Robin Smith
comments.text:
Investigation update: computing closing balance from transactions doesn't work reliably due to:

1. **Date boundary mismatches** — late-settled transactions on month boundaries don't align with statement cutoffs. Jan computed closing is off by 2,734 from statement.
2. **Possible duplicates** — Feb has two pairs of identical amounts on same dates (-5,747 x2 and -2,957 x2 on Feb 17), and total expenses of 61,880 seems too high.
3. **Cumulative drift** — errors compound across months, making single-anchor + running balance unreliable.

**Revised approach**: require opening balance per month (from statement "Inngående saldo"), not just one anchor. Closing = opening + sum(settled belop for month). This self-corrects each month when the user enters the statement opening.

Also: investigate Feb duplicates — may need a dedup pass or import fix.

title: Credit card monthly history: show expense, innbetaling, and net outstanding in footer
description: The monthly history tab footer should show three rows: total expenses, total innbetalinger, and net outstanding (expenses minus innbetalinger). The net outstanding is what feeds into the variable expense for next month's credit card payment. Also update SyncCreditCardExpense to use net outstanding instead of expenses-only, and add a credit card selector to the BudgetVariables page.
notes:
## Changes needed

### 1. Monthly history backend — `internal/creditcard/history.go`

The `month_totals` response field currently only tracks expenses. Change to return three fields:
- `month_expense_totals` — sum of non-innbetaling transactions (existing logic)
- `month_innbetaling_totals` — sum of innbetaling transactions (absolute values)
- `month_net_totals` — expenses minus innbetalinger (the outstanding balance)

The query already includes `is_innbetaling` (just fixed). Split the accumulation in the scan loop:
```go
if !isInnbetaling {
    expenseTotals[month] += total
} else {
    innbetalingTotals[month] += Math.Abs(total)
}
netTotals[month] = expenseTotals[month] - innbetalingTotals[month]
```

Update `MonthlyHistoryResponse` struct to include all three maps.

### 2. Monthly history frontend — `web/src/pages/BudgetCreditCards.tsx`

Update `MonthlyHistoryView` tfoot to render three rows instead of one:
- **Expenses** row (red) — from `month_expense_totals`
- **Innbetalinger** row (green) — from `month_innbetaling_totals`  
- **Outstanding** row (bold, red if positive, green if zero/negative) — from `month_net_totals`

Add translation keys to all three locales (`en`, `nb`, `th`) under `creditCards.history`:
- `expenses` / `Utgifter` / Thai equivalent
- `payments` / `Innbetalinger` / Thai equivalent
- `outstanding` / `Utestående` / Thai equivalent

Update the `MonthlyHistory` TypeScript interface to match.

### 3. Variable expense sync — `internal/creditcard/sync.go`

`SyncCreditCardExpense` currently sums only `is_innbetaling = 0` transactions. Change to compute net:
- Sum expenses: `COALESCE(-SUM(CASE WHEN is_innbetaling = 0 THEN belop ELSE 0 END), 0)`
- Sum innbetalinger: `COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN is_innbetaling = 1 THEN belop ELSE 0 END), 0)`
- Net = expenses - innbetalinger

Store the net as the variable entry amount. This is what the user has to pay next month.

### 4. Variable bill credit card selector — `web/src/pages/BudgetVariables.tsx`

Add a dropdown/select to link a variable bill to a credit card account. The `budget_variable_bills` table already has a `credit_card_id` column. Need:
- Fetch credit card accounts (`GET /api/credit-card/accounts`)
- Show a select in the variable bill form/edit UI
- Save `credit_card_id` on create/update

Check how the existing variable bill CRUD endpoints handle `credit_card_id`.

### 5. Tests

- Update `internal/creditcard/history_test.go` — verify all three footer totals
- Update `internal/creditcard/sync_test.go` — test with innbetaling transactions, verify net calculation
- Frontend test if applicable

title: Decrypt enc: webhook URLs when loading config
description: Forge daemon reads webhook URLs from ~/.forge/config.yaml but does not decrypt enc:-prefixed values. The Hytte GUI encrypts webhook URLs with AES-256-GCM (enc: prefix) before writing to the config file, but Forge passes the raw enc:... string to the HTTP client, causing 'unsupported protocol scheme "enc"' errors on every webhook delivery.
notes:
## Root cause

Hytte's forge config handler (`internal/forge/config_handler.go`) encrypts webhook URLs on save via `encryptSensitiveFields()` using `internal/encryption` (AES-256-GCM, key from `~/.config/hytte/.encryption_key`). The `enc:` prefix marks encrypted values.

Forge's daemon (`internal/daemon/daemon.go` lines ~254 and ~4651) reads `w.URL` from config and passes it directly to `notify.WebhookTarget` with no decryption.

## Recommended fix

Add a small `internal/crypto` package to Forge that can decrypt `enc:`-prefixed strings using the same key file (`~/.config/hytte/.encryption_key`). The logic is straightforward AES-256-GCM:

1. Strip `enc:` prefix
2. Base64-decode the remainder
3. Read 32-byte key from `~/.config/hytte/.encryption_key` (hex-encoded)
4. AES-256-GCM open (nonce is prepended to ciphertext)

Apply decryption in daemon.go where webhook targets are built (two locations: initial startup ~line 254 and hot-reload rebuild ~line 4649). If the value doesn't have the `enc:` prefix, use it as-is (plaintext backward compat).

Reference implementation: `~/source/Hytte/internal/encryption/encryption.go` — functions `DecryptField` and `Decrypt`.

## Affected code

- `source/Forge/internal/daemon/daemon.go:254` — initial webhook target setup
- `source/Forge/internal/daemon/daemon.go:4649` — hot-reload webhook target rebuild
- New: `source/Forge/internal/crypto/crypto.go` — decrypt helper

## Verification

After fix, `journalctl -u forge | grep webhook` should show successful deliveries instead of `unsupported protocol scheme "enc"` errors.

title: Mezzanine Phase 4: Detail pages + keyboard shortcuts
description:
Add detail/drill-down pages and keyboard navigation to the Mezzanine.

Detail pages:
- /forge/mezzanine/worker/{id} — full log viewer (reuse WorkerLogModal), phase timeline, token usage, cost, related events
- /forge/mezzanine/events — full paginated event log with search, date range picker, event correlation (click to see full bead lifecycle)
- /forge/mezzanine/ingots — bead lifecycle tracking with duration metrics and success rates
- /forge/mezzanine/anvils — per-anvil health overview (last activity, open PRs, queue depth)

Keyboard shortcuts (desktop):
- r=refresh, m=merge first ready PR, k=kill focused worker, q/w/e=focus panels, 1-6=focus worker, ?=help

Push notification deep links:
- PR ready → mezzanine with PR highlighted
- Bead failed → needs-attention expanded
- Cost limit → costs page

See docs/forge-mezzanine.md Phase 4.

Events panel (bottom-left):

- Filter dropdown: All, Errors only, PRs only, per-anvil

Costs panel (bottom-right):

Full cost dashboard page at /forge/mezzanine/costs:

See docs/forge-mezzanine.md Phase 3.

title: Mezzanine Phase 2: Pipeline visualization + action buttons
description:
Add the Pipeline zone and action system to the Mezzanine.

Pipeline (middle bar):
- Horizontal flow: Queue → Schematic → Smith → Temper → Warden → PR → Merged
- Active beads shown as cards at their current stage
- PRs show CI status badges, review state, merge readiness
- Merge button on ready PRs with confirmation dialog

Needs Attention panel:
- Beads that failed, stalled, or need clarification
- Retry / Clarify / Dismiss buttons per item

Queue actions:
- Run Now, Clarify, Tag, Dismiss via context menu
- Confirmation dialogs for destructive actions

Reuse: useAllPRs hook for PR data, existing IPC action endpoints.

See docs/forge-mezzanine.md Phase 2.
