title: Mezzanine Phase 1: Forge Floor — live worker panels with streaming output
description:
Build the core Mezzanine page at /forge/mezzanine with the mission control layout. Phase 1 focuses on the Forge Floor (center zone) — the most impactful feature.

- Status bar: daemon status, worker count, daily cost, last poll
- Worker panels: each active worker shown as a card with bead ID, anvil, phase, duration, provider
- Live output streaming: reuse existing LiveActivity.tsx component, adapted for per-worker panels
- Collapsed panels show last 2-3 lines as preview, click to expand
- Idle worker slots shown as empty panels
- Kill worker button per panel
- Mobile-responsive: stacked cards on small screens
- Basic queue list (left sidebar, no drag-reorder yet)
- Reuse: useForgeStatus hook, LiveActivity.tsx, WorkerLogModal.tsx, CollapsiblePanelHeader.tsx

No new backend work — all API endpoints already exist.

See docs/forge-mezzanine.md for full design.
close_reason: Decomposed into 3 sub-beads: Hytte-v7ty, Hytte-hsmk, Hytte-2shj

title: Resolve pending credit card transactions when settled version is imported
description:
When importing credit card CSV, a transaction that was previously imported as 'Reservert - X' should be replaced when the settled version 'X' appears with the same date and amount. Currently the dedup key includes the full beskrivelse, so 'Reservert - CLAUDE.AI SUBSCRIPTION' and 'CLAUDE.AI SUBSCRIPTION' are treated as different transactions, creating duplicates.

Fix: during dedup, also check for pending matches — if an existing row has is_pending=1 and the new row matches on (date, belop) with beskrivelse matching after stripping 'Reservert - ', update the existing row (clear is_pending, update beskrivelse and bokforingsdato) instead of inserting a new one.

title: Credit card monthly history overview — group totals across months
description: Add a monthly history view to the credit card page showing group subtotals across months in a grid (like the user's Google Sheet Kredittkort tab). Rows = groups (Subscriptions, Mat, Diverse, etc.), Columns = months. Each cell shows the group's total for that month. Includes a total row. This lets you see spending trends per group over time. Can be a separate tab/section on the credit card page or its own sub-page at /budget/credit-cards/history. Should cover at least 6 months of history.

title: Re-apply merchant group rules to existing credit card transactions
description: Add a 'Re-apply rules' button on the credit card page that runs all merchant_group_rules against existing transactions that are ungrouped (group_id IS NULL) or in the Diverse group. This handles the case where rules are created after transactions are already imported. API: POST /api/credit-card/transactions/reapply-rules. Should report how many transactions were reassigned.

title: Credit card page UI: upload, preview, grouped transactions display
description: Update the credit card page component to include: (1) an upload button triggering CSV file input, calling the import API for a preview modal showing new/skipped counts and a transaction list, with a confirm button; (2) grouped transaction list UI — transactions grouped by credit_card_groups with subtotals, a 'Diverse' catch-all, monthly total, pending badge for reservert rows, foreign currency notation from belop_i_valuta; (3) drag-and-drop or dropdown to reassign transactions to groups; (4) variable expense link status (showing synced amount). Uses server components/actions or React Query. Depends on all previous sub-tasks.

title: Transaction grouping: groups management and merchant rules
description: Implement CRUD API routes for credit_card_groups (create, rename, reorder). Implement merchant_group_rules management: create/delete rules with a merchant_pattern string. Add a bulk-assign endpoint to move transactions to a group. Implement auto-detect recurring merchants query: find beskrivelse values appearing in transactions across 2+ distinct months and suggest them for grouping. Seed a 'Diverse' default group per user on first import. Depends on the data model sub-task; consumed by the UI sub-task.

title: Variable expense auto-update after import
description: After a confirmed import, calculate the monthly total for the billing period: sum belop for all non-innbetaling transactions in the period. Find the linked variable expense entry for the credit card's recurring rule (existing budget_transactions / variable expense tables). Update that entry's amount to reflect the actual bill. Expose this as a utility function (e.g. syncCreditCardExpense(creditCardId, period)) called from the import confirmation handler. Depends on the CSV import sub-task and must not break existing variable expense logic.

title: CSV import API: parse, dedup, and store transactions
description: Create a server action or API route (e.g. app/api/credit-card/import/route.ts) that accepts a CSV file upload. Implement a parser for the DNB format: handle Norwegian number format (comma as decimal separator, unicode minus sign), map all 10 columns. Dedup logic: before inserting, check existing rows matching on (transaksjonsdato + beskrivelse + belop) and skip duplicates. Mark rows with 'Reservert' in beskrivelse as is_pending=true, rows containing 'Innbetaling' as is_innbetaling=true. After import, apply merchant_group_rules to auto-assign group_id. Return a preview payload (new rows count, skipped count, rows array) before final commit, supporting a two-phase preview→confirm flow. Depends on the data model sub-task.

title: Data model: credit card tables migration
description: Create database migrations for three new tables: credit_card_transactions (id, user_id, credit_card_id, transaksjonsdato, bokforingsdato, beskrivelse, mottakers_kontonummer, kid, belop, belop_i_valuta, utsatt, utsatt_periode, utlopsdato, is_pending, is_innbetaling, group_id, imported_at), credit_card_groups (id, user_id, name, sort_order), and merchant_group_rules (id, user_id, merchant_pattern, group_id). Add FK from credit_card_transactions.group_id → credit_card_groups.id and merchant_group_rules.group_id → credit_card_groups.id. This is a prerequisite for all other sub-tasks.

title: FIT file upload fails from mobile phone but works from desktop
description: Uploading a .fit file on the training page works from a computer but fails from a mobile phone. Needs investigation: could be file size limits, content-type differences, multipart form handling on mobile browsers, or the file picker returning a different MIME type on mobile. Check the upload handler for any assumptions about the request that differ between mobile and desktop browsers.

title: Credit card CSV import with dedup and variable expense auto-fill
description:
Import credit card transactions from Norwegian bank CSV export (DNB format).

CSV format: Transaksjonsdato, Bokføringsdato, Beskrivelse, Mottakers kontonummer, KID, Beløp, Beløp i valuta, Utsatt, Utsatt periode, Utløpsdato. Norwegian number format (comma decimal, unicode minus).

Features:
1. Upload CSV on the credit card page — parse and preview transactions
2. Dedup: skip rows already imported (match on transaksjonsdato + beskrivelse + beløp to handle re-uploads)
3. Store imported rows in a credit_card_transactions table (separate from budget_transactions — these are credit card line items, not budget movements)
4. Calculate monthly total (sum of all expenses excl. Innbetaling rows) for the billing period
5. Auto-update the linked variable expense entry for the credit card recurring rule — so the regning page shows the actual upcoming bill
6. Show transactions grouped by month on the credit card page with running total
7. Handle 'Reservert' (pending) transactions — include in total but mark as pending
8. Handle foreign currency amounts (Beløp i valuta shows original amount + exchange rate)

Transaction grouping:
- Transactions should be assigned to groups (e.g. 'Subscriptions', 'Mat', 'Diverse')
- Auto-detect recurring merchants: if the same Beskrivelse appears in multiple months, suggest grouping them (CLAUDE.AI SUBSCRIPTION, PAYPAL *BYTESIZED, PLAYSTATION, Microsoft*Xbox, Google *Google One, TV 2, etc.)
- User can create custom groups and drag/assign transactions to them
- 'Diverse' as a default catch-all group for ungrouped one-off purchases
- Show group subtotals on the credit card page
- Remember group assignments by merchant name so future imports auto-group

Data model addition: credit_card_groups (id, user_id, name, sort_order) and a group_id FK on credit_card_transactions. Also a merchant_group_rules table (user_id, merchant_pattern, group_id) for auto-assignment.

The credit card page should show: grouped transactions with subtotals, monthly total, status of variable expense link, and an upload button.
notes:
Additional requirements:

1. Monthly history overview: Show previous months side by side (like a spreadsheet) — each group's subtotal per month so you can see trends. Columns = months, rows = groups.

2. Food/Mat tracking across cards: The 'Mat' group is special — it combines credit card transactions AND regular debit card spending. Add an option per group to 'include budget transactions from category' — when set, the group total pulls from both credit card imports AND budget_transactions matching that category. Only Mat needs this currently but the mechanism should be generic.

3. The monthly history view should show the combined totals for cross-card groups like Mat.

title: Link recurring transactions to variable bills for dynamic amounts
description:
Add variable_id (FK to budget_variable_bills, nullable) to budget_recurring table. When a recurring rule has a variable_id set:
- The recurring amount field is ignored (can be 0)
- The actual amount comes from the sum of that month's variable entries
- The regning calculator uses the variable total instead of the fixed recurring amount
- If no entries exist for the current month, show a warning on the regning page
- The recurring transaction generation also uses the variable amount for the generated transaction

Update the recurring form to allow linking to a variable bill (dropdown of available variables).
Update the regning page to show variable amounts with a link to the variables page for editing.

title: Variable bills frontend page
description:
New page at /budget/variables (BudgetVariables.tsx) for managing variable monthly expenses.

Layout:
- List of variable bills (e.g. Strøm, Kommunale)
- Each bill expands to show sub-items for the current month (e.g. Tibber: 5063, BKK: -1633)
- Inline editing of sub-item amounts
- Total shown per variable bill
- 'Copy from last month' button per bill — copies sub-items and amounts, shows diff from previous values
- Month selector to view/edit different months
- Add/remove sub-items within a variable bill
- Navigation link from budget page

i18n keys in en, nb, th.

title: Variable bills data model and API
description:
New tables for tracking variable monthly expenses with sub-items:

budget_variable_bills: id, user_id, name (e.g. 'Strøm'), recurring_id (FK to budget_recurring, nullable)
budget_variable_entries: id, variable_id (FK), month (YYYY-MM), sub_name (e.g. 'Tibber', 'BKK'), amount (REAL)

API endpoints:
- GET /api/budget/variables — list all variable bills with current month entries
- POST /api/budget/variables — create variable bill
- PUT /api/budget/variables/{id} — update name/link
- DELETE /api/budget/variables/{id} — delete variable bill
- PUT /api/budget/variables/{id}/entries?month=YYYY-MM — set entries for a month (array of {sub_name, amount})
- POST /api/budget/variables/{id}/copy?from=YYYY-MM&to=YYYY-MM — copy previous month entries to target month (values included as starting point)

The copy endpoint duplicates all sub-items with their amounts so the user just needs to adjust changed values.

title: Income due dates on Regning page with pre-weekend/holiday adjustment
description: Add income configuration to the regning system: 1) Your income day (e.g. 20th) and partner income day - stored in user_preferences. 2) Income dates adjust BACKWARDS for weekends/holidays (payday moves to Friday if 20th is Saturday/Sunday/holiday). 3) Show both incomes with their adjusted due dates on the Regning page. 4) This is the opposite of expense adjustment (expenses defer forward, income moves backward).
notes: forgeReady

title: Business day adjustment for recurring transaction due dates
description: Recurring transactions with due dates falling on weekends or Norwegian public holidays should defer to the next business day. Requires: 1) Norwegian public holiday calendar (fixed dates + Easter-based movable holidays). 2) nextBusinessDay() helper that skips weekends and holidays. 3) Apply to recurring transaction generation (nextRecurringDueDate). 4) Show actual adjusted due date on the Regning page.
notes: forgeReady

title: Fix budget page mobile overflow — navigation icons go off-screen
description: The budget page header has 7 navigation icons + Add button in a flex row with no responsive handling. On mobile screens they overflow to the right. Options: wrap to second row, use a horizontal scroll, or collapse into a dropdown/menu on small screens.
notes: forgeReady

title: Build regning calculator frontend page
description: New page at /budget/regning (BudgetRegning.tsx) showing the bills split summary. Lists all shared recurring expenses with each partner's share, split type indicator, and totals. Shows: your transfer amount, partner's transfer amount, remaining from each salary after transfer. Add navigation link from budget page. i18n keys in en, nb, th.
notes: forgeReady

title: Build regning calculator API endpoint
description: New endpoint GET /api/budget/regning that computes the monthly bills split. For each recurring transaction marked as shared: apply its split_type/split_pct (or global income_split as fallback) to determine each partner's share. Return: list of expenses with per-person amounts, total transfer for you, total transfer for partner, remaining from each salary. Uses existing recurring transactions + the new split fields + partner income.
notes: forgeReady

title: Add partner income field to user preferences
description: Add partner_income (monthly salary) to user_preferences storage, alongside existing income_split_percentage. Add getter/setter functions in budget/seed.go, expose via settings API. Default 0. The regning calculator needs both salaries to compute what's left after transfers.
notes: forgeReady

title: Add split_type and split_pct fields to recurring transactions
description: Add split_type (percentage/equal/fixed_you/fixed_partner) and split_pct (0-100, nullable) columns to budget_recurring table. Update model, CRUD, handlers, and test schema. split_type defaults to 'percentage', split_pct defaults to null (uses global income split). This enables per-expense split ratios for the regning calculator.
notes: forgeReady

title: Budget: category seeding + income split preferences
description: Create internal/budget/seed.go with SeedDefaultCategories(store Store) that inserts the prescribed categories: forsikring, lån, husforsikring, strøm, mat, barnehage, mobil, transport, underholdning — mapped to groups Bolig, Barn, Fast, Variabel, Inntekt. Mark income categories with is_income=true. Add income_split_percentage int (default 60) to user_preferences table (migration in db.go createSchema or a migration file). Add GetIncomeSplit/SetIncomeSplit to store or a preferences store. Seed should be idempotent (upsert or skip-if-exists). Depends on sub-task 1 for schema; sub-task 2 store interface should expose Category CRUD used here.

title: Budget: CRUD storage layer
description: Create internal/budget/store.go implementing Store interface with CRUD methods for all four entities: CreateAccount/GetAccount/ListAccounts/UpdateAccount/DeleteAccount, same pattern for Category, Transaction, Recurring. Use database/sql or existing db abstraction consistent with the rest of the codebase. ListTransactions should support filtering by account_id, category_id, date range. GetRecurringDue(now time.Time) returns recurring rules where last_generated is nil or older than next due date. Depends on sub-task 1 for schema and model types.

title: Budget: schema + data model types
description: Create internal/budget/ package. In internal/budget/model.go define Go structs: Account (id, name, type enum checking/savings/credit/cash, currency, balance, icon), Category (id, name, group_name, icon, color, is_income), Transaction (id, account_id, category_id, amount, description, date, tags []string, is_transfer, transfer_to *int), Recurring (id, account_id, category_id, amount, frequency enum monthly/weekly/yearly, day_of_month, start_date, end_date, last_generated). In db.go createSchema() add CREATE TABLE statements for budget_accounts, budget_categories, budget_transactions, budget_recurring with proper FK constraints. Also add budget feature flag to auth/features.go (default false). This sub-task produces the foundation all others depend on.

title: Wordfeud: replace game dropdown with collapsible game list
description:
Replace the game dropdown selector with an expandable game list, split into sections:

1. **Your Turn** — games where is_my_turn=true, expanded by default
2. **Their Turn** — games where is_my_turn=false, collapsed by default  
3. **Finished Games** — games where is_running=false, collapsed by default

Each game entry shows: opponent name, scores, turn indicator.
Clicking/expanding a game loads it and calculates top moves immediately.

Add a refresh button at the top to re-fetch the games list.

This replaces the dropdown (which was hard to scan) with a more visual layout where you can see all games at a glance.

title: Wordfeud: remaining tiles — show full letter list with values on one line
description: The remaining tiles section shows consonant/vowel/blank counts but doesn't list the actual individual remaining letters with their point values. Add a line showing all remaining letters with values, e.g.: 'A(1) A(1) D(1) E(1) F(4) G(2) ...' sorted alphabetically. This helps the player see exactly which tiles could come next.

title: Wordfeud: document col/row swap in CLAUDE.md — forge keeps reverting it
description:
The Wordfeud API returns tile positions as [row, col] but these need to be stored as board[col][row] for correct rendering. This swap has been reverted twice by forge PRs because there's no documentation explaining WHY it's swapped.

Add a comment in CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md under the Wordfeud section explaining: 'IMPORTANT: Wordfeud API tile coordinates use [col, row] order. When mapping to board[x][y], use board[col][row] not board[row][col]. This has been verified against the official app. Do NOT revert this swap.'

title: Wordfeud: hover top moves to preview placement on board
description:
In the Top Moves list, hovering over a move should highlight the word placement on the 15x15 board:
- Show the tiles that would be placed in their positions (row, col, direction)
- Use a distinct color (e.g. semi-transparent green overlay) to differentiate from existing tiles
- Remove the preview on mouse leave
- If the move data includes position and direction, map each letter to its board cell
- This gives instant visual feedback of where a word goes without clicking

The solver results should include: word, score, start position (row, col), direction (horizontal/vertical), and the individual tile placements.

title: Wordfeud: remaining tiles breakdown — consonants, vowels, blanks, opponent rack deduction
description:
Enhance the remaining tiles section:
1. Show a full list of all remaining letters (not just count) with their point values
2. Below the list show: X consonants, X vowels, X blanks
3. Show percentage chance of getting a vowel if trading entire rack: (remaining_vowels / remaining_total) * 100
4. When bag_count <= 7 (only 7 or fewer tiles left in bag), switch display to show 'Opponent's likely rack' — the remaining tiles minus yours ARE the opponent's rack (since they must have them)
5. Keep the current remaining tile count display as well

title: Wordfeud: remove My Games section + fix unicode in game dropdown
description:
Two cleanup items:
1. Remove 'My Games' section — not needed, the dropdown handles game selection
2. Fix unicode characters in game dropdown labels: shows 'my name (99\u201393)' — the \u2013 is an en-dash that isn't being decoded. Ensure the game summary text is properly UTF-8 decoded before display.

title: Wordfeud API client and backend endpoints
description: Create internal/wordfeud/api.go with a Wordfeud API client that handles login (email+password → session token), fetching active games list, and fetching full game state (board, rack, scores, move history). Reference github.com/hillerstorm/wordfeud-api.js for endpoint URLs and request/response formats. Implement: func Login(email, password string) (sessionToken string, err error), func GetGames(sessionToken string) ([]GameSummary, error), func GetGame(sessionToken string, gameID int) (*GameState, error). Define types: GameSummary{ID, Opponent, Scores, IsMyTurn}, GameState{Board [15][15]Tile, Rack [7]Tile, Scores, MoveHistory}. Add 1-minute in-memory cache (sync.Map or similar) for game state responses keyed by gameID. Register HTTP handlers: GET /api/wordfeud/games and GET /api/wordfeud/games/{id} that use the stored session token from user_preferences. Handle errors gracefully (expired session, API changes, network failures) with meaningful error messages. This is the foundation — the settings UI and frontend game selector depend on these endpoints.

title: Wordfeud credential storage and settings UI
description: Backend: Add encrypted credential storage for Wordfeud email/password in the existing user_preferences system. Add endpoints: POST /api/wordfeud/connect (test credentials via Login, store on success), DELETE /api/wordfeud/disconnect (remove stored credentials), GET /api/wordfeud/status (return connected/disconnected). Restrict all Wordfeud settings endpoints to admin-only access. Frontend: Add a Wordfeud section to the Settings page (admin-only visibility) with email/password inputs, a 'Connect' button that calls /api/wordfeud/connect and shows success/failure, and a 'Disconnect' button. This depends on the API client from sub-task 1 for the Login function.

title: Frontend game selector and board/rack population
description: Add a game selector component to the solver frontend: a dropdown that fetches from GET /api/wordfeud/games, displays active games (opponent name, score, whose turn), and on selection calls GET /api/wordfeud/games/{id} to populate the 15x15 board grid and 7-tile rack automatically. This replaces manual board entry when a Wordfeud account is connected. When no account is connected, hide the selector or show a prompt to configure in Settings. Depends on both previous sub-tasks: API endpoints from sub-task 1 and credential management from sub-task 2.

title: Wordfeud: API login + game fetching
description:
Integrate with the Wordfeud API (community reverse-engineered, see github.com/hillerstorm/wordfeud-api.js for reference) to fetch active games, board state, and player rack.

Backend: internal/wordfeud/api.go
- Login with Wordfeud credentials (email + password or session token)
- Store credentials in user_preferences (encrypted at rest, admin only)
- GET /api/wordfeud/games — list active games with opponent name, score, whose turn
- GET /api/wordfeud/games/{id} — fetch full game state: 15x15 board, player rack (7 tiles), scores, move history
- Cache game state for 1 minute (avoid hammering Wordfeud servers)

Settings: admin-only Wordfeud section in Settings page
- Email/password inputs for Wordfeud login
- 'Connect' button to test credentials
- 'Disconnect' to remove stored credentials

This replaces manual board entry — the solver can read the board and rack directly from an active game. The frontend game selector shows a dropdown of active games, selecting one populates the board and rack automatically.

Dictionary location: data/nsf2025.txt (Norwegian Scrabble Federation word list, ~922K words)

Note: Wordfeud API is reverse-engineered, not official. It may break if Wordfeud changes their backend. Handle errors gracefully.
close_reason: Decomposed into 3 sub-beads: Hytte-3kzq, Hytte-4rsb, Hytte-s68x

title: Infra: GitHub Actions response body too large — paginate or limit workflow runs
description:
The GitHub Actions section shows 'response body too large (>1048576 bytes)' for Robin831/Forge and Robin831/Hytte. The gh API response for workflow runs exceeds 1MB because it fetches all runs without a limit.

Fix: limit the API query to only fetch the most recent N runs per workflow (e.g. --limit 5 or per_page=5). Combined with Hytte-oe6h (filter to most recent run only), this should dramatically reduce the response size.

The gh API call likely needs a '?per_page=5' or equivalent parameter to avoid fetching the entire run history.

title: Infra: tool versions 'unavailable' — Hytte service PATH missing user bins
description:
The Hytte systemd service PATH doesn't include /home/robin/bin or /home/robin/.local/bin where forge, bd, and claude are installed. The versions endpoint runs these commands but they're not found.

Quick fix: add PATH to ~/Hytte/.env. But the proper fix: the versions handler should check common user bin paths as fallback, or resolve the full path using a config/env var rather than relying on PATH alone. This avoids the issue recurring if the service is reinstalled.

title: Forge Dashboard: queue labels shown as raw JSON + missing tooltips
Allowance: team chores should also be completable solo
description:
Team chores (completion_mode='team') currently require min_team_size participants before they can be submitted for approval. But sometimes only one kid is available or willing — they should still be able to complete the chore alone, just without the team bonus.

Fix: allow a single kid to complete a team chore solo. The flow should be:
- Kid taps 'Done' on a team chore → completes it immediately as solo (no team bonus)
- Or kid taps 'Start together' → waits for siblings to join → team completion (with bonus)
- The choice is the kid's: do it alone now, or wait for help and earn more

This matches the 'either' completion mode behavior but for chores explicitly marked as 'team'. The min_team_size should only be required to earn the team bonus, not to complete the chore at all.

title: Forge Dashboard: anvils in queue should be collapsed by default + stop auto-scroll to bottom
description:
Two issues:
1. Anvil groups in the queue are expanded by default — should be collapsed so the queue doesn't take up the entire page
2. The page auto-scrolls to the bottom on load/refresh — likely the event log auto-scroll is pulling the whole page down. The auto-scroll should only apply within the event log container, not the page itself.

title: Forge Dashboard: fullQueue.title shows raw i18n key
description: The full queue section shows 'fullQueue.title' instead of translated text. The i18n key exists in the forge.json locale file but the component wiring is wrong — either wrong namespace or the key path doesn't match.

title: Resizable panels with drag splitter
description: Add drag-to-resize handles between panels, with priority on making Live Activity vertically expandable. Evaluate using a lightweight splitter library (e.g. `react-resizable-panels`) or CSS `resize` property. Implement a vertical drag handle between Active Workers and Live Activity, and between Live Activity and the sections below. Persist panel sizes in localStorage (keyed by splitter ID). This sub-task depends on the layout sub-task being merged first so the correct panel boundaries exist.

title: Dashboard layout restructure: move Live Activity to center column
description: Modify the main dashboard layout component (likely `dashboard/page.tsx` or similar) to reorganize the panel order as specified: Summary bar → Active Workers → Live Activity → Needs Attention/Ready to Merge → Queue → Event Log → Cost charts. Remove Live Activity from the right sidebar. Adjust grid/flex CSS so Live Activity occupies the full center column width. This sub-task establishes the new DOM structure that the collapsible and resizable sub-tasks will build on top of — complete this first.

title: Collapsible panels with localStorage persistence
description: Add collapse/expand toggle to each panel header (Workers, Queue, Live Activity, Events, Needs Attention, PRs). Use shadcn's Collapsible component if available (from Hytte-zim7), otherwise implement with a simple state toggle and CSS height transition. Store each panel's expanded/collapsed state in localStorage keyed by panel ID (e.g. `forge-dashboard-panel-{name}`). Create a `usePanelCollapse(panelId)` hook that returns `[isOpen, toggle]` and syncs with localStorage. Apply to all panels after the layout sub-task is complete.

title: Forge Dashboard: switch between workers to view their live output
description:
When multiple workers are running (max_total_smiths > 1), the Live Activity panel only shows one worker's output. There's no way to switch to view another worker's log.

Add:
- Clickable worker rows in the Active Workers section — clicking a worker selects it and shows its live output in the Live Activity panel
- Visual indicator of which worker is selected (highlighted border or background)
- Default to the most recently started worker
- When a worker completes, auto-switch to the next running worker (if any)
- If no workers are running, show the last completed worker's final output

title: Forge Dashboard: collapsible + resizable panels, move Live Activity to center
description:
The dashboard panels are fixed-size boxes. Make them:

1. **Collapsible**: each section (Workers, Queue, Live Activity, Events, Needs Attention, PRs) should be collapsible with a click on the header. Persist expanded/collapsed state in localStorage.

2. **Resizable**: panels should be resizable by dragging a handle between them (CSS resize or a drag splitter library). At minimum, the Live Activity panel needs to be expandable since the current small right-column box is unreadable.

3. **Layout change**: move Live Activity from the right sidebar to the main center column, between Active Workers and Needs Attention. It's the primary panel when watching the forge work — it needs screen real estate.

Suggested layout (top to bottom):
- Summary bar (cost, beads today, daemon status)
- Active Workers (compact)
- Live Activity (large, center of attention)  
- Needs Attention / Ready to Merge (side by side or stacked)
- Queue (collapsible)
- Event Log (collapsible)
- Cost charts (collapsible)

Consider using shadcn's Collapsible component if available from Hytte-zim7.

title: Forge Dashboard: remove duplicate Recent Events section — merge with Event Log
description: The dashboard has both an 'Event Log' section and a 'Recent Events' section at the bottom showing the same data. Remove one — keep the Event Log (which has filtering/count) and remove the redundant Recent Events.

title: Forge Dashboard: filter poll events from live event log
description:
The live event log shows poll events (e.g. 'Polled anvil: hytte (10 ready)') every 30 seconds which drowns out the interesting events. In Hearth these are filtered out.

Filter poll events from the event log by default. Add a toggle button 'Show polls' to include them if needed for debugging. Filter by event type — exclude 'poll' type events unless the toggle is on.

title: Forge Dashboard: full action panel — merge, bellows, kill, PR actions
description:
Port all Hearth action panel functionality to the web dashboard:

- Merge PR (with confirmation dialog)
- Assign bellows to monitor a PR
- Kill running workers
- Retry stuck beads
- Approve as-is (skip warden)
- View PR details (link to GitHub)
- Refresh/force poll

Each action should be accessible from the relevant card (PR card has merge/bellows, worker card has kill, needs-attention card has retry). All with confirmation dialogs.

title: Forge Dashboard: add full queue view with bead labeling
description:
The dashboard only shows 'Queue Ready' but to replace the Hearth TUI we need the full queue functionality:

- Full queue view: ready, unlabeled, in-progress, needs-attention beads grouped by anvil
- Label beads: add/remove forgeReady and other labels directly from the dashboard (POST to bd label add/remove via IPC or server-side exec)
- This is the key missing piece for managing the forge remotely without SSH

The bead list should show: ID, title, priority, status, labels, dependencies. Each bead should have action buttons for labeling.

title: Backend: SSE event stream and worker log endpoints
description: Create two API endpoints in the Forge web server. (1) GET /api/forge/activity/stream — an SSE endpoint that subscribes to Forge's internal IPC event stream and forwards events to the browser. Use the existing IPC/event bus mechanism (likely in the forge daemon/smith process) to subscribe; format each event as `data: <json>\n\n`. Include a reconnect-friendly `retry:` header. If SSE is not feasible due to transport constraints, fall back to GET /api/forge/events returning a JSON array of recent events for 2-second polling. (2) GET /api/forge/workers/{id}/log — streams the current worker's log file using chunked transfer or SSE; tail the file and push new lines as they arrive. Files to create/modify: the route handler file for the forge web API (likely in `src/forge/web/` or similar), plus any event-bus subscription glue. Key signatures: `streamActivityEvents(req, res)`, `streamWorkerLog(req: {params: {id}}, res)`. This sub-task produces the data contracts (event shape, log line format) that the frontend sub-task depends on.

title: Frontend: LiveActivity dashboard component
description: Build the LiveActivity React/UI component that consumes the backend endpoints from sub-task 1. The component should: (a) open an EventSource to /api/forge/activity/stream (with polling fallback every 2s to /api/forge/events if EventSource fails); (b) display the current worker's phase and bead name at the top; (c) show a real-time auto-scrolling log of worker output by fetching /api/forge/workers/{id}/log; (d) render a color-coded event log below the output panel with auto-scroll pinned to bottom unless the user scrolls up. Files to create: `LiveActivity.tsx` (or `.jsx`/`.svelte` depending on framework) in the dashboard components directory. Modify the main dashboard layout to include this panel (matching Hearth's right-column layout). Key props/types: `WorkerEvent { type, phase, bead, message, timestamp, level }`. Depends on sub-task 1 for the API shape and SSE event format.

title: Forge Dashboard: Ready to Merge card and Today Stats card
description: Create ReadyToMergeCard.tsx: renders PRs from ForgeStatus.readyToMerge that have approval + CI passing. Each row shows PR title/number and a [Merge] button that POSTs to /api/forge/prs/:id/merge. Create TodayStatsCard.tsx: displays ForgeStatus.todayStats — cost (formatted as currency), beads processed, PRs created — as a simple stat grid. Both receive props from the parent ForgeDashboardPage. Depends on the ForgeStatus type from the layout shell sub-task.

title: Forge Dashboard: Recent Events feed and Queue Summary card
description: Create RecentEventsCard.tsx: renders the last 20 events from ForgeStatus.recentEvents in a scrollable list that auto-scrolls to the latest entry (useEffect + ref.scrollIntoView). Color-code event rows by type (e.g. error=red, success=green, info=blue) using a type→className map. Create QueueSummaryCard.tsx: shows ready beads per anvil from ForgeStatus.queue in a collapsible section per anvil (controlled open/close state). Both components are purely display, receiving props from ForgeDashboardPage. Depends on ForgeStatus type from the layout shell sub-task.

title: Forge Dashboard: Workers card and Needs Attention card
description: Create WorkersCard.tsx: renders a card listing active smiths from ForgeStatus.workers — columns: bead ID, phase, duration (formatted), provider. Create NeedsAttentionCard.tsx: renders stuck beads from ForgeStatus.stuck — shows error details, and a [Retry] button that POSTs to /api/forge/beads/:id/retry. Both components receive their slice of ForgeStatus as props (no internal fetching). Use the shared ForgeStatus type from the layout shell sub-task. Mobile-first: full-width cards, large touch targets (min 44px hit areas).

title: Forge Dashboard: routing, layout shell, and sidebar nav
description: Create the top-level ForgeDashboardPage.tsx at the /forge route. Set up the page layout shell with mobile-first stacked card grid. Add sidebar nav entry with the Hammer Lucide icon (admin-only guard). Wire up the polling hook: useForgeStatus() that calls GET /api/forge/status every 5 seconds using setInterval + fetch (or React Query). Export the raw status shape as ForgeStatus TypeScript type shared by all child card components. Modify the app router (e.g. App.tsx or routes file) to register the /forge path behind an admin role check. This sub-task produces the skeleton that sibling sub-tasks fill with card components.

title: HTTP handlers + feature-flag wiring
description: Create internal/forge/handlers.go (or web/forge_handlers.go depending on project conventions). Implement admin-only handlers: GET /api/forge/status (daemon health via IPC Client.Health() + summary stats from DB), GET /api/forge/workers, GET /api/forge/queue, GET /api/forge/prs, GET /api/forge/events?limit&type&anvil, GET /api/forge/costs?period. Register routes behind a forge_dashboard feature flag (default false) checked at startup. Depends on both the DB layer and IPC client sub-tasks.

title: internal/forge: Unix IPC socket client
description: Create internal/forge/ipc.go. Implement a Client struct that dials ~/.forge/forge.sock (or FORGE_IPC_SOCKET env var) as a net.UnixConn. Provide SendCommand(cmd string) ([]byte, error) for fire-and-response IPC. Include a Health() helper used by the /api/forge/status handler to detect daemon liveness. This is a standalone transport layer with no dependency on the DB sub-task.

title: internal/forge: SQLite state.db read layer
description: Create internal/forge/db.go in the Hytte repo. Define a DB struct wrapping a *sql.DB opened in read-only WAL mode (file:~/.forge/state.db?mode=ro&_journal_mode=WAL). Implement query helpers as methods: Workers() (active/recent), PRs() (open, merge-ready), Events(limit int, eventType, anvil string), Retries() (needs_human rows), Costs(period string) aggregating bead_costs/daily_costs, QueueCache() (ready beads per anvil). Add config via FORGE_STATE_DB env var with fallback to ~/.forge/state.db. This package is consumed by the handler sub-task.

title: Forge Dashboard: live activity panel + event stream
description:
Add a live activity panel showing Smith's current output as beads are processed — the key feature from Hearth's right column.

Backend: GET /api/forge/activity/stream (SSE endpoint) that subscribes to Forge's IPC event stream and forwards events to the browser in real-time. Fallback: poll /api/forge/events every 2 seconds if SSE isn't feasible.

Also: GET /api/forge/workers/{id}/log — stream the current worker's log file for live output viewing.

Frontend: LiveActivity component on the dashboard — auto-scrolling log of what Smith is doing right now. Show the current worker's phase, bead, and real-time output. Event log below with color-coded entries and auto-scroll.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/forge-web-dashboard.md (Phase 2)

title: Forge Dashboard: action buttons — merge, retry, kill, refresh
description:
Add IPC action endpoints to Hytte backend:
- POST /api/forge/action/merge — merge a ready PR (sends merge command via IPC)
- POST /api/forge/action/retry — retry a stuck bead (sends queue retry via IPC)
- POST /api/forge/action/kill — kill a running worker
- POST /api/forge/action/refresh — trigger immediate poll

Frontend: action buttons on the overview page cards. All actions require confirmation dialog (shadcn Dialog). Show success/error toast after action completes.

Also add 'Rebuild & Restart Forge' button that runs ~/.forge/restart.sh via a server-side exec endpoint (admin-only, confirmation dialog). For deploying Forge updates from the phone.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/forge-web-dashboard.md (Phase 2)

title: Forge Dashboard: overview page — workers, PRs, queue, events
description:
Create ForgeDashboardPage.tsx at /forge. The main overview — everything important on one screen:

- Workers card: active smiths with bead ID, phase, duration, provider
- Ready to Merge card: PRs with approval + CI passing, [Merge] button
- Needs Attention card: stuck beads with error details, [Retry] button
- Today stats: cost, beads processed, PRs created
- Recent Events: last 20 events, auto-scrolling, color-coded by type
- Queue summary: ready beads per anvil (collapsible)

Mobile-first layout — stacked cards, big touch targets. Sidebar nav with Hammer Lucide icon (admin only). i18n: English only for v1 is fine (admin developer tool). Poll /api/forge/status every 5 seconds for refresh.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/forge-web-dashboard.md (Phase 1)

title: Forge Dashboard: read state.db + IPC socket backend
description:
Create internal/forge/ package in Hytte. Open Forge's ~/.forge/state.db in read-only mode (SQLite WAL supports concurrent readers). Query helpers for: workers (active/recent), prs (open, merge-ready), events (timestamped log), retries (needs_human), bead_costs/daily_costs, queue_cache. Connect to Forge's Unix socket (~/.forge/forge.sock) for sending IPC commands.

Handlers (all admin-only):
- GET /api/forge/status — daemon health + summary stats
- GET /api/forge/workers — active + recent workers
- GET /api/forge/queue — ready beads per anvil
- GET /api/forge/prs — open PRs with status
- GET /api/forge/events?limit=50&type=&anvil= — event log
- GET /api/forge/costs?period=today|week|month — cost data

Config: FORGE_STATE_DB and FORGE_IPC_SOCKET env vars (or auto-discover from ~/.forge/).
Feature flag: forge_dashboard (default false).

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/forge-web-dashboard.md (Phase 1)

title: Kiosk: show QR code after token creation for easy tablet setup
description:
After creating a kiosk token in Settings, display a QR code containing the full kiosk URL (https://robinedvardsmith.com/kiosk?token=...) so the user can scan it with the tablet's camera instead of typing a 30-40 character token manually.

Show the QR code on the Settings page right after token creation — it's a one-time setup, so it only needs to appear at creation time (with a "Show QR again" button on the token list for later).

Use a lightweight QR library like `qrcode.react` or generate server-side as SVG/PNG.

Source: https://github.com/Robin831/Hytte/issues/350

title: KioskPage: full-screen tablet display
description: Create src/pages/KioskPage.tsx as a standalone route at /kiosk with no Sidebar/nav wrapper. Reads ?token= from URL query params and polls /kiosk/data every 30 seconds. Layout (portrait, dark theme, large fonts) with four sections: (1) Clock+Date — large 24h clock and Norwegian date string via setInterval every second; (2) Bus Departures — route badge, destination, minutes-until, next 5-6 departures per stop, fade CSS transition on data refresh; (3) Weather strip — Netatmo outdoor temp/humidity/wind + 6-hour forecast icons; (4) Sunrise/Sunset one-liner at bottom. Create src/components/kiosk/ subdirectory with components: KioskClock.tsx, KioskBusDepartures.tsx, KioskWeather.tsx, KioskSunrise.tsx. Add route entry in the router config. Develop against a mock /kiosk/data JSON fixture until sub-task 1 (backend) provides real data. Connects to the token management sub-task only via the token query param that gets passed to the API.

title: Settings: Kiosk Token management UI
description: Add a 'Kiosk Tokens' section to the existing Settings page. Modify src/pages/Settings.tsx (or equivalent). Display a list of existing tokens showing name, expiry date, and last-used timestamp. Implement a 'Create Token' flow: name text input, stop selector reusing the existing transit stop search component, expiry date picker. On successful creation show a one-time token display with copy-to-clipboard button (token must not be re-fetchable). Add a revoke button per token with confirmation. Wire up to REST endpoints: GET /kiosk/tokens, POST /kiosk/tokens, DELETE /kiosk/tokens/:id. Create src/components/kiosk/TokenManager.tsx and src/components/kiosk/TokenCreateDialog.tsx. This sub-task is independent of KioskPage except that the tokens created here are consumed as ?token= query params on the kiosk route.

title: Reward icon input box
description:
The icon emoji input box should show a dropdown with some icons to choose from, similar to our other icon input selectors

Source: https://github.com/Robin831/Hytte/issues/343

title: Admin token management endpoints (POST/GET/DELETE /api/kiosk/tokens)
description: Add admin endpoints in internal/kiosk/handlers.go (or a separate admin_handlers.go): POST /api/kiosk/tokens — generates a crypto/rand token, stores its hash + metadata, returns plaintext token once in response. GET /api/kiosk/tokens — lists all tokens with metadata (id, name, config, created_by, created_at, expires_at, last_used_at), never returns hash. DELETE /api/kiosk/tokens/{id} — revokes by deleting or soft-deleting the record. All three routes protected by existing RequireAuth middleware. Wire routes in router. Depends on migration sub-task.

title: Kiosk data endpoint (internal/kiosk/handlers.go — GET /kiosk/data)
description: Implement GET /kiosk/data handler in internal/kiosk/handlers.go. Reads kiosk config from context (stop IDs, etc.) injected by KioskAuth. Fans out to: transit departures (stop IDs from config), Netatmo outdoor readings, weather forecast, and sun times. Aggregates results into a single JSON response struct. Wire route under /kiosk/data protected by KioskAuth middleware. Depends on KioskAuth sub-task for context injection. Integration-test with curl using a valid token.

title: KioskAuth middleware (internal/kiosk/auth.go)
description: Implement KioskAuth(db) HTTP middleware in internal/kiosk/auth.go. It reads the ?token= query param, hashes it (SHA-256 or bcrypt — pick consistently), queries kiosk_tokens by token_hash, checks expires_at, updates last_used_at, and injects the parsed config JSON into the request context. Define a context key type and a helper GetKioskConfig(ctx) accessor. Depends on the migration sub-task for the table schema. Used by the /kiosk/* routes sub-task.

title: DB migration: kiosk_tokens table
description: Create a SQL migration file (e.g. migrations/NNNN_create_kiosk_tokens.sql or Go migrate file) defining the kiosk_tokens table with columns: id (UUID/serial PK), token_hash (text, unique, not null), name (text), config (jsonb/text), created_by (text), created_at (timestamptz), expires_at (timestamptz nullable), last_used_at (timestamptz nullable). No application code changes — just the migration. Sibling tasks depend on this table existing.

title: Kiosk frontend: full-screen tablet page and admin token management UI
description: Create KioskPage.tsx as a standalone route (/kiosk) that reads ?token= from the URL and fetches /kiosk/data every 30 seconds. Layout (portrait, dark theme, large fonts): (1) Clock+Date at top — large 24h clock, Norwegian date string updated every second; (2) Bus Departures — route badge, destination, minutes-until, next 5-6 per stop, fade transition on refresh; (3) Weather strip — Netatmo outdoor temp/humidity/wind + 6-hour forecast icons; (4) Sunrise/Sunset one-liner at bottom. No Sidebar, no nav wrapper — full viewport. Add a 'Kiosk Tokens' section in the Settings page: list existing tokens (name, expiry, last used), 'Create Token' flow (name input, stop selector reusing existing transit stop search component, expiry picker), one-time token display with copy-to-clipboard, revoke button. Depends on sub-task 1 for real data; can be developed against a mock /kiosk/data fixture. Produces: src/pages/KioskPage.tsx, src/components/kiosk/*.tsx, Settings page additions, route entry.

title: Kiosk backend: token auth, DB schema, and API routes
description: Create the kiosk_tokens table migration (id, token_hash, name, config JSON, created_by, created_at, expires_at, last_used_at). Implement KioskAuth(db) middleware that reads ?token= query param, hashes it, looks up the record, checks expiry, updates last_used_at, and injects kiosk config into context. Add routes under /kiosk/*: GET /kiosk/data aggregates transit departures (using stop IDs from token config), Netatmo outdoor readings, weather forecast, and sun times into a single JSON response. Add admin endpoints: POST /api/kiosk/tokens (generate crypto-random token, store hash, return plaintext once), GET /api/kiosk/tokens (list with metadata, never return hash), DELETE /api/kiosk/tokens/{id} (revoke). These routes use RequireAuth for admin endpoints and KioskAuth for /kiosk/* routes. Produces: migration file, internal/kiosk/auth.go, internal/kiosk/handlers.go, router wiring. No frontend dependency — test with curl.

title: Settings: collapsible sections — default collapsed
description:
The Settings page has grown to many sections (Profile, Appearance, Language, Location, Training preferences, Push notifications, Quiet hours, Netatmo, AI prompts, Kiosk, Sessions, Danger zone). Users have to scroll far to reach bottom sections.

Make each section collapsible (accordion-style) with a click-to-expand header. Default state: all collapsed. Persist expanded/collapsed state in localStorage so it remembers which sections the user had open. Consider using the shadcn Collapsible or Accordion component if available from Hytte-zim7.

title: Scroll-to-adjust minutes on time input
description: Add a mouse wheel event handler to the time input component used on the Work Hours page. When the input is focused and the dropdown is closed, intercept wheel events: scroll up = +1 min, scroll down = -1 min, Shift+scroll = +/-15 min (or 5 min). Clamp result to 00:00–23:59. Files to modify: the time input/picker component (add onWheel handler, track focus state to gate the behaviour, prevent default scroll when active). No dependency on the persistence sub-task; can be worked in parallel.

title: Persist punch-in state via open work sessions
description: Modify the Work Hours page to persist punch-in state. On punch-in: POST to backend creating a work_session record with start_time set and end_time null. On page load: GET open sessions (end_time IS NULL) and restore punch-in UI state with the saved start_time. On punch-out: PATCH the session to set end_time. Files to modify: the WorkHours page/component (move state out of React useState into server-synced state), the API client (add createSession, getOpenSession, closeSession calls), and any backend route/controller handling work_sessions if it doesn't already support open sessions. No dependency on the scroll sub-task.

title: Work Hours: punch-in not persisted + time input scroll to adjust minutes
description:
Two issues on the Work Hours page:

## 1. Punch-in state not persisted
Clicking 'Punch In' updates the time display, but navigating away and back loses the state — the punch-in is gone. The punch-in start time needs to be saved to the backend (or at least localStorage) immediately when clicked, so it survives navigation. Currently it's only in React state.

When the user punches in:
- Save the start time to the backend (create a work_session with start_time set, end_time null)
- On page load: check for an open session (end_time IS NULL) and restore the punch-in state
- When they punch out: update the session with the end_time

## 2. Scroll-to-adjust minutes on time input
After selecting a time from the dropdown (e.g. 08:30), the user should be able to hover/focus the input and scroll the mouse wheel to increment/decrement minutes. This allows fine-tuning without re-opening the dropdown or typing.

- Scroll up = +1 minute, scroll down = -1 minute
- Hold Shift + scroll = +/- 5 minutes (or 15 minutes)
- Only active when the input is focused/selected (not when the dropdown is open)
- Respect min 00:00 and max 23:59 boundaries
- Works on both hour and minute segments ideally, but minute-only is fine for v1

title: Kiosk mode: dedicated entrance tablet page with token auth
description:
A full-screen, always-on display page for a wall-mounted tablet in the entrance. No sidebar, no nav, no login — just glanceable info for people heading out the door.

## Auth: Kiosk Tokens

Create a kiosk token system (NOT a regular user session):
- Admin generates a long-lived token in Settings → Kiosk section
- Token stored in kiosk_tokens table: id, token (hashed), name ('Entrance tablet'), created_at, expires_at (default 90 days), config (JSON — which stops to show, etc.)
- Access via: https://robinedvardsmith.com/kiosk?token=abc123
- Token grants read-only access to: transit departures, netatmo weather, weather forecast, sun times
- No personal data, no admin access, no sidebar/nav
- Admin can revoke tokens from Settings
- Multiple tokens supported (different tablets, different configs)

## Kiosk Page Layout (Vertical tablet, top to bottom)

### 1. Clock + Date (top)
Large 24h clock (14:35), current day and date in Norwegian ('Søndag 30. mars 2026'). Big enough to read from 2+ meters.

### 2. Bus Departures (main section, most screen space)
Next 5-6 departures per configured stop. Large text. Route number badge, destination, minutes until departure. Auto-refresh every 30 seconds. Stops configured per-token when creating the kiosk token (different from the user's personal transit stops).

### 3. Weather (compact)
Netatmo outdoor readings: temperature, humidity, wind speed/direction (from the admin's connected Netatmo station — no per-kiosk OAuth needed). Plus a compact 6-hour forecast strip with weather icons and temps. Indoor temp less relevant here (you're leaving).

### 4. Sunrise/Sunset (bottom, one-liner)
Compact: '☀️ 06:42 → 🌙 19:58 · 13h 16min' with day length. Default location from admin's settings.

## Design

- **Dark theme** — matches the app, good for always-on display
- **Large fonts** — readable from 2 meters, designed for glancing not reading
- **No interaction** — everything auto-refreshes, no tapping needed
- **Subtle animations** — gentle transitions on data updates (fade in new departure times, smooth clock tick) for visual polish and burn-in prevention
- **NO auto-rotation** between views — show everything stacked, always visible
- **Full viewport** — no browser chrome visible (tablet runs in kiosk/full-screen browser mode)
- **Vertical orientation** — optimized for portrait tablet

## Backend

- New table: kiosk_tokens (id, token_hash, name, config JSON, created_by, created_at, expires_at, last_used_at)
- Middleware: KioskAuth(db) — validates token from query param, loads config, injects into context
- Routes under /kiosk/* — no RequireAuth, uses KioskAuth instead
- GET /kiosk/data — returns all kiosk data in one response (transit + netatmo + weather + sun times) to minimize requests from the always-on tablet
- Admin endpoints: POST /api/kiosk/tokens (create), GET /api/kiosk/tokens (list), DELETE /api/kiosk/tokens/{id} (revoke)

## Frontend

- KioskPage.tsx — standalone page, no Sidebar/nav wrapper
- Fetches /kiosk/data every 30 seconds
- Settings section: 'Kiosk Tokens' with create/revoke UI, stop selector per token

## Token Creation Flow

1. Admin goes to Settings → Kiosk
2. Clicks 'Create Token'
3. Names it ('Entrance tablet')
4. Configures bus stops (search + select, like the transit settings)
5. Sets expiry (30/60/90 days or never)
6. Token displayed once (copy to clipboard) — then only the hash is stored
7. Admin opens the URL on the tablet browser and bookmarks it

Implementation plan: consider creating .forge/plans/hytte-kiosk.md

title: Dashboard: clock should use 24-hour format
description:
The greeting widget clock shows 12-hour format (02:35:28 PM) instead of 24-hour (14:35:28). The formatTime utility in utils/formatDate.ts uses i18n.language as the locale, but when the locale is 'en' it defaults to 12-hour. Since this is a Norwegian app, force 24-hour everywhere.

Simple fix: add hour12: false to the formatTime call in GreetingWidget.tsx line 52-56. Or better: set hour12: false as a default in the formatTime utility itself so all time displays across the app are consistent (Transit page, Work Hours, etc.).

title: Settings: AI prompt save fails with 500 when saving multiple prompts simultaneously
description:
The frontend fires all 4 PUT /api/settings/ai-prompts/{key} requests in parallel. SQLite can only handle one concurrent writer, so 2 of 4 requests typically fail with 500 (database is locked). The success/failure pattern is random — different keys fail each time.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Frontend: save prompts sequentially (await each PUT before firing the next)
2. Backend: batch all prompts into a single PUT /api/settings/ai-prompts endpoint that updates all keys in one transaction
3. Backend: add retry-on-busy logic to the SQLite write (retry after short delay on SQLITE_BUSY)

Option 1 is simplest. Option 2 is cleanest. The frontend currently does Promise.all() on all 4 PUTs — change to sequential awaits or a single batch endpoint.

title: Work Hours: icon dropdown for deduction presets (like Allowance chore icons)
description:
The deduction preset settings in Work Hours have a plain text input for the icon field. Replace with an emoji picker dropdown showing relevant deduction icons. Similar to what we're building for the Allowance chore setup (Hytte-l1n9) but with deduction-relevant emojis:

Transport: 🚗🚌🚲🚶
Childcare: 👶🏫🎒
Medical: 🏥💊🦷
Errands: 🛒📬🏦
Meetings: ☕📞💼
General: ⏰🔧📋

Show as a grid of tappable emoji buttons with an option to type a custom one.

title: Work Hours: add date picker for day navigation
description: The Work Hours page currently only has back/forward arrows to navigate between days, requiring many clicks to reach a specific date. Add a date picker (calendar popup) that lets the user jump directly to any date. Click the date text to open the picker. Keep the arrows for quick prev/next day navigation.

title: Transit: show more departures for filtered routes
description:
When a stop has many bus lines but the user filters to specific routes (e.g. bus 3 and 3E), the API returns 10 departures total across ALL lines, then the frontend filters to the selected routes. This means you might only see 1-2 departures for your bus because the other 8 slots were taken by other lines.

Fix: Either increase numberOfDepartures when route filters are active (e.g. fetch 30 and filter client-side), or pass the route filter to the query somehow. The user needs to see the next 5+ departures for their specific routes to plan ahead — 'the next bus 3 is in 4 min, then 19 min, then 34 min' is much more useful than just seeing the next one.

title: Training AI: Settings prompt fields show hardcoded system prompt as default — should be empty
description:
The AI prompt fields in Settings (single workout analysis, comparison, training load) are pre-filled with the hardcoded system prompt text (e.g. 'Classify this {sport} workout...'). This is misleading — the user thinks they're editing the full prompt, but these fields should be for ADDITIONAL personal context that gets appended to the auto-generated prompt.

Fix:
1. Default value should be empty string, not the system prompt
2. Add a label/helper text: 'Additional context appended to the analysis prompt. Leave empty to use defaults.'
3. Show a collapsible 'View default prompt' section so the user can see what the AI already receives
4. The backend should append this text to the auto-generated prompt, not replace it

Currently the user might overwrite the system prompt with personal context, losing the JSON format instructions and workout data injection.

title: Training compare: HR overlay still shows raw i18n key + identical legend labels
description:
Two remaining issues after Hytte-ilnh:

1. Raw i18n key visible: 'compare.hrOverlay.workoutA' and 'compare.hrOverlay.workoutB' are rendered as text somewhere in the HR overlay section. The key doesn't exist in training.json — the hrOverlay section only has title/minutes/ariaLabel. Either add the missing keys or fix the code to use compare.workoutA/workoutB which do exist.

2. Legend still doesn't distinguish same-titled workouts: The Line name prop (TrainingCompare.tsx:784-785) uses comparison.workout_a.title raw. When both workouts share the same title (e.g. 'Threshold Intervals'), the legend is useless. Prefix with 'A:' / 'B:' or append date + tags. E.g. 'A: Threshold Intervals (Mar 12, 6x6min)' in blue, 'B: Threshold Intervals (Mar 28, 6x6min)' in orange.

title: Training compare: missing i18n keys for HR overlay labels
description: The workout comparison HR overlay chart shows raw i18n keys instead of translated text: 'compare.hrOverlay.workoutA' and 'compare.hrOverlay.workoutB'. These keys are missing from the training.json locale files (en/nb/th). Add the translations — something like 'Workout A' / 'Trening A' and 'Workout B' / 'Trening B', or simply 'A' and 'B' if used as legend labels alongside the workout title.
close_reason: The `compare.hrOverlay.workoutA` and `compare.hrOverlay.workoutB` keys already exist in all three locale files (en/nb/th) with value "A" and "B" respectively. The fix was already applied (likely in the same commit that added the HR overlay chart per the recent commit `38e1367`).

title: Training compare: add legend to HR overlay chart
description:
The heart rate overlay chart in workout comparison shows two colored lines but the legend is inadequate — when both workouts have the same title (e.g. 'Threshold Intervals'), the legend shows identical labels. The name prop on each Line component (TrainingCompare.tsx:784-785) uses comparison.workout_a.title raw, without date or 'A'/'B' prefix.

Fix: prefix each legend entry with 'A:' / 'B:' or append the date. E.g. 'A: Threshold Intervals (Mar 12)' in blue, 'B: Threshold Intervals (Mar 28)' in orange. Also add the workout tags to the legend entries for extra differentiation.

Also check if there are any raw i18n keys showing — the user reported seeing 'compare.hrOverlay.workoutA' rendered as text, which suggests a label somewhere is using a key that doesn't exist in the hrOverlay section of training.json. The hrOverlay section has title/minutes/ariaLabel but NOT workoutA/workoutB.

title: Training compare: show tags in workout dropdown selector
description: The workout comparison dropdown shows title and date for each workout, but when you have multiple workouts of the same type (e.g. several 'Threshold Intervals' sessions) it's hard to tell them apart. Add the auto-generated tags to the dropdown entries — e.g. 'Threshold Intervals — 10x1km — Mar 25' or show tags as small badges next to the title. Tags are already available from the workout data (auto-tags like '10x1km', '6x6min', sport type, etc.).

description:
Add a dropdown/select at the top of the deductions section in WorkHoursPage that lists saved presets (e.g. 'Kindergarten drop-off — 15 min', 'Doctor appointment — 30 min'). Selecting a preset pre-fills the Description and Minutes input fields but does NOT auto-submit — the user can adjust the values before adding (e.g. kindergarten took 20 min instead of the usual 15 today).

Presets are managed via the existing work_deduction_presets table (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/workhours/presets). The dropdown should:
- Show preset icon + name + default minutes
- On select: fill description with preset name, fill minutes with default_minutes
- User can edit both fields before clicking Add
- A small 'Manage presets' link opens the preset settings (create/edit/delete)

This is a convenience feature — fast entry for the 2-3 deductions used almost daily.

Handle OAuth callback route, exchange code for token, persist via task 1's token storage. Show connected/disconnected status for admin. Only admin can connect; token is shared for all authenticated users. Depends on task 1.

Add feature flag check middleware that returns 404 when flag is off. Depends on tasks 2 and 3.

title: Wordfeud: word finder frontend page
description:
Create WordfeudPage.tsx at /wordfeud. Phase 1 UI: letter input (enter your 7 tiles including blanks), word suggestions list ranked by base points. Each result shows: word, length, points. Search modes: anagram (find words from letters), contains (words containing pattern), starts with, ends with. Sidebar nav item with Gamepad2 Lucide icon. i18n namespace wordfeud.json for all 3 locales. Norwegian only. Mobile-friendly for this phase.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-wordfeud-solver.md (Phase 1)
notes:
## API integration available

The Wordfeud API client is working (Hytte-2suz). The frontend should:
- Show a game selector dropdown (from POST /api/wordfeud/games)
- Selecting a game populates the rack with the player's tiles
- The word finder uses the rack tiles as available letters

title: Wordfeud: dictionary trie + word finder backend
description:
Create internal/wordfeud/ package. Load nsf2025.txt Norwegian dictionary (922K words, copy from C:\Users\robs\source\repos\WordfeudHelper\Data\nsf2025.txt to data/nsf2025.txt). Build trie data structure on startup for O(log n) prefix lookups. Norwegian letter values (A=1, E=1, Æ=8, Z=10 etc. matching Wordfeud scoring). Word finder: given available letters (including blanks as *), find all valid words ranked by base points. Handlers: POST /api/wordfeud/find (letters + optional pattern → word suggestions), POST /api/wordfeud/validate (check if word exists), GET /api/wordfeud/tiles (Norwegian tile distribution — 104 tiles total). Feature flag: wordfeud (default false).

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-wordfeud-solver.md (Phase 1)
notes:
## Working Wordfeud API details (verified)

Base URL: https://game06.wordfeud.com/wf
All endpoints use POST (not GET)
User-Agent must be: Wordfeud/4.0.0 (Android; 14; Pixel 8)
Session: comes from Set-Cookie header (sessionid=...), not JSON body
Session expires after 2 days (Max-Age=172800)

Endpoints:
- POST /user/login/email/ → {email, password (SHA1 of password+JarJarBinks9)}
- POST /user/games/ → list active games (Cookie: sessionid=...)
- POST /game/{id}/ → full game state (board, rack, scores, moves)
- POST /board/{id}/ → board multiplier layout
- POST /tile_points/{rulesetId}/ → letter point values

Login response includes: username, email, id, avatar info
Games response includes: game ID, players with scores, is_my_turn flag
Game response includes: 15x15 board tiles, player rack, move history

The API client (internal/wordfeud/api.go) handles login, redirect following, and session management. Credentials stored encrypted in user_preferences, auto-refreshes when session expires.
## Dictionary location on Hetzner

The NSF 2025 dictionary is already in place at: /home/robin/source/Hytte/data/nsf2025.txt (11MB, 922K words). The data/ directory is gitignored. Load from this path on startup.

title: Netatmo: dashboard widget with live readings

Dashboard widget card showing current Netatmo readings. Indoor: temperature, humidity, CO2 (color-coded: green <1000, yellow 1000-1500, red >1500). Outdoor: temperature (blue if <5, green normal, orange >25), humidity. Wind: speed and gust if module available. 'Updated X min ago' timestamp. Gracefully skip offline modules. 24h temperature trend sparkline/mini chart using Recharts. Widget expandable to fuller historical view. i18n keys in all 3 locales.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-netatmo-widget.md (Phase 1-2)

title: Netatmo: OAuth2 client + API integration
description:
Create internal/netatmo/ package. Netatmo OAuth2 setup (read_station scope). Token storage encrypted at rest — reuse pattern from google_tokens or a generic oauth_tokens table. Client: GetStationsData() call to /api/getstationsdata. Parse response for indoor module (temp, humidity, CO2, noise, pressure), outdoor (temp, humidity), wind module (speed, gust, direction). Cache readings for 5 minutes. Handlers: GET /api/netatmo/current (all module readings), GET /api/netatmo/history?hours=24 (historical readings from Netatmo API). Store historical data in SQLite, retain 7 days max. Feature flag: netatmo (default false). Settings UI: 'Connect Netatmo' OAuth redirect button. One household connection (admin's token), served to all authenticated users.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-netatmo-widget.md (Phase 1)

title: UI: set up shadcn/ui + replace hand-rolled components
description:
Set up shadcn/ui foundation and replace inconsistent hand-rolled UI patterns across all pages.

## Setup (one-time)
- Install dependencies: class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge, @radix-ui/react-* (as needed)
- Add cn() utility (lib/utils.ts)
- Configure shadcn CSS variables mapped to Hytte's existing dark theme (gray-900/950 palette)
- Add components directory at web/src/components/ui/

## Components to add (via shadcn CLI or manual copy)
1. **Skeleton** — pulsing placeholder. Replace all 'Loading...' text across ~15+ pages (Admin, AllowancePage, Chat, Family, FamilyChildDetail, FamilyRewards, Stars, Training, Settings, Transit, etc.)
2. **Tabs** — consistent tab bar. Refactor AllowancePage (5 tabs), LactateInsights, and any future tabbed pages
3. **Dialog** — styled modal. Replace window.confirm() in Notes.tsx and the hand-rolled LactateImportDialog. Use for all confirmation/destructive actions (delete chore, remove child, etc.)
4. **Select** — dropdown component. Used by the deduction preset dropdown (Hytte-2q7w) and anywhere else we have native HTML selects
5. **Popover + TimePicker pattern** — for the Work Hours page time inputs. Smart time input: dropdown with 15-min increments, keyboard up/down to adjust, auto-format typed input (0600→06:00). Mobile-friendly.

## Migration approach
- Add components incrementally — don't rewrite all pages at once
- Replace the most visible/ugly patterns first (Loading... text → Skeleton, raw confirm → Dialog)
- Leave pages that work fine as-is until they need changes for other reasons
- Update i18n: add shared keys to common.json for all 3 locales (confirm.cancel, confirm.confirm, confirm.delete, loading placeholder alt text)
notes:
## Additional: TimePicker component

The Work Hours page uses plain HH:MM text inputs for session start/end times. Replace with a proper time picker component — either a dropdown with 15-min increments, a scroll-wheel picker on mobile, or a smart input that auto-formats as you type (eg typing '0600' becomes '06:00'). Should support keyboard navigation (up/down to increment by 15 min). Add to the shared components alongside Skeleton, Tabs, and ConfirmDialog.
## Additional: DatePicker + Popover for icon pickers

6. **DatePicker** — Calendar popup for date selection. Used by Work Hours day navigation (Hytte-02j3) and potentially Calendar page. shadcn's DatePicker is built on Popover + a calendar grid. Needs date-fns or similar for date math.

7. **Popover** — Generic popover container. Powers both the DatePicker and the emoji icon pickers used in:
   - Allowance chore icon selector (Hytte-l1n9)
   - Work Hours deduction preset icon selector (Hytte-yz6k)
   - Both show a grid of emoji buttons inside a Popover triggered by the current icon/input field
   - Same Popover component, different content grids
## IMPORTANT: Update CLAUDE.md after setup

After adding shadcn components, update CLAUDE.md Frontend section with:
- shadcn/ui is available — prefer shadcn components (Skeleton, Tabs, Dialog, Select, Popover, DatePicker) over hand-rolling UI
- Components live in web/src/components/ui/
- Use cn() utility from lib/utils.ts for conditional classNames
- When building new pages, check if a shadcn component exists before creating a custom one
- Dark theme CSS variables are configured to match Hytte's gray-900/950 palette

Implement push notifications to siblings when a team chore is started and when the team is complete (using existing notification infrastructure). Either-mode chores show both 'Do alone' and 'Do together' options. Depends on sub-tasks 1 and 2 for data shape and endpoints.

description: Extend the chore creation/edit form component (likely ChoreForm.tsx or similar) with a 'Completion mode' dropdown (Solo/Team/Either). Conditionally show min_team_size and team_bonus_pct inputs when Team is selected. Display 🤝 icon on team chores in the parent chore list. Wire new fields to the existing chore create/update API calls. Depends on sub-task 1 (new schema fields) and sub-task 2 (API accepts the new fields).

title: Backend API: team-start, team-join endpoints & calculator update
description: Implement POST /api/allowance/my/team-start/{chore_id} (creates a completion in 'waiting_for_team' status, inserts initiator row into allowance_team_completions) and POST /api/allowance/my/team-join/{completion_id} (inserts joining child into allowance_team_completions, promotes status to 'pending' when min_team_size reached). Update GET /api/allowance/my/chores to include completion_mode, active team sessions, and sibling participation status. Update the approval/calculator logic to apply team_bonus_pct for completions that have team_completions rows, crediting all team members. Depends on sub-task 1.

title: DB schema & migration: team completion tables
description: Add columns to allowance_chores (completion_mode TEXT DEFAULT 'solo', min_team_size INTEGER DEFAULT 2, team_bonus_pct REAL DEFAULT 10.0) and create allowance_team_completions table as specified. Add a new migration file (e.g. migrations/XXXX_team_chores.sql). Update any ORM/schema definition files that mirror the DB structure. Also extend the allowance_completions status enum/check constraint to include 'waiting_for_team'. This sub-task is a prerequisite for all others.

title: Allowance: shared chores — teamwork mode for siblings
description:
Support chores that multiple kids complete together, with a teamwork bonus to encourage collaboration.

## Problem
Some chores are done by 2+ kids together (e.g. dishwasher, tidying the living room). Currently each chore can only be completed by one child at a time. We need a way for siblings to share a task and both get credit.

## Data Model Changes

Add to allowance_chores:
- `completion_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'solo'` — 'solo' (one child completes), 'team' (multiple kids complete together), 'either' (any one child can claim it)
- `min_team_size INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2` — minimum kids needed for team mode
- `team_bonus_pct REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 10.0` — percentage bonus when done as a team (e.g. 10% = if chore is worth 20kr, each kid gets 22kr when teaming up)

Add a new table for tracking team completions:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS allowance_team_completions (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    completion_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES allowance_completions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    child_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    joined_at       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    UNIQUE(completion_id, child_id)
);
```

## How It Works

### Solo mode (default, existing behavior)
One child completes the chore, gets the full amount. No change.

### Team mode
1. First kid taps 'Start together' on the chore → creates a completion in 'waiting_for_team' status
2. Other siblings see a notification/badge: '🤝 Oliver started Dishwasher — join in!'
3. Each kid who joins taps 'Join team' → added to allowance_team_completions
4. When min_team_size is reached, status changes to 'pending' (awaiting parent approval as normal)
5. Parent approves once → ALL team members get credited: base amount + team_bonus_pct each
6. If min_team_size isn't reached by end of day, the initiator can still complete it solo (no team bonus)

### Either mode
Any single child can claim and complete it (like today's child_id=NULL behavior), but if multiple kids join it becomes a team completion with the bonus. Flexible — works solo or together.

## UI Changes

### Parent chore creation/edit form
- New dropdown: 'Completion mode' with options Solo / Team / Either
- When Team selected: show min_team_size input (default 2) and team_bonus_pct input (default 10%)
- Show a 🤝 icon on team chores in the chore list

### Kid chore view (MyChoresPage.tsx)
- Team chores show a 🤝 badge and 'Do together' button instead of regular complete
- When a sibling has started a team chore: show a prominent 'Join [sibling name]!' card with their avatar
- Team progress indicator: '2/3 joined' with sibling avatars
- Celebration animation when team is complete ('Team effort! 🎉')
- Either-mode chores show both 'Do alone' and 'Do together' options

### Notifications
- When a kid starts a team chore: push notification to siblings '🤝 [name] started [chore] — join in!'
- When team is complete: push to all team members 'Team [chore] done! +X kr each (includes Y% team bonus!)'

## Gamification
- 'Teamwork' streak: track consecutive days where at least one team chore was completed by 2+ kids
- Potential Stars integration: 'Team Player' badge when 10 team chores completed
- The team bonus percentage is the main incentive — working together literally pays more

## API Changes
- GET /api/allowance/my/chores: include completion_mode, active team sessions, sibling participation status
- POST /api/allowance/my/team-start/{chore_id}: initiate a team completion
- POST /api/allowance/my/team-join/{completion_id}: join a sibling's team completion
- Calculator: apply team_bonus_pct when team completions are approved

title: Allowance: emoji picker dropdown for chore icon field
description:
The chore creation form has a text input for the icon field where you type an emoji manually. Replace it with a dropdown/picker showing a curated set of relevant household chore emojis. Suggested categories:

Cleaning: 🧹🧽🧺🪣🫧🗑️
Kitchen: 🍽️🧑‍🍳🥘🫕
Outdoors: 🌿🪴🚗🐕🪵
School/homework: 📚✏️🎒📐
Personal: 🛁🪥👕🛏️
General: ⭐✅💪🏠🔧

Show as a grid of tappable emoji buttons — no typing needed. Keep the option to type a custom emoji for flexibility (small input below the grid). Remember these are for boys aged 7-10, so the picker should be easy to use on mobile too.
