title: Stars: Quick Stats shows 'coming soon' for distance and time
description: The Quick Stats card on the Stars page shows the workout count correctly but 'coming soon' for Total distance and Total time. These values should be computed from the user's workouts for the current week. Query workouts table for the current week, sum distance and duration, and return them from the /api/stars/balance or a dedicated endpoint. Display with proper units (km for distance, hours/minutes for time).

title: Stars: Challenges page missing — card links to nowhere
description: The Stars page has a Challenges navigation card (🎯 Challenges - Active challenges and goals) but it links to a page that doesn't exist. Either the StarChallenges.tsx page isn't routed, or the route isn't registered in App.tsx. Verify the route exists at /stars/challenges and that StarChallenges.tsx is properly imported and accessible to child users.

title: Stars: balance doesn't update after banking stars until page refresh
description: When a child deposits stars into the Star Bank (savings), the total stars balance displayed at the top of the Stars page doesn't update until a full page refresh. The savings endpoint returns successfully but the frontend state isn't refreshed. After the deposit/withdraw API call succeeds, re-fetch the balance (GET /api/stars/balance) and update the displayed total.

title: Family page: show family info for child users
description: When a child user views /family, the page is empty. It should show: the family they belong to (parent name), siblings (other linked children with avatar/nickname), and basic family stats. Currently the Family page only has parent-facing content (manage children, rewards, challenges). Add a child-friendly view that shows who's in their family.

title: Transit: departures showing 'No upcoming departures' for all stops
description: The transit departures page shows 'No upcoming departures' for both Bjørndalsbakken and Olav Kyrres gate, even during service hours. The Entur GraphQL API query may be malformed, the stop IDs may be incorrect (NSR:StopPlace IDs need to be verified for these stops), or the response parsing may have a bug. Debug by: 1) Verify the stop IDs are correct via Entur geocoder (GET https://api.entur.io/geocoder/v1/autocomplete?text=Bjørndalsbakken&layers=venue), 2) Test the GraphQL query directly against https://api.entur.io/journey-planner/v3/graphql, 3) Check the backend logs for API errors or empty responses, 4) Verify the ET-Client-Name header is set.

title: PWA manifest icon missing — vite.svg download error
description: Browser console shows: 'Error while trying to use the following icon from the Manifest: https://robinedvardsmith.com/vite.svg (Download error or resource isn't a valid image)'. The PWA manifest references vite.svg as an icon but it's either missing from the build output, not served correctly, or is the default Vite placeholder that was never replaced with a proper app icon. Replace with a proper Hytte icon (PNG/SVG) and update the manifest accordingly.

title: Settings: evaluate and hide non-essential sections for kids
description: Review all Settings.tsx sections and hide those irrelevant to non-admin child users. Currently visible to kids but shouldn't be: Hetzner API token (separate bug), training preferences (max HR, threshold pace etc — kids don't set these), possibly push notification management. Keep visible: profile, appearance, language, sessions. Consider a simplified 'kid settings' view with just the basics.

title: Stars: admin endpoint to manually award/adjust stars
description: There is no way to manually award stars. Needed for: (1) migrating existing star balances from an older system, (2) special parent-granted awards, (3) corrections. Add POST /api/admin/stars/award with admin-only auth: { user_id, amount, reason, description }. Inserts into star_transactions and updates star_balances. Also add a simple UI on the Family page — per-child 'Award Stars' button that opens a form (amount + reason).

title: Salary: year projection + actual payslip tracking
description:
Endpoints: GET /api/salary/estimate/year?year= (project all 12 months based on working days, expected utilization, commission projections), GET/PUT /api/salary/records (monthly records), POST /api/salary/records/{month}/confirm (mark estimate as actual from payslip). Frontend: year overview table (Jan-Dec: working days, hours, billable, base, commission, gross, tax, net). Past months show actuals, current month live estimate, future months projected. Utilization chart (Recharts line chart: monthly billable% vs 100% target). Estimate vs actual variance highlighting.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-salary-estimator.md (Phase 2)

title: Salary: this-month estimate + frontend page
description:
HTTP handlers: GET /api/salary/config, PUT /api/salary/config, GET /api/salary/estimate/current (uses work hours data for current month), GET /api/salary/estimate/month?month=, GET /api/salary/absence-cost. Frontend: SalaryPage.tsx at /salary. Hero card: this month's estimate (gross = base + commission, tax, net). Hours worked vs standard (from Work Hours integration). Working days done/remaining. Commission tier progress bars showing which tier is active and how far. Per-absence-day cost display. Sidebar nav with Banknote Lucide icon. i18n namespace salary.json for all 3 locales.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-salary-estimator.md (Phase 1)

title: Salary: config + commission calculation engine
description:
Create internal/salary/ package. Tables in db.go createSchema(): salary_config (user_id, base_salary, hourly_rate, standard_hours 7.5, currency NOK, effective_from), salary_commission_tiers (config_id, floor, ceiling, rate — 4 tiers: 0-60k at 0%, 60-80k at 20%, 80-100k at 40%, 100k+ at 50%), salary_tax_brackets (user_id, year, income_from, income_to, rate — Norwegian progressive brackets), salary_records (user_id, month, working_days, hours_worked, billable_hours, internal_hours, base_amount, commission, gross, tax, net, vacation_days, sick_days, is_estimate flag). Calculator: EstimateMonth (prorate base by hours/standard, calculate tiered commission, apply tax), CalculateCommission (progressive tiers), AbsenceDayCost. Feature flag: salary (default false). Add to auth/features.go. Integrates with Work Hours data (pulls hours_worked from work_days table).

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-salary-estimator.md (Phase 1)

title: Work Hours: week + month overview
description:
Summary endpoints: GET /api/workhours/summary/week?date=, GET /api/workhours/summary/month?month=, GET /api/workhours/flex?month=. Frontend: week overview table (Mon-Fri: start, end, net, reported, +/- vs 7.5h standard). Monthly calendar grid with hours per day (color coded: green=7.5h, yellow=short, blue=over). Monthly totals: worked, target, balance. Flex pool trend line chart over the month.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-work-hours.md (Phase 2)

title: Sky Watch: planet visibility calculations + display
description:
Planet position calculations via astronomy library (VSOP87/Keplerian). GetPlanetPositions(time, lat, lon) for Mercury through Saturn. Visibility determination: above horizon, not too close to sun, approximate magnitude. Handlers: GET /api/skywatch/now enhanced with planets array. Frontend: planet cards showing status (Visible now / Rises at XX:XX / Not visible), compass direction (N/NE/E/SE/S/SW/W/NW), altitude. Planet colors: Mercury grey, Venus yellow, Mars red, Jupiter beige/orange, Saturn gold.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-sky-watch.md (Phase 2)

title: Budget: CSV import for migration
description:
POST /api/budget/import/csv endpoint. Upload CSV, specify column mapping (date, description, amount, category). Server-side processing, file not persisted. Review imported transactions before committing. For migrating data from existing Google Sheets budget.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-budget.md (Phase 1)

title: Work Hours: API handlers + daily entry page
description:
HTTP handlers: GET/PUT/DELETE /api/workhours/day?date=, POST/PUT/DELETE session and deduction sub-resources, GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/workhours/presets. Frontend: WorkHoursPage.tsx at /workhours. Date selector with arrow nav. Sessions section: HH:MM start/end inputs, add/remove sessions, gross time display. Deductions: lunch checkbox (always shown), preset buttons as toggle chips for quick add (eg Kindergarten), manual add custom name+minutes. Summary card: gross, deductions, net, reported (0.5h), remainder, flex pool running total. Sidebar nav with Clock Lucide icon. i18n namespace workhours.json for all 3 locales. Mon-Fri only, no weekends.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-work-hours.md (Phase 1)

title: Allowance: extras + bonus rules
description:
Extra tasks (one-off jobs): POST /api/allowance/extras (parent creates), GET /api/allowance/extras, POST /api/allowance/my/claim-extra/{id} (kid claims). Bonus rules: full_week (complete all daily chores Mon-Sun = +20%), early_bird (before noon = +5 NOK), streak (consecutive all-chores days = escalating bonus), quality bonus (parent discretionary tip). GET/PUT /api/allowance/bonuses. Frontend: Extras Board on kid page (card layout with Claim button). Bonus rules settings tab on parent page.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-allowance.md (Phase 2)

title: Sky Watch: moon + sun frontend page
description:
Create SkyWatchPage.tsx at /skywatch. Hero section: current moon phase (large visual icon/SVG with illumination), phase name in Norwegian (Nymåne, Voksende sigd, etc.), illumination %, next full/new moon dates. Moon calendar: horizontal scrollable row of 30 days with tiny phase icons. Sun card: sunrise/sunset times, golden hour, day length, how much longer/shorter than yesterday. Dark navy/black backgrounds with subtle star particles, moon glow pulse animation. Sidebar nav with Moon Lucide icon. i18n namespace skywatch.json for all 3 locales.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-sky-watch.md (Phase 1)

title: Work Hours: data model + calculation engine
description:
Create internal/workhours/ package. Tables in db.go createSchema(): work_days (user_id, date, lunch bool, notes), work_sessions (day_id, start_time HH:MM, end_time HH:MM), work_deductions (day_id, name, minutes, preset_id), work_deduction_presets (user_id, name, default_minutes, icon, active). Calculator: gross minutes (sum sessions), net (gross - lunch 30min - deductions), reported hours (round down to nearest 0.5h), remainder to flex pool. Flex pool: cumulative remainder minutes with manual reset. Standard day: 7.5h (450 min). User prefs for standard_day, rounding increment, lunch_minutes. Feature flag: work_hours (default false). Add to auth/features.go.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-work-hours.md (Phase 1)

title: Allowance: kid chore view + completion flow
description:
Kid HTTP handlers: GET /api/allowance/my/chores (today's chores), POST /api/allowance/my/complete/{id} (mark done), GET /api/allowance/my/earnings (this week running total), GET /api/allowance/my/history (past weekly earnings). Frontend: MyChoresPage.tsx at /chores. Checklist style with big icons, chore name, NOK value. Tap to mark done, shows 'Waiting for approval' state. Approved = green check + amount. Use bright fun styling like Stars page — big buttons, clear feedback, minimal reading for young boys.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-allowance.md (Phase 1)

title: Sky Watch: astronomy backend + moon/sun calculations
description:
Create internal/skywatch/ package. Add github.com/cosinekitty/astronomy Go library (pure Go, no CGO). Wrapper functions: GetMoonPhase(time, lat, lon) MoonInfo (phase name, illumination %, moonrise/set), GetSunTimes(date, lat, lon) SunTimes (sunrise, sunset, golden hour, civil/nautical/astronomical twilight, day length, solar noon). Default location: 60.36091N, 5.24056E (home, Bergen). Fall back to weather location if set. Handlers: GET /api/skywatch/now (current moon + sun), GET /api/skywatch/moon?days=30 (phase calendar). Feature flag: skywatch (default true). Add to auth/features.go.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-sky-watch.md (Phase 1)

title: Allowance: parent API + chore management page

Parent HTTP handlers: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/allowance/chores, GET /api/allowance/pending (pending approvals), POST /api/allowance/approve/{id}, POST /api/allowance/reject/{id}, POST /api/allowance/quality-bonus/{id}. Frontend: AllowancePage.tsx at /allowance with tabs: Today (pending approvals with approve/reject buttons), Chores (manage definitions), Payouts (weekly summaries). Sidebar nav with Users Lucide icon. i18n namespace allowance.json for all 3 locales. Target UX: boys aged 7, 8, 10.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-allowance.md (Phase 1)

title: Budget: budget limits + category progress bars
description:
Budget limits table: budget_limits (category_id, amount, period=monthly, effective_from). PUT /api/budget/limits endpoint. Monthly summary enhanced with budget vs actual per category. Frontend: progress bars per category (green under budget, yellow 80-100%, red over budget). Income vs expenses bar at top. Net amount and 'igjen' (remaining) display.

Credit card tracking section: show credit card account with monthly spending breakdown by category (matching Kredittkort sheet). Subscription list view (recurring charges like PayPal, Microsoft, etc. with monthly cost). Budget vs actual comparison. Running credit balance, limit remaining, used amount.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-budget.md (Phase 2)

title: Allowance: data model + chore CRUD
description:
Create internal/allowance/ package. Tables in db.go createSchema(): allowance_chores (parent_id, child_id, name, amount NOK, frequency daily/weekly/once, icon, requires_approval, active), allowance_completions (chore_id, child_id, date, status pending/approved/rejected, approved_by, notes, UNIQUE per chore/child/date), allowance_extras (parent_id, name, amount, status open/claimed/completed/approved), allowance_bonus_rules (parent_id, type full_week/early_bird/streak/quality, multiplier, flat_amount), allowance_payouts (parent_id, child_id, week_start, base/bonus/total amount, paid_out flag). Storage CRUD. Feature flag: kids_allowance (default false). No deductions concept. Support base allowance (minimum weekly amount). Auto-approve after 24h for unapproved completions.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-allowance.md (Phase 1)

title: Budget: API handlers + transaction list page
description:
HTTP handlers for all budget CRUD endpoints. Accounts: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/budget/accounts. Categories: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE (with group_name grouping). Transactions: GET (with month, category, account filters)/POST/PUT/DELETE. Monthly summary: GET /api/budget/summary?month= showing income, expenses, net, remaining (igjen) per category.

Frontend: BudgetPage.tsx with month selector (arrow nav), transaction list with inline quick-add row. Each transaction shows: date, description, category badge (colored), amount (red expense/green income), account. Running balance display matching the sheet's 'igjen' concept. Sidebar nav item. i18n namespace budget.json for all 3 locales.

Support per-person tracking via family_links (spouse + children). Income split ratio display.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-budget.md (Phase 1-2)

title: Transit: departures page frontend
description:
Create TransitPage.tsx at /transit. One card per favorite stop showing next 5-8 departures. Each departure: route number (colored badge), destination, minutes until departure (X min for <30min, actual time for later). Real-time indicator (green dot if live, grey if scheduled). Auto-refresh every 30 seconds. Add sidebar nav item with Bus/TrainFront Lucide icon. Add i18n namespace transit.json for all 3 locales (en/nb/th). Keys: title=Departures/Avganger, minutes, addStop, removeStop, searchStops, realtime, scheduled, noDepartures.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-skyss-departures.md (Phase 1)

title: Budget: data model + CRUD storage
description:
Create internal/budget/ package. Data model: budget_accounts (name, type checking/savings/credit/cash, currency NOK, balance, icon), budget_categories (name, group_name for grouping like 'Bolig', 'Transport', icon, color, is_income), budget_transactions (account_id, category_id, amount negative=expense/positive=income, description, date, tags, is_transfer, transfer_to), budget_recurring (account_id, category_id, amount, frequency monthly/weekly/yearly, day_of_month, start/end date, last_generated). All tables in db.go createSchema(). Storage CRUD for all entities. Feature flag: budget (default false). Add to auth/features.go.

Categories to seed from existing sheet: forsikring, lån (loan payment), husforsikring, strøm (electricity), mat (groceries), barnehage (kindergarten), mobil, transport, underholdning (entertainment). Category groups: Bolig (housing), Barn (children), Fast (fixed), Variabel (variable), Inntekt (income).

Income split support: configurable percentage split between partners (default 60/40) stored in user_preferences.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-budget.md (Phase 1)

Support shared family calendars. Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with i18n.language for locale-appropriate dates. Add i18n keys (weekView, monthView, agenda, today, noEvents, etc.) to all 3 locales.\n\nImplementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-calendar.md (Phase 1)

Create internal/transit/ package. GraphQL client for Entur journey planner API (https://api.entur.io/journey-planner/v3/graphql). Query stopPlace estimatedCalls for real-time departures. No API key needed, just ET-Client-Name header. Models: Departure (line, destination, departure time, is_realtime, platform, delay_minutes). Cache departures 30 seconds. Handlers: GET /api/transit/departures?stops=..., GET /api/transit/search?q=... (Entur Geocoder proxy), GET/PUT /api/transit/settings. Default stops: Bjørndalsbakken and Olav Kyrres gate (bus 3/3E westbound). Feature flag: transit (default true). Add feature to auth/features.go.

Handlers: GET /api/calendar/events (date range), GET /api/calendar/calendars (list user calendars), PUT /api/calendar/settings (save visible calendars in user_preferences). Cache events in calendar_events table with incremental sync via updatedMin.\n\nImplementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-calendar.md (Phase 1)

Store full OAuth tokens (access_token, refresh_token) in a new google_tokens table, encrypted at rest. Request access_type=offline for refresh tokens. Existing users will need re-consent.\n\nImplementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-calendar.md (Phase 1)

title: Scheduler tests
description: Create internal/stars/scheduler_test.go. Use a mock/stub SchedulerDB (implement the interface defined in the scheduler file). Cover: CheckStreakWarnings fires only when streak is active and no workout today, and is suppressed outside quiet hours; SendWeeklySummaries only sends on Monday 8 AM window; CheckChallengeExpiry sends at 2-day and 1-day milestones and does not duplicate; CheckCloseToReward triggers at <=20% threshold and respects 7-day cooldown per reward. Tests should use table-driven style with injected fake clocks for time-sensitive assertions.

title: Close-to-reward balance check
description: Add CheckCloseToReward(ctx, db, userID string, newBalance int) to internal/stars/scheduler.go. After a balance update event, checks if newBalance is within 20% of the cost of any active reward for that user. Sends a notification at most once per reward per 7-day rolling window (store last-notified timestamp per user+reward, skip if within 7 days). Extend SchedulerDB interface with queries for active rewards and cooldown state. This function is called from the balance-update path rather than a cron schedule — document the call site in the daemon/service layer where balances are updated.

title: Challenge expiry reminder scheduler
description: Add CheckChallengeExpiry(ctx, db) to internal/stars/scheduler.go. Queries challenges expiring in exactly 2 days and 1 day (two milestone windows). Sends a reminder notification for each milestone, with deduplication: track per-challenge per-milestone that a reminder was already sent (e.g. a sent_reminders table or a flag column) and skip if already notified at that milestone. Wire into the daemon daily schedule. Add to SchedulerDB interface the queries needed (find expiring challenges, check/record dedup state).

title: Streak warning & weekly summary schedulers
description: Create internal/stars/scheduler.go with two functions: CheckStreakWarnings(ctx, db) — queries children with active streaks and no workout recorded today, sends a streak-at-risk notification respecting quiet hours (skip if current time is outside allowed window, e.g. 7-9 PM); SendWeeklySummaries(ctx, db) — aggregates weekly stats per child grouped by parent, sends summary notification targeting Monday 8 AM (guard with day/time check). description: Create internal/stars/scheduler.go implementing: CheckStreakWarnings(ctx, db) — queries children with active streaks and no workout today, sends streak-at-risk notification respecting quiet hours; SendWeeklySummaries(ctx, db) — aggregates weekly stats per child grouped by parent, sends Monday 8 AM summary; CheckChallengeExpiry(ctx, db) — finds challenges expiring in 2 days or 1 day and sends reminders with deduplication per milestone; CheckCloseToReward(ctx, db, userID, newBalance) — checks if balance is within 20% of any active reward cost, sends at most once per reward per 7 days. Wire these into the existing daemon/background loop at appropriate schedule points (daily 7 PM for streak warnings, Monday 8 AM for weekly summaries, daily for challenge expiry). title: Kids Stars: Star Savings Account
description: Create star_savings table (migration file). Implement internal/stars/savings.go with Deposit, RequestWithdrawal, CompleteWithdrawal, and PayInterest functions. Deposit debits star_balances via negative star_transaction (reason='savings_deposit') and credits star_savings.balance. RequestWithdrawal sets pending_withdrawal and withdrawal_available_at=now+24h. CompleteWithdrawal checks timestamp then moves stars back via positive star_transaction (reason='savings_withdrawal'). PayInterest does floor(balance*0.10) weekly for all users with balance>0 (reason='savings_interest'). Wire GET /api/stars/savings, POST /api/stars/savings/deposit, POST /api/stars/savings/withdraw. Schedule PayInterest on Sundays. Frontend: Star Bank card on Stars.tsx with piggy bank emoji, deposit/withdraw inputs, pending withdrawal countdown, interest explanation. Tests: deposit/withdraw flow, 24h delay enforcement, compound interest over multiple weeks.

Each checker compares workout/userStats/streakInfo against thresholds, returns newly earned badges. For each earned badge: INSERT into user_badges and send push notification. Call SeedBadges during app startup. This sub-task produces the core domain logic consumed by the API sub-task.

title: Auto-tag incorrectly labels steady easy runs as interval workouts (e.g. 10x1km)
description:
When a 10km easy run at a steady pace is imported from a FIT file (COROS), the auto-tagger labels it as '10x1km' as if it were an interval workout. This happens because COROS auto-lap creates a lap every 1km, and the tag detection logic interprets these equal-distance laps as structured intervals.

The auto-tag logic needs to distinguish between:
- Auto-laps (every 1km/1mi, triggered by the watch automatically) — should NOT generate interval tags
- Manual laps (user presses lap button at interval boundaries) — SHOULD generate interval tags

Possible detection approaches:
- Check if all laps are exactly the same distance (within tolerance) AND the pace variation between laps is low — this suggests auto-lap, not intervals
- Check the FIT file's lap trigger field (mesgdef.Lap.LapTrigger) — auto-laps have trigger=distance vs manual laps have trigger=manual
- Check pace/HR variance between laps — true intervals have significant pace differences, easy runs with auto-laps have consistent pace

Investigate how the auto-tag is generated (search for interval detection in internal/training/) and fix the false positive for steady-pace runs.

(2) Training status badge: color-coded pill (green/blue/yellow/red per status) with description text, data from the `training_status` field of the load API. (3) ACR gauge: simple linear or arc gauge with colour zones (<0.8 blue, 0.8–1.3 green, 1.3–1.5 yellow, >1.5 red). (4) Weekly AI summary section: a 'Generate summary' button that POSTs to `/api/training/summary/analyze`, then displays structured sections (volume, intensity, recovery, recommendations) plus risk-flag warning badges and highlight success badges. description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-lactate-auto-import.md (Phase 2 + 3)

Create internal/lactate/import_handlers.go with two handlers:

1. PreviewFromWorkoutHandler — POST /api/lactate/tests/preview-from-workout
   Request: {workout_id, lactate_data (pasted text), warmup_duration_min, stage_duration_min}
   Loads workout with laps + samples, calls ParseLactateInput on lactate_data, calls ExtractStageHR, returns proposed test with stages (speed, lactate, HR, confidence) and matching details. Does NOT persist.

2. ImportFromWorkoutHandler — POST /api/lactate/tests/from-workout  
   Same request, but persists the lactate test via existing Create() function. Sets workout_id on the created test. Returns the created test.

Register both routes in internal/api/router.go within the lactate feature group.

Error responses: parse failures return hint about expected format, no matching laps returns warning but allows proceeding without HR.

2. ExtractStageHR(laps, samples, pairs, opts) (*ImportResult, error) — matches parsed pairs to workout laps by speed (primary) or duration (fallback), extracts HR averaged over last 30 seconds of each matched lap from time-series data.
3. Types: SpeedLactatePair, ImportOptions (WarmupDurationMin, StageDurationMin, HRWindowSeconds), ImportResult (Stages, Warnings, Method), ProposedStage.

description: Update `web/src/utils/timeAgo.ts` to use translation keys for relative time strings ('2 hours ago', 'yesterday', etc.) — add keys to `common` namespace or a dedicated `time` section. Update all `formatDate`/`formatNumber` call sites throughout the codebase to pass `i18n.language` as locale instead of `undefined`. Verify the `formatDate`/`formatNumber` utilities from the infrastructure bead are used consistently. This sub-task should be done last as it touches shared utilities used across all pages; run full `npm run build` and visual check of all pages after completion.

title: i18n: Extract strings from Notes, Chat, Links, Infra, CalendarPage, and Login pages
description: Create `web/public/locales/en/notes.json`, `chat.json`, `infra.json`. For `Notes.tsx`: page title, editor UI labels, empty state — do NOT translate note content. For `Chat.tsx`: page title, input placeholder, send button, status indicators — do NOT translate message content. For `Links.tsx`: add to `common` namespace (small). For `Infra.tsx`: server/health/uptime labels; technical service names may stay English. For `CalendarPage.tsx`: use `Intl` APIs with `i18n.language` for month/day names; extract custom nav/event labels into `common`. For `Login.tsx`: form labels, placeholders, submit button, error messages into `common`. Copy new locale files to `nb/` and `th/`. Independent of other sub-tasks.

title: i18n: Extract strings from Lactate pages
description: Create `web/public/locales/en/lactate.json`. Modify `LactateTests.tsx`, `LactateNewTest.tsx`, `LactateTestDetail.tsx`, `LactateInsights.tsx`. Extract list headings, column headers, form labels, placeholders, validation messages, chart labels, and mmol/L unit strings (use the `units.mmol` key pattern). Add `useTranslation(['lactate', 'common'])` to each component. Copy `lactate.json` to `nb/` and `th/`. Independent of Training and Settings sub-tasks.

title: i18n: Extract strings from Settings.tsx and Admin/Webhooks pages
description: Create `web/public/locales/en/settings.json` covering Settings.tsx (~900 lines): section headings, form labels, placeholders, help text, button labels, toggle descriptions, validation errors, toast messages. Also extract Admin.tsx and Webhooks.tsx strings — if small, add to `common` or `settings` namespace rather than creating new files. Add `useTranslation(['settings', 'common'])` in each component. Copy new/updated files to `nb/` and `th/`. This sub-task is independent of others but should be done after the infrastructure bead establishes the i18n setup.

title: i18n: Extract strings from Training pages
description: Create `web/public/locales/en/training.json` with all Training page strings including a `units` section (km, m, hours_minutes, minutes, pace, bpm, mmol). Modify `Training.tsx`, `TrainingDetail.tsx`, `TrainingCompare.tsx`, `TrainingTrends.tsx`. Add `useTranslation(['training', 'common'])` to each. Replace hardcoded strings and unit suffixes in `formatDuration`, `formatDistance`, `formatPace` helpers — if these are shared utilities, add a `t` parameter or import `i18n` directly. Copy `training.json` to `nb/` and `th/` as English placeholders. Verify `npm run build` succeeds and pages render identically.

title: Kids Stars: Push notifications — stars, streaks, rewards, levels, weekly summaries
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-stars.md

## Overview
Wire up all push notifications for the kids star system using the existing internal/push infrastructure. This bead consolidates and completes all notification triggers across the system (plan Section 9).

## Backend: internal/stars/notifications.go

Create a dedicated notifications file that centralizes all star-system notification logic. This integrates with the existing internal/push package for Web Push (VAPID/AES-128-GCM).

### Notification Types to Implement

**Per-Workout Notifications (sent immediately after workout processing):**
- Stars earned: "You earned {X} ⭐ from your {sport_type}! Total: {balance} ⭐"
  - Include breakdown if multiple awards: "Base: 2⭐, Distance: +10⭐, Effort: +2⭐"
  - Recipient: child

**Level Notifications (sent immediately on level-up):**
- Level up: "LEVEL UP! You're now a {Title} (Level {N})! 🎉"
  - Recipient: child
- Parent notification: "{Nickname} leveled up to Level {N} — {Title}!"
  - Recipient: parent

**Badge Notifications (sent immediately when badge earned):**
- New badge: "New badge unlocked: {emoji} {name}!"
  - Recipient: child

**Streak Notifications:**
- Streak milestone (immediate): "🔥 {N}-day streak! Keep it burning!"
  - Triggered at 3, 7, 14, 30 day marks
  - Recipient: child
- Streak at risk (scheduled, 7 PM daily): "Your {N}-day streak will break tomorrow if you don't work out! 🔥⚠️"
  - Only send if: no workout today AND current_streak > 0
  - Recipient: child
  - Must respect quiet hours (internal/quiethours)

**Reward Notifications:**
- Reward claimed: "{Nickname} claimed '{RewardTitle}' ({cost} ⭐). Approve?"
  - Recipient: parent
- Reward approved: "Your reward '{RewardTitle}' was approved! 🎉"
  - Recipient: child
- Reward denied: "Your reward claim for '{RewardTitle}' was denied. {cost} ⭐ have been refunded."
  - Recipient: child
- Close to a reward: "You're only {N} ⭐ away from '{RewardTitle}'! 🍦"
  - Check after each star award: if balance is within 20% of any active reward cost
  - Max 1 per reward per week (don't spam)
  - Recipient: child

**Challenge Notifications:**
- Challenge completed: "You completed '{title}'! +{stars} ⭐"
  - Recipient: child + parent
- Challenge expiring (2 days before): "Only 2 days left in '{title}'! You need {remaining} more."
  - Recipient: child
- Challenge final day: "Last day for '{title}'! Go for it!"
  - Recipient: child

**Weekly Summary (scheduled, Monday 8 AM):**
- "This week: {Child1} earned {X} ⭐, ran {Y} km. {Child2} earned {Z} ⭐, ran {W} km."
  - Recipient: parent
  - Aggregate all children's stats

**Family Workout (immediate):**
- "Family workout day! Both {Child1} and {Child2} worked out today! +10 ⭐ each"
  - Sent when 2+ family members log workouts on the same day
  - Recipient: all family members

### Scheduled Notification Runner

Create a notification scheduler that runs as part of the existing daemon/background loop:

- **CheckStreakWarnings(ctx, db)** — run daily at 7 PM
  - For all children with active streaks and no workout today: send streak warning
  - Respect quiet hours

- **SendWeeklySummaries(ctx, db)** — run Monday at 8 AM
  - For all parents with linked children: compile and send weekly summary

- **CheckChallengeExpiry(ctx, db)** — run daily
  - For all active challenges: check if 2 days or 1 day from expiry, send reminders

- **CheckCloseToReward(ctx, db, userID, newBalance)** — run after each star award
  - Check if user is within 20% of any affordable reward

### Notification Deduplication

To prevent notification spam:
- Track sent notifications in a simple table or in-memory cache:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notification_log (
    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    type       TEXT NOT NULL,
    reference  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',  -- e.g., "streak_warning_2026-03-22" or "close_to_reward_5"
    sent_at    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notification_log_user ON notification_log(user_id, type, reference);
```
- Before sending, check if this exact notification (user+type+reference) was sent recently (within cooldown period)
- Cooldowns: streak warning = 1 day, close to reward = 7 days, challenge expiry = 1 per milestone

### Age-Appropriate Messaging
- Keep notifications positive and encouraging, never shaming
- Use emoji liberally — kids love them
- Keep messages short (under 100 characters for the body when possible)
- No pressure language — "if you want to" rather than "you must"

## Integration Points

This bead ties together notification triggers from all previous beads. Ensure:
1. Star engine (bead 2): after EvaluateWorkout, call SendStarsEarnedNotification
2. Levels (bead 5): after AddXP with level-up, call SendLevelUpNotification
3. Badges (bead 6): after badge award, call SendBadgeNotification
4. Rewards (bead 7): after claim/approve/deny, call appropriate notification
5. Streaks (bead 8): after streak milestone, call SendStreakMilestoneNotification
6. Challenges (bead 9): after completion, call SendChallengeCompletedNotification

Add the notification calls at each integration point (if not already added in previous beads, some may have been — consolidate here).

## Tests

### notifications_test.go
- Test each notification message format
- Test deduplication (same notification not sent twice within cooldown)
- Test streak warning only sent when streak is actually at risk
- Test weekly summary aggregation across multiple children
- Test close-to-reward detection with various balance/reward combinations
- Test quiet hours respected (notification not sent during quiet hours)
- Test scheduled runner triggers at correct times

title: Kids Stars: Creative extras — Story Mode, Star Savings, Bingo, Beat My Parent
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-stars.md

## Overview
Implement the creative gamification extras: Workout Story Mode (fictional journey), Star Savings Account (compound interest), Workout Bingo, and Beat My Parent mode (plan Section 12: 12.2, 12.9, 12.10, 12.8).

## 1. Workout Story Mode (Section 12.2)

Frame cumulative workout distance as progress along a fictional journey. "You ran 3.2km today — that's like running from the Shire to Bree! You're 15% of the way to Rivendell!"

### Backend

Add table:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS story_journeys (
    user_id      INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    theme        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'middle_earth',  -- middle_earth, space, pirate
    total_distance_m REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    current_leg  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    started_at   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
);
```

Themes with waypoints (distances in km):
- **Middle Earth**: Shire→Bree (50km), Bree→Rivendell (150km), Rivendell→Moria (200km), Moria→Lothlórien (50km), Lothlórien→Mordor (300km), Mordor→Mount Doom (50km). Total: 800km
- **Space Exploration**: Earth→Moon (384km), Moon→Mars (500km), Mars→Jupiter (600km), Jupiter→Saturn (1000km). Total: 2484km
- **Pirate Treasure Map**: Port→Skull Island (100km), Skull Island→Shipwreck Cove (200km), Shipwreck Cove→Treasure Island (300km). Total: 600km

Store theme definitions as Go constants/maps. Each theme has legs with start/end names and distances.

API:
- **GET /api/stars/journey** — current journey progress: { theme, total_distance_km, current_leg_name, current_leg_progress_percent, next_waypoint, distance_to_next_km, overall_progress_percent }
- **PUT /api/stars/journey/theme** — change theme (body: { theme: "space" })

After each workout, update total_distance_m and check if a waypoint was crossed. If so, award bonus stars (10 per waypoint) and send notification: "You've arrived at {waypoint}! ⭐+10"

### Frontend: Journey Component on Stars.tsx
- Visual progress bar styled as a path/road
- Current position marker with avatar emoji
- Waypoint markers along the path
- Current leg description: "Traveling from {A} to {B} — {X}km to go!"
- Theme selector dropdown

## 2. Star Savings Account (Section 12.9)

Kids can put stars in "savings" with 10% weekly compound interest. Stars in savings can't be spent until withdrawn (1-day withdrawal delay).

### Backend

Add table:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS star_savings (
    user_id        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    balance        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    last_interest  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',  -- date of last interest payment
    pending_withdrawal INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    withdrawal_available_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
);
```

Functions in internal/stars/savings.go:
- **Deposit(ctx, db, userID, amount) error** — move stars from spendable to savings
  - Deduct from star_balances (INSERT negative star_transaction with reason="savings_deposit")
  - Add to star_savings.balance
- **RequestWithdrawal(ctx, db, userID, amount) error** — initiate withdrawal
  - Set pending_withdrawal=amount, withdrawal_available_at=now+24h
  - Cannot withdraw more than savings balance
- **CompleteWithdrawal(ctx, db, userID) error** — complete after delay
  - Check withdrawal_available_at <= now
  - Move from star_savings to star_balances (INSERT positive star_transaction with reason="savings_withdrawal")
- **PayInterest(ctx, db) error** — called weekly (Sunday)
  - For all users with star_savings.balance > 0:
  - interest = floor(balance * 0.10)
  - Add interest to savings balance
  - Record as star_transaction with reason="savings_interest"
  - last_interest = today

API:
- **GET /api/stars/savings** — { balance, pending_withdrawal, withdrawal_available_at, interest_earned_total }
- **POST /api/stars/savings/deposit** — { amount }
- **POST /api/stars/savings/withdraw** — { amount }

### Frontend: Savings Component on Stars.tsx
- "Star Bank" card with savings balance (use piggy bank or vault emoji 🏦)
- Deposit/withdraw buttons with amount input
- Show pending withdrawal with countdown timer
- Interest info: "Earn 10% interest every week!"
- Simple explanation for kids about compound interest

## 3. Workout Bingo (Section 12.10)

Weekly 3x3 bingo card with challenges. Complete a row/column/diagonal for bonus stars. Full card = jackpot.

### Backend

Add table:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bingo_cards (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    week_start  TEXT NOT NULL,
    cells       TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON array of 9 cells: [{challenge, completed}]
    completed_lines INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    full_card   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bingo_user_week ON bingo_cards(user_id, week_start);
```

Bingo cell challenge pool (pick 9 randomly each week):
- "Run over 2km", "Workout before school (before 8 AM)", "Try a new sport", "Beat yesterday's distance", "30+ minute workout", "Workout with a family member", "Zone 3+ for 10 minutes", "Weekend workout", "Burn 200+ calories", "5000+ steps equivalent", "Workout after dinner", "Two workouts in one day", "Outdoor workout", "Indoor workout", "Hit a personal record"

Functions in internal/stars/bingo.go:
- **GetOrCreateBingoCard(ctx, db, userID) (*BingoCard, error)** — get this week's card or generate new one
- **UpdateBingoProgress(ctx, db, userID, workout) error** — check each cell against workout, mark completed
- **CheckBingoLines(card) (lines int, fullCard bool)** — check rows, columns, diagonals
  - Each completed line: 15 bonus stars
  - Full card: 50 bonus stars (jackpot)

API:
- **GET /api/stars/bingo** — current bingo card with cell status

### Frontend: Bingo Component on Stars.tsx
- 3x3 grid card
- Each cell shows challenge text, completed cells have checkmark overlay and green background
- Completed lines highlighted
- Full card triggers celebration animation

## 4. Beat My Parent Mode (Section 12.8)

If parent also tracks workouts, kids can challenge them. If kid beats parent's weekly distance (scaled by age factor), massive bonus.

### Backend

Functions in internal/stars/challenges.go (extend existing):
- **GetBeatMyParentStatus(ctx, db, childID) (*BeatParentStatus, error)**
  - Get parent's weekly distance and child's weekly distance
  - Age scaling factor: child's distance is multiplied by (parent_age / child_age) for fair comparison
  - Need child's age — use birthday from user preferences (if set)
  - Return: { parent_distance_km, child_distance_km, child_scaled_distance_km, is_beating_parent, bonus_stars }
  - If child beats parent: 25 bonus stars at end of week

API:
- **GET /api/stars/beat-parent** — current week's Beat My Parent status

### Frontend: BeatMyParent Component on Stars.tsx
- Versus-style display: Kid avatar vs Parent avatar
- Distance bars side by side (child's bar scaled)
- "You're WINNING!" or "Keep going!" message
- Bragging rights badge if kid wins

## Routes
Add any new routes to App.tsx as needed.

## Tests
- Story Mode: test waypoint crossing detection, theme switching, progress calculation
- Savings: test deposit, withdrawal delay, interest calculation, compound interest over multiple weeks
- Bingo: test card generation randomness, line detection (rows, columns, diagonals), full card detection
- Beat My Parent: test age scaling, weekly distance comparison, bonus awarding
close_reason: Decomposed into 4 sub-beads: Hytte-olyq, Hytte-0fda, Hytte-gt09, Hytte-tnex

title: Kids Stars: Family leaderboard — weekly/monthly standings
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-stars.md

## Overview
Implement the family leaderboard with weekly and monthly standings, activity comparisons, and optional parent participation (plan Section 7.3).

## Backend: internal/stars/leaderboard.go

### Leaderboard Queries

- **GetWeeklyLeaderboard(ctx, db, parentID) (*Leaderboard, error)**
  Returns ranked family members for the current week:
  - Stars earned this week
  - Total distance this week (km)
  - Workout count this week
  - Current streak length
  Each entry: { user_id, nickname, avatar_emoji, level, stars_this_week, distance_this_week_km, workouts_this_week, current_streak, rank }

  Query logic:
  1. Get all children linked to parentID from family_links
  2. Optionally include parent themselves if they have workouts (parent participates by default, configurable)
  3. For each family member, aggregate:
     - Stars: SUM(amount) FROM star_transactions WHERE user_id=X AND created_at >= week_start AND amount > 0
     - Distance: SUM(distance) FROM workouts WHERE user_id=X AND date >= week_start
     - Workout count: COUNT(*) FROM workouts WHERE user_id=X AND date >= week_start
     - Streak: current_count FROM streaks WHERE user_id=X AND streak_type='daily_workout'
  4. Rank by stars_this_week DESC

- **GetMonthlyLeaderboard(ctx, db, parentID) (*Leaderboard, error)**
  Same structure but for the current month.

- **GetAllTimeLeaderboard(ctx, db, parentID) (*Leaderboard, error)**
  All-time stats: total stars earned, total distance, total workouts.

### Leaderboard Period Handling
- Week starts Monday (ISO week)
- Month starts on the 1st
- Helper functions: weekStart(date), monthStart(date)

### API Endpoint

- **GET /api/stars/leaderboard?period=weekly** — family leaderboard
  - period: "weekly" (default), "monthly", "alltime"
  - Returns the leaderboard for the requesting user's family
  - If user is a child: find their parent, then get family leaderboard
  - If user is a parent: get leaderboard for their children + self

## Frontend: Leaderboard Component

Create a LeaderboardCard component used on Stars.tsx:

- Compact view (on Stars.tsx dashboard):
  - Show top 3 family members for this week
  - Each entry: rank medal (🥇🥈🥉), avatar emoji, nickname, stars earned
  - "View Full Leaderboard" link

- Full view (expanded, possibly on a sub-route or modal):
  - Period toggle: Weekly / Monthly / All Time
  - Table with columns: Rank, Avatar+Name, Stars, Distance, Workouts, Streak
  - Highlight the current user's row
  - Fun crown emoji on #1 position
  - If parent participates: show them with "(Parent)" tag

### Design Notes
- Use podium-style layout for top 3 on the compact view
- Bright competitive colors but friendly — no shame for lower positions
- Responsive: single column on mobile

## Parent Configuration

Add to parent settings (per-child or family-wide):
- kids_stars_leaderboard_visible: boolean (default true) — whether leaderboard is shown to kids
- kids_stars_parent_participates: boolean (default true) — whether parent appears on leaderboard

Store in user_preferences. Include in GET/PUT /api/family/children/{id}/settings.

## Tests

### leaderboard_test.go
- Test weekly leaderboard with multiple family members
- Test ranking order (highest stars first)
- Test period boundaries (week start/end, month start/end)
- Test parent inclusion/exclusion based on config
- Test leaderboard with no workouts (empty week)
- Test user who is a child can see their family's leaderboard
- Test that cross-family data is never exposed

title: Kids Stars: Challenges system — parent-created & system-generated
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-stars.md

## Overview
Implement the challenges system where parents create time-boxed challenges for children and the system auto-generates weekly challenges. Includes progress tracking and challenge rewards (plan Sections 7.4).

## Database

Tables already created in bead 1: challenges, challenge_participants. This bead implements CRUD, progress tracking, and system-generated challenges.

## Backend: internal/stars/challenges.go

### Challenge CRUD (parent-facing)

- **CreateChallenge(ctx, db, parentID, challenge Challenge) (*Challenge, error)**
  - Encrypt title and description at rest
  - Validate: start_date < end_date, star_reward >= 0, challenge_type is valid
  - challenge_type options: "distance", "duration", "workout_count", "streak", "custom"
  - target_value meaning by type: distance=meters, duration=minutes, workout_count=count, streak=days

- **UpdateChallenge(ctx, db, parentID, challengeID, fields) error**
- **DeleteChallenge(ctx, db, parentID, challengeID) error**
- **GetChallenges(ctx, db, parentID) ([]Challenge, error)** — all challenges created by parent
- **GetActiveChallenges(ctx, db, userID) ([]ChallengeWithProgress, error)** — active challenges for a participant

### Participant Management

- **AddParticipant(ctx, db, challengeID, userID) error** — add child to challenge
  - Verify the child is linked to the challenge creator
- **RemoveParticipant(ctx, db, challengeID, userID) error**

### Progress Tracking

- **UpdateChallengeProgress(ctx, db, userID, workout) error**
  Called after each workout. For each active challenge the user participates in:
  - "distance": add workout distance to current_value
  - "duration": add workout duration (minutes) to current_value
  - "workout_count": increment current_value by 1
  - "streak": set current_value to current daily streak count
  - Check if current_value >= target_value → mark completed, award stars
  - When completed: INSERT star_transaction, update star_balances, send notifications

### System-Generated Weekly Challenges (Section 7.4)

Function: GenerateWeeklyChallenges(ctx, db) error
Called Monday morning (or on first Monday login). Creates auto-challenges for all active kids:

1. **"Beat Last Week"** — improve on last week's total distance
   - target_value = last week's distance + 10%
   - star_reward = 15

2. **"Try a New Sport"** — log a workout in a sport not done this month
   - challenge_type = "custom" (checked specially)
   - star_reward = 10

3. **"Consistency King"** — at least 4 workouts this week
   - challenge_type = "workout_count", target_value = 4
   - star_reward = 10

4. **"Heart Zone Challenge"** — 30+ minutes in Zone 3+ this week
   - challenge_type = "custom" (cumulative HR zone time)
   - star_reward = 10

System challenges are created with created_by = 0 or a special system user ID. They auto-add all active children as participants.

### API Endpoints

**Parent endpoints (add to internal/family/handlers.go):**
- **POST /api/family/challenges** — create challenge (body: title, description, challenge_type, target_value, star_reward, start_date, end_date, participant_ids[])
- **GET /api/family/challenges** — list parent's challenges (active + past)
- **PUT /api/family/challenges/{id}** — update challenge
- **DELETE /api/family/challenges/{id}** — delete challenge

**Kid endpoints (add to internal/stars/handlers.go):**
- **GET /api/stars/challenges** — active challenges with progress
  Returns: { id, title, description, challenge_type, target_value, current_value, progress_percent, star_reward, start_date, end_date, completed, completed_at }

### Push Notifications

- Challenge completed: notify child ("You completed '{title}'! +{stars} ⭐") and parent ("{child} completed '{title}'!")
- Challenge expiring (2 days before end_date): notify child ("Only 2 days left in '{title}'! You need {remaining} more.")
- Challenge expiring (final day): notify child ("Last day for '{title}'! Go for it!")

## Frontend: StarChallenges.tsx at /stars/challenges

Kid-facing challenges page:
- List of active challenges as cards
- Each card shows: title, description, progress bar (current_value/target_value), star reward, days remaining
- Completed challenges show with checkmark and "Completed!" badge
- Past/expired challenges in a collapsible "History" section

## Frontend: FamilyChallenges.tsx at /family/challenges

Parent challenge management:
- "Create Challenge" form: title, description, type (dropdown), target value, star reward, date range, select children to participate
- Active challenges list with progress per participant
- Past challenges with results

## Routes
Add in App.tsx: /stars/challenges, /family/challenges

## Tests

### challenges_test.go
- Test CreateChallenge with valid data
- Test progress update for each challenge type (distance, duration, workout_count, streak)
- Test challenge completion detection and star awarding
- Test that completed challenges don't re-award stars
- Test system-generated challenge creation
- Test "Beat Last Week" target calculation
- Test challenge expiry (past end_date challenges are inactive)
- Test authorization: parent can only manage challenges for their own children
close_reason: Previously decomposed into children; no remaining work

title: Settings UI polish, confidence scoring, richer analysis display, goal-aware analysis
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-ai-analysis-expansion.md

## Goal
Polish the UI and AI quality: complete the threshold settings UI, add confidence scoring to AI outputs, build richer structured analysis display components, and add goal-aware analysis.

## What to build

### 1. Enhanced insights display (web/src/pages/TrainingDetail.tsx)
Currently the insights display is a simple text block. Expand to structured cards:

#### a) Effort and Pacing Card
- Show effort_summary prominently at the top
- Pacing analysis with visual indicator (e.g., color-coded text or badge: even/negative/positive split)
- If HR drift and pace CV are available (from bead 3 metrics), show them as compact stat pills

#### b) HR Zone Card
- Zone distribution bar chart (horizontal stacked bar showing time in each zone, color-coded)
- Use the existing ZoneDistribution data from GET /api/training/workouts/{id}/zones endpoint
- Show threshold_context from insights if available
- If HR drift is available, show drift assessment

#### c) Trend Card (if trend_analysis present in insights)
- Fitness direction indicator (arrow icon: up green, right yellow, down red)
- comparison_to_recent as a highlighted callout box
- notable_changes as a compact bullet list
- Mini sparkline of recent similar workout paces if data available

#### d) Risk Flags Section
- If risk_flags array is non-empty, show yellow/red warning badges
- Each flag as a pill/chip with appropriate color
- Only show this section when flags exist

#### e) Race Predictions Card (if predictions available)
- Show predicted times for 5K, 10K, half marathon, marathon
- Note the basis workout
- Small disclaimer about formula-based estimates

#### f) Suggestions Card
- Existing suggestions array displayed as actionable cards/chips
- Consider using a lighter visual weight than the main analysis

### 2. Confidence scoring on AI insights
Add confidence metadata to AI responses so users know how reliable the analysis is.

#### a) Update prompt instructions
In all AI prompts (insights, comparison, classification), add instructions asking Claude to include a confidence field:
Add to the JSON schema: confidence_score (0.0-1.0) and confidence_note (brief explanation of what limits confidence).

Example factors that reduce confidence:
- No user thresholds set (guessing zones) -> lower confidence
- No historical data available -> lower confidence on trend analysis
- Very short workout (<15 min) -> lower confidence on classification
- Missing HR data -> lower confidence on effort analysis

#### b) Update models
Add to TrainingInsights in internal/training/models.go:
- ConfidenceScore float64  json:confidence_score,omitempty
- ConfidenceNote  string   json:confidence_note,omitempty

Similarly for ComparisonAnalysis.

#### c) Frontend display
Show confidence as a subtle indicator near the insights:
- High (>0.8): green checkmark, Highly confident
- Medium (0.5-0.8): yellow info icon, Moderate confidence
- Low (<0.5): orange warning, Low confidence - set your thresholds in Settings for better analysis
- Include the confidence_note as a tooltip or expandable detail

### 3. Goal-aware analysis
Allow users to set a goal race and have AI factor it into analysis.

#### a) New settings
Add to allowed preferences in internal/auth/settings_handlers.go:
- goal_race_name (string, e.g. Oslo Marathon)
- goal_race_date (string, ISO date, e.g. 2026-09-19)
- goal_race_distance (string, e.g. marathon, half_marathon, 10k, 5k)
- goal_race_target_time (string, e.g. 3:30:00)

#### b) Include in user profile block
Update BuildUserProfileBlock in internal/training/prompt_context.go to include:
Goal Race:
- Event: Oslo Marathon (2026-09-19)
- Distance: Marathon (42.195 km)
- Target Time: 3:30:00
- Weeks Until Race: 26

#### c) AI prompt update
When a goal is set, add to the insights prompt: Consider this workout in the context of the users goal race. Is the current training appropriate for the goal? Are they on track?

#### d) Frontend goal settings (web/src/pages/Settings.tsx)
Add a Goal Race section:
- Race name text input
- Race date picker
- Distance dropdown (5K, 10K, Half Marathon, Marathon, Custom)
- Target time input (HH:MM:SS format)
- Countdown display showing weeks until race

### 4. Complete threshold settings UI (web/src/pages/Settings.tsx)
Ensure the Training Zones section (started in bead 1) is polished:
- Threshold HR input with validation (100-220 range)
- Threshold Pace input with mm:ss/km formatting and conversion
- Easy pace range inputs (min and max)
- Resting HR input with validation (30-100 range)
- Auto-detect from lactate test button: calls GET /api/lactate/tests to find latest test, extracts threshold HR and speed, populates the fields
- Visual preview: when threshold values are entered, show a computed zone table preview (call the zones calculation or compute client-side using the same percentages as the Olympiatoppen model)
- Save feedback: show success/error toast on save

### 5. Tests
- Test confidence scoring: verify prompts request confidence, verify models parse it
- Test goal-aware prompt inclusion when goal is set vs not set
- Test new settings keys are accepted (goal_race_name, goal_race_date, etc.)
- Frontend: verify Settings page renders new sections without errors

### Key files to create/modify:
- web/src/pages/TrainingDetail.tsx (richer insights display with cards)
- web/src/pages/Settings.tsx (threshold settings UI, goal race section, auto-analyze toggle)
- internal/training/models.go (confidence fields on TrainingInsights and ComparisonAnalysis)
- internal/training/insights.go (update prompt to request confidence)
- internal/training/compare_analyze_handler.go (update prompt to request confidence)
- internal/training/analysis.go (update prompt to request confidence)
- internal/training/prompt_context.go (add goal race to user profile block)
- internal/auth/settings_handlers.go (add goal_race_* settings)

title: Kids Stars: Weekly bonuses & streak system
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-stars.md

## Overview
Implement streak tracking, weekly bonus calculations, streak shields, and the visual streak flames UI (plan Sections 4.3, 5, 7.5).

## Database

Table already created in bead 1: streaks. This bead implements streak logic and weekly bonuses.

## Backend: internal/stars/streaks.go

### Streak Tracking

- **UpdateStreak(ctx, db, userID, workoutDate time.Time) error**
  Called after every workout is processed. Updates the streaks table:
  - For "daily_workout" streak type:
    - If last_activity is yesterday: increment current_count
    - If last_activity is today: no change (already counted today)
    - If last_activity is older than yesterday: reset current_count to 1 (streak broken)
    - Update longest_count if current_count exceeds it
    - Set last_activity to workoutDate

  - For "weekly_workout" streak type:
    - Track consecutive weeks with >= N workouts (N from parent config, default 3)
    - If current week has enough workouts and last qualifying week was previous week: increment
    - Otherwise: appropriate reset or maintain

- **GetStreaks(ctx, db, userID) ([]Streak, error)** — return all streak info for user

- **CheckStreakAtRisk(ctx, db, userID) (bool, error)** — check if daily streak will break tomorrow if no workout today. Used for streak warning notifications.

- **UseStreakShield(ctx, db, parentID, childID) error**
  - Parent grants a streak shield (1 per week, configurable)
  - When shield is active and no workout logged: streak is not broken
  - Track shield usage (add shield_used_at column to streaks or separate table)
  - Verify parent has shields remaining this week

### Consistency Stars (Section 4.3)

Add to star engine evaluation (extend EvaluateWorkout from bead 2):

| Criterion | Stars | Condition |
|-----------|-------|-----------|
| 3-Day Streak | 5 | current_count reaches exactly 3 (award once per streak) |
| 7-Day Warrior | 15 | current_count reaches 7 |
| 14-Day Legend | 35 | current_count reaches 14 |
| 30-Day Ironkid | 100 | current_count reaches 30 |
| Weekend Warrior | 5 | Both Saturday and Sunday have workouts this weekend |
| Early Bird | 3 | Workout started before 8:00 AM |
| Night Owl | 3 | Workout started after 8:00 PM |

Important: streak milestone stars (3-day, 7-day, etc.) should only be awarded ONCE per streak. Track with a star_transaction reason like "streak_3" with reference to streak period.

## Backend: internal/stars/weekly.go

### Weekly Bonus Evaluation (Section 5)

Function: EvaluateWeeklyBonuses(ctx, db, userID, weekEndDate) ([]StarAward, error)

Triggered either:
- On Sunday at midnight via a background goroutine/cron in the daemon, OR
- On Monday's first login/API call (check if this week's bonuses have been evaluated yet)

Weekly bonuses (all stack):

| Bonus | Stars | Condition |
|-------|-------|-----------|
| Week Complete | 10 | >= min_workouts this week (default 3, parent-configurable) |
| Active Every Day | 25 | Workout logged every day of the week |
| Distance Goal | 15 | Total weekly distance >= target (default 15km, parent-configurable) |
| Duration Goal | 15 | Total weekly duration >= target (default 3 hours, parent-configurable) |
| Improvement Bonus | 10 | Weekly distance OR duration improved over previous week |
| Streak Multiplier | x1.5 | If on 2+ week streak of hitting "Week Complete", multiply all above by 1.5 |
| Perfect Week | 50 | ALL of the above conditions met (before multiplier) |

### Parent-Configurable Weekly Targets

Store in user_preferences (as JSON, per plan Section 14):
- kids_stars_weekly_distance_target_km (default 15)
- kids_stars_weekly_duration_target_hours (default 3)
- kids_stars_weekly_min_workouts (default 3)
- kids_stars_streak_shields_per_week (default 1)

Add API endpoints:
- **GET /api/family/children/{id}/settings** — get per-child star settings
- **PUT /api/family/children/{id}/settings** — update per-child settings

These settings are stored per-child (the parent sets them for each child).

## Push Notifications

- **Streak warning**: sent at 7 PM if no workout logged today and streak is at risk
  - "Your {N}-day streak will break tomorrow if you don't work out! 🔥⚠️"
  - Implement as a check in the daemon's periodic loop or a scheduled task
  - Respect quiet hours (internal/quiethours)

- **Weekly summary to parent**: sent Monday morning at 8 AM
  - "This week: {Child1} earned {X} ⭐, ran {Y} km. {Child2} earned {X} ⭐, ran {Y} km."

## Frontend Updates

### Stars.tsx — Streak Flames Enhancement
The streak display (from bead 4) now has real data. Ensure the flame visualization works with real streak counts. Add:
- "Streak Shield" indicator if parent has granted one (show shield emoji)
- "X days!" counter with appropriate flame style

### Weekly Bonus Summary (new component on Stars.tsx)
- Show last week's bonuses earned (or "Check back Monday!" if current week)
- List each bonus achieved with star amounts
- Highlight Perfect Week with special styling if achieved

### Parent Settings (add to FamilyChildDetail.tsx)
- Section for "Weekly Targets" with editable fields:
  - Weekly distance target (km)
  - Weekly duration target (hours)
  - Minimum workouts per week
  - Streak shields per week
- Save button calls PUT /api/family/children/{id}/settings

## Tests

### streaks_test.go
- Test streak continuation (consecutive days)
- Test streak break (skip a day)
- Test streak not broken on same-day duplicate workout
- Test streak shield prevents break
- Test shield usage limit (1 per week)
- Test longest_count tracking
- Test timezone edge cases (workout at 11:59 PM vs 12:01 AM)

### weekly_test.go
- Test each weekly bonus condition independently
- Test streak multiplier calculation (1.5x)
- Test Perfect Week detection (all conditions met)
- Test with parent-configured custom targets
- Test improvement bonus (compare with previous week)
- Test weekly evaluation idempotency (don't double-award)

title: Advanced features: VO2max estimation, race predictions, auto-analysis on upload
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-ai-analysis-expansion.md

## Goal
Add differentiating features: VO2max estimation tracked over time, formula-based race predictions contextualized by AI, workout type distribution analysis, and auto-triggering AI analysis on FIT file upload.

## What to build

### 1. VO2max estimation (internal/training/vo2max.go -- new file)

#### a) Database table
CREATE TABLE vo2max_estimates (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    workout_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    estimated_vo2max REAL NOT NULL,
    method TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id),
    FOREIGN KEY (workout_id) REFERENCES workouts(id)
);

#### b) Estimation function
func EstimateVO2max(workout *Workout, maxHR int, restingHR int) *VO2maxEstimate

Use the Jack Daniels VDOT approach for running workouts:
- Only estimate from steady-state running workouts (easy runs, tempo runs) where HR and pace are relatively stable (pace CV < 8%)
- Use the formula: VO2max ~ 15.3 * (maxHR / restingHR) as a simplified estimate when resting HR is available
- Better approach: use the Daniels/Gilbert oxygen cost formula based on pace:
  - VO2 at running velocity v (m/min) = -4.60 + 0.182258 * v + 0.000104 * v^2
  - percent VO2max from fraction of max HR: use exponential formula where t = time in minutes
  - VO2max = VO2 / (percent VO2max / 100)
- Skip interval workouts (type intervals, hill_repeats) -- they do not give reliable steady-state data
- Skip short workouts (<15 minutes)
- Return the estimate with the method used (daniels or hr_ratio)

#### c) Storage functions
func SaveVO2maxEstimate(db *sql.DB, estimate *VO2maxEstimate) error
func GetVO2maxHistory(db *sql.DB, userID int64, limit int) ([]VO2maxEstimate, error)
func GetLatestVO2max(db *sql.DB, userID int64) (*VO2maxEstimate, error)

#### d) Model
type VO2maxEstimate struct {
    ID        int64
    UserID    int64
    WorkoutID int64
    Value     float64  // estimated_vo2max
    Method    string
    CreatedAt string
}

#### e) Compute on upload
After computing metrics (from bead 3), also estimate VO2max for qualifying workouts and save.

### 2. Race predictions (internal/training/race_predict.go -- new file)

#### a) Formula-based predictions
type RacePredictions struct {
    FiveK        string  // e.g. 22:15
    TenK         string
    HalfMarathon string
    Marathon     string
    BasedOn      string  // e.g. recent tempo workout on 2026-03-15
}

func PredictRaceTimes(recentVO2max float64, recentThresholdPace float64) *RacePredictions

Use the Riegel formula: T2 = T1 * (D2/D1)^1.06
- Find the users best recent threshold/tempo workout (last 3 months) as the reference performance
- Use the reference pace and distance to predict times at 5K, 10K, half marathon (21.0975km), marathon (42.195km)
- Format times as H:MM:SS or MM:SS as appropriate

Alternative: if VO2max is available, use VDOT tables to look up equivalent race times.

#### b) AI-contextualized predictions
When calling the insights API, include race predictions in the prompt so Claude can add nuance. Add the computed predictions as a section in the prompt, noting they are formula-based estimates.

### 3. Workout type distribution analysis
Add to the historical context or training load endpoint:

func GetWorkoutTypeDistribution(db *sql.DB, userID int64, weeks int) (map[string]int, error)

Query AI classification tags (ai:easy_run, ai:tempo, ai:intervals, etc.) for workouts in the last N weeks. Return counts per type. Include in AI prompts showing the distribution and comparing to 80/20 polarized recommendation.

### 4. Auto-analysis on upload (internal/training/ upload flow)
When the ai_auto_analyze preference is set to true:
- After a successful FIT file upload and workout save, automatically trigger:
  1. RunClaudeAnalysis (classification) -- already exists
  2. Insights generation (the same flow as InsightsHandler but called programmatically)
- Use a goroutine with a detached context (the pattern already exists in RunClaudeAnalysis)
- Add ai_auto_analyze to the allowed preferences in internal/auth/settings_handlers.go
- Log but do not fail the upload if AI analysis fails

You will need to extract the insights generation logic from InsightsHandler into a standalone function RunInsightsAnalysis(ctx, db, workoutID, userID) that can be called without an HTTP request.

### 5. API endpoints

#### a) GET /api/training/vo2max?limit=20
Returns VO2max history for charting. Include latest value and trend (improving/stable/declining computed from last 5 estimates).

#### b) GET /api/training/predictions
Returns current race predictions with the basis workout reference.

### 6. Frontend updates

#### a) VO2max trend chart (web/src/pages/TrainingTrends.tsx)
Line chart showing estimated VO2max over time. Add to existing trends page or the dashboard from bead 4.

#### b) Race predictions card (web/src/pages/TrainingTrends.tsx or TrainingDetail.tsx)
Card showing predicted race times for 5K, 10K, half marathon, marathon. Show the basis workout reference. Note about formula-based estimates.

#### c) Auto-analyze toggle in settings (web/src/pages/Settings.tsx)
Add toggle in the AI Preferences section: Auto-analyze workouts on upload. Saves ai_auto_analyze preference.

### 7. Tests
- internal/training/vo2max_test.go: Test estimation from a steady-state run, test intervals are skipped, test short workouts skipped, test with/without resting HR
- internal/training/race_predict_test.go: Test Riegel formula against known examples (e.g., 20:00 5K -> ~41:30 10K), test formatting of times
- Test auto-analysis triggers on upload when enabled, does not trigger when disabled

### Key files to create/modify:
- internal/training/vo2max.go (NEW)
- internal/training/vo2max_test.go (NEW)
- internal/training/race_predict.go (NEW)
- internal/training/race_predict_test.go (NEW)
- internal/training/models.go (VO2maxEstimate, RacePredictions)
- internal/training/storage.go (VO2max CRUD, workout type distribution query)
- internal/training/handlers.go (new endpoints)
- internal/training/insights.go (extract RunInsightsAnalysis standalone function)
- internal/training/prompt_context.go (add race predictions + distribution to prompt)
- internal/auth/settings_handlers.go (add ai_auto_analyze)
- Upload handler (hook in auto-analysis)
- DB migration (vo2max_estimates table)
- web/src/pages/TrainingTrends.tsx (VO2max chart, race predictions card)
- web/src/pages/Settings.tsx (auto-analyze toggle)
- Router (register new endpoints)

title: Kids Stars: Reward shop — parent config, kid claims, approval flow
description:
Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-kids-stars.md

## Overview
Implement the reward/gift shop system where parents configure rewards with star costs and kids can claim them. Includes the full claim lifecycle: claim, deduct stars, parent approve/deny, refund on denial (plan Section 6).

## Database

Tables already created in bead 1: rewards, reward_claims. This bead implements CRUD and claim logic.

## Backend: internal/family/ additions

### Reward Storage (add to storage.go or new rewards_storage.go)

- **CreateReward(parentID, title, description, starCost, iconEmoji, maxClaims) (*Reward, error)**
  - Encrypt title and description at rest (per Hytte conventions using existing AES-256-GCM helpers)
- **GetRewards(parentID) ([]Reward, error)** — all rewards by this parent
- **GetActiveRewards(parentID) ([]Reward, error)** — only is_active=1 rewards
- **UpdateReward(parentID, rewardID, fields...) error** — update title/desc/cost/emoji/active/maxClaims
- **DeleteReward(parentID, rewardID) error** — soft or hard delete (hard is fine)
- **GetRewardByID(rewardID) (*Reward, error)**

### Claim Storage

- **ClaimReward(userID, rewardID) (*RewardClaim, error)**
  - In a transaction:
    1. Check reward exists, is_active, user can afford it (check star_balances.current_balance >= star_cost)
    2. Check max_claims not exceeded (count existing non-denied claims for this reward by this user)
    3. INSERT star_transactions with negative amount (reason="reward_purchase", description="Claimed: {reward_title}")
    4. UPDATE star_balances: increment total_spent
    5. INSERT reward_claims with status="pending"
  - Return error if insufficient balance or max claims reached
  - This prevents double-spending via the transaction

- **GetClaimsByUser(userID) ([]RewardClaim, error)** — user's claim history with reward details
- **GetPendingClaims(parentID) ([]RewardClaim, error)** — all pending claims from parent's children
- **GetAllClaims(parentID) ([]RewardClaim, error)** — all claims from parent's children
- **ResolveClaim(parentID, claimID, status, parentNote) error**
  - If status="denied": refund stars (INSERT positive star_transaction with reason="reward_refund", UPDATE star_balances decrement total_spent)
  - If status="approved" or "fulfilled": just update claim status and resolved_at
  - Encrypt parent_note at rest
  - Verify the claim belongs to one of the parent's children

### API Handlers (add to handlers.go)

**Parent endpoints:**
- **GET /api/family/rewards** — list parent's reward catalog
- **POST /api/family/rewards** — create reward (body: title, description, star_cost, icon_emoji, max_claims)
- **PUT /api/family/rewards/{id}** — update reward
- **DELETE /api/family/rewards/{id}** — delete reward
- **GET /api/family/claims?status=pending** — pending claims from children (filterable by status)
- **PUT /api/family/claims/{id}** — approve/deny claim (body: status, parent_note)

**Kid endpoints (add to internal/stars/handlers.go):**
- **GET /api/stars/rewards** — available rewards (active rewards from parent, with affordability info)
  - Include: can_afford boolean, times_claimed count
- **POST /api/stars/rewards/{id}/claim** — claim a reward
- **GET /api/stars/claims** — user's claim history with status

### Suggested Default Rewards

When a parent first enables the kids_stars feature (or first visits reward management), offer to seed these defaults (from plan Section 6.1):

| Title | Cost | Emoji |
|-------|------|-------|
| Extra 30 min screen time | 50 | 🎮 |
| Choose dinner tonight | 75 | 🍕 |
| Skip one chore | 100 | 🧹 |
| Movie night pick | 80 | 🎬 |
| Ice cream outing | 120 | 🍦 |
| New book | 150 | 📚 |
| Stay up 30 min later | 60 | 🌙 |
| Friend sleepover | 300 | 🏠 |
| Special outing | 500 | 🎢 |
| $5 spending money | 200 | 💵 |

### Push Notifications

- When kid claims a reward → notify parent: "{ChildNickname} claimed '{RewardTitle}' (cost: {cost} ⭐). Approve?"
- When parent approves → notify child: "Your reward '{RewardTitle}' was approved! 🎉"
- When parent denies → notify child: "Your reward claim was denied. {cost} ⭐ have been refunded."

## Frontend: StarRewards.tsx at /stars/rewards

Kid-facing reward shop:
- Grid of reward cards showing: icon_emoji, title, star_cost, description
- Each card has "Claim" button (disabled if can't afford, with "Need X more ⭐" text)
- Already-claimed rewards show claim status badge
- Star balance displayed prominently at top
- Fun shop theme — bright colors, card hover effects

## Frontend: FamilyRewards.tsx at /family/rewards

Parent reward management:
- List of configured rewards with edit/delete actions
- "Add Reward" form: title, description, star cost, emoji picker, max claims
- "Seed Defaults" button (only shown if no rewards exist)
- **Pending Claims Section** at top with approve/deny buttons
  - Show child name, reward title, star cost, claim date
  - Approve button (green), Deny button (red) with note input
- Claim history table below

## Frontend: Sidebar Enhancement
- Add pending claims badge count on "Family" sidebar item (red dot with number)
- Poll or check on page load for pending claim count

## Routes
Add in App.tsx: /stars/rewards, /family/rewards

## Tests

### Backend Tests
- Test ClaimReward happy path: stars deducted, claim created, balance updated
- Test ClaimReward insufficient balance: returns error, no side effects
- Test ClaimReward max claims exceeded: returns error
- Test double-claim prevention (race condition — transaction should prevent)
- Test ResolveClaim approve: status updated, stars NOT refunded
- Test ResolveClaim deny: status updated, stars refunded, balance corrected
- Test authorization: parent can only manage their own rewards
- Test authorization: parent can only resolve claims from their own children
- Test encryption of title, description, parent_note fields
