title: Family Chat: attachments (photos + files)
description:
Part of the Family Chat chain seeded from Suggestions id=145. See that suggestion's plan in the Suggestions page for the full architecture, trust model, and phase plan. Trust model: at-rest encryption with the server holding the key (same as Notes), NOT Signal-style E2EE.

This bead is step 6/7: Attachments of the chain.

## Scope

Add photo + file attachments to messages.

### Backend

- New endpoint POST /api/familychat/conversations/{id}/upload accepts multipart form upload. Limits: 10 MB per file, only the following MIME types accepted: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/heic, image/heif, application/pdf, audio/mpeg, audio/mp4. Reject everything else with 400.
- Store on disk under `<UPLOAD_ROOT>/familychat/<conversation_id>/<uuid>`; persist the relative path encrypted (`attachment_path_enc` in `family_chat_messages`).
- Returns `{ upload_id, mime, size }`. The frontend then includes this in the subsequent POST /messages body.
- New endpoint GET /api/familychat/conversations/{id}/attachments/{message_id} streams the file with the appropriate content-type, membership-checked. Decrypts the stored path, opens the file, copies to response. No Range support needed initially.

### Frontend

- Composer adds a paperclip button → file picker. Selected file uploads first, gets an upload_id, then send button POSTs the message including the attachment reference.
- Inline rendering in the message bubble:
    - Images: thumbnail with click-to-enlarge in a lightbox.
    - Audio: audio element with play controls.
    - Other (PDF etc): download link with filename + size.

### Tests

- Upload handler: size limit enforced; bad MIME rejected; success persists the row.
- Attachment GET: membership check enforced; 404 for non-members.
- Frontend: upload-then-send round-trip rendering an image bubble.

### Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` pass.
- Sending an image from a phone and viewing it in another tab works end-to-end.
- A 12 MB file is rejected with a clear error toast.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

title: Family Chat: schema, feature flag, minimal store
description:
Part of the Family Chat chain seeded from Suggestions id=145. See that suggestion's plan in the Suggestions page for the full architecture, trust model, and phase plan. Trust model: at-rest encryption with the server holding the key (same as Notes), NOT Signal-style E2EE.

This bead is step 1/7: Schema + store of the chain.

## Scope

### Schema (internal/db/db.go createSchema)

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS family_chat_conversations (
      id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      name_enc    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
      owner_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      created_at  TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
      updated_at  TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS family_chat_members (
      conversation_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES family_chat_conversations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      role            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member',
      joined_at       TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
      last_read_at    TIMESTAMP,
      PRIMARY KEY (conversation_id, user_id)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS family_chat_messages (
      id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      conversation_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES family_chat_conversations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      sender_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      body_enc        TEXT NOT NULL,
      attachment_path_enc TEXT,
      attachment_mime TEXT,
      sent_at         TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
    );
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_family_chat_messages_conv ON family_chat_messages(conversation_id, sent_at);

### Feature flag

Add `"family_chat": false` to `FeatureDefaults` in `internal/auth/features.go:11`.

### Package internal/familychat/

- `models.go`: Conversation, Member, Message structs + DB helpers using `encryption.EncryptField` / `DecryptField` for `name_enc`, `body_enc`, `attachment_path_enc`. Mirror `internal/notes/` pattern.
- `store.go`: helper functions only, no HTTP yet:
    - CreateConversation(ctx, db, ownerID, name, memberIDs)
    - ListUserConversations(ctx, db, userID)
    - GetConversation(ctx, db, conversationID, askerID) — enforces membership
    - AppendMessage(ctx, db, conversationID, senderID, body, attachmentPath, attachmentMime)
    - ListMessages(ctx, db, conversationID, askerID, sinceID int64, limit int)
    - MarkRead(ctx, db, conversationID, userID, ts)
- All free text encrypted at write, decrypted at read; cross-user / non-member reads return ErrForbidden.

### Tests

- `store_test.go` with `:memory:` SQLite. Round-trip encryption verified. Membership enforcement covered.

### Non-goals (NOT in this bead)

- HTTP handlers — next chain link.
- SSE / webpush — later links.
- Frontend — later links.
- Group conversations of arbitrary size; design supports it but acceptance only covers 1:1 today.

### Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test ./internal/familychat/...` pass.
- Schema applied at startup.
- `SELECT body_enc FROM family_chat_messages` returns ciphertext.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).


## Cleanup note (added after the chain was re-planned)

Hytte-3bk5 (SSE stream, 7-link Family Chat chain step 3/7) merged out of order before this schema bead ran. The smith for 3bk5 anticipated this and added defensive code in `internal/familychat/membership.go`: the `DefaultMembership` function queries `family_chat_members` and treats query errors as "not a member" so the SSE handler returns 404 instead of crashing when the table is missing.

Once this bead lands and the schema exists, that defensive fallback becomes redundant and obscures real errors. Remove it: a query error AFTER the schema exists is a genuine bug, not a missing-table case. The function should return the error to the caller, who logs and decides whether to treat as 404 or 500. Also update the comment block above `DefaultMembership` to drop the "if it does not exist yet" sentence — the table is now part of the canonical schema.

title: Family Chat: SSE live-delivery stream
description:
Part of the Family Chat chain seeded from Suggestions id=145. See that suggestion's plan in the Suggestions page for the full architecture, trust model, and phase plan. Trust model: at-rest encryption with the server holding the key (same as Notes), NOT Signal-style E2EE.

This bead is step 3/7: SSE stream of the chain.

## Scope

Server-Sent Events endpoint for live message delivery.

### Endpoint

`GET /api/familychat/conversations/{id}/stream` returns `text/event-stream`. The client subscribes once; the server pushes events as new messages arrive.

Event shapes:
- `message_new` { message: <full Message JSON> } — fired on POST /messages success.
- `message_deleted` { message_id } — for future delete operations (don't implement delete itself yet; just emit the type).
- `read_receipt` { user_id, at } — fired on POST /read.

### Implementation

- In `internal/familychat/`, add a `hub.go` with an in-memory pub/sub keyed by conversation_id. Each subscriber holds a buffered channel. On POST /messages, the handler publishes to the hub AFTER persisting the row.
- The SSE handler subscribes the client's HTTP response writer, holds the connection open, writes events as they arrive, sends `:` heartbeat every 30s.
- On client disconnect (ctx cancellation), unsubscribe and free the channel.
- Membership re-verified at subscribe time. Non-members get 404.

Reference: `internal/forge/handlers.go` already has an SSE activity stream — mirror that pattern. Don't reuse the Forge hub; build a sibling for family chat.

### Tests

- `hub_test.go` with two subscribers + a publisher; both receive the event.
- Disconnect cleanup verified (no goroutine leak).
- E2E test using `httptest` + `server.URL` confirming an SSE client receives a message_new event after a POST /messages call.

### Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test ./internal/familychat/...` pass.
- A second browser tab to the same conversation sees new messages within milliseconds of POST.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

title: Family Chat: REST handlers
description:
Part of the Family Chat chain seeded from Suggestions id=145. See that suggestion's plan in the Suggestions page for the full architecture, trust model, and phase plan. Trust model: at-rest encryption with the server holding the key (same as Notes), NOT Signal-style E2EE.

This bead is step 2/7: REST handlers of the chain.

## Scope

REST handlers under `/api/familychat/...` wired in `internal/api/router.go` inside `RequireAuth` + `RequireFeature(db, "family_chat")`:

- GET /api/familychat/conversations → list current user's conversations (id, name, last_message_at, unread_count, member ids).
- POST /api/familychat/conversations → body { name, member_user_ids } → create + return.
- GET /api/familychat/conversations/{id} → full conversation metadata.
- GET /api/familychat/conversations/{id}/messages?since=<id>&limit=<n> → list messages, default newest first; `since` for incremental loads.
- POST /api/familychat/conversations/{id}/messages → body { body, attachment_path?, attachment_mime? } → append; returns the inserted message decrypted.
- POST /api/familychat/conversations/{id}/read → body { at: timestamp } → updates `last_read_at`.
- DELETE /api/familychat/conversations/{id} → owner only; cascade-delete.

Member auth: every endpoint resolves membership via `family_chat_members`; non-members get 404 (not 403, to avoid leaking existence).

### Tests

- `handlers_test.go` covers each handler success + cross-user 404 path.
- Pagination `since` + `limit` works correctly across multiple pages.

### Acceptance

- All endpoints behind feature flag.
- 404 for non-members, 200 for members.
- `make build`, `make test ./internal/familychat/...` pass.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

title: Tasks: frontend page + routing + sidebar + i18n
description:
Part of the Task list chain seeded from Suggestions id=141 (step 2/2: Frontend). See that suggestion's plan for the full design.

Frontend slice of the Tasks page. Depends on the backend that landed in the previous chain link. See Suggestions id=141 plan for full design.

## Scope

### web/src/pages/Tasks.tsx

- Top: text input "What needs doing?" + tag chips (built-in: work, personal; custom tags entered free-text). Submit creates the task.
- List view: tasks rendered as cards. Each card shows title, created/updated timestamps (relative + tooltip absolute), tag chips, "+N notes" badge.
- Expand a card to reveal: body editor, notes list (chronological), add-note composer, "Archive" / "Unarchive" toggle.
- "Show archived" toggle at the top of the list. Archived tasks rendered with a dim style.
- Tag filter chips: click a tag chip to filter the visible list.
- All API calls via plain `fetch` to /api/tasks endpoints with credentials: 'include'.
- Mobile-first per CLAUDE.md — single column at 375px, tag chips wrap.

### Routing + Sidebar

- Add `/tasks` route in `web/src/App.tsx` inside `<ProtectedRoute>`.
- Sidebar entry in `web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` using `ListTodo` Lucide icon, gated on the `tasks` user_features flag (same pattern as other feature-gated nav entries).

### i18n

- New namespace `tasks` in `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/tasks.json`.
- Keys: page title, input placeholder, tag-chip labels (built-in work / personal), archive toggle, archived label, empty state, note composer placeholder, timestamps formatting.
- `Intl.DateTimeFormat` uses `i18n.language` per CLAUDE.md convention.

### Tests

- `Tasks.test.tsx`: render with mocked fetch responses. Cover create flow, tag attach, archive toggle, note add, filter chip, archive-visible toggle.

### Acceptance

- `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test -- --run src/pages/Tasks` pass.
- User with the `tasks` flag can: create a task, tag it, add notes, archive it, see it under "Show archived". User without the flag has no nav entry.
- Mobile at 375px works (no horizontal scroll).
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

title: Tasks: schema + backend handlers + feature flag
description:
Part of the Task list chain seeded from Suggestions id=141 (step 1/2: Backend foundation). See that suggestion's plan for the full design.

Backend foundation for the Tasks page. See Suggestions id=141 in the Suggestions page for the full plan; this slice is the schema + handlers + feature flag (no frontend).

## Scope

### Schema (internal/db/db.go createSchema)

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS tasks (
      id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      user_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      title_enc     TEXT NOT NULL,
      body_enc      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
      archived      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      created_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
      updated_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
      archived_at   TIMESTAMP
    );
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_user_archived ON tasks(user_id, archived);

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_tags (
      id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      label_enc  TEXT NOT NULL,        -- encrypted (so custom tag names stay private)
      UNIQUE(user_id, label_enc)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_tag_assignments (
      task_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      tag_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES task_tags(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      PRIMARY KEY (task_id, tag_id)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS task_notes (
      id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      task_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      content_enc TEXT NOT NULL,
      created_at  TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
    );
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_task_notes_task ON task_notes(task_id, created_at);

### Feature flag

Add `"tasks": false` to `FeatureDefaults` in `internal/auth/features.go:11`.

### Package internal/tasks/

- `models.go`: Task, TaskTag, TaskNote structs + DB helpers using `encryption.EncryptField` / `DecryptField` for title/body/tag label/note content. Mirror the pattern in `internal/notes/`.
- `handlers.go`:
    - GET /api/tasks?archived=<bool> → list user's tasks with tags and note counts.
    - POST /api/tasks → body { title, body?, tags? } → create.
    - PATCH /api/tasks/{id} → body { title?, body?, tags?, archived? } → update.
    - DELETE /api/tasks/{id} → delete cascade.
    - POST /api/tasks/{id}/notes → body { content } → add a note.
    - DELETE /api/tasks/{id}/notes/{note_id} → remove note.
- All endpoints gated by `auth.RequireFeature(db, "tasks")` and `RequireAuth`.

### Tests

- `handlers_test.go` with `:memory:` SQLite. Cover: create, list (active vs archived), update, archive/unarchive, notes CRUD, tag reuse across tasks, cross-user isolation (user A cannot see/mutate user B's tasks).
- Round-trip encryption: SELECT directly against the DB shows ciphertext, GetTask returns plaintext.

### Wiring

Register routes in `internal/api/router.go` inside the existing `RequireAuth` group, wrapped in `RequireFeature(db, "tasks")`.

### Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `go vet ./...` pass.
- Hitting any /api/tasks endpoint without the feature flag returns 403.
- Hitting with the flag works as specified above.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

title: Stride: remove Previous week feedback section
description:
The Stride page has a "Previous week feedback" section above the weekly history list (search for `plan.previousWeekFeedback` around line 1601 in `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx`). With Hytte-xg7v shipped, every previous week is now accessible as a summary row that opens a modal with full details — including the same feedback the standalone section was showing. The section is redundant and visually competes with the new list.

## Change

In `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx`:
- Remove the JSX block that renders the `plan.previousWeekFeedback` section.
- Remove any state, fetches, or memoised computations that only fed that block. If a fetch is shared with the modal/list, leave it.
- Drop the now-unused i18n keys: `plan.previousWeekFeedback` and any helper strings that only render inside this section. Update en/nb/th.

## Tests

- Update `StridePage.test.tsx` to drop assertions that look for the previous-week-feedback block.
- Add a smoke assertion that the section is NOT in the DOM (regression guard).

## Acceptance

- `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test -- --run src/pages/StridePage` all pass.
- The Stride page no longer renders the standalone previous-week-feedback section.
- Clicking a row in the weekly history list still opens the modal that includes the previous week's plan + feedback.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Removed).

## Reference

- web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx:1601 — current section.
- Hytte-xg7v — paginated week list with modal; the feature this redundancy follows from.

title: Frontend: dense summary rows + Load more pagination on StridePage
description: Modify web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx (current fetch at line 359, week-list rendering from line 447). Replace the fixed limit=12 fetch with paged loads (offset 0, limit 12) consuming the new backend response. Replace large cards with dense summary rows showing: week label + completion % chip, total distance (km, 1 decimal), total moving time (hh:mm), a horizontal proportional zone-split bar (green=easy, yellow=threshold, red=hard, reusing the TrainingPage zone palette) with hover/focus tooltip showing percentages, and a chevron 'Open' affordance. Append a 'Load older weeks' button at the bottom when has_more is true; clicking fetches the next page and appends results. Show inline spinner during fetch and inline error+retry on failure. Mobile-first: rows must wrap cleanly at 375px. Add the listed i18n keys (history.week.totalDistance, totalTime, zoneSplit, openAria, loadMore, loadingMore) to the stride namespace in en/nb/th. Tests in StridePage.test.tsx cover: rows render with new summary shape, Load more loads next page, Load more disappears when has_more=false. Depends on sub-task 1 for response shape; row click handler is wired in sub-task 3.

title: Frontend: click-to-expand week details modal
description: Add a modal to StridePage that opens when a summary row is clicked, mirroring the existing rich card layout. Reuse the existing modal pattern in the app (e.g. BeadDetailModal or the modal pattern referenced in web/src/pages/BudgetCharts.tsx) rather than building a new primitive. Modal contents: week-of header + completion %, day-by-day evaluation list (verdict, critical flags, coach notes, the workout-context summary panel from Hytte-ajpg if context exists), the existing plan.previousWeekFeedback block, and the generated plan for that week if available. Include a Close button and Escape key handling; on mobile the modal should be full-screen. Add i18n keys history.weekModal.title and history.weekModal.close to en/nb/th stride namespace. Tests in StridePage.test.tsx cover: clicking a row opens the modal with the expected day-by-day content. Add a changelog fragment under changelog.d/ (category: Changed) summarising the paginated rows + modal experience. Depends on sub-task 2 for the row click wiring; consumes the same week payload already fetched there (no additional endpoint).

title: Backend: paginate /api/stride/history with offset and zone-split fields
description: Modify internal/stride/handlers.go (around line 177) to extend GET /api/stride/history. Add `offset` query param (default 0), keep `limit` (default unchanged) but cap server-side at 24. Cap pagination depth at 156 weeks; beyond that return has_more=false. Extend the response struct to include `has_more bool` (or `next_cursor`) and per-week zone fields (`easy_seconds`, `threshold_seconds`, `hard_seconds`) — first search the existing response struct and the zone-distribution helpers in internal/hrzones/ and internal/training/ to reuse existing field names if present. Default behaviour without offset must preserve current response shape (additive only). Add tests in internal/stride/handlers_test.go covering: offset pagination correctness, has_more=true when more rows exist, has_more=false at the end, server-side limit cap clamps oversized requests, and depth cap behaviour. This sub-task is the data foundation for the frontend sub-tasks; the field names chosen here (zone seconds + has_more shape) must be agreed before sub-task 2/3 can render them.

title: Stride: remove the nightly evaluation cron entirely
description:
Follow-up to Hytte-bn8s. With the on-save trigger covering the normal flow and a per-day Re-run Evaluation button available in the Stride UI, the nightly cron is redundant — it just re-evaluates dates that already have evaluations, burning Claude tokens for no incremental signal. Remove it.

## Backend changes

In `cmd/server/main.go` (around line 148 onwards):
- Delete the goroutine that calls `stride.NextNightlyEvaluationRun` / `stride.RunNightlyEvaluation`.
- Remove any state derived only by that goroutine (the `evalHTTPClient` if unused elsewhere — verify with grep before deleting).

In `internal/stride/evaluate.go`:
- `RunNightlyEvaluation` and `NextNightlyEvaluationRun` lose their only caller. Decide:
    - **Option A**: keep them as exported helpers, since `ReEvaluateDate` already exists for per-day work and these add nothing. Probably worth deleting for cleanliness.
    - **Option B**: leave them in case someone re-introduces a scheduler later. Adds dead-code smell.
- Recommend **Option A** — delete both functions and their tests. The per-day code path (`ReEvaluateDate`, `EvaluateWorkout`) carries all the actual evaluation logic and stays.
- If `RunNightlyEvaluation` has shared helpers worth keeping (e.g. user enumeration, date computation), promote them into `ReEvaluateDate` or a small shared helper rather than orphaning them.

The on-save trigger from Hytte-bn8s and the existing manual Re-run Evaluation button are the only two ways evaluations should now fire.

## Tests

- Remove tests that exclusively cover `RunNightlyEvaluation` / `NextNightlyEvaluationRun`.
- If any of those tests covered evaluation logic still in use, move/rewrite them to drive `ReEvaluateDate` directly.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- No goroutine in main.go runs a stride evaluation timer.
- Saving the context modal still produces an evaluation (Hytte-bn8s path intact).
- The Re-run Evaluation button on a per-day card still works.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Removed).

## Non-goals

- Adding new UI affordances. The Re-run button stays as-is.
- Re-evaluating retroactively (the nightly used to back-fill dates with newly added Coach Notes — the user now re-runs manually).

## Sequencing

`blocks-on:Hytte-bn8s` so we don't pull out the fallback before the replacement path is verified merged.

## Reference

- cmd/server/main.go:148–175 — current nightly scheduler goroutine.
- internal/stride/evaluate.go:48–95 — NextNightlyEvaluationRun, RunNightlyEvaluation.
- internal/stride/evaluate.go:308 — ReEvaluateDate, the per-day entry that stays.

title: Stride: paginated week list with summary rows and click-to-expand modal
description:
The Stride page currently fetches the last 12 weeks via `GET /api/stride/history?limit=12` and renders each as a card. The cards are large enough that 12 of them is already a long scroll, and 12 weeks is an arbitrary cap that hides older history.

Replace with paginated **summary rows** + **click-to-expand modal** showing the full week details. Rows are dense; details live in a modal.

## Summary-row content

Each row shows for the week:
- Week label (e.g. "Week of May 5") + completion percentage chip (kept from today).
- Total distance (km, one decimal).
- Total moving time (hh:mm format).
- Zone split as a single horizontal bar: green for easy/slow, yellow for threshold/tempo, red for hard/intervals — proportional widths reflecting time spent. Hover/focus shows the percentages numerically.
- Small "Open" affordance (chevron or icon) signaling the row is clickable.

If the zone split data isn't already on the history endpoint, extend the response to include `easy_seconds`, `threshold_seconds`, `hard_seconds` (or equivalent already-existing fields — search `internal/stride/handlers.go`'s response struct and the zone-distribution helpers in `internal/hrzones/` / `internal/training/`).

Use the existing color palette from the TrainingPage zone visualisations for consistency.

## Modal content (click-to-expand)

The modal shows the full week, mirroring the existing rich card layout:
- Week-of header + completion percentage.
- Day-by-day evaluation list (verdict, critical flags, coach notes, the workout-context summary panel from Hytte-ajpg).
- Previous-week feedback section (the existing "plan.previousWeekFeedback" block).
- The generated plan for that week if available.
- Close button + keyboard escape.

Use the existing modal pattern in the app (e.g. BeadDetailModal or similar) rather than a new component primitive.

## Pagination

Backend:
- Extend `GET /api/stride/history` to accept `offset` (or `cursor`) in addition to `limit`. Default behaviour without offset returns the same response shape as today.
- Add a `has_more` boolean (or `next_cursor`) to the response so the frontend knows whether to render the "Load more" affordance.
- Hard cap `limit` server-side at, say, 24 to keep payloads bounded.
- Cap pagination depth at something sensible (e.g. 156 weeks ≈ 3 years) — beyond that, return `has_more=false`.

Frontend:
- Replace the fixed `limit=12` fetch with paged loads, starting at offset 0, limit 12 (or the existing default).
- "Load older weeks" button at the bottom of the list when `has_more` is true. Click loads the next page and appends.
- Show a small loading spinner during the fetch. Errors render inline with a retry.

## i18n

Add to the stride namespace in en/nb/th:
- `history.week.totalDistance` — "{{km}} km"
- `history.week.totalTime` — "{{hours}}h {{minutes}}m"
- `history.week.zoneSplit` — "{{easy}}% easy · {{threshold}}% threshold · {{hard}}% hard"
- `history.week.openAria` — "Open details for week of {{date}}"
- `history.loadMore` — "Load older weeks"
- `history.loadingMore` — "Loading…"
- `history.weekModal.title` — "Week of {{date}}"
- `history.weekModal.close` — "Close"

## Tests

- Backend: `internal/stride/handlers_test.go` covers offset pagination, `has_more` true/false, and the server-side limit cap.
- Frontend: `StridePage.test.tsx` covers (a) rows render with the new summary shape, (b) clicking a row opens the modal with the expected day-by-day content, (c) Load more loads the next page, (d) Load more disappears when `has_more` is false.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Stride page initially shows 12 dense rows. Clicking any row opens a modal with full week details.
- "Load older weeks" appears when older history exists; clicking loads more.
- Older weeks are accessible (no hard 12-week cap).
- Mobile-first: rows wrap cleanly at 375px; modal is full-screen on mobile.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Non-goals

- Migrating away from offset to cursor pagination. Offset is fine at this scale.
- Sorting or filtering by zone composition. Default order (newest first) is enough.
- Bulk actions on weeks.
- Charts of weekly totals over time. Could be a follow-up.

## Reference

- internal/stride/handlers.go:177 — current `GET /api/stride/history` handler.
- web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx:359 — current fetch; line 447 onwards — week-list rendering.
- internal/hrzones/ — zone time aggregation (likely the source for the zone split data).
- web/src/pages/BudgetCharts.tsx or similar — existing modal pattern to mirror.
- Hytte-ajpg — workout_context inline panel; the modal must include this panel for each day if context exists.

title: Stride: collapse Coach Notes history
description:
The Coach Notes section on the Stride page now lists every note ever written. With weeks of accumulated notes, the section is long enough that the user has to scroll past it to see last week's plan + feedback. Collapse the history under a toggle so the section stays compact by default.

## Change

In `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx` (around line 1424, the Coach Notes section):

- Split the notes into two buckets: "active" (notes from the current week — same week-anchor logic used elsewhere on the Stride page) and "older" (everything else).
- Render active notes inline as today.
- Render older notes under a collapsed `<details>` (or equivalent React-Aria collapsible) with a header like "Older notes ({{count}})". Collapsed by default.
- Keep the create-note textarea + form at the top of the section unchanged.
- Preserve all existing note actions (edit, delete, consumed-by indicator) inside the collapsed group.

Per-section expand state lives in component state, no localStorage.

If "current week" is the wrong cutoff and "last 7 days from today" reads better, use that instead — pick whichever lines up cleanest with the existing date helpers in the file. Both are acceptable.

## i18n

Add to the existing stride namespace in en/nb/th:
- `notes.olderNotes` — "Older notes ({{count}})" / "Eldre notater ({{count}})" / Thai
- `notes.toggleOlderAria` — "Toggle older notes"

## Tests

Update `StridePage.test.tsx`:
- Active notes (current week) render inline.
- Older notes are collapsed; clicking the header expands them.
- Counts match.
- Editing/deleting works in both buckets.

## Acceptance

- `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test -- --run src/pages/StridePage` all pass.
- Coach Notes section is short enough that last week's plan + feedback is visible without scrolling past acres of history.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Reference

- web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx:1424 — Coach Notes section.
- Existing week-boundary helpers in the same file (look near the history/plan rendering for the current convention).

title: Stride: trigger date evaluation on workout_context save
description:
Today Stride's nightly evaluation runs at 03:00 Europe/Oslo. The original reason was to give the user time to type Coach Notes before evaluation. After Hytte-ajpg (workout_context surfaced as the inline coach-saw panel) and Hytte-3m54 (analysis runs on save), the workout-context modal IS the coach-input mechanism. We can run the evaluation immediately when the modal is saved and stop waiting for nightly.

The nightly cron should NOT be removed — it stays as a fallback for any workout where context is never saved.

## Backend

In the workout_context save handler (search for `UpsertWorkoutContext` callers in `internal/training/handlers.go` — same place Hytte-3m54 touched):

After successfully persisting the context AND after kicking off the existing AI-summary / Insights goroutines from Hytte-3m54, also spawn a goroutine that:

1. Resolves the workout's date in Europe/Oslo (use the workout's `started_at` converted to the Oslo TZ).
2. Calls `stride.ReEvaluateDate(ctx, db, httpClient, userID, date)` — this is the existing per-date entry that the nightly path uses internally.
3. Logs errors but does NOT bubble them up — the HTTP save succeeded regardless.
4. Respects the same gating as nightly: skip if the user does not have `stride_enabled` preference, or if Claude is disabled. Same gating shape as the AI-summary trigger from 3m54.
5. Uses `context.Background()` with a 5-minute timeout so a client disconnect cannot kill the eval mid-flight.

If the date already has a completed evaluation, `ReEvaluateDate` already handles re-runs (the user can also force it by adding a Coach Note and clicking re-run from the UI today). That semantic stays.

## Frontend

The Stride page already polls and renders evaluation results when present. After the context modal save resolves, the user should land on a freshly evaluated day. No new UI surfaces are required, but:

- If the existing pending/in-flight state for evaluations isn't surfaced (today's nightly path is invisible to the UI until completion), add a small toast or inline indicator on the Stride evaluation card for that day: "Coach is evaluating…" / Norwegian / Thai. Dismiss when the evaluation row appears.
- If implementing the toast is non-trivial, defer it — the user will see the evaluation when they next refresh.

## Tests

- `internal/training/handlers_test.go`: extend the workout_context save test to assert the stride trigger fires (mock `ReEvaluateDate` via a package-variable seam, mirroring the `runPromptFn` pattern in chat / suggestions / training).
- Gating tests: when `stride_enabled` is false, ReEvaluateDate is NOT called. When Claude is disabled, NOT called.
- Failure path: ReEvaluateDate returns an error → save handler still returns 200; the error is logged.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Save the context modal for a fresh workout → within ~2 minutes the Stride page shows an evaluation for that date without needing to wait for nightly.
- Nightly cron still runs and still evaluates dates that were not covered by the immediate-save path (workouts where context was never saved).
- A second context save for the same workout re-runs the evaluation (existing ReEvaluateDate semantics preserved).
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Non-goals

- Removing the nightly cron. Stays as a fallback.
- A UI button to manually trigger evaluation. The save action is the trigger.
- Real-time progress streaming. Polling / refresh is enough; the existing page reload pattern works.

## Reference

- internal/training/handlers.go — workout_context save handler (UpsertWorkoutContext caller); already extended by Hytte-3m54 with the AI-summary trigger.
- internal/stride/evaluate.go:308 — `ReEvaluateDate(ctx, db, httpClient, userID, date) (int, error)`.
- internal/stride/evaluate.go:148 onwards — the nightly scheduler in cmd/server/main.go for reference gating.
- internal/training/models.go — workout `started_at` shape for date extraction.

title: Stride: show the workout_context summary inline in the evaluation card
description:
The workout_context the user fills in on the Training detail modal IS fed into Stride's nightly evaluation prompt today (`internal/stride/evaluate.go:674` calls `appendWorkoutContextNote`, which loads the row and formats it via `training.FormatWorkoutContextNote` into a synthetic Note for that evaluation). But the synthetic note is constructed in-memory only — it never appears in the Coach Notes UI, so the user can't see what data informed the coach's evaluation.

This bead surfaces that data inline on the Stride evaluation card, so the user sees "this is what the coach saw for this day" alongside the evaluation itself.

## Backend

Add the workout_context summary to the evaluation response payload so the frontend can render it without a second fetch.

- In whatever struct populates the evaluation list response (likely `internal/stride/handlers.go` and `internal/stride/models.go` — search for the type that carries `Verdict`, `CriticalFlags`, `CoachNotes`, etc.), add a new field `WorkoutContextSummary string` (or `*string` if you prefer absent vs empty distinction).
- Populate it in the handler that returns evaluations: for each evaluation that links to a workout, call `training.GetWorkoutContext(db, workoutID)` and `training.FormatWorkoutContextNote(wctx)`. On `sql.ErrNoRows`, leave the field empty/nil.
- Treat decryption failures the same as the rest of the codebase (log + skip).
- Don't modify the evaluation prompt path — the synthetic-note injection in `appendWorkoutContextNote` stays as-is; this bead only adds a presentation channel.

If multiple evaluations share a single workout, they all get the same summary string — that's expected.

## Frontend

In `web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx`, on the evaluation card:

- When the new `workout_context_summary` field is non-empty, render a small read-only panel under the evaluation body (above or below CoachNotes — pick whichever reads better in the current layout). Title via i18n: "What coach saw for this day" / "Det treneren så for denne dagen" / Thai equivalent.
- Render the summary as plain text or simple markdown — no editing, no actions.
- If empty/null, omit the panel entirely (no "no context saved" placeholder; the absence of a saved modal is the absence of the panel).

i18n keys under the existing stride namespace:
- `evaluation.contextPanelTitle`
- (no other strings; the panel body is the formatted summary itself, generated by `FormatWorkoutContextNote`)

## Tests

- Backend: extend the evaluation list handler test to assert the new field is populated when context exists, empty when it doesn't.
- Frontend: small RTL test that the panel renders when the field is non-empty and is absent when null.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Open Stride for a date with a saved workout context: the evaluation card shows the new panel with the summary text.
- A date without saved context: panel absent, layout unchanged from today.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

## Non-goals

- Persisting the context summary as a real Note row. Considered and rejected — it would create dual-edit semantics and stale-duplication risk.
- Editing context from the Stride page. Edit lives in the Training detail modal; Stride just mirrors.
- Surfacing context in weekly plan generation cards. Could be a follow-up; nightly evaluation is the primary signal.

## Reference

- internal/stride/evaluate.go:996–1021 — `appendWorkoutContextNote` shows the existing synthesis path and the call into `FormatWorkoutContextNote`.
- internal/stride/handlers.go — the GET handler for evaluations (search for the response struct that carries the verdict).
- internal/training/models.go — `GetWorkoutContext` and `FormatWorkoutContextNote` are the helpers to reuse.
- web/src/pages/StridePage.tsx:1424 onwards — current Coach Notes section; the new panel sits near the evaluation card, NOT inside the user-authored notes list.

title: Training: Analyze button should also produce Insights, not just tag analysis
description:
The Analyze button on the workout detail page currently only triggers the "tag" analysis (`RunClaudeAnalysis`). The richer Insights output (TrendCard, EffortSummary, RiskFlagsSection, SuggestionsCard, ConfidenceIndicator, threshold context) is produced by `RunInsightsAnalysis`, which only runs at FIT upload time (and only when `ai_auto_analyze` is on). There is no UI button that triggers Insights manually.

Result: a user who fills the workout-context modal *after* upload and clicks Analyze gets only the small AI Analysis output, missing the rest. Hytte-3m54 fixes the auto-on-save path; this bead fixes the manual button so a single Analyze click also produces the rich output.

## Backend change

In `internal/training/analysis_handler.go` `AnalyzeHandler`, after the existing `RunClaudeAnalysis` succeeds and analysis_status is set to 'completed':

- Run `RunInsightsAnalysis` synchronously (or under the same `claudeSemaphore` if cost-bound). Errors are logged but should NOT fail the response — the tag analysis succeeded and that's the bare minimum.
- Gate Insights on the `claude_ai` feature flag, NOT on the `ai_auto_analyze` preference. The user clicked Analyze; that's the explicit consent. `ai_auto_analyze` continues to gate the upload-time auto-trigger, where consent is implicit.
- Reuse `ErrInsightsAlreadyCached` semantics — if Insights is already cached, skip silently (don't re-bill the user for a cached result).

The response shape (`{ analysis, cached }`) stays the same so the existing frontend keeps working. The frontend's load-time GET `/api/training/workouts/{id}/insights` will pick up the freshly stored Insights row on its next fetch (the detail page already re-fetches on analysis_status transitions).

## Frontend tweak

The Analyze button's `runAnalysis` flow already calls `/analyze`, then refetches the workout. Add a refetch of `/insights` after the analyze call resolves so the new Insights data appears without a page reload:

    // After setAnalysis(data.analysis) in TrainingDetail.tsx around line 278:
    const iRes = await fetch(`/api/training/workouts/${id}/insights`, { credentials: 'include' })
    if (iRes.ok) {
      const iData = await iRes.json()
      setInsights(iData.insights || null)
    }

The existing pending-status polling effect (line ~244) also handles the refresh; verify nothing duplicates fetches.

## Tests

- `analysis_handler_test.go`: add a test that mocks both `RunClaudeAnalysis` and `RunInsightsAnalysis` (introduce a seam similar to `runPromptFn` if needed) and asserts both ran when AnalyzeHandler completed successfully.
- A test for the Insights-failure path: tag succeeds, insights fails — response is still 200 and contains the tag analysis; the failure is logged.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Click Analyze on a workout with no prior analysis: tag analysis appears AND TrendCard / SuggestionsCard / RiskFlagsSection / EffortSummary appear.
- Click Analyze again on the same workout: returns the cached results, no extra Claude spend.
- Frontend renders the freshly stored Insights without requiring a page reload.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Reference

- internal/training/analysis_handler.go — AnalyzeHandler (extend after the existing RunClaudeAnalysis success branch).
- internal/training/insights.go — RunInsightsAnalysis, ErrInsightsAlreadyCached, GetCachedInsightsHandler / InsightsHandler.
- internal/training/handlers.go:264–296 — the upload-time goroutine that runs both kinds; mirror its gating logic where applicable.
- web/src/pages/TrainingDetail.tsx:258–294 — runAnalysis function; insert insights refetch after the existing analysis load.

## Sequencing

This bead is `blocks-on:Hytte-3m54` so it lands on top of the auto-on-save changes already in flight. forgeReady will be added separately once 3m54 merges.

description: Backend-only change in internal/training/handlers.go. Locate the UpsertWorkoutContext handler (POST/PUT for workout_context) and, after successful persistence, spawn the same auto-analysis goroutine pattern used at FIT upload time around handlers.go:264. title: Training: auto-run AI summary on context save, add long/tempo run types, mins+secs duration
description:
Three improvements to the WorkoutContextModal flow on the Training detail page. Forge can decompose if it wants — they touch overlapping files.

## 1. Auto-run AI summary when the context modal is saved

Today: filling out the modal saves the workout context, but the AI summary still requires a manual click on the Analyze button. The intended flow is: fill modal → save → analysis kicks off automatically. The auto-analysis goroutine that runs at FIT upload time (`internal/training/handlers.go:264`) is exactly what we need; it just isn't called from the context-save path.

### Backend change

In the handler that processes `POST` (or `PUT`) for workout_context (search for `UpsertWorkoutContext` callers in `internal/training/handlers.go`):

After successfully persisting the context row, spawn the same auto-analysis goroutine that the upload path uses. Conditions to gate on (mirror the upload path):
- User has the `claude_ai` feature flag enabled.
- (Implicit) The workout_context row now exists — by definition true after the save.

The goroutine path: set analysis_status='pending', then `RunClaudeAnalysis` under the existing `claudeSemaphore`, with status updates to 'completed' or 'failed' as applicable. Reuse `RunInsightsAnalysis` if `ai_auto_analyze` is enabled, the same as upload.

If the workout already has a non-failed analysis (status='completed' or 'pending'), skip the auto-trigger — the user can still click Re-analyze manually. This avoids surprise re-runs when the user edits a single context field.

### Frontend tweak

The detail page already polls `analysis_status === 'pending'` (line ~244 of TrainingDetail.tsx) and refreshes the analysis when complete. After the modal save resolves, if the workout's new analysis_status comes back as 'pending', the existing poll will pick up the result. Verify this works without additional changes; if not, add a small "we're running the summary" toast referencing the same i18n key that already covers the pending state.

## 2. Add "long run" and "tempo" run types

Today `RunType = 'slow' | 'interval' | ''` in `web/src/components/training/WorkoutContextModal.tsx`. Add two more:

```ts
export type RunType = 'slow' | 'long' | 'tempo' | 'interval' | ''
```

Update the `ToggleGroup` options at line 248–251 to include the new entries. Order suggestion: slow, long, tempo, interval (slowest → fastest).

Backend has no enum constraint on `run_type` (it's a free string in the DB and prompt), so no migration or backend validation change is required.

i18n keys to add under `workoutContextModal.runType.` in en/nb/th:
- `long` — "Long" / "Langtur" / Thai
- `tempo` — "Tempo" / "Tempo" / Thai

The Claude prompt builder consumes `run_type` verbatim. Verify the prompt template's wording still reads naturally for "long" and "tempo" — if the prompt has a `switch run_type { ... }` block (search `prompt.go` and `prompt_context.go`), extend the cases. If it just inlines the value as text, no change needed.

## 3. Interval/pause duration: minutes AND seconds

Today the SpeedPlanEditor (`web/src/components/training/SpeedPlanEditor.tsx`) takes interval and pause durations as **integer seconds only** — entering a 5-minute interval means typing 300. Two inputs side-by-side fix this without changing storage.

### UI change

For each `duration_sec` field (interval rows AND the shared pause duration block at line ~155), replace the single `<input type="number" />` with two adjacent inputs: minutes (number, min=0, step=1) + seconds (number, min=0, max=59, step=1). On change, derive `duration_sec = minutes * 60 + seconds` and write it to state. On render from existing state, split: `minutes = floor(duration_sec / 60)`, `seconds = duration_sec % 60`. Round-trip cleanly — entering 1m 30s persists as 90, then renders back as 1m 30s.

Keep the storage unit as `duration_sec` to avoid migrating any existing rows or backend code. Only the input widget changes.

i18n keys to add under `speedPlan.`:
- `min` — "min" (already may exist, check)
- `sec` — "sec" (existing — keep)
- `mmss` — "mm:ss" if you'd rather render a single mm:ss formatted input. Either UX is fine; pick the one that reads cleaner in the existing modal layout. Two inputs side-by-side is the lower-effort default.

Update the existing tests in `SpeedPlanEditor.test.tsx` (or add new): entering 2 min + 30 sec produces `duration_sec: 150`; loading a segment with `duration_sec: 90` shows 1 min and 30 sec; backwards-compatibility — a stored value of 75 renders as 1 min 15 sec.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test -- --run src/pages/TrainingDetail src/components/training/` all pass.
- Save the context modal → analysis_status flips to pending → polling completes → summary appears, no manual click required.
- Run-type toggle shows four options; selecting "long" or "tempo" persists and round-trips.
- Speed plan interval and pause duration accept minutes and seconds; existing workouts with `duration_sec` load correctly.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added and/or Changed).

## Non-goals

- Migrating existing `duration_sec` storage to a different unit. It stays in seconds; only the widget changes.
- Validating `run_type` server-side. Free-string today; remains so.
- Auto-running insights analysis from this path. Upload-time path already handles `ai_auto_analyze`; mirror it on save (not separate non-goal — same comment).
- Replacing the Analyze button. Manual re-run stays available.

## Reference

- Backend: `internal/training/handlers.go:225–298` (existing auto-analysis goroutine), `internal/training/analysis_handler.go` (AnalyzeHandler — the manual-click target), `internal/training/handlers.go` for the workout_context save handler (search for `UpsertWorkoutContext`).
- Frontend modal: `web/src/components/training/WorkoutContextModal.tsx` (lines 8 type, 248 ToggleGroup).
- Speed plan editor: `web/src/components/training/SpeedPlanEditor.tsx` (lines 155 shared pause, 211 per-row interval).
- Detail page polling: `web/src/pages/TrainingDetail.tsx:244–255`.
- i18n: `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/training.json` (or whichever namespace the modal uses).
