title: AI Summary
description:
### Scope
Add a per-workout "context" form (modal on first navigation to a workout) that captures treadmill vs outside, run type, HR source, free-text feel notes, and — for treadmill — a structured speed plan (warmup, intervals with same/varying speeds, pause speed/duration, cool-down). Defer Claude analysis until this context is provided, and feed the same context into the nightly stride evaluation as a per-workout note.

### Files to touch
- `internal/db/db.go` — add `workout_context` table (workout_id, surface, run_type, hr_source, feel_notes, speed_plan JSON, completed_at) via `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`; encrypt free-text and notes fields per CLAUDE.md.
- `internal/training/models.go` — `WorkoutContext` + `SpeedSegment` structs (kind, speed_kmph, duration_sec, repeats, "same as previous" flag).
- `internal/training/handlers.go` (new file `context_handlers.go`) — `GET/PUT /api/training/workouts/{id}/context`; encrypt/decrypt sensitive fields.
- `internal/api/router.go` — register the two routes under `RequireAuth` + `RequireFeature("training")`.
- `internal/training/analysis.go` / `analysis_handler.go` — gate auto-analysis on import: only enqueue/run when a context row exists; expose context to prompt builder.
- `internal/training/prompt_context.go` — inject surface, HR source, run type, feel notes, and resolved speed plan (override pace/speed when treadmill) into the Claude prompt.
- `internal/training/storage.go` — on FIT import, skip auto-trigger of analysis when no context; add helper to read context.
- `internal/stride/evaluate.go` — when assembling per-workout notes for the nightly run, pull `workout_context` and append the feel notes + structured plan.
- `web/src/pages/TrainingDetail.tsx` — detect missing context on first view, open modal; show "AI summary pending — provide workout context" state; allow re-opening to edit.
- `web/src/components/training/WorkoutContextModal.tsx` (new) — form with surface/run-type/HR-source toggles, conditional treadmill speed-plan builder (warmup, intervals with `+` to add segments and "same speed" toggle, pause speed+duration, cool-down), feel free-text, save handler.
- `web/src/components/training/SpeedPlanEditor.tsx` (new) — segment list editor used by the modal.
- `web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/training.json` — all new strings (modal labels, validation, status messages).
- `changelog.d/Hytte-<id>.md` (new) — `Added` entry.
- Tests: `internal/training/context_handlers_test.go` (new), update `analysis_test.go` to assert auto-run is skipped without context, `web/src/components/training/WorkoutContextModal.test.tsx` (new).

### Acceptance criteria
- Navigating to a workout that has no saved context opens the modal automatically; closing it leaves a visible "Provide context" CTA on the page.
- Modal lets user pick surface (Treadmill/Outside), run type (slow/interval), HR source (chest/watch), and write feel notes; submits via `PUT /api/training/workouts/{id}/context`.
- When Treadmill is selected, a speed-plan editor appears with default single flat speed and a `+` to add segments; each segment has speed (km/h) and duration; intervals support a "same speed for all" toggle that collapses to one input; a single pause speed+duration applies to all gaps.
- Outside workouts skip the speed editor (pace from device is used).
- A workout with no `workout_context` row never triggers Claude analysis automatically on import; the analysis endpoint also refuses (or returns 409) until context exists; once saved, analysis runs and uses the context.
- Nightly stride evaluation includes the per-workout context (surface, HR source, feel notes, treadmill plan) as a note for that workout.
- Encrypted at rest: feel notes and any free-text in the context use `encryption.EncryptField` on write, decrypt on read.
- All new UI strings exist in `en`, `nb`, and `th` and are used via `useTranslation`; modal usable at 375px width.
- `make test` and `npm run build` pass; changelog fragment present.

### Non-goals
- No retroactive backfill prompts for existing analysed workouts (context is optional for already-analysed ones; users can add it to re-run manually).
- No edits to FIT parsing or device pace calculation.
- No new admin/global settings; context is strictly per-workout.
- No changes to VO2max, race prediction, or training-load math.
- No mobile push notifications when context is missing.

## Source

Created from Suggestions page (suggestion id 143, page slug "training", source=user).

## Original suggestion

I think we need some manual input on a workout before the AI summary. Now it just blindly tries to figure out the workout, and ie if I have a treadmill one the pace could be way off, and the analysis doesn't really make sense anymore. So I'd like a modal/popup window the first time I enter/navigate to a workout. There i want to choose some information about it, Treadmill or Outside, slow run or interval, chest strap hr or watch hr, for outside the pace is fine, for treadmill i need to input the pace (or speed really as that translates easier frommy treadmill in kmph). Default it's a flat speed for the whole workout, but I need a + sign to add addional speeds for various intervals which also includes how long the speed is valid for. So for warmup, intervals, pauses, and cool down. I need speed and time for each interval, along with something like 'the same speed' for multiple. Same for pause, they're always at the same pace and time, so if I have 8 intervals either same of variying speed for each one, and one input for the pause which'll be for 7 pauses in this example. The AI summary won't run automatically on import anymore, but will wait until I provide the input. The same input should also be provided to the stide evaluation that runs nightly (it should be added as a note for that spesific workout). I also need a freetext field I can write how the workout felt, and other information if needed.

title: Suggestions: collapse page groups by default in Pending tab
description:
Follow-up to Hytte-ggkf. Page-group sections under the Pending tab currently render expanded by default. With many pages each holding ~3 cards, the tab is still long enough to need endless scrolling even with cards collapsed. Collapse the page-group sections by default so the operator sees a list of page headers and expands one section at a time.

## Change

In whatever component renders the page-grouped sections (introduced in Hytte-ggkf, likely under `web/src/components/suggestions/` or in `Suggestions.tsx`):

- Pending tab: each page-group section starts **collapsed**. Click the section header to expand.
- Per-section expand state still lives in component state (no localStorage persistence).
- Section header still shows the page title + count even when collapsed.
- Rejected and Created tabs: leave expanded by default. They are typically shorter, and the visual signal "what just shipped" / "what got dismissed" benefits from being at-a-glance visible.
- Planned tab: leave expanded by default for the same reason — the operator wants to see what's in the pipeline.

If the implementer thinks consistency across tabs is worth more than the per-tab tuning above, collapsing all four tabs by default is acceptable too. The operator can re-evaluate.

## Tests

Update the Suggestions tests:
- Pending tab page-group sections start collapsed; clicking expands.
- Counts on the section header still match the (collapsed) cards inside.
- Planned/Created tabs unchanged from Hytte-ggkf behaviour.

## Acceptance

- `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test -- --run src/pages/Suggestions` all pass.
- Open the Pending tab: only section headers are visible until clicked.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Reference

- The page-group section component from Hytte-ggkf is the only thing that changes — likely a single `useState(false)` instead of `useState(true)` per section, gated on `tab === 'pending'`.

title: Suggestions: cap pending per page and generate only the deficit
description:
With the rotation now running nightly, suggestions are accumulating faster than the operator can triage. After 2 runs there are already 61 suggestions across the registry. Cap pending per page at a configurable limit (default 3) and only generate the deficit, so a backlog never grows unbounded.

## Behaviour

For each page picked into a run:
- Count the page's existing **pending** suggestions (status='pending').
- `target = max(0, MaxPendingPerPage - pendingCount)`
- If `target == 0`, skip the page entirely (no Claude call, no spend, log a one-line "skip page X — at cap").
- If `target > 0`, ask Claude for exactly `target` suggestions.

`planned` and `bead_created` and `rejected` rows do NOT count against the cap — only `pending`. Rationale: once a suggestion has been planned, beaded, or rejected, it has been acted on; the page is fair game again.

## Two-stage filter

1. **Pre-rotation eligibility**: Before `PickRotation`, drop any page whose pending count is already at the cap. This keeps the rotation slots focused on pages that actually need work.
2. **Per-page target**: Inside `runForPage`, recompute the deficit (in case multiple pages share a slug or the cap was hit between filter and execution) and ask Claude for exactly the deficit count.

## Same cap for new-page suggestions

`RunNewPageSuggestion` should also respect the cap: if pending suggestions with `page_slug='__new_page__'` are at or above the cap, skip the new-page pass for that run. Reuse the same constant.

## Implementation sketch

In `internal/suggestions/`:
- Add `MaxPendingPerPage = 3` constant in `generate.go` (or `rotation.go` — same package).
- In `store.go`, add `PendingCountForPage(ctx, db, userID, pageSlug) (int, error)`. SELECT COUNT(*) FROM suggestions WHERE user_id=? AND page_slug=? AND status='pending'.
- In `rotation.go`, add `FilterUnderCap(ctx, db, userID, pages []Page, cap int) ([]Page, error)`. Drops pages already at or above cap. Used between `RotationEligible` and `PickRotation` in both the manual-run handler (`handlers.go`) and the scheduler goroutine (`cmd/server/main.go`).
- In `runForPage` (generate.go):
    - Compute `target := MaxPendingPerPage - pendingCount` after entering the function. If `<= 0`, log and return (0,0,0,nil).
    - Pass `target` down to `buildPagePrompt`, `callAndParse`, and `parseSuggestionsResponse`. They currently hardcode `3`; thread it through as a parameter.
- In `prompt.go` `buildPagePrompt`: change "Return ONLY a JSON array of exactly 3 objects" to "Return ONLY a JSON array of exactly N objects" (use the target). Update the example shape comment to show 1, 2, or 3 example objects depending on target.
- In `parseSuggestionsResponse`: accept the expected count as an argument; assert `len(items) == expected`. The "distinct types" check still applies for >1 results; it's vacuously true for 1.
- In `new_page.go`: at the top of `RunNewPageSuggestion`, count pending new_page suggestions; if at cap, return zero RunResult and a nil error with a one-line log line.

## Logs and run summary

The streaming run handler emits per-page events. Add a new event variant `page_skipped_cap` with `{ page_slug, pending_count, cap }` so the UI shows when a page is skipped because it's full. The frontend lives in the handler today (Hytte-39fp). Render this in the live progress log alongside the existing ok/error variants.

For the suggestion_runs row, the `page_slugs` CSV should still include skipped pages (so the operator sees what was attempted). The `generated` count won't increase for skipped pages, which is the correct outcome.

## Tests

In `internal/suggestions/`:
- `pending_count_test.go` (or extend store_test): round-trip for `PendingCountForPage`, including ignoring rejected/planned/bead_created statuses.
- `generate_test.go`: 
    - Page with 0 pending → mock Claude returns 3 → 3 inserted.
    - Page with 2 pending → target=1 → mock Claude returns 1 → 1 inserted; assert prompt asked for 1 (string-contains "exactly 1 object").
    - Page with 3 pending → skip; assert Claude was NOT called.
- `rotation_test.go`: `FilterUnderCap` drops capped pages, preserves order.
- `new_page_test.go`: at-cap branch skips entirely.

## i18n

Add to suggestions: namespace in en/nb/th:
- `run.pageSkippedCap` — "↳ {{slug}} (skipped — already at {{cap}} pending)" / Norwegian / Thai.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Trigger a manual run twice in succession on a page that started at 0 pending: first run produces 3, second run produces 0 and logs "skipped — already at 3 pending".
- Reject one of the 3, run again: 1 generated.
- Plan one of the 2 remaining: cap math doesn't count it (pending count drops to 1, target = 2 on next run).
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Non-goals

- A configurable per-page cap. Single global constant is fine; revisit if needed.
- Counting planned or bead_created against the cap.
- Auto-pruning old pending suggestions to make room. The user triages.
- Surfacing the cap value in the UI rotation settings panel. Could be a follow-up.

## Reference

- internal/suggestions/generate.go — RunSuggestionsForPages, runForPage, callAndParse, parseSuggestionsResponse.
- internal/suggestions/prompt.go — buildPagePrompt (the "exactly 3" string lives here).
- internal/suggestions/rotation.go — RotationDefaultN, PickRotation; new FilterUnderCap goes here.
- internal/suggestions/new_page.go — RunNewPageSuggestion.
- internal/suggestions/handlers.go — RunHandler (manual run, SSE).
- cmd/server/main.go — nightly scheduler goroutine; needs FilterUnderCap inserted between RotationEligible and PickRotation.
- web/src/components/suggestions/ + Suggestions.tsx — SSE event consumer; add page_skipped_cap variant rendering.

title: Forge nav link should route to Mezzanine instead of the old Forge page
description:
The "Forge" entry in the sidebar (`web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx:87`) currently navigates to `/forge`, which is the legacy ForgeDashboardPage. The Mezzanine page at `/forge/mezzanine` has fully replaced it for daily use.

## Change

Update the sidebar nav entry to point at `/forge/mezzanine` directly:

    { to: '/forge/mezzanine', icon: <Hammer size={20} />, label: 'nav.forge', requiresAuth: true, requireAdmin: true },

Don't remove the `/forge` route from `App.tsx` in this bead — that's a separate cleanup decision (deep-link bookmarks, internal links). Just retarget the nav.

## Tests

Update or add a small Sidebar test that asserts the Forge link's `href` is `/forge/mezzanine`.

## Acceptance

- Clicking the Forge sidebar entry takes you to Mezzanine.
- `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test` all pass.
- Active-link highlighting still works (the NavLink will be marked active on `/forge/mezzanine` and its sub-routes).
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed).

## Non-goals

- Removing the `/forge` route or `ForgeDashboardPage` component — defer to a follow-up if you want it gone.
- Migrating any internal links that hardcode `/forge`.

title: Suggestions: collapsible cards, group by page, separate Created tab
description:
The Suggestions page is hard to navigate now that real data flows through it. Three UX tweaks to make Pending / Planned / "shipped to a bead" tabs scan-friendly:

1. Collapse cards by default — only header info visible.
2. Group suggestions by page within each tab.
3. Move bead-created suggestions out of the Planned tab into a new Created tab.

## 1. Collapse cards by default

Today each suggestion card shows page chip, type/size badges, title, body, metadata, and actions all expanded. With dozens of cards per tab this is overwhelming.

New default: only the **header row** is visible. Header = page chip, type badge, size badge, title, source label ("by Claude" / "by you"), date, and a chevron. Clicking the row toggles expanded state, which reveals:

- The body (Pending) / plan markdown (Planned) / plan + bead link (Created)
- The action buttons (Reject, Plan it / Create bead, Open bead)
- The feedback textarea (when applicable)

Implementation notes:
- Add `expanded` state per card to `SuggestionCard.tsx` (or wherever the row is rendered today). Default `false`.
- Use `aria-expanded` on the toggle button for accessibility.
- Keep the header row clickable AND keyboard-activatable (`role="button"`, Enter/Space).
- When the user submits feedback or otherwise mutates the card, keep it expanded — only the initial state defaults to collapsed.
- Remember per-card expansion state across in-tab switches **but not** across page reloads (no localStorage). A simple `Map<id, boolean>` in the tab component is enough.

## 2. Group by page within each tab

Within Pending / Planned / Created, render suggestions as page-grouped sections instead of one flat list. Group order: alphabetical by page title (so "Budget" before "Weather"), with `__new_page__` rendered last under a section called "New page ideas" (i18n key, not hardcoded).

Each section has:
- A small section header: page title, route, count of suggestions in that section, and a toggle chevron.
- The collapsible cards listed under it.
- Section header itself is collapsible (clicking collapses the whole group). Sections expanded by default; remember per-tab in-component state across tab switches.

For sections containing zero items in the active tab, omit the section entirely.

## 3. New Created tab

Today bead-created suggestions live in the Planned tab (per the existing `counts` derivation in `Suggestions.tsx`). Split them out:

- Add a fourth tab "Created" between Planned and Rejected.
- The Pending/Planned tabs no longer include `bead_created` cards.
- The Created tab renders bead_created suggestions with the same collapsed-by-default + group-by-page treatment. The expanded view shows the plan + the bead ID as a clickable link to the bead detail (`/forge/anvils/Hytte/<bead-id>` if that route exists; otherwise plain text is fine).
- Tab counts on labels.

## i18n

Add new keys under `suggestions:` in en/nb/th:
- `tabs.created` — "Created" / "Opprettet" / Thai
- `groups.newPageIdeas` — "New page ideas" / "Nye sideforslag" / Thai
- `groups.toggleAria` — accessible label, e.g. "Toggle {{page}} section"
- `card.toggleAria` — accessible label, e.g. "Toggle {{title}}"
- `card.collapsed` / `card.expanded` — for SR readers if needed

## Tests

Update `Suggestions.test.tsx`:
- Cards render collapsed by default; clicking the header expands.
- Sections render in alphabetical-by-page-title order; `__new_page__` last.
- Created tab exists and contains only bead_created cards; Planned tab no longer does.
- Tab counts reflect the new partitioning.
- Section toggle hides/shows the cards under it.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, `npm test -- --run src/pages/Suggestions` all pass.
- Visual: a Pending tab with 3 pages × 3 suggestions = 9 cards is one scrollable page of section headers + collapsed rows, expanding any single card reveals its body and actions.
- Created tab is present, shows the cards previously bundled into Planned.
- i18n keys present in en/nb/th.
- Mobile-first works at 375px (sections wrap; chevron is tappable).
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Changed and/or Added).

## Non-goals

- Persisting expand/collapse state across page reloads. Simple in-memory state is enough.
- Bulk actions (select multiple, reject all). Could be a follow-up.
- Filtering or search within a tab. Same.
- Drag-to-reorder. No.

## Reference

- web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx — current shell with three tabs and the `counts` derivation that bundles bead_created into planned.
- web/src/components/suggestions/SuggestionCard.tsx — current card rendering.
- web/src/components/suggestions/SuggestionActions.tsx — Pending/Planned action buttons; should appear inside the expanded body.
- web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/suggestions.json — i18n target.

title: Split BudgetLoan.tsx into per-component files and a useLoans data hook
description:
### Scope
Refactor `web/src/pages/BudgetLoan.tsx` (~860 lines) by splitting its five embedded components into per-component files under `web/src/pages/budget/loan/`, extracting loans CRUD into a `useLoans` hook, simplifying the AmortizationTable cache to a single `cacheKey` string, and collapsing the duplicated effective-rate formatting in RateHistoryPanel. Behavior must remain identical.

### Files to touch
- `web/src/pages/BudgetLoan.tsx` (becomes a thin page shell that composes the extracted pieces and uses `useLoans`)
- `web/src/pages/budget/loan/CurrencyInput.tsx` (new)
- `web/src/pages/budget/loan/LoanForm.tsx` (new)
- `web/src/pages/budget/loan/RateHistoryPanel.tsx` (new — fold duplicated `Math.pow` block into a single `fmtPct(effectiveRate(...))` call; drop the `> 0` ternary)
- `web/src/pages/budget/loan/AmortizationTable.tsx` (new — replace `loadedLoanId`/`loadedRows`/`loadedVersion` with one `cacheKey` string compared per effect run)
- `web/src/pages/budget/loan/types.ts` (new — shared `Loan`, `AmortizationRow`, `LoanRateChange`, `AmortizationResponse`)
- `web/src/pages/budget/loan/format.ts` (new — shared `fmtPct`, `effectiveRate`, `localDateString` helpers)
- `web/src/pages/budget/loan/useLoans.ts` (new — list/create/update/delete loans + loading/error state)
- `web/src/App.tsx` (only if the import path needs updating; the existing `/budget/loans` route must still resolve to `BudgetLoan`)

### Acceptance criteria
- `/budget/loans` renders identically to today: list, create/edit form, rate history, and amortisation table all behave the same (golden-path manual click-through).
- `web/src/pages/BudgetLoan.tsx` is reduced to a small page shell; no extracted component is defined inline.
- `useLoans` owns the loans list fetch, create, update, delete, and exposes loading/error state; the page no longer manages those `useState`/`fetch` calls directly.
- `AmortizationTable` uses a single `cacheKey` string of the form `${loanId}:${rows}:${version}` for its load-skip check; the three separate `loadedLoanId`/`loadedRows`/`loadedVersion` fields are gone.
- `RateHistoryPanel` formats the effective rate via one `fmtPct(effectiveRate(...))` call; the duplicated `Math.pow(...)` expression and the meaningless `> 0` ternary are removed.
- All user-facing strings continue to use `react-i18next` `t(...)` keys; no new hardcoded English.
- `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, and `go test ./...` pass; `make build` produces a working binary.
- No changes to backend code (`internal/budget/loans.go`) or API routes.

### Non-goals
- No new loan features (extra payments, exports, CSV download, etc.).
- No backend changes, schema changes, or API contract changes.
- No styling, copy, or i18n key changes beyond what is mechanically required by the file split.
- No conversion to a state library (Redux/Zustand/React Query) — plain hooks + `fetch` only, per project conventions.
- No changes to other budget pages or shared sidebar/nav.

## Source

Created from Suggestions page (suggestion id 139, page slug "budget-loans", source=claude).

## Original suggestion

BudgetLoan.tsx is now ~860 lines containing five components (CurrencyInput, LoanForm, RateHistoryPanel, AmortizationTable, BudgetLoan) plus the loans-list fetching state, and the AmortizationTable's three-key cache check (`loadedLoanId`/`loadedRows`/`loadedVersion`) is hard to reason about. Extract each component into its own file under `web/src/pages/budget/loan/`, lift loans CRUD into a `useLoans` hook, and replace the manual cache fields with a single `cacheKey` string (`${loanId}:${rows}:${version}`) compared on each effect run. The duplicated effective-rate formatting in RateHistoryPanel (`Math.pow(...)` computed twice with a meaningless `> 0` ternary) collapses to a single `fmtPct(effectiveRate(...))` call. No user-visible change, but future loan features (extra payments, exports) become small per-file diffs instead of edits to a 900-line file.

title: Internationalize Dashboard activity feed and widget labels
description:
### Scope
Move English label construction out of the dashboard activity API and return structured fields so the frontend can render fully localized labels via react-i18next, restoring i18n compliance for nb/th users on the landing widget.

### Files to touch
- `internal/dashboard/activity.go` — extend `ActivityItem` with structured fields (`sport`, `title`, `comment`, `code`); stop building English labels; drop `sportLabel`. `Title` becomes the raw user-supplied value (may be empty).
- `internal/dashboard/activity_test.go` (new, if no existing test) — assert handler returns structured fields and no English-only labels.
- `web/src/components/widgets/ActivityFeedWidget.tsx` — update `ActivityItem` type, render labels via `t()` based on `item.type` and which optional fields are present.
- `web/public/locales/en/dashboard.json` — add `activity.workout`, `activity.workoutWithTitle`, `activity.lactate`, `activity.lactateWithComment`, `activity.note`, `activity.noteWithTitle`, `activity.shortLink`, `activity.linkWithTitle`, and a `activity.sports.*` map (mirror `widgets.training.sports`).
- `web/public/locales/nb/dashboard.json` — same keys, Norwegian Bokmål translations.
- `web/public/locales/th/dashboard.json` — same keys, Thai translations.
- `changelog.d/<id>-i18n-dashboard-activity.md` (new) — `Fixed` entry referencing the bead id.

### Acceptance criteria
- `GET /api/dashboard/activity` returns items with structured fields (`type`, `timestamp`, `link`, plus `sport`/`title`/`comment`/`code` where applicable) and no human-readable English label fields like "Lactate test recorded" or "Short link created: /go/...".
- `ActivityFeedWidget` renders all four item types using `t('dashboard:activity.*')` keys; no hardcoded English strings remain in the widget for activity rows.
- Switching language to `nb` or `th` in the UI immediately updates each row text (sport name, "Note:" prefix, "/go/CODE" template, etc.) without a page reload.
- An item with an empty user-supplied title falls back to the generic key (e.g. "Running workout recorded") in the active language; an item with a title uses the `*WithTitle`/`*WithComment` variant.
- Existing `go test ./...`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass; `en`, `nb`, `th` locale files contain identical key sets under `activity.*`.
- The widget continues to link rows to `/training`, `/lactate`, `/notes`, `/links` and to render `timeAgo` correctly.

### Non-goals
- Redesigning the activity feed UI, icons, colors, or sorting/limit logic.
- Adding new activity sources (only the four current types: workout, lactate, note, link).
- Encrypting or changing storage of titles/comments/codes.
- Localizing other dashboard widgets beyond the activity feed (they already use i18n or are out of scope here).
- Backend-side translation or `Accept-Language` negotiation — translation stays purely client-side.

## Source

Created from Suggestions page (suggestion id 1, page slug "dashboard", source=claude).

## Original suggestion

The Dashboard page itself has no JSX text, but the activity feed backend builds English-only labels like "Lactate test recorded", "Note: ...", and "Short link created: /go/..." in activity.go. This violates the project's i18n rule that all user-facing strings go through react-i18next, leaving Norwegian and Thai users with hardcoded English on a prominent landing widget. Move label construction into the frontend ActivityFeedWidget by returning structured fields (type, sport, title, code) from the API and rendering with t('dashboard:activity.workout', { sport, title }) keys added to en/nb/th. Users on nb/th finally see a fully localized dashboard.

title: Frontend Recent runs panel + i18n + tests
description: In web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx, add a collapsible 'Recent runs' panel (in header area or alongside rotation settings) that fetches GET /api/suggestions/runs?limit=20. Render rows with: started_at formatted in local time, a trigger badge (manual/scheduled), pages count (split page_slugs CSV), generated/errors numbers, and cost formatted as $0.0123 (4 decimal places) or '—' when cost_usd === 0. Below the list, show aggregate 'Last 7 days: $X.XX total, Y suggestions.' computed client-side from the returned rows. Add i18n keys for: panel title, trigger labels (manual/scheduled), cost-unknown placeholder, and the summary line. Add tests covering: empty state renders placeholder, populated list renders correctly, and currency formatting boundary (cost_usd of 0 shows '—', non-zero shows formatted dollars). Consumes the API shape from sub-task 2.

title: Suggestions runner integration + runs API
description: In the suggestions package, change runPromptFn type to the cost-aware signature and update RunSuggestionsForPages and RunNewPageSuggestion to accumulate cost across all Claude calls (including the new-page pass — depends on parent's sub-task 1). At the end of each run (both the manual HTTP handler and the scheduler goroutine), insert a suggestion_runs row with started_at, finished_at, trigger ('manual' or 'scheduled'), page_slugs CSV (include '__new_page__' when the new-page pass ran), generated count, errors count, and total cost_usd. Add GET /api/suggestions/runs?limit=20 returning rows newest first, admin-only (reuse existing admin middleware), default limit 20 with hard cap 100. Returns JSON array matching the table columns. Connects to sub-task 3 by defining the JSON shape the frontend consumes.

title: DB schema + RunPromptWithCost helper
description: In internal/db/db.go, add the suggestion_runs table (id, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL, started_at, finished_at, trigger TEXT CHECK in ('manual','scheduled'), page_slugs TEXT CSV that may include '__new_page__', generated INTEGER, errors INTEGER, cost_usd REAL DEFAULT 0, FOREIGN KEY user_id REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE) plus index idx_suggestion_runs_user_started on (user_id, started_at DESC). Wire it into the existing migration/init path. In internal/training/claude.go, add RunPromptWithCost(ctx, cfg, prompt) (text string, costUSD float64, err error) that invokes claude with --output-format=json and parses total_cost_usd from the JSON envelope (fall back to cost=0 with a sync.Once log on parse failure, never fail the call). Refactor the existing RunPrompt to be a thin wrapper that discards the cost. Foundation for siblings: sub-tasks 2 and 3 depend on both the table and the new helper.

title: New-page suggestion prompt and wiring
description: Add a separate Claude pass that proposes one entirely new page idea per run. Create internal/suggestions/new_page.go with RunNewPageSuggestion(ctx, db, cfg, userID) (RunResult, error) producing exactly one row with page_slug='__new_page__', type='new_page', source='claude', status='pending'. Add buildNewPagePrompt(allRegistered []Page, recentNewPageSuggestions []Suggestion) string that includes the registry (slug+title+description) and last-30-days new_page suggestions for de-duplication; strict JSON shape {size,title,body}. Add a parser mirroring parseSuggestionsResponse with one-retry-on-malformed-JSON, returning a single result. Wire into cmd/server/main.go:183 nightly scheduler (after per-page rotation, log-but-don't-abort on failure) and POST /api/suggestions/run handler in handlers.go RunHandler, folding generated/error counts into the existing RunResult. Frontend: SuggestionCard renders a distinct chip ('New page idea', nb/th i18n) when page_slug==='__new_page__'; new-suggestion form's page-slug dropdown includes '__new_page__' as a labelled option. Tests in internal/suggestions/new_page_test.go: happy path, malformed-JSON-then-retry-fails (no row), prompt context contains registry titles and recent new_page titles. Sibling dependency: cost-tracking sub-task will switch runPromptFn to a cost-aware version — write this pass to call runPromptFn so the swap is transparent.

title: Expand page registry to full Hytte surface

title: Suggestions: new-page prompt, cost tracking, and full page registry
description:
Final slice of the Suggestions feature. Three loosely-related chunks Forge can decompose if it likes:

1. A separate "propose a new page" Claude pass that runs alongside the nightly per-page rotation.
2. Cost tracking per run, so the operator can see what nightly suggestions are costing and adjust the rotation size.
3. Expansion of the curated page registry from 5 seeded pages to the full set of major Hytte feature pages.

## 1. New-page suggestion prompt

### Why

So far the generator only proposes improvements to existing pages. The original spec also asked for at least one entirely new page idea per night. This adds that pass.

### Backend

Add `internal/suggestions/new_page.go`:

- Function `RunNewPageSuggestion(ctx, db, cfg, userID) (RunResult, error)` — produces exactly one suggestion with `page_slug = "__new_page__"`, `type = "new_page"`, `source = "claude"`, `status = "pending"`. Uses runPromptFn just like RunSuggestionsForPages.
- Function `buildNewPagePrompt(allRegistered []Page, recentNewPageSuggestions []Suggestion) string` — prompt asks Claude to propose ONE new page idea given the current registry as context (slug + title + description for each entry). Context also includes recent (last 30 days) `new_page` suggestions across all statuses so Claude doesn't repeat itself. Strict JSON shape: `{ "size": "s|m|l", "title": "...", "body": "..." }` — note: `type` is not asked for here because we already know it's `new_page`.
- Parser similar to parseSuggestionsResponse but returning a single result. Same one-retry-on-malformed-JSON behaviour.

### Wiring

- Update the nightly scheduler goroutine in cmd/server/main.go: after the per-page rotation finishes for an admin user, also call `suggestions.RunNewPageSuggestion` once for that admin. Failure here should log but not abort.
- Update POST /api/suggestions/run (handlers.go RunHandler) to also trigger the new-page pass after the per-page run. Add the new-page generated/error counts into the existing RunResult.

### Tests

In a new `internal/suggestions/new_page_test.go`:
- Happy path: Claude returns valid JSON, one row inserted with the right shape.
- Malformed JSON triggers one retry then fails, no row inserted.
- The prompt context contains the registry titles and recent new_page titles (string-contains assertions are fine).

### Frontend

The Suggestions page already groups by status, not by page. New-page suggestions just appear in Pending alongside per-page ones. Two minor tweaks:

- Detect `page_slug === "__new_page__"` in SuggestionCard and render a distinct chip ("New page idea" / equivalent in nb/th) instead of the page slug.
- The new-suggestion form's page-slug dropdown should include `__new_page__` as an option labelled "New page idea" (or equivalent), so the user can author new-page ideas too.

## 2. Cost tracking

### Why

Robin asked to surface costs after a few runs to decide whether to adjust the rotation. Storage + a small UI element so this is visible without ssh-ing in.

### Schema

Add `internal/db/db.go`:

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS suggestion_runs (
      id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      user_id       INTEGER NOT NULL,
      started_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
      finished_at   TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
      trigger       TEXT NOT NULL,            -- 'manual' | 'scheduled'
      page_slugs    TEXT NOT NULL,            -- comma-separated, includes '__new_page__' if that pass ran
      generated     INTEGER NOT NULL,
      errors        INTEGER NOT NULL,
      cost_usd      REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- sum of per-call cost; 0 if unknown
      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
    );
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_suggestion_runs_user_started ON suggestion_runs(user_id, started_at DESC);

### Capturing cost

`internal/training/claude.go` — RunPrompt currently returns just (string, error). The Claude CLI's `--output-format=json` (or `--output-format=stream-json`) emits a `total_cost_usd` field in the result envelope. This may already be parsed somewhere in the codebase — look at training/claude.go and the chat package's session-aware variant before deciding. Two viable routes:

a. Add a sibling function `RunPromptWithCost(ctx, cfg, prompt) (text string, costUSD float64, err error)` that re-uses the JSON output path. RunPrompt stays as a thin wrapper for callers that don't care about cost.

b. Expose cost via a typed result struct `PromptResult { Text string; CostUSD float64 }` and return it from RunPrompt; update existing callers (chat, stride, this package) to read .Text. Bigger churn.

Pick (a) unless (b) is materially cleaner. Either way, the suggestions package's runPromptFn variable should switch to the cost-aware version so the generator can sum costs across calls.

If parsing cost from the CLI proves brittle or unavailable in the current configuration, store cost_usd = 0 and log once. Do NOT block the feature on cost data — the runs table is still useful even if cost is unknown.

### Recording runs

Wherever a run is initiated (the manual handler, the scheduler goroutine):

- Capture started_at at the start.
- Sum per-call cost into a running total.
- After the run completes, insert a `suggestion_runs` row with the totals.

### Backend endpoint

`GET /api/suggestions/runs?limit=20` — admin-only, returns the most recent N runs for the current admin user, newest first. Default limit 20, hard cap 100.

### Frontend

Small "Recent runs" panel on the Suggestions page (collapsible, in the header area or alongside the rotation settings panel). Each row: started_at (local time), trigger badge (manual/scheduled), pages count, generated/errors, cost (formatted as $0.0123 or "—" if zero). Add an aggregate at the top: "Last 7 days: $X.XX total, Y suggestions."

Tests cover: empty state (no runs), populated state with several runs, and currency formatting boundary (zero shows "—").

i18n keys for: panel title, trigger labels, cost-unknown placeholder, summary line.

## 3. Full page registry

### Why

The registry currently has 5 pages. The full Hytte app has many more — Dashboard, Calendar, Family, Stars, Math, Lactate, Stride, Grocery, Homework, Wordfeud, Vault, Webhooks, Infra, Transit, Skywatch, Allowance, Salary, Kiosk, Anvils/Forge dashboards, Workhours, Mezzanine, Chat, etc. Without expanding the registry, the rotation is artificially small.

### What to do

In `internal/suggestions/pages.go`, expand the `Pages` slice to cover every major feature with a Page entry. Group strongly-related sub-pages under one registry entry rather than registering each sub-route separately:

- e.g. one "training" entry whose SourceFiles includes Training.tsx, TrainingDetail.tsx, TrainingTrends.tsx, TrainingCompare.tsx, internal/training/handlers.go.
- e.g. one "lactate" entry whose SourceFiles includes LactateTests.tsx, LactateNewTest.tsx, LactateTestDetail.tsx, LactateInsights.tsx, internal/lactate/handlers.go.
- e.g. one "family" entry covering Family.tsx, FamilyChildDetail.tsx, FamilyRewards.tsx, family/* — and so on.

Walk `web/src/pages/` and `internal/*/handlers.go` to identify the full set. Aim for roughly 25–35 registry entries — enough to reflect the app's surface area, but grouped so each entry has a coherent description. If a page is purely admin/internal (e.g. ForgeCostsDashboardPage, AnvilsPage, IngotsPage), include it but mark its FeatureFlag and Description accordingly so Claude knows the audience is one user.

For each entry:
- Slug — short, lowercase, stable.
- Title — short human label.
- Route — one canonical route (use the parent route if there are sub-routes).
- Description — one sentence describing what the page does.
- SourceFiles — the relevant frontend page(s) AND backend handler file(s); cap at ~6 files per entry to keep the prompt bounded.
- FeatureFlag — the user_features key gating the page if any (see `internal/auth/middleware.go` `RequireFeature` callers and the feature list in CLAUDE.md). Empty string is fine if the page isn't feature-gated.
- Enabled / rotation — leave the in-code default to true; the DB layer (suggestion_page_settings) is the source of truth at runtime. If the registry's Enabled field was removed in the rotation bead (Hytte-5aq6), don't reintroduce it.

The init() check that enforces unique slugs already exists; new entries will be validated automatically.

### Tests

The existing tests should still pass. Optionally add a small test that asserts every registered page's SourceFiles all exist on disk relative to the repo root — catches typos. (`os.Stat` against `repoRoot()/<file>`.)

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- After a manual run with the rotation set to 5 pages, the response counts include the new-page suggestion (so generated >= 16 if no errors: 5 × 3 + 1).
- A new-page suggestion appears in Pending with the "New page idea" chip rendering distinctly.
- The new-suggestion form lets the user pick "New page idea" as a target.
- A `suggestion_runs` row is recorded for both manual and scheduled runs.
- The Recent runs panel shows the latest N runs with cost, or "—" if cost was not captured.
- The page registry covers the major Hytte feature pages (rough target: 25+ entries), with valid SourceFiles paths.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

## Non-goals

- Per-suggestion cost attribution. Run-level is enough.
- Cost budgets / alerts. Just display, no enforcement.
- Auto-tuning the rotation size based on cost. Manual decision after observing.
- A separate "New page ideas" tab on the Suggestions page. They live in Pending alongside per-page suggestions.

## Reference

- internal/suggestions/generate.go — RunSuggestionsForPages and parseSuggestionsResponse for the per-page pattern this should mirror.
- internal/training/claude.go — current RunPrompt; check for any existing cost parsing before adding a new path.
- cmd/server/main.go:183 — nightly suggestions scheduler from Hytte-ira9; this bead extends it.
- web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx — current page shell.
- web/src/components/suggestions/* — existing component layout to extend.

description:

  - PATCH /api/suggestions/pages/{slug} — body `{ rotation_enabled: bool }`. Upserts a row in suggestion_page_settings with updated_at = now. 404 if slug not in registry (validate against AllRegistered()). Returns the updated record.

description:
Foundation work that all other sub-tasks depend on.

Files to modify:

title: Backend: PickRotation + nightly scheduler
description:
Depends on sub-task 1 (RotationEligible + schema).

Files to create/modify:
- internal/suggestions/rotation.go (new): `func PickRotation(ctx, db, eligible []Page, n int) ([]Page, error)`. Single SQL query: `SELECT page_slug, MAX(generated_at) FROM suggestions GROUP BY page_slug`. Join in Go with eligible registry. Sort: pages with no prior suggestions first (alphabetical by slug among themselves), then ascending most-recent generated_at. If n<=0 or n>=len(eligible), return all. Const `RotationDefaultN = 10`.
- internal/suggestions/scheduler.go (new): `func NextScheduledRun(now time.Time, loc *time.Location) time.Time` mirroring stride.NextNightlyEvaluationRun — if before 03:00 today in Europe/Oslo, today 03:00; else tomorrow 03:00. Handle DST.
- cmd/server/main.go: add nightly goroutine mirroring the Stride pattern around line 148. On each 03:00 tick: enumerate admin users, for each load training.LoadClaudeConfig(db, adminID) — skip if !cfg.Enabled with a log line; compute eligible := RotationEligible; rotation := PickRotation(eligible, RotationDefaultN); call RunSuggestionsForPages with a 30-minute timeout context derived from notifCtx. Log one-line summary per admin: `suggestions: nightly run for admin=%d generated=%d errors=%d pages=%v`.

Tests:
- internal/suggestions/rotation_test.go: N=2 of 5 picks two least-recently-run; pages with no prior suggestions come first alphabetically; n<=0 and n>=len return all.
- internal/suggestions/scheduler_test.go: NextScheduledRun before/after 03:00 same day, DST spring-forward and fall-back transitions in Europe/Oslo.

Connects: consumes RotationEligible from sub-task 1; produces the runtime behaviour the admin UI in sub-task 4 controls. Independent from sub-task 3 except both depend on sub-task 1.

title: Suggestions: nightly scheduler, rotation, and per-page enable toggle
description:
Fourth slice of Suggestions. Adds (1) a nightly scheduler that runs the generator unattended at 03:00 Europe/Oslo, (2) rotation logic so only a subset of pages runs each night even as the registry grows, and (3) an admin UI for toggling which pages participate in the rotation.

## Context

Today RunSuggestionsForPages must be triggered manually via POST /api/suggestions/run. With ~70 pages eventually in the registry and three Claude calls per page, running everything every night is expensive and noisy. Rotation lets each page get touched ~once per week instead. Per-page toggle lets the admin opt pages out without touching the code registry.

User-authored suggestions are unaffected — they can target any page in the registry regardless of rotation enable state.

## Backend

### 1. New table for per-page settings

Add to internal/db/db.go createSchema():

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS suggestion_page_settings (
      page_slug    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
      rotation_enabled INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,  -- 0/1
      updated_at   TIMESTAMP NOT NULL
    );

No migration files; inline per project convention.

### 2. Page filtering — combine registry + DB

In internal/suggestions/pages.go, replace the existing EnabledPages() with two functions:

- AllRegistered() []Page — the unfiltered Pages slice (exposes the full registry to the UI).
- RotationEligible(ctx, db) ([]Page, error) — left-joins the registry with suggestion_page_settings. A page is eligible if:
    - It exists in the registry, AND
    - The DB has no row for it (default eligible) OR rotation_enabled = 1.

The existing in-code `Enabled` struct field can either be removed (the registry no longer carries an enable bit; DB is the source of truth) or repurposed as a "default" — recommend removing to avoid two sources of truth. If kept, document its meaning explicitly.

Also: the existing manual-run endpoint (POST /api/suggestions/run) should switch to using RotationEligible so it matches what the scheduler will pick. Do NOT apply rotation-quota limits to the manual-run endpoint — the manual run is for testing and should hit all eligible pages.

### 3. Rotation logic

In internal/suggestions/generate.go (or a new rotation.go file in the same package), add:

- `func PickRotation(ctx, db, eligible []Page, n int) ([]Page, error)`
- Selects up to n pages, ordered by ascending most-recent generated_at (so pages that haven't been run in the longest time go first). Pages with no prior suggestions for the user come first, ranked alphabetically by slug among themselves.
- Implementation: one SQL query over the suggestions table grouped by page_slug returning MAX(generated_at), join in code with the eligible registry, sort, slice to n.
- If n <= 0 OR n >= len(eligible), return all eligible pages (no slicing). The scheduler default is RotationDefaultN.
- Add a constant `RotationDefaultN = 10` in the same file. Easy to bump later.

### 4. Scheduler

Add a new goroutine in cmd/server/main.go, mirroring the Stride nightly evaluation pattern (cmd/server/main.go:148 onwards):

- Wake at 03:00 Europe/Oslo.
- Compute next-run time via a new helper `suggestions.NextScheduledRun(now time.Time, loc *time.Location) time.Time` (mirrors stride.NextNightlyEvaluationRun).
- On each tick:
    - Look up all admin users in DB. For each admin user:
        - Load training.LoadClaudeConfig(db, adminUserID). If !cfg.Enabled, log "claude not enabled for admin %d, skipping" and continue (do not spam errors).
        - eligible := RotationEligible(ctx, db)
        - rotation := PickRotation(ctx, db, eligible, RotationDefaultN)
        - result := RunSuggestionsForPages(ctx, db, cfg, adminUserID, rotation)
        - Log a one-line summary: "suggestions: nightly run for admin=%d generated=%d errors=%d pages=%v"
- Cap the overall scheduled run at, say, 30 minutes (30 * time.Minute) — enough headroom for 10 pages × ~90s + buffer.
- Honor notifCtx (the existing cancellation context used by other schedulers in main.go) so a graceful shutdown stops the scheduler cleanly.

Add a corresponding test file `internal/suggestions/scheduler_test.go` covering NextScheduledRun semantics (e.g. before 03:00 the same day → today 03:00; after 03:00 → tomorrow 03:00; DST transition correctness around Europe/Oslo).

### 5. Admin endpoints for page settings

Add to internal/api/router.go inside the existing is_admin block:

- GET    /api/suggestions/pages  — extend existing handler to also return the rotation_enabled state per page (nullable: null means default true). Today this endpoint returns just registry slug+title; add a `rotation_enabled` field. UI will use it for the toggle.
- PATCH  /api/suggestions/pages/{slug}  — body: `{ rotation_enabled: bool }`. Upserts a row in suggestion_page_settings. 404 if slug is not in the registry. Returns the updated record.

Tests for both endpoints in handlers_test.go: success path, validation, admin-only enforcement.

## Frontend

### Settings UI for page rotation

The cleanest place for this is a new "Settings" panel on the Suggestions page itself (a small sliding panel or a dedicated tab). Pick whichever fits Suggestions.tsx today. Acceptable shapes:

- A small gear icon in the header that opens a modal/sheet with a list of pages and a toggle for each.
- A new "Pages" tab alongside Pending/Planned/Rejected.

Either is fine — pick the one that's lower-friction in the current codebase.

Behaviour:
- Fetch the pages list (with rotation_enabled state) from GET /api/suggestions/pages.
- Render each page row: title, slug, route, toggle for rotation_enabled.
- On toggle, PATCH /api/suggestions/pages/{slug} with the new value. Show optimistic UI; revert on failure with an error toast.
- Show a small note explaining: "Pages off rotation are skipped by the nightly run, but you can still write your own suggestions for them."

### i18n

Add new keys under suggestions: namespace in en/nb/th:
- settings.title — "Pages in rotation" / "Sider i rotasjon" / Thai
- settings.description — the explanatory note above
- settings.toggleAria — accessible label for the toggle

### Header tweak

The existing header currently shows static "Next run: tonight 03:00" text per Hytte-30ko. Update it to read from a new client-side helper that uses Europe/Oslo and shows the next 03:00 in the user's locale (e.g. "Next run: tonight at 03:00" if before 03:00 today, "Next run: tomorrow at 03:00" otherwise). Keep it simple — no need for a backend endpoint.

### Frontend tests

In Suggestions.test.tsx (or a new test file for the settings panel):
- Toggling a page off optimistically updates the UI and PATCHes the backend.
- A failed PATCH reverts the toggle and shows an error.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- New schema applied on startup; verifiable by `sqlite3 hytte.db ".schema suggestion_page_settings"`.
- The scheduler logs a daily run summary.
- Picking N=2 with 5 eligible pages picks the two least-recently-run pages, deterministically.
- Toggling a page off in the UI causes it to be skipped by RotationEligible and by the manual-run endpoint.
- Toggling back on causes it to participate again on the next run.
- Manual-run endpoint still hits all eligible pages, no quota.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

## Non-goals

- New-page suggestion prompt — later bead.
- Cost tracking — later bead.
- A CLI for tweaking page settings — UI-only.
- Per-page schedule overrides (e.g. "weather every night, budget weekly") — later if ever; not now.

## Reference

- internal/stride/evaluate.go and cmd/server/main.go:148 — existing nightly-scheduler pattern (NextNightlyEvaluationRun, time.Timer, notifCtx wiring).
- internal/suggestions/handlers.go — admin handler pattern.
- internal/suggestions/generate.go — RotationEligible + PickRotation should live near RunSuggestionsForPages.
- web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx — current UI shell from Hytte-30ko.

title: Suggestions: bd create integration for Planned suggestions
description:
Third slice of Suggestions. Wires up the "Create bead" button on Planned cards (currently disabled per Hytte-30ko) so it shells out to `bd create` against the Hytte anvil, applies the `forgeReady` label, and links the resulting bead in the UI.

## Context

Planned suggestions today have a disabled "Create bead" button with a tooltip explaining the wiring lands later. This bead does that wiring. After this bead, a typical flow is: Claude generates a suggestion → user clicks Plan it → user reviews the plan → user clicks Create bead → Forge picks up the new forgeReady bead and dispatches it.

## Backend

### New endpoint

`POST /api/suggestions/{id}/bead` (admin-only, inside the existing is_admin block in internal/api/router.go).

Behaviour:

1. Load the suggestion via store.GetByID. 404 if not found.
2. Reject (400) if status is not "planned" — there must be a plan to use as the bead description. Suggesting "auto-plan first" is tempting but explicit two-step is what we agreed on.
3. Construct the bd create invocation:
   - cwd: resolve via `anvilDirForBead("Hytte-x")` from internal/forge/handlers.go (we just want the Hytte anvil path; an alternative is to just use `repoRoot()` from forge/handlers.go — pick whichever is simpler). The intent: `bd create` runs from the Hytte anvil so the issued bead gets the Hytte- prefix.
   - command: `bd create <title> --type feature --priority 3 --labels forgeReady --body-file -`
   - title: the suggestion's title, capped at ~120 chars.
   - description on stdin: the plan body, with a footer that includes:
     - "## Source"
     - "Created from Suggestions page (suggestion id N, page slug, source=claude|user)."
     - If feedback is non-empty: "## User feedback when planning" then the feedback text.
     - The original suggestion body under "## Original suggestion".
   - Use `exec.CommandContext` with a 60-second timeout, mirroring the patterns in internal/forge/handlers.go (AddLabelHandler, BeadDetailHandler).
4. Parse the bead ID from bd create's stdout. The output starts with `✓ Created issue: Hytte-XXXX — ...`. A simple regex `Hytte-[a-z0-9]+` against stdout is sufficient. If parsing fails but the command succeeded, log loudly and return 500 — we cannot link a bead we cannot identify.
5. On success, call store.MarkBeadCreated(ctx, db, id, beadID) and return the updated Suggestion (decrypted) in the response.
6. On bd create failure (non-zero exit), return 500 with the trimmed stderr in the error message. Do NOT mark the suggestion as bead_created.
7. The handler must be admin-only, follow the same auth gating pattern as the other suggestions handlers.

### Resolution of the bd binary

Use `resolveCommand("bd")` from internal/forge/handlers.go (or the equivalent helper there). This is important because forge runs under systemd which can strip user paths — the existing helper handles that.

### Tests

In internal/suggestions/handlers_test.go:

- Add a test for the new endpoint that mocks the bd invocation. The simplest approach: introduce a package-level `bdCreateFn` variable in handlers.go (or a small new file) that defaults to the real exec call and can be replaced in tests, mirroring the runPromptFn pattern. The test substitutes a stub that returns canned stdout containing "Hytte-test1234".
- Cover: 404 on missing id, 400 on non-planned status, success path returns updated Suggestion with bead_id set, bd failure returns 500 and does not move the row to bead_created.
- Confirm the description body assembled on stdin contains the plan body, the suggestion body, and the feedback when present.

### What NOT to do

- Do NOT actually call `bd label add forgeReady` as a follow-up command — the `--labels forgeReady` flag on `bd create` already does this. (We confirmed this in the earlier filing of Hytte-ipyx.)
- Do NOT support new_page suggestions specially yet — they go through the same flow with the same Hytte- prefix; the new_page treatment lives in a later bead.

## Frontend

### Suggestion actions

In web/src/components/suggestions/SuggestionActions.tsx (or wherever the Planned actions are rendered today, per Hytte-k8tl):

- Replace the disabled Create bead button with an enabled button.
- On click, POST to `/api/suggestions/{id}/bead` with credentials.
- Show a loading spinner while the call is in flight (typical 2–10s; cap UX expectations at 60s).
- On success, replace the card's display with a Planned → bead-created variant: the card stays in the Planned tab visually for the rest of this bead OR moves to a new "Created" subsection — pick whichever is simpler given the current code structure. The minimum: surface the new bead ID prominently with a link to `/forge/anvils/Hytte/<bead-id>` if that route exists today; otherwise just plaintext like `Hytte-abcd` is fine.
- On error, show the error inline beneath the button with a retry option. Do NOT remove the suggestion from Planned on error.

### Status filtering

The list endpoint already returns suggestions partitioned by status. Suggestions in `bead_created` status currently come back in the `planned` array (per the existing list handler) only if the handler doesn't filter them out — verify this and update either backend (add a 4th array key "createdBead" to the response) or frontend (filter client-side and render bead_created cards as a sub-list under Planned with a small visual distinction). Pick whichever is simpler. Either way: bead_created cards should still be visible somewhere in the UI so the user can see what they have shipped.

### i18n

Add new keys to web/public/locales/{en,nb,th}/suggestions.json:
- `actions.createBead` already exists from Hytte-30ko — reuse.
- `actions.createBeadInProgress` — "Creating bead…" / "Oppretter bead…" / Thai equivalent
- `actions.createBeadError` — "Failed to create bead: {{message}}"
- `status.beadCreated` — "Bead created"
- `meta.beadId` — "Bead: {{id}}"

### Frontend tests

Update web/src/pages/Suggestions.test.tsx:

- Add a test for the Create bead happy path (mock fetch returning the updated suggestion with bead_id set), assert the resulting card shows the bead ID.
- Add a test for the failure path (mock fetch returning 500), assert the error is shown and the card stays Planned.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- Backend: hitting `POST /api/suggestions/{id}/bead` against a planned suggestion creates a real Hytte- bead labelled `forgeReady` and updates the row to status=bead_created with the bead id stored.
- Backend: hitting it against a pending or rejected suggestion returns 400.
- Backend: non-admin returns 403/401.
- Frontend: clicking Create bead on a planned card kicks off the call, shows a loading state, and on success surfaces the bead id. On failure, shows an error and keeps the card in Planned.
- The created bead's description includes the plan body, the original suggestion body, and the feedback (when provided).
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

## Reference

- internal/forge/handlers.go — `anvilDirForBead`, `resolveCommand`, AddLabelHandler / BeadDetailHandler for the exec pattern.
- internal/suggestions/handlers.go — admin handler pattern; runPromptFn-style mock pattern.
- internal/suggestions/store.go — MarkBeadCreated already exists.
- web/src/components/suggestions/SuggestionActions.tsx — current Planned actions.

title: Suggestions: admin page UI + write-your-own + plan generation
description:
Second slice of the Suggestions feature. Builds on Hytte-ipyx (schema, registry, generator, manual-run endpoint already in place). Adds the admin UI, the user-authored suggestion path, and the suggestion → plan transformation step. Bead-creation integration is the next bead, not this one.

## Context

The Suggestions page is admin-only. Three tabs: Pending, Planned, Rejected. Each card shows page slug + type/size badges + title + expandable body + actions.

Two ways suggestions enter the system:
1. Claude-generated, via the existing POST /api/suggestions/run (already shipped in Hytte-ipyx).
2. User-authored, via a new "+ New suggestion" button on the page.

Both flows share the same downstream pipeline. The "Plan it" action sends the suggestion (plus optional feedback text) to Claude and stores the resulting plan; status moves pending → planned. "Plan it" is always required before bead creation — even tiny user-authored suggestions go through the plan step so the eventual bead has a useful description.

## Backend

### New endpoints (admin-only, inside the existing is_admin block in internal/api/router.go)

- GET    /api/suggestions                 → returns all non-deleted suggestions for the current admin user, grouped or filterable by status. Decrypts body/feedback/plan at the boundary. Response shape: { "pending": [...], "planned": [...], "rejected": [...] } so the UI does not need to make three calls.
- POST   /api/suggestions                 → user-authored creation. Body: { title, body, type, size, page_slug }. Validates: type in {addition, bugfix, improvement, refactor, new_page}, size in {s, m, l}, page_slug must either equal "__new_page__" or match a slug in the registry. Inserts with source="user", status="pending". Returns the created Suggestion.
- POST   /api/suggestions/{id}/reject     → marks rejected. Idempotent: rejecting a rejected suggestion returns 200 with the existing row (do not error).
- POST   /api/suggestions/{id}/plan       → body: { feedback?: string }. Calls Claude with a "turn this suggestion into an implementation plan" prompt that includes (a) the page metadata, (b) the suggestion title/body, (c) the optional feedback, (d) the standard reference patterns from CLAUDE.md (encryption, admin gating, etc., kept short). Stores the result via store.MarkPlanned, sets status=planned. Synchronous; cap at 120s timeout in the handler. Returns the updated Suggestion. Not idempotent — re-planning replaces the existing plan and refreshes planned_at.

### Plan prompt

Add internal/suggestions/plan_prompt.go with buildPlanPrompt(suggestion Suggestion, page *Page, feedback string) string. The prompt should ask Claude to produce a concrete implementation plan suitable for use as a Hytte bead description: scope, files to touch, acceptance criteria, non-goals. Output is plain markdown, NOT JSON — we want prose suitable for a bead body. Keep the prompt under ~3KB; do not include source files (that's the per-page generator's job; here we already have the suggestion body).

If the suggestion's page_slug is "__new_page__" or no longer in the registry, pass page=nil and let the prompt handle the absent-page case gracefully.

### Tests

- handlers_test.go: extend with admin-gating tests for each new endpoint, success paths, validation rejections (bad type, bad size, unknown page_slug), 404 on missing id, and the plan endpoint mocking runPromptFn (use the same package-level variable pattern that already exists in generate.go).
- store changes: GET /api/suggestions returns properly decrypted bodies even after Insert via the user endpoint (round-trip test).

## Frontend

### Routes & nav

- Add /suggestions to web/src/App.tsx, wrapped in <ProtectedRoute>. Render only when the auth context's user is admin (mirror what Settings does for admin-only sub-pages — feel free to add a small <AdminRoute> wrapper if it doesn't already exist; if it does, reuse it).
- Add a sidebar entry in web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx using the Lightbulb icon (Lucide), shown only when user.is_admin is true. Place it near the Settings entry.

### Page: web/src/pages/Suggestions.tsx

- Header: title, "Next run: tonight 03:00" hint (static for now — scheduler lands later), "+ New suggestion" button, "Run now" button (calls POST /api/suggestions/run).
- Three tabs: Pending (default), Planned, Rejected. Counts shown on tab labels.
- Card layout per suggestion:
    - Top row: page slug (chip), type badge (color-coded: addition=green, bugfix=red, improvement=blue, refactor=amber, new_page=purple), size badge (S/M/L pill).
    - Title (always visible).
    - Body (collapsed by default, expand on click).
    - Source indicator ("by Claude" / "by you") — small, low-emphasis.
    - Generated date.
    - Actions vary by status:
        - Pending: "Reject" (secondary), "Plan it" (primary, opens an inline form with optional feedback textarea + Submit). Submit shows a loading spinner; sync wait up to 2 minutes.
        - Planned: shows the plan body (markdown rendered); "Create bead" button is present but DISABLED with a tooltip "Bead creation lands in the next bead." (Wiring it up is Hytte-XXXX, the next bead.)
        - Rejected: no actions (or a small "Restore" button if trivial — optional, skip if it adds complexity).
- Empty states per tab.
- "+ New suggestion" form: page_slug dropdown populated from a small new endpoint OR from a static list mirroring the registry — your call. Simpler: add a tiny GET /api/suggestions/pages that returns [{slug, title}] from EnabledPages() plus the synthetic "__new_page__" entry. Form fields: page (dropdown), title (max 120 chars), body (textarea, max 4KB), type (dropdown), size (dropdown). Validate client-side, post to POST /api/suggestions, refresh the list.

### Styling & i18n

- Tailwind only, dark theme like the rest of the app.
- All user-facing strings via react-i18next. Add keys to web/public/locales/en, nb, th. Use one namespace, e.g. "suggestions:".
- Mobile-first: at 375px, the badge row should wrap and the card should remain readable. No fixed widths.

### Frontend tests

- Add a small test alongside Suggestions.tsx (vitest + RTL, follow Settings.tsx's existing test if there is one — otherwise minimal smoke test). Cover: tab switching, "+ New suggestion" form happy path with mocked fetch, "Plan it" loading state.

## Non-goals (NOT in this bead)

- bd create wiring on the "Create bead" button — this is the next bead. Show the button disabled with a tooltip.
- Scheduler / nightly cron loop — later bead.
- Page rotation logic — later bead.
- Per-page enable toggle UI — later bead.
- New-page prompt generation — later bead. (User-authored suggestions can already use page_slug="__new_page__".)
- Cost surfacing — later bead.

## Acceptance

- make build, make test, npm run lint, npm run build all pass.
- Visiting /suggestions as a non-admin redirects or 403s.
- Visiting as admin shows three tabs; with a fresh DB the empty states render correctly.
- "Run now" populates Pending with up to 15 cards (5 pages × 3).
- "+ New suggestion" creates a card that appears in Pending immediately.
- "Reject" moves a card from Pending → Rejected.
- "Plan it" with empty feedback turns a Claude-generated suggestion into a plan and moves it to Planned, plan markdown rendered.
- "Plan it" with non-empty feedback also works, and the feedback is reflected in the resulting plan.
- "Create bead" button is visible on Planned cards but disabled.
- Mobile viewport (375px): page is usable, no horizontal scroll, badges wrap.
- i18n keys present in en/nb/th; switching language updates UI text.
- Changelog fragment in changelog.d/ (category: Added).

## Reference

- internal/suggestions/handlers.go — existing admin handler pattern.
- internal/suggestions/generate.go — runPromptFn package-variable pattern for mocking Claude in tests.
- internal/training/claude.go — Claude CLI invocation (training.RunPrompt).
- web/src/pages/Settings.tsx — pattern for complex admin pages.
- web/src/components/Sidebar.tsx — sidebar entry placement, admin gating.

title: Suggestions: keep distinct types but drop distinct-sizes constraint
description:
Small follow-up to Hytte-ipyx. The current parser hard-enforces both distinct types and distinct sizes across the three suggestions per page. Distinct types is useful (avoids getting three refactors). Distinct sizes is too rigid — a page may legitimately have three small bug fixes, and forcing one s/m/l every time is artificial. The prompt itself only says "unless the page genuinely warrants it", so the hard size check causes wasted retries on otherwise-valid Claude responses.

## Changes

In `internal/suggestions/generate.go`, function `parseSuggestionsResponse`:

- Keep the distinct-type check (`seenTypes`).
- Remove the distinct-size check (`seenSizes` and the duplicate-size error branch).

In `internal/suggestions/prompt.go`, function `buildPagePrompt`:

- Update the "Distribute types and sizes" sentence to talk only about types. Keep the encouragement to vary sizes but make it clearly soft: e.g. "Use a mix of sizes when the work genuinely varies, but it is fine to return three small or three medium suggestions if that reflects the page."

## Tests

In `internal/suggestions/generate_test.go`:

- Update `validJSONResponse` (or whichever fixture currently uses three distinct sizes) so one size is repeated. Confirm parsing succeeds.
- Keep an existing case (or add one) where two suggestions share a type and assert it still fails with the duplicate-type error.
- Remove or invert the test that asserts duplicate sizes fail.

## Acceptance

- `make build`, `make test`, `npm run lint`, `npm run build` all pass.
- A response with three suggestions where two share `size: "s"` parses successfully.
- A response with two suggestions sharing a `type` still fails parsing (one retry, then the page reports an error).
- No changes outside `internal/suggestions/` and `changelog.d/`.
- Changelog fragment in `changelog.d/` (category: Changed).

title: Suggestions: schema, page registry, and manual generation run
description:
First slice of a new admin-only Suggestions feature. This bead lays the foundation: DB schema, a curated page registry, the nightly-style generator function, and a manual-trigger endpoint. No UI in this bead — that's the next bead.

## Context

The full feature: Claude generates 3 improvement suggestions per page on a nightly rotation (so each page is touched ~weekly, not all 70 pages every night), plus at least one new-page idea. Robin (admin only) reviews suggestions on a Suggestions page, can reject, can give optional feedback, can click "Plan it" to have Claude expand it into a plan, then "Create bead" to file it as a forgeReady bead. Robin can also write his own suggestions and run them through the same plan→bead pipeline. Pending suggestions carry over indefinitely until acted on.

This bead implements only the backend foundation. Subsequent beads will add: UI, bd-create integration, scheduler+rotation+per-page enable toggle, new-page prompt + cost surfacing.

## Scope of this bead

### 1. DB schema

Add to internal/db/db.go createSchema():

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS suggestions (
      id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
      user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL,
      generated_at    TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
      page_slug       TEXT NOT NULL,
      source          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- 'claude' | 'user'
      type            TEXT NOT NULL,         -- 'addition' | 'bugfix' | 'improvement' | 'refactor' | 'new_page'
      size            TEXT NOT NULL,         -- 's' | 'm' | 'l'
      title           TEXT NOT NULL,
      body_enc        TEXT NOT NULL,
      status          TEXT NOT NULL,         -- 'pending' | 'rejected' | 'planned' | 'bead_created'
      feedback_enc    TEXT,
      plan_enc        TEXT,
      bead_id         TEXT,
      rejected_at     TIMESTAMP,
      planned_at      TIMESTAMP,
      bead_created_at TIMESTAMP,
      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
    );
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_suggestions_user_status ON suggestions(user_id, status);
    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_suggestions_page_slug ON suggestions(page_slug);

No migration files — inline in createSchema() per project convention.

### 2. Encryption

Per CLAUDE.md, free-text fields must be encrypted at rest. Use encryption.EncryptField on write and encryption.DecryptField on read for: body_enc, feedback_enc, plan_enc. Title is short and not sensitive — leave plaintext for searchability. Handle decrypt errors gracefully (log + return ciphertext) per existing convention.

### 3. New package: internal/suggestions/

Files:

- pages.go — curated page registry. Hand-written struct slice, NOT auto-discovered. Each entry: { Slug, Title, Route, Description, SourceFiles []string, FeatureFlag string, Enabled bool }. Seed with exactly 5 pages for this bead: weather, budget, notes, training, links. Full registry comes in a later bead.
- generate.go — RunSuggestionsForPages(ctx, db, pages []Page) error: for each page, build a prompt (page metadata + truncated contents of page's main React file + truncated backend handler + git log -n 20 --oneline -- <files> + last 14d of pending/planned/bead_created suggestions for that page slug to discourage repeats), call training.RunPrompt, parse strict JSON response, insert 3 rows. Skip new-page generation in this bead. Errors per page should log but not abort the whole run.
- prompt.go — buildPagePrompt(page Page, recentGitLog string, recentSuggestions []Suggestion) string. Response schema requested: array of exactly 3 objects with fields {type, size, title, body}. Reject and retry once if JSON is malformed.
- store.go — Insert, ListByStatus, GetByID, MarkRejected, MarkPlanned (with plan), MarkBeadCreated (with bead_id), with encryption applied/reversed at the boundary.
- handlers.go — admin-gated:
    - POST /api/suggestions/run: triggers RunSuggestionsForPages over all enabled pages. Synchronous, with a per-page timeout (90s) and overall ctx timeout (10min). Returns counts: { generated, errors }.
- Wire routes in internal/api/router.go inside an admin-gated group (existing pattern from Claude AI settings — RequireAuth + is_admin check).

### 4. Tests

- store_test.go: round-trip insert/list/update with encryption verified (body_enc in DB ≠ plaintext, decrypted result matches).
- generate_test.go: mock training.RunPrompt (replace the package-level function variable, same pattern as chat/handlers.go runPromptFn). Verify: (a) 3 rows inserted per page on success, (b) malformed JSON triggers one retry then logs+continues, (c) per-page error doesn't abort other pages.
- handlers_test.go: admin-only enforcement, success path returns counts.

## Non-goals (explicitly NOT in this bead)

- Any frontend code
- Scheduler / cron loop in main.go (later bead)
- Rotation logic (later bead — for now, all 5 seeded pages run every invocation)
- Per-page enable toggle UI (the Enabled struct field is added now but not configurable yet)
- New-page suggestion prompt
- bd create integration
- Cost tracking
- Plan generation endpoint and feedback flow (later bead)

## Acceptance

- make build, make test, npm run lint, npm run build all pass
- Manually: hitting POST /api/suggestions/run as admin generates 15 rows (5 pages × 3) with encrypted bodies, distinct types/sizes, returns 200 with counts
- Hitting it as non-admin returns 403
- Changelog fragment added in changelog.d/ (category: Added)

## Reference

Follow these existing patterns:
- training/claude.go for Claude invocation
- chat/handlers.go for the runPromptFn package variable pattern (so tests can mock)
- stride/evaluate.go for nightly-style generation logic structure
- forge/handlers.go for admin gating pattern

title: Stride: feed recent nightly evaluations into the weekly plan-generation prompt
description:
The weekly plan generator (internal/stride/generate.go buildGeneratePrompt) sees 8 weeks of weekly volume aggregates and the previous week's plan, but it does NOT see the per-workout nightly evaluations from stride_evaluations. That means the coach plans next week without knowing whether last week's prescribed sessions were actually executed, skipped, missed, or hit with concerning flags (overtraining, HR-too-high, etc).

Add a 'Recent Workout Evaluations' section to the prompt, populated from the last ~14 days of stride_evaluations rows for the user.

Backend changes (internal/stride/generate.go):
- Add listRecentEvaluations(ctx, db, userID int64, since time.Time) function that returns evaluations from stride_evaluations (joining stride_plans for plan context if useful, plus workouts for date/sport/distance), ordered by workout/eval date ascending
- eval_json holds the structured Evaluation (PlannedType, ActualType, Compliance, Notes, Flags, Adjustments, Date) — unmarshal it for prompt rendering
- For each evaluation, render: date, sport (or 'rest day' if workout_id IS NULL), planned vs actual session type, compliance status, key flags (if any), the narrative notes (truncate to ~200 chars), and any adjustments (truncate similarly)
- Insert the new section into buildGeneratePrompt AFTER the 'Previous Week's Plan' block and BEFORE 'Additional Instructions' (custom prompt). Section heading: '## Recent Workout Evaluations (last 14 days)'
- Pass evaluations through buildGeneratePrompt's signature
- If the list is empty, omit the section entirely (don't emit an empty heading)
- Window: 14 days back from time.Now().UTC() — covers the previous full week plus a couple of days of buffer for end-of-week sessions evaluated late

Tests:
- generate_test.go: add a test that seeds stride_evaluations rows and asserts the prompt contains the rendered section with expected fields
- Test the empty-window path (no evaluations → section absent)
- Test truncation of long notes/adjustments
- Test that rest-day evaluations (workout_id IS NULL) render correctly

Non-goals:
- Not modifying nightly evaluation logic
- Not changing the schema
- Not exposing this in any UI — purely a prompt change
- Not changing the 8-week weekly summary window (separate signal)

Why this matters: the plan generator currently flies blind on whether prescribed sessions actually happened. Weekly aggregates show total volume but not session-level adherence. Adding the eval feedback closes the planning loop and lets the coach react to compliance, fatigue flags, and adjustment recommendations from the previous week.
acceptance_criteria:
- New listRecentEvaluations function returns evaluations from the last 14 days, ordered chronologically
- buildGeneratePrompt accepts the evaluations and renders them under '## Recent Workout Evaluations (last 14 days)' between the previous plan block and the custom-prompt block
- Each evaluation row in the prompt shows: date, sport (or 'rest day'), planned vs actual session type, compliance, flags (if any), notes (truncated to ~200 chars), adjustments (truncated)
- Empty evaluation list → section is omitted entirely
- Tests cover: populated section content; empty-window absence; rest-day rendering; long-text truncation
- go test ./internal/stride/... passes
- No schema changes, no new dependencies, no UI changes
