- Beads: runs the install script from the beads repo
- All with confirmation dialog (admin only)

This replaces the need to SSH in just to check 'what version am I running?'

title: Infra: 'Overall: Unknown' status — investigate and fix
description:
The Infrastructure page shows 'Overall: Unknown' even though services are running. Either no health checks are configured, or the health check logic has a bug when aggregating statuses. Check internal/infra/ for how the overall status is computed and why it returns Unknown.

Also add Hytte itself as a default health check service pointing to /api/health (or https://robinedvardsmith.com/api/health).

title: Forge Dashboard: cost charts + daily/weekly trends
description:
Cost dashboard section using Recharts:
- Today's spending: total, per anvil, per bead breakdown
- Weekly/monthly trend line chart
- Cost per bead bar chart
- Daily budget indicator (if daily_cost_limit is set)
- Top 5 most expensive beads this week

Read from bead_costs and daily_costs tables in state.db.

Also add push notification deep links — when a Forge webhook notification arrives (PR ready to merge, bead failed), tapping it opens the relevant Forge dashboard section instead of the GitHub PR URL.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/forge-web-dashboard.md (Phase 4)

title: Deploy: atomic dist swap to prevent 404 during builds
description:
During deploys, 'npm run build' rebuilds web/dist in-place. Any request hitting the server mid-build gets a 404 because dist/index.html is temporarily missing. This affects the kiosk tablet especially since it does fresh page loads every 30 seconds.

Fix: build to a temp directory, then atomically swap:
```bash
npm run build -- --outDir dist-new
rm -rf dist-old
mv dist dist-old
mv dist-new dist
rm -rf dist-old
```

Update .github/workflows/deploy.yml to use this pattern. The dist directory is always available — only the final mv is a brief moment where it doesn't exist, and mv on the same filesystem is atomic.

title: Kiosk: 't is undefined' error on Android 5 Firefox after e8ei fix
description:
Follow-up to Hytte-e8ei. The blank page was fixed (now shows an error message), but the kiosk still doesn't render on Android 5 Firefox. Error: 'Kiosk failed to load t is undefined'.

The 't' is the i18next translation function from useTranslation(). Either:
1. i18next fails to initialize on old browsers (missing Promise, fetch, or other polyfills)
2. The destructuring const { t } = useTranslation() results in undefined because the hook failed silently
3. The i18n bundle itself uses syntax the old browser can't parse

The e8ei fix likely added build.target or @vitejs/plugin-legacy but i18next or react-i18next still has code paths that need polyfilling. Check if the kiosk page needs a standalone i18n init that's simpler (hardcoded strings instead of async JSON loading) or if a polyfill for Promise/fetch is needed for the i18n HTTP backend loader.

title: Kiosk: blank white page on Android 5 / old Firefox
description:
The kiosk page renders as a blank white page on an old Android 5 tablet (Firefox). The main Hytte site works fine on the same browser/device — only the kiosk page fails.

Likely causes:
1. Kiosk page may use modern JS not supported by old Firefox (optional chaining ?., nullish coalescing ??, Promise.allSettled, etc.)
2. Kiosk auth flow (token from query param, fetch to /kiosk/data) may fail silently
3. CSS features specific to the kiosk layout (container queries, newer flexbox/grid) may not render
4. The kiosk route might not be bundled correctly (separate entry point?)

The fix should ensure basic compatibility with older browsers — or at minimum, show an error message instead of a blank page. Consider adding a Vite build target for older browsers (browserslist) or polyfills for the kiosk page specifically.

Test: load /kiosk?token=... in Firefox ESR or an old Chrome to reproduce.
notes:
Confirmed: fails in Firefox on Android 5 too, not just Chrome/WebView. Error: 'Kiosk failed to load t is undefined' — the i18n t() function or a destructured variable fails to parse. The Vite build target is too modern for Android 5 era browsers.

Fix must be in vite.config.ts — add build.target: 'es2015' for the kiosk page, or use @vitejs/plugin-legacy to generate polyfilled bundles. Since only the kiosk page needs old browser support, consider a separate build entry point for kiosk with lower target, rather than downgrading the entire app.

title: Webhooks: use summary field from Forge payload for richer push notifications
description:
The Forge webhook payload includes a 'summary' field with the PR title (e.g. 'PR #481 ready to merge: Fix crucible branch targeting (forge)') but the Hytte formatter (webhooks/format.go:76) only reads the 'message' field which lacks the title.

Update formatForgeNotification to prefer 'summary' over 'message' when available, as summary contains the human-readable title. This makes push notifications more useful on mobile — you can see WHAT the PR is about at a glance.

Depends on Forge-hdn1 also being fixed (Forge side includes the title in the message/summary).

title: Settings: use shared time picker for Quiet Hours input
description: The Quiet Hours time input in Settings uses a basic text/native input. Replace with the same time picker component used in Work Hours (dropdown with 15-min increments, scroll-to-adjust when focused). Consistent time input UX across the app.

title: Client-side image compression before upload
description: Before uploading the confirmed photo, resize/compress it client-side. Use a Canvas element: draw the image onto a canvas resized to max 1024px wide (maintain aspect ratio), then call canvas.toBlob('image/jpeg', 0.85) to get a compressed Blob. Replace the raw File in the FormData/upload payload with this compressed Blob. Wire this into the 'Confirm' action from sub-task 2 so compression happens between preview confirmation and actual upload. No new dependencies needed — pure browser APIs.

title: Photo preview with retake/confirm flow
description: After the user selects/captures a photo via the file input, display a thumbnail preview before the form is submitted. Add state to the upload component: 'idle' | 'previewing'. In 'previewing' state, show the image thumbnail plus 'Retake' (clears input, resets to idle) and 'Confirm' (proceeds with upload) buttons. This sub-task depends on the input changes from sub-task 1 being in place.

title: Direct camera capture input with mobile UI
description: Modify the chore photo upload component (find the file input related to Hytte-jq8k/6csl). Change <input type='file' accept='image/*'> to <input type='file' accept='image/*' capture='environment' />. On mobile viewports (CSS media query or JS window.innerWidth check), hide the default input and show a large, tappable camera button styled prominently. On desktop, keep existing file picker behavior. This is the minimal, cross-browser-compatible change.
close_reason: This task was already fully implemented and pushed in commits fc6d9bc and 90cfd83 on this branch. The chore photo input has `capture="environment"`, a large mobile camera button (full-width, py-4, Camera icon), and desktop retains the compact file picker. The changelog fragment is also in place.

title: Allowance: direct camera capture on mobile for chore photos
description:
The chore photo proof feature (Hytte-jq8k/6csl) uses <input type='file' accept='image/*'> which on mobile shows a file picker dialog (choose between camera, gallery, files). On a phone, kids should be able to tap a camera button and go straight to the camera — no picker menu.

Use capture='environment' attribute on the file input to prefer the rear camera directly:
<input type='file' accept='image/*' capture='environment' />

Also consider:
- Show a large camera button (not a small file upload) on mobile viewports
- Preview the photo before submitting (show thumbnail with retake/confirm buttons)
- Compress/resize the image client-side before upload (kids' phone cameras are 50MP+ now, no need to upload full resolution for chore verification — resize to max 1024px wide)
- Consider using the MediaDevices API (navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia) for a more app-like camera experience with a live viewfinder, though the input capture attribute is simpler and works across all mobile browsers

title: Frontend: camera button on chore completion and thumbnail in approval view
description: On MyChoresPage, after tapping 'Done', show a camera icon button that opens <input type='file' accept='image/*' capture='environment'>. On file selection, POST to /api/allowance/my/complete/{id} as multipart form data (photo is optional — submit without it if skipped). In the parent's pending-approvals view, show a camera icon badge on completions that have photos; tapping it shows a thumbnail (img src pointing to GET /api/allowance/photos/{completion_id}) with a tap-to-enlarge interaction. Depends on backend sub-task for the API endpoints.

title: Backend: photo upload, storage, and serving
description: Add photo_path TEXT column to allowance_completions table (migration). Update POST /api/allowance/my/complete/{id} to accept multipart form data — save file to data/chore-photos/{completion_id}.jpg and store path in DB. Add GET /api/allowance/photos/{completion_id} endpoint that serves the file with appropriate Content-Type. Add a cleanup job (cron or startup task) that deletes photo files and clears photo_path for completions older than 7 days. This sub-task produces the API surface consumed by the frontend sub-task.

title: Allowance Bingo: Frontend — 3x3 grid on MyChoresPage
description: Add a BingoCard component (3x3 grid) to MyChoresPage that fetches GET /api/allowance/my/bingo on load. Completed cells highlighted green. Trigger a celebration animation (CSS or existing animation utility, similar to Stars workout bingo in Hytte-gt09) on new line completion and full card. Display bonus rewards earned. Depends on sub-task 2 (API endpoint) being available. Reference Hytte-gt09 Stars bingo implementation for UI patterns.

title: Allowance Bingo: API endpoint GET /api/allowance/my/bingo
description: Add GET /api/allowance/my/bingo handler that calls GetOrCreateBingoCard and UpdateBingoProgress for the authenticated user, then returns the 3x3 grid with per-cell completion status, completed_lines count, full_card flag, and bonus amounts earned. Wire into the existing allowance router. Depends on sub-task 1 (backend logic). No frontend dependency.

title: Allowance Bingo: Backend — DB, model, and business logic
description: Create the allowance_bingo_cards table migration (user_id, week_start, cells JSON, completed_lines, full_card booleans). Implement GetOrCreateBingoCard (generate a random 3x3 grid from the challenge pool at week start), UpdateBingoProgress (compare cell challenges against this week's chore completions and mark cells done), and CheckBingoLines (detect completed rows/columns/diagonals and full card; award +15 NOK per new line, +50 NOK jackpot for full card — idempotent via completed_lines bitmask or stored flags). Challenge pool defined as a constant slice. No frontend dependency.

title: Allowance: optional photo proof for chore completion
description:
Kids can optionally attach a photo when marking a chore as done — proof of work before parent approves. Useful for chores where the result is visible (cleaned room, loaded dishwasher, etc.).

Flow:
1. Kid taps 'Done' on a chore → option to 'Add photo' appears (camera icon button)
2. Opens device camera or photo picker (use <input type='file' accept='image/*' capture='environment'>)
3. Photo uploaded to POST /api/allowance/my/complete/{id} as multipart form data
4. Photo stored on disk (not in SQLite) — e.g. data/chore-photos/{completion_id}.jpg
5. Thumbnail shown in parent's approval view — tap to enlarge
6. Photo deleted after 7 days (no long-term storage needed, just for verification)

The photo is OPTIONAL — the 'Done' button still works without one. Parent sees a camera icon on completions that have photos.

Backend: store photo path in allowance_completions (new column: photo_path TEXT). Serve via GET /api/allowance/photos/{completion_id}. Cleanup job: delete photos older than 7 days.
Frontend: camera button on MyChoresPage completion flow, photo thumbnail in parent's pending approvals.

title: Allowance: chore bingo card with bonus rewards
description:
Add a weekly bingo system for chores, similar to the Stars workout bingo (Hytte-gt09). 3x3 grid of chore-related challenges that refresh weekly. Complete a row/column/diagonal for bonus NOK.

Challenges from a pool like: 'Complete all daily chores before noon', 'Do a chore without being asked', 'Help a sibling with their chore', 'Do an extra chore', 'No missed chores today', 'Complete a team chore', 'Do the hardest chore first', 'Complete all chores 3 days in a row', etc.

Rewards: line = +15 NOK bonus, full card = +50 NOK jackpot.

Backend: allowance_bingo_cards table (user_id, week_start, cells JSON, completed_lines, full_card). GetOrCreateBingoCard, UpdateBingoProgress (check cells against completions), CheckBingoLines.
Frontend: 3x3 grid on MyChoresPage, completed cells highlighted green, celebration animation on line/full card.
API: GET /api/allowance/my/bingo

title: Frontend: AI Prompts section in admin Settings page
description: In the admin Settings page component (likely src/pages/Settings or similar), add an 'AI Prompts' section. On mount, fetch GET /api/settings/ai-prompts. Render one <textarea> per prompt key with a per-key 'Reset to default' button (calls DELETE /api/settings/ai-prompts/:key) and a single 'Save' button that calls PUT for each dirty key. Show success/error feedback. Depends on the backend sub-task being deployed or mocked.

description: Create or extend internal/settings/ai_prompts.go (or handlers/settings.go) with admin-only routes: GET /api/settings/ai-prompts (returns all keys + bodies), PUT /api/settings/ai-prompts/:key (upserts override), DELETE /api/settings/ai-prompts/:key (removes override, reverts to default). Update internal/training/insights.go to load prompts from DB at call time with in-memory default fallback (so missing DB rows fall back to the hardcoded strings). The treadmill caveat append logic stays in insights.go, appended after the stored base prompt. Depends on the DB migration sub-task.

title: DB migration: ai_prompts table (or columns) with defaults
description: Create db/migrations/<timestamp>_add_ai_prompts.sql (or .go migration). Add a dedicated ai_prompts table with columns: id, prompt_key (string, unique), prompt_body (text), created_at, updated_at. Seed default rows for keys 'analysis', 'comparison', 'training_load' with the existing hardcoded strings as default values. If using user_preferences, add prompt_key + prompt_body columns instead, but a dedicated table is cleaner. This migration is the shared foundation for the backend sub-task.

title: User-editable AI prompts in Settings (admin UI + persistence)
description: Schema: add prompt_key (string) + prompt_body (text) columns to user_preferences, or a dedicated ai_prompts table, with sensible default values for keys: 'analysis', 'comparison', 'training_load'. Migration in db/migrations/. Backend: expose GET/PUT /api/settings/ai-prompts (admin-only) endpoints; update internal/training/insights.go to load prompts from DB (with in-memory default fallback) instead of hardcoded strings — this is the integration point with sub-task 1 (treadmill caveat is appended after the stored base prompt). Frontend: add an 'AI Prompts' section to the admin Settings page; render one <textarea> per prompt key, a Save button, and a 'Reset to default' button per prompt. The Reset button calls DELETE or a reset endpoint to remove the override and revert to the default.
close_reason: Decomposed into 3 sub-beads: Hytte-434x, Hytte-xo68, Hytte-l6oy

title: Treadmill pace caveat in AI prompts
description: Modify internal/training/insights.go (or wherever analysis/comparison prompts are built) to detect treadmill workouts — check sport type field and/or workout tags for 'treadmill'. When detected, inject a caveat paragraph into the system prompt: 'This is a treadmill workout. GPS-based pace data is unreliable — base analysis on heart rate, HR zones, duration, and perceived effort rather than pace.' For cross-workout comparisons where one is treadmill and the other is outdoor, append an explicit note that pace comparison is not meaningful. No schema changes required; pure prompt-construction logic. Depends on sub-task 2 only insofar as the injected text should be appended after the user-editable base prompt, not hardcoded into it.

title: Training AI: treadmill pace unreliable — adjust analysis prompt + user-editable prompts
description:
Two related improvements to the AI workout analysis:

## 1. Treadmill pace caveat
When a workout is detected as treadmill (sport type or tag), the AI analysis and comparison prompts should de-emphasize pace data and focus more on heart rate, HR zones, and effort. Treadmill pace from a wrist GPS watch is notoriously inaccurate — it varies wildly between sessions even at the same actual speed. The prompt should include something like: 'This is a treadmill workout. GPS-based pace data is unreliable for treadmills — base your analysis primarily on heart rate, HR zones, duration, and perceived effort rather than pace metrics.'

For comparisons between a treadmill and outdoor workout, explicitly note that pace comparison is not meaningful.

## 2. User-editable AI prompts in Settings
Currently the AI analysis and comparison prompts are hardcoded — any adjustment requires a code change. Add a Settings section (admin-only) where the user can view and edit the system prompts used for:
- Single workout analysis
- Workout comparison
- Training load/status analysis
- Any other AI-powered analysis

Store in user_preferences with sensible defaults. The Settings UI should show the current prompt in a textarea, with a 'Reset to default' button. This way the user can tweak the prompts without deploying code — experiment with different analysis styles, add personal context (e.g. 'I'm training for a half marathon in June'), or adjust emphasis.

Check internal/training/ for where the AI prompts are built (likely in insights.go or similar) and make them configurable.

title: Wordfeud: anchor-based move solver + scoring engine
description:
Full board-aware solver in Go. Anchor-based move generation: find empty cells adjacent to occupied cells, for each anchor try all valid word placements (horizontal + vertical). Cross-word validation: all perpendicular words formed must be valid dictionary words. Scoring engine: letter values + board multipliers (2xL, 3xL, 2xW, 3xW) + 40-point bonus for using all 7 tiles. Endpoint: POST /api/wordfeud/solve (board state + rack → ranked moves with position, direction, score, breakdown). Target: <500ms for full solve. Frontend: top moves list, click to highlight move on board preview.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-wordfeud-solver.md (Phase 2)

title: Wordfeud: 15x15 board editor + multiplier layout
description:
Board component: interactive 15x15 grid with standard Wordfeud multiplier positions (TW=red, DW=pink, TL=blue, DL=lightblue, center star). Click cell to place letter, arrow keys to navigate, delete to clear. Board state: 2D array of cells with letter, isBlank flag. Color distinction for placed tiles. Multiplier cells visible as subtle background colors. Board state sent to backend as JSON. Tile tracker: show remaining tiles from the 104-tile Norwegian bag, subtracting placed board tiles and rack tiles. Display counts per letter.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-wordfeud-solver.md (Phase 2-3)
notes: With Wordfeud API integration (Hytte-2suz), the board editor can auto-populate from an active game. Keep manual editing as fallback, but the primary flow is: select game → board + rack auto-filled → solve.

The canary build 7.1.0-canary-46103596-20260305 is currently in use as it supports eslint@10. Track a stable release of eslint-plugin-react-hooks that declares eslint@10 peer support.

description:
Fetch NOAA solar wind data and Kp index for aurora probability. Bergen at 60N is prime aurora territory on active nights. Show current Kp index, aurora probability for tonight, best viewing direction and time. Could use NOAA SWPC API or similar. Display as a card on SkyWatchPage with color-coded probability (green=unlikely, yellow=possible, red=likely).

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-sky-watch.md (Phase 2+)

title: Work Hours: presets + Norwegian public holidays

Deduction preset management UI (create, edit, set default minutes, icon). Copy yesterday feature (prefill today from yesterday's sessions). Start/stop punch clock button (captures current time as session start, tap again to end). Norwegian public holidays: static list or library, auto-mark as day off in calendar view. Show holiday name.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-work-hours.md (Phase 3)

title: Sky Watch: tonight's highlights + conjunctions

Generate 'what to look for tonight' from computed positions. Detect conjunctions (two objects within ~5 degrees). Flag planets at opposition (brightest). Narrative text: 'Venus is the bright star in the southwest after sunset', 'The Moon is near Jupiter tonight'. GetTonightHighlights(date, lat, lon) []Highlight. Weekly earnings calculator (base allowance + completed chores + extras + bonuses). Auto-generate payout summary every Sunday (configurable). GET /api/allowance/payouts, POST /api/allowance/payouts/{id}/paid (mark as paid). Savings goals: kid sets target ('I want X, costs Y NOK'), shows progress bar and weeks remaining. Earnings history chart using Recharts. Push notifications: weekly summary to parent, approval notifications to kid.

title: Budget: charts + spending trends
description:
Reporting endpoints: GET /api/budget/trends?months=6. Frontend: BudgetCharts section using Recharts. Pie chart (spending by category, current month). Bar chart (monthly spending over 6-12 months). Line chart (net worth over time, sum of account balances). Monthly cash flow summary matching the sheet layout. Year-over-year comparison.

Loan/mortgage section: amortization table display (payment number, payment amount, principal, interest, remaining balance, date, rate). Support variable interest rates (4.8-5.0% from sheet). LTV ratio display (current vs max 85%). Could pull income data from salary estimator for loan servicing capacity. Property valuation tracker.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-budget.md (Phase 4)

title: Budget: recurring transactions + management UI
description:
Recurring transaction auto-generation: run daily or on page load, create transactions from recurring definitions that are due. Handlers: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/budget/recurring. Frontend: BudgetRecurring.tsx showing all recurring items with next occurrence, active toggle, edit frequency/amount/category.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-budget.md (Phase 3)

title: Transit: stop management + route filtering
description:
Stop search UI via Entur Geocoder API. Save/remove favorite stops in user_preferences (key: transit_stops, JSON array). Per-stop route filtering (only show specific line numbers). Drag to reorder stops. Settings panel (expandable section or separate route). Remove stop button with confirmation.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-skyss-departures.md (Phase 2)

title: Calendar: month + week grid views
description: Add month view (grid with event dots/pills) and week view (hourly slots with event blocks) to CalendarPage. View mode toggle. Consider @schedule-x/react or react-big-calendar for the grid. Day view with detailed hourly layout. Drag-to-navigate between views.\n\nImplementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-calendar.md (Phase 2)

title: Training page: auto-refresh workout list when new workouts are added
description:
When uploading FIT files via the monitor (file watcher in forgeLocal) or any other method, the training/workout list page doesn't update until the user manually refreshes the browser. It would be much better if the page detected new workouts and refreshed automatically.

Options to investigate (in order of simplicity):

1. **Polling** (simplest): The frontend polls GET /api/training/workouts every 10-30 seconds in the background. Compare the count or latest workout timestamp — if changed, re-fetch the list. Low overhead, works everywhere, no backend changes needed.

2. **Server-Sent Events (SSE)**: Add a GET /api/training/events endpoint that streams workout events (new_workout, updated_workout). The backend checks for DB changes on a timer and pushes events. Frontend uses EventSource to listen. More efficient than polling but needs backend work.

3. **WebSocket**: Similar to SSE but bidirectional (not needed here). More complex setup for no benefit over SSE.

4. **DB change detection**: SQLite doesn't have native change notifications, but the backend could:
   - Track the last-seen max workout ID or updated_at timestamp
   - On each poll/SSE tick, query for changes since the last check
   - Push to connected clients if changes detected

Recommended approach: Start with polling (option 1) — add a useEffect with setInterval that checks for new workouts every 15 seconds. If the count or latest timestamp changes, trigger a re-fetch of the workout list. This requires zero backend changes and works immediately.

For a better UX, show a subtle toast or banner 'New workouts available — click to refresh' rather than auto-replacing the list (which could be jarring if the user is scrolling/selecting).

title: Encrypt claude_cli_path in user preferences
description: Two touch points: (1) `internal/auth/settings_handlers.go` `PreferencesPutHandler()` — encrypt `claude_cli_path` value with `crypto.Encrypt()` before storing. (2) `internal/training/claude.go` `LoadClaudeConfig()` — decrypt the value with `crypto.Decrypt()` after reading; fall back to plaintext if decryption fails (legacy support). Uses the same `internal/crypto/` package as the VAPID sub-task but is independently changeable.

title: Encrypt VAPID private key in push notification storage
description: Modify files in `internal/push/` where VAPID keys are stored and retrieved. On write: call `crypto.Encrypt()` on the `private_key` field before inserting/updating the `vapid_keys` table. On read: call `crypto.Decrypt()` after fetching — if decryption fails (legacy plaintext), return the raw value as-is. The public key requires no change. No dependency on the other sub-task.

title: Encrypt VAPID private key and Claude CLI path in user preferences
description:
## Encrypt VAPID private key and Claude CLI path

### VAPID private key
The VAPID private key in the `vapid_keys` table is cryptographic material that signs push notifications. Currently stored in plain text.

- Find where VAPID keys are stored/read (likely `internal/push/`)
- On storage: encrypt the private_key field using `crypto.Encrypt()`
- On read: decrypt using `crypto.Decrypt()`
- The public key does NOT need encryption (it's meant to be shared)

### Claude CLI path in user_preferences
The `claude_cli_path` preference contains the user's home directory path. Encrypt it.

- In `internal/training/claude.go` `LoadClaudeConfig()`: decrypt the path after reading from preferences
- In `internal/auth/settings_handlers.go` `PreferencesPutHandler()`: encrypt before storing
- Or simpler: encrypt/decrypt at the preferences layer for specific keys

### Pattern
Use the shared `internal/crypto/` package. Handle decrypt failures gracefully (legacy plaintext values should still work — return as-is if decrypt fails).
close_reason: Previously decomposed into children; no remaining work

title: Add startup migration to encrypt existing plaintext data in-place
description:
## Startup migration to encrypt existing plaintext data in-place

Add a one-time migration in `internal/db/db.go` (in the `Migrate()` function) that encrypts all existing plaintext sensitive data. Follow the EXACT same pattern as the session token hash migration.

### Migration steps
1. Check sentinel: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_preferences WHERE user_id=0 AND key='data_encryption_migrated'`
2. If not migrated, iterate through each table:
   - `workouts`: encrypt title, notes columns
   - `lactate test stages`: encrypt stage data
   - `notes`: encrypt content column
   - `push subscriptions`: encrypt endpoint column
   - `vapid_keys`: encrypt private_key column
   - `user_preferences`: encrypt claude_cli_path values
3. For each row: read current value → `crypto.Encrypt(value)` → UPDATE in place
4. Set sentinel: `INSERT INTO user_preferences (user_id, key, value) VALUES (0, 'data_encryption_migrated', '1')`

### Safety
- Skip empty/null values
- If a value is already encrypted (starts with base64 pattern and decrypts successfully), skip it
- Wrap in a transaction per table for atomicity
- Log progress: 'Encrypting N workout titles...'

### Dependencies
- Requires the shared `internal/crypto/crypto.go` package to exist first
- Requires the storage layer encryption (Hytte-to51) to be in place so new data is also encrypted

title: Encrypt sensitive fields in training, lactate, notes, and push storage layers
description:
## Encrypt sensitive fields in storage layers

Add encrypt-on-write and decrypt-on-read for sensitive fields across all storage packages. Uses the shared crypto package (from the parent bead's first sub-bead which extracted `internal/crypto/crypto.go`).

### training/storage.go
- Encrypt: workout title (where title_source != 'user' — preserve user-entered titles as searchable), workout notes
- Decrypt on read in: GetByID, List, getWorkoutWithLaps
- Encrypt on write in: Create, Update

### lactate/storage.go  
- Encrypt: stage data, test notes/comments if any
- Decrypt on read, encrypt on write

### notes/storage.go
- Encrypt: note content (the markdown body)
- Decrypt on read in: Get, List
- Encrypt on write in: Create, Update

### push/push.go (or push/storage.go)
- Encrypt: subscription endpoint URLs
- Decrypt when sending notifications

### Pattern
```go
import "github.com/Robin831/Hytte/internal/crypto"

// On write:
encTitle, err := crypto.Encrypt(workout.Title)
// store encTitle in DB

// On read:
decTitle, err := crypto.Decrypt(row.Title)
// return decTitle to caller
```

### Important
- Do NOT encrypt fields used for filtering/sorting (sport, duration, distance, HR, timestamps, IDs, tags)
- Handle decrypt errors gracefully — if a value fails to decrypt, it might be legacy plaintext from before migration. Return as-is with a log warning.
- The migration bead (Hytte-5nuh) handles converting existing data. This bead handles ongoing encrypt/decrypt for new data.

title: Extract shared crypto package from existing infra EncryptToken/DecryptToken
description: Sub-task decomposed from Hytte-mxv9: Encrypt sensitive data at rest using application-level AES-256-GCM

title: Encrypt sensitive data at rest using application-level AES-256-GCM
description:
## Feature: Data encryption at rest

Extend the existing AES-256-GCM encryption infrastructure (used for Hetzner/GitHub API tokens) to cover all sensitive user data. This makes the database file unreadable without the encryption key.

### What to encrypt
- Workout data: title, notes (not sport/duration/distance — those are needed for queries)
- Lactate test stage data
- User notes content
- Push notification subscription endpoints
- VAPID private key
- Claude CLI path (contains home directory)
- Any future sensitive preferences

### What NOT to encrypt (needed for queries/filtering)
- Bead IDs, workout IDs, user IDs
- Timestamps, status fields
- Sport type, duration, distance, heart rate (needed for filtering/sorting)
- Tags and labels
- Email (needed for login matching)

### Existing infrastructure
- `EncryptToken(plaintext, key)` / `DecryptToken(ciphertext, key)` already exist in `internal/infra/`
- Encryption key stored at `~/.hytte/.encryption_key` (auto-generated, mode 0600)
- Uses AES-256-GCM with random nonce

### Implementation

1. **Extract crypto helpers** from infra into a shared `internal/crypto/` package (or `internal/security/`)
2. **Create encrypt/decrypt wrappers** that load the key once on startup and provide simple `Encrypt(plaintext) string` / `Decrypt(ciphertext) string` functions
3. **Encrypt on write**: In storage functions (Create, Update), encrypt sensitive fields before INSERT/UPDATE
4. **Decrypt on read**: In storage functions (GetByID, List), decrypt sensitive fields after SELECT
5. **Migration**: On startup, detect unencrypted data (plaintext vs base64-encoded ciphertext) and encrypt in place. Use a sentinel in user_preferences to track migration status (same pattern as session hash migration).
6. **VAPID private key**: Encrypt using the same infrastructure, decrypt when needed for push

### Important
- Encryption key is still on disk — an admin with server access AND the key can decrypt
- But the DB file alone (backup, leak, misconfigured permissions) reveals nothing
- Performance: AES-256-GCM is fast, encrypt/decrypt adds negligible overhead
- Workout list queries still work because we don't encrypt the filterable fields

### Files
- `internal/crypto/crypto.go` — shared Encrypt/Decrypt with key management
- `internal/training/storage.go` — encrypt/decrypt workout notes, titles
- `internal/lactate/storage.go` — encrypt/decrypt stage data
- `internal/notes/storage.go` — encrypt/decrypt note content
- `internal/push/push.go` — encrypt subscription endpoints
- `internal/infra/` — migrate existing EncryptToken to use shared crypto package
- `internal/db/db.go` — migration to encrypt existing plaintext data
notes:
IMPORTANT: Migration must follow the exact same pattern as the session token hash migration in internal/db/db.go. On startup:

1. Check sentinel: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM user_preferences WHERE user_id=0 AND key='data_encryption_migrated'`
2. If not migrated, iterate through each table with sensitive fields:
   - workouts: encrypt title (where title_source != 'user'), notes
   - lactate stages: encrypt stage data
   - notes: encrypt content
   - push subscriptions: encrypt endpoint URLs
   - VAPID keys: encrypt private_key
   - user_preferences: encrypt claude_cli_path
3. For each row: read plaintext → encrypt → update in place
4. Set sentinel: `INSERT INTO user_preferences (user_id, key, value) VALUES (0, 'data_encryption_migrated', '1')`
5. Migration runs ONCE — the sentinel prevents re-encryption of already-encrypted data

Detection: encrypted values will be base64-encoded and longer than the original plaintext. If a value is already base64 + correct ciphertext length, skip it (belt-and-suspenders safety).

title: Tag workouts without GPS data as treadmill and pass location context to Claude analysis
description:
## Feature

Workouts without GPS data (no position records in .fit file) are treadmill workouts. This should be automatically detected and communicated to Claude during analysis so it can tag appropriately (e.g. 'Treadmill Intervals' instead of just 'Intervals').

### Detection

During .fit parsing or workout creation, check if the workout has GPS/position data. If not, it's a treadmill workout. This could be:
- A flag on the workout record (`is_treadmill bool` or `location_type TEXT` — 'outdoor'/'treadmill'/'indoor')
- Or simply checked at analysis time by looking at whether samples have lat/lng data

### Pass to Claude prompt

In `BuildClassificationPrompt()` (`internal/training/analysis.go`), add context about the workout environment:

```
Location: Treadmill (no GPS data)
```

or:

```
Location: Outdoor (GPS track available)
```

This lets Claude generate more accurate titles ('Treadmill Intervals' vs 'Interval Training') and tags.

### Also check .fit metadata

The .fit file may contain additional clues beyond GPS absence:
- `sub_sport` field (e.g. 'treadmill', 'indoor_running')
- `device_info` / product name
- The referenced file `476070571738693837.fit` from forgeLocal should be checked for any treadmill indicators in its session/sport records

### Rule-based autotag

The rule-based autotagger (`internal/training/autotag.go`) should also apply an `auto:treadmill` tag when no GPS data is present.

### Files
- `internal/training/analysis.go` — `BuildClassificationPrompt()`, add location context
- `internal/training/autotag.go` — add treadmill detection
- `internal/training/fitparser.go` — check if GPS/sub_sport data is available during parsing

title: AI-generated workout titles based on workout classification
description:
## Feature

After Claude classifies a workout (Hytte-yjm), update the workout title to a human-friendly name like 'Treadmill Intervals', 'Long Run', 'Recovery Run', 'Tempo Run', etc. Currently workouts have generic titles from the watch (or none at all).

### How it works

Extend the `POST /api/training/workouts/:id/analyze` response (from Hytte-yjm). After Claude returns the classification tags, also ask it for a short descriptive title.

Update the prompt to include:

```
Respond: {"type": "intervals", "tag": "6x6min (r1m)", "summary": "...", "title": "Interval Training"}
```

The `title` should be a short (2-4 word) human-readable workout name — NOT the interval details (that's the tag). Examples:
- 6x6min intervals → 'Interval Training' or 'Threshold Intervals'
- 20x45s intervals → 'Short Intervals' or 'Speed Work'
- Steady 12km → 'Long Run'
- Easy 5km → 'Recovery Run' or 'Easy Run'
- Fast 10km → 'Tempo Run'

### Implementation

1. Update the Claude prompt in the analyze endpoint to request a `title` field
2. After receiving the response, update the workout's title in the `workouts` table: `UPDATE workouts SET title = ? WHERE id = ?`
3. Store the AI-generated title in `workout_analyses.title` as well (for reference)
4. Only update the workout title if the user hasn't manually set one (add a `title_source` column or check if current title is empty/default)
5. Show the AI title in the workout list and detail page

### Dependencies
- Hytte-yjm (AI workout tagging) must be completed first

title: Frontend route gating: hide pages and nav items based on feature permissions
description:
## Feature: Frontend feature gating

Gate frontend routes and navigation items based on the user's feature permissions returned by `/api/auth/me`.

### Changes to `/api/auth/me` response

The me endpoint now returns a `features` map (added in Hytte-bac):

```json
{
  "user": {
    "id": 1,
    "email": "robin@example.com",
    "name": "Robin",
    "is_admin": true,
    "features": {
      "dashboard": true,
      "weather": true,
      "calendar": true,
      "training": true,
      "lactate": true,
      "notes": false,
      ...
    }
  }
}
```

### Auth context

Update the auth context/hook (`useAuth`) to include `features` from the user object. Add a helper:

```typescript
function hasFeature(key: string): boolean {
  return user?.features?.[key] ?? false
}
```

### Route gating in App.tsx

Wrap protected routes with a feature check. If the user doesn't have the feature, redirect to `/dashboard` (or show a 'no access' message):

- `/training/*` → requires `training`
- `/lactate/*` → requires `lactate`
- `/infra` → requires `infra`
- `/notes` → requires `notes`
- `/links` → requires `links`
- `/webhooks` → requires `webhooks`
- `/admin` → requires `is_admin`

Dashboard, weather, calendar, settings, and login are always accessible.

### Navigation

Hide nav items for features the user doesn't have access to. Only show links/menu items for enabled features. The admin link only shows for admin users.

### Dashboard widgets

Conditionally render dashboard widgets based on features:
- FitnessWidget → requires `training`
- LactateSummaryWidget → requires `lactate`
- InfraStatusWidget → requires `infra`
- GitHubStatusWidget → requires `infra`
- ActivityFeedWidget → always show (filters items based on accessible features)

### Dependencies
- Hytte-bac (feature permissions backend + /api/auth/me features field) must be completed first

title: AI-powered workout comparison with natural language insights
description:
## Feature

Enhance the workout comparison page with Claude-powered analysis that provides natural-language insights about differences between two workouts. Store results to avoid re-running analysis, and allow browsing previous comparisons.

### Database: comparison_analyses table

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comparison_analyses (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    workout_a_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES workouts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    workout_b_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES workouts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    model           TEXT NOT NULL,
    prompt          TEXT NOT NULL,
    response_json   TEXT NOT NULL,
    summary         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    fitness_trend   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    created_at      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    UNIQUE(user_id, workout_a_id, workout_b_id)
);
```

Store the full Claude response JSON so we can display it without re-running. The UNIQUE constraint means re-analyzing the same pair overwrites the previous result (or use ON CONFLICT REPLACE).

### API endpoints

- `POST /api/training/compare/analyze` — accepts `{"workout_a_id": 1, "workout_b_id": 2}`. Checks for cached result first; if exists and not stale, returns it. Otherwise calls Claude, stores result, returns it.
- `GET /api/training/compare/analyses` — returns list of previous comparisons for the current user, ordered by created_at desc. Each entry includes: workout A/B titles, dates, summary, fitness_trend, created_at.
- `GET /api/training/compare/analyses/:id` — returns full analysis detail (including the full response_json).
- `DELETE /api/training/compare/analyses/:id` — delete a cached analysis (re-analyze next time).

### Prompt design

Send structured lap data, not prose:

```
Compare these two running workouts and provide training insights. Respond with JSON only.

Workout A (2026-02-21, 51:05, 10.0km):
| Lap | Duration | Distance | Avg HR | Pace/km |
| 1 (work) | 360s | 1237m | 151 | 4:50 |
...

Workout B (2026-03-12, 45:04, 8.9km):
| Lap | Duration | Distance | Avg HR | Pace/km |
| 1 (work) | 358s | 1302m | 163 | 4:34 |
...

Respond: {"summary": "...", "pacing": "...", "cardiac_drift": "...", "fitness_trend": "improving|stable|declining", "observations": [...], "recommendations": [...]}
```

### Frontend

#### On comparison page (`/training/compare`)
- Add 'Analyze with Claude' button below the existing numeric comparison
- If a cached analysis exists for the pair, show it immediately with a 'Re-analyze' option
- Show loading spinner while Claude processes
- Display the analysis in a styled card: summary, pacing analysis, fitness trend badge, observations list, recommendations list

#### New: Previous comparisons list (`/training/compare` or sidebar)
- Show a list of previously analyzed comparisons (from `GET /api/training/compare/analyses`)
- Each item shows: Workout A title vs Workout B title, date, summary snippet, fitness trend badge
- Clicking loads the full comparison page with cached analysis
- Delete button to remove old analyses

### Dependencies
- Hytte-gjx (Claude CLI setup) must be completed first
- Should respect feature gating: requires `training` + `claude_ai` features (from Hytte-bac/Hytte-kkz if landed, otherwise just auth check)

title: AI-powered workout tagging via Claude CLI
description:
## Feature

Add an 'Analyze with Claude' button on the workout detail page that sends lap data to Claude for intelligent workout classification. Store results to avoid re-running, and display cached analysis on the workout detail page.

### Database: workout_analyses table

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workout_analyses (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    workout_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES workouts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    analysis_type   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'tag',
    model           TEXT NOT NULL,
    prompt          TEXT NOT NULL,
    response_json   TEXT NOT NULL,
    tags            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    summary         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    created_at      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    UNIQUE(user_id, workout_id, analysis_type)
);
```

- `analysis_type`: 'tag' for classification, 'insights' for Hytte-5co training insights (same table, different type)
- `tags`: comma-separated AI tags (e.g. 'ai:6x6min intervals,ai:threshold')
- `summary`: human-readable summary from Claude
- UNIQUE constraint means re-analyzing overwrites the previous result

### API endpoints

- `POST /api/training/workouts/:id/analyze` — check for cached result first. If exists, return it. Otherwise call Claude, store result, return it.
- `GET /api/training/workouts/:id/analysis` — get cached analysis for a workout (returns 404 if none).
- `DELETE /api/training/workouts/:id/analysis` — delete cached analysis (triggers re-analyze next time).

### How it works

1. User clicks 'Analyze with Claude' on a workout detail page
2. Backend checks `workout_analyses` for cached result — if found, returns immediately
3. If not cached: loads the workout's lap data (duration, distance, avg HR, avg pace per lap)
4. Builds a structured prompt with the lap table + sport type + total duration/distance
5. Calls `training.RunPrompt()` (from Hytte-gjx) with a classification prompt
6. Parses Claude's response, stores in `workout_analyses`, applies `ai:` tags to workout
7. Returns the analysis

### Prompt design

Send structured data, not prose:

```
Classify this running workout. Respond with ONLY a JSON object.

Sport: running
Duration: 51:05
Distance: 10.00 km
Laps:
| # | Duration | Distance | Avg HR | Pace/km |
| 1 | 598s | 1450m | 132 | 6:52 |
| 2 | 360s | 1237m | 151 | 4:50 |
| 3 | 60s | 150m | 140 | 6:40 |
...

Respond: {"type": "intervals", "tag": "6x6min (r1m)", "summary": "6 intervals of 6 minutes at ~4:44/km with 1 minute recovery jogs"}
```

### Frontend

- Add 'Analyze with Claude' button on workout detail page
- If cached analysis exists, show it immediately with AI tags and summary
- Show a 'Re-analyze' option to force a fresh analysis
- Loading spinner while Claude processes
- Display: AI-generated tags (as badges), workout type, summary text

### Dependencies
- Hytte-gjx (Claude CLI setup) must be completed first
- Should respect feature gating: requires `training` + `claude_ai` features

title: Add Forge webhook source and PR Ready to Merge notification filter
description:
Currently Forge webhooks are classified as "generic" in the notification filter UI because there's no way to distinguish them from other webhooks. Once Forge sends X-Forge-Event header (tracked separately), Hytte should detect it and offer dedicated filtering.

## What to add

### push_dispatch.go
Detect X-Forge-Event header (case-insensitive, same pattern as X-Github-Event):
  forgeEvent := r.Header.Get("X-Forge-Event")
  if forgeEvent != "" { source = "forge" }
Pass forgeEvent as the eventType to isFilteredOut() and FormatWebhookNotification().

### format.go
Already handled by existing Forge-specific formatting (Hytte-q3x). No additional changes needed here beyond what that bead adds.

### filters.go
No changes needed — isFilteredOut() already handles arbitrary source/event combinations generically.

### Settings.tsx (web/src/pages/Settings.tsx)
1. Add forge to the sources array:
   { key: 'forge', label: 'The Forge', desc: 'Automated agent notifications (PR created, ready to merge, failures, etc.)' }

2. Add pr_ready_to_merge to event types:
   { key: 'pr_ready_to_merge', label: 'PR Ready to Merge', desc: 'PR passed CI and review, ready to merge' }

3. Show event type section when forge source is enabled (in addition to github).

### Preference keys
notification_filter_sources already handles arbitrary keys — "forge" works without backend changes.
notification_filter_events already handles arbitrary keys — "pr_ready_to_merge" works without backend changes.

## Depends on
Forge bead for X-Forge-Event header must ship first (or simultaneously).

## Full Forge event_type values for reference
pr_created, bead_failed, daily_cost, worker_done, bead_decomposed, release_published, pr_ready_to_merge, release

title: Auto-learn warden rules: quote multi-sentence YAML values to prevent parse errors
description:
When the auto-learn writer saves a Copilot-learned rule with a multi-sentence check: value containing a colon-space (e.g. \"convention: either...\"), the unquoted YAML string causes a parse error on next load:

  yaml: line N: mapping values are not allowed in this context

This breaks ALL warden rule loading until manually fixed. The writer in internal/warden/learn.go (or wherever rules are serialised) must quote check: and pattern: values that contain colon-space sequences, or use block scalar (|) style for multi-line values.

Fix: when writing rule fields, detect if the value contains \": \" and wrap in double quotes (escaping any interior quotes), or always use block scalars for check/pattern fields.
close_reason: Bead created in wrong repo — fix is in Forge's internal/warden, not Hytte

title: Improve push notification formatting for Forge webhooks
description:
Forge-originated webhooks currently fall through to the generic fallback formatter and produce useless notifications like:

**Title:** Webhook received
**Body:** POST api/hooks/<id> — 189 bytes

This happens because Hytte's formatter checks for GitHub's \`X-Github-Event\` header and for JSON keys \`event\`, \`action\`, or \`type\` — but Forge sends \`event_type\` (not \`event\`), causing the fallback path.

## Forge Webhook Payload Format

Forge sends a \`GenericPayload\` with these fields:
\`\`\`json
{
  "event_type": "pr_ready_to_merge",
  "bead_id": "ext-53",
  "anvil": "hytte",
  "message": "PR #53 ready to merge: https://github.com/Robin831/Hytte/pull/53",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-14T20:16:19Z"
}
\`\`\`

Known \`event_type\` values: \`pr_created\`, \`bead_failed\`, \`daily_cost\`, \`worker_done\`, \`bead_decomposed\`, \`release_published\`, \`pr_ready_to_merge\`, \`release\`

## Expected Result

**Title:** Forge: PR Ready to Merge
**Body:** PR #53 ready to merge: https://github.com/Robin831/Hytte/pull/53 (ext-53, hytte)

## Implementation

**\`internal/webhooks/format.go\`** — before the generic JSON fallback, detect Forge payloads by \`event_type\` field:
- Title: \`"Forge: <Human Readable Event>"\` (e.g. \`pr_ready_to_merge\` → \`"PR Ready to Merge"\`)
- Body: use the \`message\` field; append \`bead_id\` and/or \`anvil\` if present

**\`internal/webhooks/format_test.go\`** — add test cases for each Forge event type

The source classification (\`github\` vs \`generic\`) in \`push_dispatch.go\` can remain as-is — source detection is separate from display formatting.

description: .forge-logs/ directory in the root is untracked. It contains smith logs and should be ignored by git.

title: Webhooks: use summary field from payload for display
description:
After Forge-si43 lands, webhook payloads will include a pre-formatted 'summary' field. The webhooks page should prefer this field over its own parsing when available.

## Changes

In the generic handler (parseWebhook in Webhooks.tsx), before falling back to event/action/type extraction:

1. Check if parsed.summary exists and is a non-empty string
2. If so, use it directly as the summary — no further parsing needed
3. Check if parsed.source exists — if it matches a known source (e.g., 'forge'), use a dedicated badge
4. Check if parsed.detail exists — show it as a secondary line in the expanded view

## Forge Source Badge
Add a Forge entry to SOURCE_STYLES:
  forge: { label: 'FG', cls: 'bg-amber-900/60 text-amber-300' }

Detect via: parsed.source === 'forge'

## Display
List view: [FG] POST 'Release published: v0.3.1 (Forge)' — 3 hr ago
Expanded: detail text shown prominently above the raw JSON body
If parsed.url exists, show it as a clickable link in the summary area

## Backwards Compatible
If summary field is absent, fall back to existing generic parsing (event/action/type extraction). This ensures old payloads and non-Forge webhooks still display correctly.

## Convention for Other Senders
Any webhook sender can include a summary field to get rich display for free. Document this as a convention in the webhooks page or API docs.
notes: Cross-repo dependency: requires Forge-si43 (Send rich webhook payloads with pre-formatted summary) to land first in the Forge repo.

title: Webhooks: smarter event summaries with source-aware formatting
description:
PR #40 (Hytte-cbb) added source detection and smart summaries for known webhook sources (GitHub via X-GitHub-Event header, Slack, Stripe). However, custom/reformatted webhooks like those from The Forge fall through to the generic handler, which only shows the raw event field.

## Root Cause

The Forge sends simplified webhook payloads — NOT raw GitHub webhooks. The payload looks like:
{"event":"release_published","version":"v0.3.1","tag":"v0.3.1","release_url":"https://...","changelog_summary":"Scoop, Homebrew, tar.gz archives"}

This has no X-GitHub-Event header and no standard GitHub payload structure (no repository.full_name, no release.tag_name, etc.), so it falls through to the generic handler which only extracts the event field as-is.

The generic fallback (Webhooks.tsx line 205-214) does:
  event = parsed.event || parsed.action || parsed.type
  name = parsed.name || parsed.repository?.name
  summary = [event, name].join(': ')

Since the Forge payload has no 'name' field, it just shows 'release_published'.

## Fix: Smarter Generic Handler

The generic handler should enrich the summary by looking for common contextual fields beyond just event+name:

1. Humanize the event string: release_published → 'Release published'
2. Look for version/tag fields: append version info → 'Release published: v0.3.1'
3. Look for descriptive fields: changelog_summary, description, message, text, title
4. Extract repo/project name from URLs if not in a dedicated field (parse 'Forge' from release_url)
5. Show a secondary detail line with the summary text if available

### Desired Output for Forge Webhooks
List view: 'Release published: v0.3.1' (or even 'Release published: v0.3.1 (Forge)')
Expanded: changelog_summary shown prominently

### Generic Field Priority for Summary Enrichment
After extracting event/action/type, also check for:
- version, tag, tag_name → append to summary
- title, subject, name → append to summary  
- message, text, description, changelog_summary → show as detail line
- *_url fields → extract project/repo name as context

### Humanize Event Strings
Convert snake_case to Title Case: release_published → 'Release published', pull_request_opened → 'Pull request opened', etc.

## Additional: Forge as a Known Source
Consider detecting Forge webhooks specifically (User-Agent: Go-http-client + payload has event+version+release_url pattern) and adding a Forge source badge, similar to GH/SL/ST badges.

title: Webhooks: parse and display payload content in a readable format
description:
The webhooks page currently shows raw headers and body as plain text, making it hard to quickly understand what happened. The expanded view dumps everything equally — headers and body get the same visual weight, but the body (the actual message) is what matters most.

## Current Behavior
- List view shows only: METHOD, path, timestamp, source IP
- Expanded view shows all headers as a flat list, then raw body text
- JSON body is displayed as a single unformatted string
- No way to quickly see what the webhook is about without reading raw JSON

## Example
A GitHub release webhook currently shows as:
- List: 'POST / 12:47:11 [::1]:35856'
- Expanded body: '{"event":"release_published","version":"v0.3.1","tag":"v0.3.1","release_url":"https://github.com/Robin831/Forge/releases/tag/v0.3.1","changelog_summary":"Scoop, Homebrew, tar.gz archives for Linux/macOS"}'

## Desired Behavior

### 1. Smart summary in the list view
Parse the body and show a human-readable summary line instead of just method/path/IP:
- GitHub: 'Release published: v0.3.1 (Forge)' or 'Push to main: 3 commits'
- Generic JSON: show the event/action/type field if one exists
- Unknown: fall back to 'POST / — 204 bytes'

### 2. Formatted body in expanded view
- Pretty-print JSON with syntax highlighting (indented, colored keys/values/strings)
- Collapsible sections for large payloads
- Body should be visually prominent (above or more prominent than headers)
- Headers collapsed by default (most of the time you don't care about Cf-Ray)

### 3. Known webhook source detection
Detect common webhook sources from headers/payload and add context:
- GitHub (X-GitHub-Event header): show event type, repo, actor, action
- Slack (user-agent or payload structure): show channel, user, text
- Stripe (Stripe-Signature header): show event type, object
- Generic: show any 'event', 'action', 'type' field from top-level JSON
- Show a small source icon/badge (GitHub logo, etc.) in the list view

### 4. Improved list view columns
- Source icon/badge (GitHub, Slack, generic)
- Event summary (parsed from body)
- Timestamp (relative: '3 min ago', with full time on hover)
- Status indicator (payload size, or success/error if we track response)

### 5. Copy and link support
- Copy body as formatted JSON button
- Copy curl command to replay the webhook
- If body contains URLs (like release_url), make them clickable links

title: Precipitation chart for next 24 hours
description: Add a small bar chart or area chart showing precipitation amounts per hour for the next 24 hours. The data is already in the yr.no response (next_1_hours.details.precipitation_amount). Use a lightweight charting approach (either a simple SVG/CSS bar chart, or Recharts if we add it for the lactate feature anyway). Color-code: blue for rain, white/light for snow. This gives a quick visual answer to 'when will it rain?' which is arguably the most important weather question in Norway.

title: Sunrise/sunset times and daylight hours
description: Show sunrise and sunset times for the selected location. Can be calculated client-side from latitude/longitude using a sunrise equation (no API needed), or use the MET sunrise API (api.met.no/weatherapi/sunrise/3.0). Display in the current conditions section: sunrise time, sunset time, and total daylight hours. Useful context for a Norwegian app where daylight varies dramatically by season and latitude.

title: Expand hourly view to 24/48 hours with toggle
description: Currently showing next 12 hours in the hourly scroll. Add a toggle (12h / 24h / 48h) to expand the view. The data is already available from yr.no (returns ~72 hours of hourly data). Add subtle date separators when the view crosses midnight. Keep the horizontal scrollable layout but consider showing time labels more prominently for longer views.

title: Wind direction indicator and feels-like temperature
description: Enhance the current conditions card: 1) Show wind direction as a rotated arrow icon using the wind_from_direction field already in the API response (e.g. 180 degrees = arrow pointing north, meaning wind from south). 2) Calculate and display feels-like temperature using wind chill formula (when temp < 10C and wind > 1.3 m/s) or heat index (when applicable). The data is already available in the yr.no response. Show as a secondary value next to the actual temperature.

title: Home lab / infrastructure status page
description:
Monitor and display status of personal infrastructure on a modular, opt-in dashboard. Each monitoring module is configurable — only shows on the page if enabled. Auto-refresh with WebSocket or polling.

## Architecture

Pluggable module system where each data source is independent. Modules can be enabled/disabled per user or globally. Each module defines its own data fetching, display component, and refresh interval.

## Modules to implement

### 1. Hetzner VPS Stats
- CPU usage, memory usage, disk usage, network I/O
- Via Hetzner Cloud API (requires API token in server config)
- Show current values + sparkline history

### 2. Docker Containers (if running on VPS)
- Container name, status (running/stopped/restarting), uptime
- CPU and memory per container
- Via Docker API (socket or TCP)

### 3. Service Health Checks
- Configurable list of HTTP(S) endpoints to ping
- Response time, HTTP status code, up/down indicator
- Hytte itself as a default check
- Color-coded status (green/yellow/red based on response time thresholds)

### 4. System Services
- Systemd unit status for key services (nginx, app binary, etc.)
- Running/stopped/failed state
- Via SSH or agent on VPS

### 5. SSL Certificate Expiry
- Check TLS cert expiry dates for configured domains
- Warning thresholds (e.g. yellow < 30 days, red < 7 days)

### 6. DNS Monitoring
- Verify configured DNS records resolve to expected values
- Alert on unexpected changes

### 7. Uptime History
- Store health check results over time in SQLite
- Show availability percentage (99.9% etc.)
- Incident timeline with downtime periods highlighted

### 8. GitHub Actions Status
- Show CI/CD status for configured repositories
- Latest workflow run: success/failure/in-progress per repo
- Via GitHub API (requires personal access token or GitHub App)
- Link to workflow run details

### 9. Database Stats (Hytte internal)
- SQLite database file size
- WAL file size
- Number of users, sessions, lactate tests, etc. (table row counts)
- Could expand to query performance metrics later

### 10. Bandwidth / Transfer Usage (Hetzner)
- Monthly bandwidth usage from Hetzner Cloud API
- Current month vs quota/limit
- Historical usage trend (month over month)

## Future opt-in modules (not in initial scope)
- Kubernetes: pod status, deployments, node health (when/if cluster exists)
- Custom shell command modules (run arbitrary command, parse output)
- Log tail viewer (stream recent logs from services)

## Configuration
- Server-side config for API tokens and connection details (env vars or config file)
- Per-module enable/disable
- User preferences for which modules to show on their dashboard
- Configurable refresh intervals per module

## UI
- /infra route (protected)
- Grid/card layout, one card per module
- Each card shows module name, status summary, last updated time
- Click card to expand for detail view
- Auto-refresh indicator (polling interval or WebSocket)
- Global status bar: all-green / degraded / issues detected

## Backend API (all RequireAuth)
- GET /api/infra/status — aggregated status from all enabled modules
- GET /api/infra/modules — list available modules and their enabled state
- PUT /api/infra/modules/:name — enable/disable a module
- GET /api/infra/modules/:name/detail — detailed data for a specific module
- GET /api/infra/uptime?range=7d|30d|90d — uptime history data

## Technical Notes
- Backend: Go handlers in internal/infra/ package
- Each module implements a common interface (Name, Enabled, Fetch, etc.)
- Frontend: React page in web/src/pages/Infra.tsx, module cards as components
- Sidebar nav: Server icon from lucide-react, pointing to /infra
- Secrets (API tokens) via environment variables, never stored in DB
- Health check polling runs server-side on a timer, results cached in SQLite
notes: When implementing, add a nav link in the sidebar (Server icon from lucide-react) pointing to /infra

title: Personal dashboard: the command center
description:
Transform the dashboard from a sad profile card into a gorgeous, widget-based personal command center. This is the first thing you see after login — it should feel like mission control for your life.

## Layout

Responsive grid of widget cards. Each widget is a self-contained card with a consistent style (bg-gray-800 rounded-xl, subtle borders). Desktop: 2-3 column grid. Mobile: single column stack. Widgets should be reorderable via drag-and-drop (later enhancement).

## Widgets

### 1. Greeting & Time Hero
Full-width top banner. Dynamic greeting based on time of day ('God morgen', 'God ettermiddag', 'God kveld'). Current time in large elegant font with seconds ticking. Current date in Norwegian format. Subtle animated gradient background that shifts with time of day (warm sunrise tones in morning, cool blues at night). Show a motivational or humorous quote (rotating daily — could be a hardcoded set of running/cabin/Norway quotes, or fetched from an API).

### 2. Weather Snapshot
Compact weather widget for the user's home_location. Current temp (large), icon, and condition text. High/low for today. Next 3-hour precipitation preview (tiny bar chart or dots). 'Wind: X m/s' and 'Feels like: Y' in smaller text. Clicking anywhere navigates to /weather. Does NOT duplicate the full weather page — just a glanceable summary.

### 3. Quick Links / Bookmarks
User-configurable grid of bookmark tiles. Each tile: icon/favicon + short label. Default set: commonly used sites (GitHub, Gmail, etc.). Add/remove/edit via a small gear icon. Store in user_preferences as JSON. Compact 3x3 or 4x3 grid of small square tiles. Auto-fetch favicons via Google's favicon service or similar.

### 4. Lactate Fitness Summary (ties into Hytte-o9l)
Only shows if the user has lactate test data. Latest test date and key metrics: LT2 speed/pace, LT2 HR. Trend arrow (up/down/flat) comparing to previous test. Mini sparkline of LT2 speed over recent tests. Clicking navigates to /lactate.

### 5. Activity Feed / Recent Activity
Show recent actions across the app: 'You checked weather for Tromsø 2h ago', 'New lactate test recorded 3 days ago', 'Short link created: /go/foo'. Gives life to the dashboard even when you're just browsing. Simple chronological list, max 5-7 items.

### 6. Infrastructure Status (ties into Hytte-204)
Only shows if infra monitoring is configured. Traffic-light summary: green/yellow/red circle per monitored service. 'All systems operational' or 'N issues detected'. Clicking navigates to /infra.

### 7. Calendar / Upcoming Events (ties into Hytte-k5b)
Only shows if Google Calendar is connected. Next 3-5 upcoming events with time, title, and a color dot. 'Nothing scheduled today' if empty. Relative times ('in 2 hours', 'tomorrow at 09:00').

### 8. Norwegian Fun Widget
Because this is a hytte app, add personality. Rotating Norwegian fun facts, cabin wisdom ('Det finnes ikke dårlig vær, bare dårlige klær'), or dad jokes. Maybe a 'This day in Norwegian history' tidbit. Small, delightful, and changes daily.

### 9. Daylight Widget
Current sunrise/sunset for user's location. Visual arc showing where in the day you are (sun position on the arc). Hours of daylight + change from yesterday ('+2 min' in spring, '-3 min' in autumn). Especially meaningful in Norway with extreme seasonal variation.

### 10. GitHub Status (ties into Hytte-204)
Latest CI status for your repos. Green/red badges. Last commit message + time. Quick link to open PRs.

## Design Principles

- Widgets only render if they have data / are configured — no empty placeholders
- Each widget loads independently (parallel fetches, individual loading states)
- Dark theme consistent with rest of app (bg-gray-800 cards on bg-gray-900)
- Subtle animations: fade-in on load, smooth number transitions
- Time-of-day theming: the greeting banner could have a subtle color shift
- Mobile-first: widgets stack cleanly on narrow screens
- Performance: lazy-load widgets below the fold

## Technical Notes

- Dashboard page at /dashboard (existing protected route)
- Each widget is a separate React component in web/src/components/dashboard/
- Widget data fetched client-side from existing API endpoints where possible
- Quick links stored in user_preferences (key: 'dashboard_bookmarks', value: JSON)
- No new backend endpoints needed initially — reuse /api/weather/forecast, /api/settings/preferences, /api/lactate/tests, /api/auth/me
- Add new endpoints as other features land (calendar, infra)
- Consider a /api/dashboard/activity endpoint later for the activity feed

title: Create TodayView layout shell and route
description: Create the main TodayView component that serves as the Home page. Set up the route to replace the current Home page. Build the compact, single-screen layout grid (no-scroll design, watch-face aesthetic). Include a role-based content switcher that renders different widget sets for Parent, Kid, and Guest roles. Files: create `src/pages/TodayView.tsx`, modify `src/router.tsx` (or equivalent) to point `/` at TodayView. Key types: `type FamilyRole = 'parent' | 'kid' | 'guest'`. Use CSS grid or flexbox for a tight, above-the-fold layout. This is the shell that all other sub-tasks plug into.

title: Build data-summary widget components
description: Create compact, read-only widget components that display one-line summaries pulled from existing feature APIs/stores: BusDepartureWidget (next departure line), NetatmoWidget (indoor/outdoor temp), CalendarWidget (next 2-3 events), WorkHoursWidget (hours worked + remaining), KidsSummaryWidget (pending chores or stars earned), MoonPhaseWidget (tiny icon), ClockWeatherWidget (compact clock + weather). Each widget: files in `src/components/today/` (e.g. `BusDepartureWidget.tsx`). Each takes typed props or reads from existing stores/hooks. Keep rendering minimal — single line or small block. These widgets are consumed by the TodayView shell from sub-task 1.

title: Home: glanceable 'Today' summary page
description:
Replace the Home page with a single-screen 'Today' view that pulls the most useful data from all features — the stuff you want to see in 2 seconds without navigating:

- Next bus departure: one line, e.g. 'Bus 3 → Vadmyra in 12 min'
- Netatmo: indoor/outdoor temp, one line
- Today's calendar: next 2-3 events
- Work hours: 'Worked 5.5h today, 1.5h to go'
- Kids summary: '3 chores pending approval' or 'Emma earned 15 ⭐ today'
- Moon phase: tiny icon
- Clock + weather (compact)

Think watch face — tightest possible summary, all above the fold, no scrolling. The Dashboard is where you interact; this is where you glance.

Different content per family role:
- Parent: quick actions (approve chores, check schedule, bus times)
- Kid: stars balance, today's chores, streak flame
- Guest: login prompt

Depends on most other features being built first so there's data to show.

title: Wordfeud: multiplayer score tracking + game persistence
description:
Track both players: names, running scores, whose turn. Save/load board states in SQLite (multiple concurrent games). Move history with undo/redo. Game list page showing active games. Per-game state: board, rack, scores, turn, move log. Simple — just local state management, no Wordfeud API integration.

Implementation plan: .forge/plans/hytte-wordfeud-solver.md (Phase 4)
notes: Game persistence is largely handled by the Wordfeud API now — active games are fetched live. Local persistence is for: caching game state, storing analysis history, and offline fallback.

title: Hytte API proxy for beads commands (GitHub Actions integration)
description: Add authenticated endpoints to Hytte that proxy bd commands (close, update, etc.) so GitHub Actions can interact with beads without direct Dolt access. Hytte runs on the same Hetzner server as Dolt, so no tunnel needed. Endpoints: POST /api/beads/{id}/close, POST /api/beads/{id}/update. Auth via Bearer token (API key stored in user_preferences or a dedicated api_tokens table). Use case: GitHub Actions closing beads on PR merge, updating status on CI pass, etc.

title: Per-workout AI training insights on detail page
description:
## Feature (Stretch Goal)

Add an AI-generated training summary on the workout detail page that provides coaching-style feedback.

### How it works

1. 'Training Insights' button on workout detail page
2. Sends workout data (laps, HR, pace, sport, duration, distance) to Claude
3. Claude returns observations about effort distribution, HR zones, pacing, and suggestions

### Prompt

```
Analyze this running workout and provide coaching insights. Respond with JSON.

Date: 2026-02-21
Sport: running
Duration: 51:05, Distance: 10.00 km
Laps: [structured table]

Respond: {
  "effort_summary": "...",
  "pacing_analysis": "...",
  "hr_zones": "...",
  "observations": ["..."],
  "suggestions": ["..."]
}
```

### API endpoint

`POST /api/training/workouts/:id/insights`

### Frontend

Show in a collapsible panel on workout detail page. Cache in DB to avoid re-running.

### Dependencies
- Hytte-gjx (Claude CLI setup) must be completed first

title: Training dashboard: .fit file import, workout analysis, and progression tracking
description:
Import workouts from .fit files (exported from Coros) into Hytte for analysis and progression tracking. No Strava API needed.

## Core: .fit File Import
- Upload .fit files via drag-and-drop or file picker on /training page
- Parse using Go FIT SDK (github.com/tormoder/fit or similar)
- Extract: sport type, date/time, duration, distance, HR samples, pace samples, laps/intervals, cadence, calories
- Store parsed workout data in SQLite (workout summary + time-series samples)
- Support bulk import (drop a folder of .fit files)

## Workout Display
Keep it simple — the detailed view is in Coros/Strava. Show:
- List view: date, type (run/bike/swim/strength), duration, distance, avg HR, intensity indicator
- Summary cards: weekly/monthly volume (hours, km)
- Basic charts per workout: HR over time, pace over time (using Recharts)

## Workout Comparison (key feature)
Compare two workouts that share the same structure (e.g. two 6x6min interval sessions):
- Auto-detect interval structure from lap markers or HR/pace patterns
- Match workouts by structure: same number of intervals, similar durations (within tolerance)
- Side-by-side overlay: HR curves aligned by interval start, pace curves
- Delta view: HR difference per interval (is HR lower at the same pace = fitter?)
- Table: interval-by-interval comparison (avg HR, avg pace, max HR per interval)

## Progression Tracking (key feature)
For recurring workout types (e.g. weekly 6x6):
- Group workouts by detected structure
- Trend charts over weeks/months:
  - Average HR per interval at same pace → trending down = improving
  - Pace at same HR → trending up = improving
  - Recovery HR between intervals → faster drop = fitter
  - Time to reach target HR → longer = fitter
- Visual: sparkline per workout type showing fitness direction

## Threshold Analysis
Cross-reference with lactate test data (from Hytte-o9l):
- Map each interval to lactate zone (Z1-Z5) based on HR
- Flag workouts that are too hard (too much time above LT2) or too easy (never reaching LT1)
- Show zone distribution per workout and over time
- Compare actual training distribution to 80/20 or Norwegian model targets
- Indicate: 'This 6x6 was at 92% of LT2 avg HR vs 88% last month — getting easier'

## Technical Notes
- Backend: internal/training/ package — .fit parser, workout storage, comparison engine, progression calculator
- DB tables: workouts (summary), workout_samples (time-series, compressed), workout_tags (user labels like '6x6', 'long run')
- Frontend: /training page with upload, list, detail, compare, and trends views
- .fit parsing: Go library, runs server-side on upload
- No Strava API, no OAuth, no rate limits, no AI restrictions on data
- User can optionally tag/label workouts for easier grouping (auto-detect + manual override)
notes: I care about running, not cycling or anything else, so concentrate your effort towards running.

title: AI chat page via Claude CLI
description:
## Feature: Personal AI chat page

A personal AI assistant page at `/chat` using the Claude CLI (already installed on the Hetzner server at `/home/robin/.local/bin/claude`). No API key needed — uses the admin's Max plan via CLI.

### How it works

The backend spawns `claude` CLI with `-p - --model <model> --output-format text`, writes the prompt to stdin, reads the response from stdout. Uses `training.RunPrompt()` from Hytte-gjx (already implemented).

For conversation history, each message is sent with the full conversation context (system + prior messages) in a single prompt. The CLI is stateless — context is rebuilt per request.

### Database: chat tables

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_conversations (
    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    title       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    model       TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    conversation_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES chat_conversations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    role            TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'user' or 'assistant'
    content         TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
```

### API endpoints

- `GET /api/chat/conversations` — list conversations for current user (id, title, model, updated_at)
- `POST /api/chat/conversations` — create new conversation (optional title, model from user settings)
- `GET /api/chat/conversations/:id` — get conversation with all messages
- `DELETE /api/chat/conversations/:id` — delete conversation and messages
- `POST /api/chat/conversations/:id/messages` — send a message. Body: `{"content": "..."}`. Backend:
  1. Stores the user message
  2. Builds prompt from conversation history (all prior messages)
  3. Calls `training.RunPrompt()` with the full context
  4. Stores the assistant response
  5. Returns the assistant message
- `PATCH /api/chat/conversations/:id` — rename conversation

### Auto-title

After the first assistant response, if the conversation has no title, send a quick follow-up prompt: `"Give this conversation a short title (max 6 words). Respond with just the title, nothing else."` and store it.

### Frontend: `web/src/pages/Chat.tsx`

- Sidebar: list of conversations (newest first), new conversation button, delete option
- Main area: message list with user/assistant bubbles, input box at bottom
- Markdown rendering for assistant messages (use existing markdown renderer if available, or react-markdown)
- Code syntax highlighting in code blocks
- Loading indicator while waiting for Claude response
- Model shown in conversation header (from user's claude_model setting)
- Responsive: sidebar collapses on mobile

### Route

Add `/chat` to protected routes in App.tsx. Feature-gated by `claude_ai` feature (from Hytte-bac/Hytte-kkz).

### Context building

When sending a message, build the prompt as:

```
You are a helpful AI assistant. Respond in markdown when appropriate.

[Previous messages as User:/Assistant: pairs]

User: <new message>
```

Keep context window manageable — if conversation exceeds ~50 messages, include only the last 30 with a summary of earlier context.

### Dependencies
- Hytte-gjx (Claude CLI setup) — already done
- Should respect feature gating: requires `claude_ai` feature
